Navigate non-Hexagon file formats using a keyboard, keypad, and mouse
The following table sorts the action by navigation type.
Navigation
Action
Zoom in
Press CTRL + mouse wheel forward
Zoom out
Press CTRL + mouse wheel backward
View non-Hexagon files exceeding the size limit
You will get notifications when viewing files exceeding the size limit for the file type. This is because large file sizes may cause the rendition generation to fail. Below are the size limits for file types:
File Type
Size Limit
Excel
2 MB
CSV
1 MB
Word
10 MB
PDF
50 MB
CAD
2 MB
If the file size exceeds the size limit, you will see a notification message in Notifications 
 . The message will show whether the rendition has been generated for viewing or not. If the file is ready for viewing, you can open it in the View
and Markup Control.
Using the Markup Toolbar
From the Markup Toolbar, you can:
Download the document and markups locally
Print the document
Navigate pages of the document
Zoom in and out of the document
Change how the document is displayed
Pan and select within the document
Search the document
View and search multiple comments for editing
Select this
To do this
Menu
View and download the markups as comments and flattened.
Download the document (and any markups) as described in Download a file with markup layers.
 Download (Markups as comments)
 Download (Markups flattened)
 Print
Print the document as described in Print a non-Hexagon file format.
Switch to view mode
Toggle the switch to view mode to view the original document to the displayed document.
Panel
Toggle the panel open or close to view the following, if available:
Thumbnails
Outlines
Signatures
Zoom Controls
Zoom in to or out of the document. Use custom percentages to zoom or enter a value.
Fit to Width
Fit to Page
Marquee Zoom
Page Controls
Page Orientation
Rotate Clockwise
Rotate Counterclockwise
Page Layout
Single Page
Double Page
Cover Facing Page
Display Controls
Change how the document is displayed.
 Black
Background Color
Converts the background to black and inverts the color of the content.
 The Background Color settings are ignored if you print or download the document.
 Gray
Converts the background to gray.
 White
Retains the original background color.
 Original
Display Color
Retains the original color of the content.
 Grayscale
Converts the color of the content to different shades of gray.
 The Grayscale display color setting is ignored if you download the document.
 Black and White
Converts the color of the content to black or white, excluding any shade of gray.
The Black and White display option is available only when viewing CAD file types (.dwg, .dgn, and .dxf).
The option is not displayed when viewing a non-CAD file.
The Main Scheduler must be enabled when using the Black and White option.
Pan and Select
Toggle between panning around the document when the mouse is clicked and dragged, and using the mouse to
select items in the document.
 The currently selected option is shown in blue.
Search
Toggle the Search panel open or close to search the document for a specific word or phrase.
Comments
Toggle the Comments panel open or close to view or search any comments in the document. Multiple comments
can be selected for viewing or editing.
Download a file with markup layers
1. Select Menu
 from the Markup Toolbar.
2. Select a download option:
a. Download (Markups as comments) 
Only markups that are being reviewed or edited will be saved as editable comments in the PDF.
 Annotations change to a draft image if you download a file containing stamp, symbol, or image annotations into Adobe Acrobat with Markups as comments selected and you rotate the annotations. This is an
Adobe Acrobat limitation.
b. Download (Markups flattened) 
Only markups that are being reviewed or edited will be burned into the PDF, making the markups uneditable.
Print a non-Hexagon file format
1. Select Print 
 from the Markup Toolbar and select the required print options.
Print options
Option
Description
Pages to print:
All
Print each page of the file you are viewing.
Current Page
Print only the page you are currently viewing.
Current View
Print your current view. For example, if you are zoomed into a file, only the content that is included in your view is printed.
 This is recommended for better performance.
Specify Pages
Print each page you enter in the text box. You can enter a single page number, a comma-separated list, and/or a range of pages.
For example: 5, 9-15, 23, 62-86
Include comments
Include an additional page after each page printed. This page includes a summary of each comment made on the printed page.
Include markups
Include all annotations in the markup layers that are open for review.
Print Grayscale
Print page elements, including annotations, in shades of gray.
Print Quality
You can select High or Normal print quality.
Watermark
Add a watermark to the printed file. See the "Watermark options" table.
Watermark options
Option
Description
Choose a location to edit watermarks
Select where the watermark is placed on each page.
Text
Enter the watermark text.
Font
Choose a font for the watermark text.
Size
Choose a font size (from 1 to 1600) for the watermark text.
Opacity
Set the opacity of the watermark.
Style
Choose a color, or set the watermark text to boldface, italicized, or underlined.
Reset All Settings
Resets to default settings and empties the Text field.
Cancel
Cancels changes and returns you to the print dialog box.
OK
Saves changes and returns you to the print dialog box.
2. When you are done, select Print.
Using the Markup Layers Manager
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Using the Markup Layers Manager <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/63abcb25e0b03f43f0f762e9>
Markup layers are listed in the Markup Layers Manager panel, where you can:
Create, edit, consolidate, copy, or delete markup layers.
View a list of elements belonging to each markup layer.
Select which markup layers you want to view or edit.
Lock or unlock markup layers, which disables or enables the markup layer for editing, consolidating, or deleting.
Display or hide markup layers, which disables or enables the markup layer for editing, copying, consolidating, or deleting.
What are markup layers?
Markup layers are database objects that are related to the PDF rendition of a file. Each markup layer can contain multiple annotation elements that are used to add extra information, such comments, embedded callouts, or stamps and
other graphical details.
What can I do in the Markup Layers Manager panel?
Click this
To do this
NEW LAYER
Create a markup layer.
CONSOLIDATE
Combine the markups on the selected layers into a new layer.
Create a new markup layer by copying the selected markup layer.
Delete one or more selected markup layers.
Stop editing the markup layer.
 This is only displayed when the layer is being edited.
Display or hide the markup layer. If a markup layer is hidden, you cannot edit, copy, consolidate, or delete it.
Lock or unlock the markup layer. If a markup layer is locked, you cannot edit, consolidate, or delete it.
 The Markup Layers Manager panel is hidden by default. Select 
 to expand the panel.
What kind of files can I annotate?
You can annotate the following file formats:
File format

.dxf

.dgn

PDF

Text

 You can configure custom file extensions of the supported file types through the Native file extension parameter. For example, isometric file types have custom file extensions such as .i01, .i02, and .i03 for the .dgn native file
extension. Custom file extensions such as t01, t02, and t03 use the .tif native file extension. For more information, see Configuring custom file extensions.
In this section:
Create a markup layer
Edit a markup layer
View a markup layer
Copy a markup layer
Consolidate markup layers
Lock or unlock markup layers
Delete one or more markup layers
Create a markup layer
1. On the Markup Layers Manager toolbar, select NEW LAYER.
2. In the Create Markup Layer dialog, enter a Description.
3. Select FINISH when you are done.
For annotation help, see Annotating a file using markup layers.
Markup layers remain in files attached to documents throughout a workflow.
Markup layers saved in a previous file format are automatically converted to a supported file format when viewing a file or when generating an alternate rendition. You see a notification after the conversion.
Markup layers are automatically related to the markup rendition of a file. This applies to all file formats except PDFs, which do not require a markup rendition.
View a markup layer
To view a markup layer, select it from the Markup Layers Manager panel.
The status is indicated as follows:
Status
Icon
Description
Displayed
The markup layer is displayed along with its annotations.
Hidden
The markup layer is hidden along with its annotations.
 To display a hidden markup layer, select Review Layer 
.
PDF files that contain third-party annotations created outside of HxGN SDx2, such as embedded call outs, stamps, and so on, are displayed in the Default Layer. The default layer is read-only and cannot be copied, modified,
consolidated, or deleted.
If a markup layer is hidden, you cannot edit, copy, consolidate, or delete it.
Changing the visibility of a markup layer will have no effect on annotations hidden by default.
Edit a markup layer
To edit a markup layer, select it from the Markup Layers Manager panel. The layer must be either unlocked or visible.
For annotation help, see Annotating a file using markup layers.
 is displayed in the Markup Layers Manager panel to indicate the layer being edited.
You cannot hide a markup layer if it is marked for editing.
Layers must be under review to be edited, or the option will be unavailable.
Copy a markup layer
1. Select a single markup layer, and select Copy markup layer 
on the Markup Layers Manager toolbar.
2. In the Copy markup layer dialog, enter a Name and a Description.
3. Select COPY. The markup layer is copied along with its annotations to a new layer.
 Layers must be under review to be copied, or the option will be unavailable.
Consolidate markup layers
To consolidate two or more markup layers into a single markup layer:
1. Select two or more markup layers from the Markup Layers Manager panel.
2. Select CONSOLIDATE.
3. In the Create Markup Layer dialog, enter a Name and a Description.
4. Select FINISH when you are done.
 Layers must be under review to be consolidated, or the option will be unavailable.
Lock or unlock markup layers
You can lock a markup layer so the layer cannot be edited or deleted.
The status is indicated as follows:
Status
Icon
Description
Locked
The markup layer is locked from editing or deleting.
Unlocked
The markup layer is unlocked and can be edited or deleted.
Delete one or more markup layers
1. Select one or more markup layers from the Markup Layers Manager panel.
2. Select Delete Layer(s) 
.
 Layers must be under review to be deleted, or the option will be unavailable.
Annotating a file using markup layers

 If you are viewing a file requiring a markup rendition, click Switch to markup mode 
 to begin marking up the file. You can click Switch to view mode to return to the previous viewer.

Add annotations to a markup layer
You can add different annotations, such as callouts, stamps, symbols, or uploaded images, to a markup layer using the annotations toolbar.
1. Select a markup layer from the Markup Layers Manager panel. Edit Mode 
 displays in the row.
 To exit edit mode, select Edit Mode 
 in the row.
2. Select an annotation type from the toolbar:
What are the different annotation types?
You can quickly and easily add annotations by selecting the annotation type from the annotation toolbar on the View and Markup page.
Type
Name
What is annotated
Text
Inserts typed text, text callouts, and so on. You can emphasis text in different colors.
Shapes
Inserts geometric shapes, straight lines, arrows, drawn lines, and so on. You can edit line weight, opacity, and fill color.
Stamps
Inserts stamps, symbols, uploaded images, action pins, and dynamic stamps that can be configured to display names and dates for
approval and sign off or token values.
 Each annotation type has enhanced styling options that allow you to change colors, add comments, add links, and delete the annotation.
How do I make text annotations in a markup layer?
1. Select Text Annotations from the toolbar:
2. Select the required type of annotation from the Text Annotations toolbar.
Select this
To do this
 Highlight
Highlight the selected text.
 Underline
Underline the selected text.
 Strikeout
Add a line through the selected text.
 Squiggly
Add a squiggly underline to the selected text.
 Free Text
Add a text box.
 Callout
Add a text callout.
How do I add shapes to a markup layer?
1. Select Shape Annotations from the toolbar:
2. Select the required type of annotation from the Shape Annotations toolbar.
Select this
To draw this
 Free Hand
A free hand line.
 Line
A straight line.
 Arrow
An arrow.
 Polyline
A line with multiple segments.
 Rectangle
A rectangle.
 You can change a rectangle into a cloud by changing the Style to Cloudy. After you draw the rectangle, select
the shape and select Style from the toolbar.
 Ellipse
An ellipse.
 Polygon
A polygon.
 Cloud
A cloud. This is a polygon with a squiggly line as the border instead of a straight line.
 For a better experience, press and hold the SHIFT key while drawing the cloud shape. Double click when you are
done.
 Select the Eraser 
 to remove marks made by Shape Annotations.
How do I add a stamp to a markup layer?
1. Select Stamp Annotations from the toolbar:
2. Select the required type of annotation from the Stamp Annotations toolbar. Select:
 Stamp Image to select an image file to add to the markup layer.
 Stamps and Symbols to open the Stamps and Symbols tabs where you can select the required stamp or symbol to add to the markup layer.
Some stamps prompt you to type some text that will be included in the stamp. For example, you may be requested to enter your name if a stamp requires a signature.
Do not use the percent sign (%) in the stamp text.
When you make your first selection, a blue border appears around the selected stamp or symbol. If you change the category or switch between stamps or symbols, your selection is cleared.
You can view which annotations were added to a specific markup layer by selecting Expand annotations 
 in Markup Layers Manager panel.
You can locate annotations in a markup layer by selecting Locate annotation 
 in the annotation layer.
Add dynamic stamps with token values to a markup layer
Dynamic stamps can contain text strings or available token values. To add one:
1. Create a new markup layer or open an existing markup layer for edit using the Markup Layers Manager panel.
2. Select Stamp Annotations from the toolbar:
3. Select Stamps 
 from the Stamp Annotations toolbar.
 When you make your first selection, a blue border appears around the selected stamp or symbol. If you change the category or switch between stamps or symbols, your selection is cleared.
4. Select a dynamic stamp from the Stamps panel, and click anywhere in the file to place it.
 The markup file is automatically saved.
The available tokens and their descriptions are listed in the table below.
Token
Description
%Time
The time the print was spooled based on a 12-hour clock (AM/PM). If the tags are viewed on screen, the date on which the screen was last refreshed
displays.
 Time is based on the browser locale.
%MilTime
The time the print was spooled based on a 24-hour clock.
 MilTime is based on the browser locale.
%Title
The name of the file.
%TotalPages or %Pages
The total number of pages.
%Page
The current page number.
%Hostname
The location.hostname of the server url hosting the site.
%SysDatePlusDays(n)
The date generated by a specified number of days past the system date. Replace n with the desired number of days. Negative numbers are
acceptable. Make sure you close the parenthesis when adding the number of days.
%BatesPgNo(n)
Indicates the starting page number and the number of digits to use. Replace n with the desired format and starting page number. For example,
%BatesPgNo(0002) would place 0002 on the first page, 0003 on the next page, and so on. Make sure you close the parenthesis when adding the page
number.
%Date
The date the print was spooled. If the tags are viewed on screen, the time at which the screen was last refreshed displays.
 Date is based on the browser locale.
%Login
The login name of the user who is currently logged on.
%User
The user name of the user who is currently logged on.
%Prompt
Adds text strings entered by the user.
Format annotations in a markup layer
You can format existing annotations in a markup layer.
1. Select a markup layer from the Markup Layers Manager panel, and click Edit 
.
2. Click an annotation in the markup layer.
3. Select an option from the toolbar:
Click this
To do this
 Comment
Add a comment to the annotation.
 Style
Change the annotation style, such as color fill or line style.
 Delete
Delete the annotation.
 Link URL or Page
Link the annotation to a URL or to a specific page of the file.
You can view which annotations were added to a specific markup layer by selecting Expand annotations 
 in Markup Layers Manager panel.
You can locate annotations in a markup layer by selecting Locate annotation 
 in the annotation layer.
Comparing files using the viewer
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Comparing Files Using the View and Markup Control <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/65658603989d2e938cc534aa>
You can see the differences between two files by comparing them in the viewer.
 You cannot compare files on a tablet or other mobile device.
What kinds of files can I compare?
Depending on how the software is configured for your company, you can compare these file types:
Hexagon 2D (.sma, .spe, .sha, .pid, .igr, .zyq)
CAD files (.dwg, .dxf, .dgn)
Word (.doc, .docm, .docx)
Excel (.xls, .xlsm, .xlsx)
PowerPoint (.ppt, .pptm, .pptx)
PDF (.pdf)
You can also compare different file types:
PDF to text
CAD, Word, Excel, or PDF to each other
You can compare files with any relationship to a document, including:
Files attached to a document
Reference files attached to any of those files
Alternate renditions of the files attached to a document
Alternate renditions attached directly to the document
 If there is more than one file that can be compared, you can tell which files are alternate renditions from the information that appears when you pick which files you want to compare.
Why can't I see the text changes inline for all the files?
To show files containing a lot of text, such as Word or Excel files, the software uses a graphic representation of the file, like an electronic photocopy. That means that the text is not available for the software to compare.
 You can generate PDF renditions and use the PDF renditions for comparison.
In this section:
Compare files attached to different documents
Compare design files
Compare text files
Compare files attached to different documents
If the files you want to compare are attached to documents that are difficult to find with a single search, you can use the Pinboard to compare them.
1. Search for each document, and select it in the Results table.
2. Click Actions 
 > Add to Pinboard.
3. Click Pins 
, and click View in Grid.
4. Select the two documents in the grid.
5. Click Actions 
, and choose either Compare Documents or Compare Documents as Text.
6. Use the toolbars to see the files in different ways or with different information, as described in the topics above.
Compare design files
1. Search for the documents with attached files that you want to compare.
2. Select the two documents in the Results table.
 If you want to compare files from documents that are difficult to find with a single search, see Compare files attached to different documents.
3. Click Actions 
 > Compare Documents.
4. If a list of files appears, all the retrieved results are returned in pages in the Select a file to view window. Select the two files you want to compare.
The two files appear side by side in the browser.
5. Use the toolbars to see the files in different ways or with different information:
If you want to
Then do this
See the files superimposed in one view
The superimposed view is generated automatically in the middle pane with each file appearing in the left and
right panes.
In the middle pane, shared graphics are colored in gray, graphics appearing in the right file are colored in red,
and graphics appearing in the left file are colored in blue.
See just one of the files
Click Settings 
 on the toolbar in the pane, then select Full screen to 
view the file in full screen
mode.
Print one of the files
Click Settings 
 on the toolbar in the pane, then select Print 
.
 The superimposed view in the middle pane cannot be printed.
Align the graphics of the two files
Use the buttons on the nudge tool to scale, rotate, or translate the graphics until the two files are aligned.
Zoom in or out of a file
Each pane has controls to zoom out or in:
 The three panes are synchronized so that they maintain the same zoom level and graphics alignment.
Compare text files
You can compare the contents of two text files by doing the following:
1. Search for two documents, each with at least one PDF file attached.
2. Select the two documents and click Actions 
 > Compare Documents as Text.
If more than one PDF file is attached to one or both documents, the Select two files to compare dialog appears.
What can I do with the top pane?
The plain text content of the two files appears side-by-side in the top pane. Any differences in the two files are highlighted. You can expand condensed blocks of text by clicking (...). The contents in the pane update as you navigate one of
the original files loaded in the bottom pane.
What can I do with the bottom pane?
You can adjust the comparable text in the top pane by navigating one of the original files in the bottom pane. Use the power bar options in the middle of the page to adjust your view.
Power Bar Options
Option
Description
[filename].pdf
Toggle your view between the two original files you are comparing
Navigate by thumbnails or outline
Page layout options
Zoom in or out of the original file
Navigate the original file by panning
Navigate directly to a page in the original file
Search for a word in the original file
Change settings, such as enter full screen mode, download the file you are viewing, print the file you are viewing, or
enter dark or light mode.
Using the hotspot bubble
Hotspot bubbles appear when you click tags in a published drawing or 3D model. The following options are available from the hotspot bubble:
Option
Description
Action
Opens the Actions menu for the selected object.
Info Map
Opens the Info Map for the selected object in a new tab.
For more help, see Using the Info Map
Show in Drawing
Show in Model
Opens the object in a drawing or model file in split-screen mode.
Pin
Adds the object to your Pinboard, where it can be managed and recalled in pins lists for future use.
Details
Displays the Object Details form for the selected object.
Using visual intelligence to analyze your data
Visual intelligence offers a very flexible and visual way to analyze large amounts of data in a single model view.
With Visual Intelligence, you can:
Search for tags in published models (that are transferred to the Facilities Data Warehouse using Data Take-On) or models streamed from the Visualization Data Service (VDS).
Group and view tags belonging to more than one change request or work package.
Run visual intelligence reports on published models (that are transferred to the Facilities Data Warehouse using Data Take-On) or models streamed from the Visualization Data Service (VDS).
Generate shareable URLs to directly open the results of a visual intelligence search or a report in a browser.
Search for tags
You can search for tags in the following file types using View and Markup:
File Format
Native application
File extension
Note
Hotspotted Hexagon 2D Drawings
SmartSketch
.igr
.sma
You can search by tag
name in these
hotspotted file formats.
For example, you can
enter "6-G-" to search
for all pump tags named
"6-G-" in the file.
Smart P&ID
.pid
Smart Orthographic Drawings
.sha
Smart Electrical
.spe
Aspen Basic Engineering
.zyq
Streaming 3D Models
-
-
Streamed from the
Visualization Data
Service (VDS).
You can search by
tag name or
description
keywords in these
file formats. For
example, you can
enter "6-G-*" to
search for all
pump tags named
"6-G-" or "pump"
to search for all
pump tags in the
file.
1. Open a supported file type using View and Markup.
2. Select the Open panel arrow to open the Visual Intelligence panel.
3. Expand the Search collapsible menu.
4. Add any defining parameters or wildcards in the Search Model or Drawing box, and select Search.
 You must enter at least three characters to get search suggestions.
If more than 300 results are returned, you are asked if you want to continue. Select Ok to continue or Cancel to refine your search.
How can I improve my search for tags?
You can improve your search by:
Selecting Use 'Is equal to' Operator below the search bar to refine your search to a targeted set of parameters.
Using the pie chart to navigate your search results.
Toggling search results on or off.
Organizing search results by color.
Hiding non-matching results from your view.
How can I share my search results?
To share your search results:
1. Open a supported file type using View and Markup.
2. Run a visual intelligence search.
3. Select  and Generate Share URL.
4. Select Close on the Share URL dialog box.
The URL to launch a view of your search results is automatically copied to your clipboard. You can send this URL to another user.
Run a visual intelligence report
Visual intelligence reports allow you to group and assign colors to graphical objects based on certain property values stored on the object itself or on related objects. This is a very flexible and visual way to analyze large amounts of data
within a single view.
1. Open a supported file type using View and Markup.
2. Select the Open panel arrow to open the Visual Intelligence panel.
3. Select a report name under the Reports collapsible menu to run that report.
4. Select a group name to view tags in that group.
5. Select one or more tag numbers to access Fit selected items 
.
6. To close all reports and reset the display, expand the Search collapsible menu, and select Reset to default on the  menu.
How can I share my report?
To share your search results:
1. Open a supported file type using View and Markup.
2. Run a visual intelligence report.
3. Select  > Generate Share URL.
4. Select Close on the Share URL dialog box.
The URL to launch a view of your report is automatically copied to your clipboard. You can send this URL to another user.
What visual intelligence reports are delivered with the software?
The following reports are available for 3D models:
Consolidated Tags by Class
 This report is available only when viewing 3D models with the Integration Data Filters set to Consolidated. Integration Data Filters is in Settings 
 > Scope.
Equipment by Type 1
Objects by Insulation Thickness
Model Instrument Loops
 This report is available only when viewing 3D models with the Integration Data Filters set to Consolidated. Integration Data Filters is in Settings 
 > Scope.
Pipelines by Fluid Code
 This report is available only when viewing 3D models with the Integration Data Filters set to Consolidated. Integration Data Filters is in Settings 
 > Scope.
Pipes by Lining Material
Tags by Approval Status
Tags by Construction Requirement
Tags by Construction Type
Tags with Inconsistencies
The following reports are available for 2D drawings:
Active Tags by Class
 This report is available only when viewing 2D drawings with the Integration Data Filters set to P&ID or 2D. Integration Data Filters is in Settings 
 > Scope.
Consolidated Drawings Tags by Class
 This report is available only when viewing 2D drawings with the Integration Data Filters set to Consolidated. Integration Data Filters is in Settings 
 > Scope.
Drawing Instrument Loops
 This report is available only when viewing 2D drawings with the Integration Data Filters set to Consolidated. Integration Data Filters is in Settings 
 > Scope.
Visual intelligence options
These visual intelligence options help you navigate data in a Hexagon model opened in View and Markup.
Click this
To do this
Search
Searches for tags in a model or a hotspotted drawing and groups the search results by color.
Toggle display of listed results
Turns the color of selected items on or off.
Change color
Changes the color of the item in the displayed file.
 If more than 30 groups are displayed, the same color is used for group 30 and all subsequent groups.
Show chart
Shows a pie chart divided into sectors representing selected items.
Sort
Sorts groups by frequency or by name in ascending or descending order.
By default, groups sort by descending frequency.
Fit selected items
Fits the display to the selected items.
Show repeated results
Shows groups of tags belonging to more than one change request or work package. This option is only available when
repeated results exist.
 This is only supported for published Hexagon models that are transferred to the Facilities Data Warehouse using
Data Take-On.
 and ...
Hide/Show non-matching items
Hides non-selected items from the view or shows non-selected items with low opaqueness in a model.
Reset to default
Resets the displayed file, refreshes the list of reports, and deletes search results.
Generate Share URL
Copies the shareable URL to your clipboard. If the user has the necessary permissions, the URL navigates straight to the
visual intelligence report or search results.
Printing your files
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Printing your files <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/666b24603ec4ed0d925e5406>
 Print options depend on the viewer and browser you are using. For more information about these options, please see the viewer's or browser's Help.
You can print a file you are currently viewing. How you print depends on the file type:
To print a non-Hexagon file, see Print a non-Hexagon file format.
To print Hexagon files, use your browser.
You can print a file without viewing it. There are four ways to do this:
Navigate to the file, and click Actions 
 > Print File 
.
Hover the cursor over the file, and choose Print File 
.
Navigate to the document, and click Actions 
 > Print File 
. Select the file from the list.
Navigate to the document, hover the cursor over the document, and choose Print File 
. Select the file from the list.
 When printing files through documents, the list does not appear if there is one file in the document.
You can combine two or more files into a single PDF for printing. There are three ways to do this:
Select a document with two or more files attached in the results grid and select Actions 
 > Files > Generate Merged Rendition For Files.
Select two or more documents with files attached in the results grid and select Actions 
 > Files > Generate Merged Rendition For Files.
Select two or more files in the results grid and select Actions 
 > Generate Merged Rendition For Files.
When done, select the notification for PDF generation. You can click DOWNLOAD PDF 
 and print it locally, or click PRINT PDF 
.
Why is printing not working like I expect it to?
Here are some reasons that printing might not work like you expect it to:
You cannot change the color settings if printing to PDF.
All displayed markups are automatically printed.
If you print a master file, all of its reference files print too.
When using a tablet or other mobile device, printing might not work.
If you are printing without viewing the file and an alternate rendition exists, the alternate rendition is printed. You might need to regenerate the alternate rendition to get the latest markups included.
Why am I getting notifications for large files?
You will get notifications for large files if the file size exceeds the size limit for the file type. This is because large file sizes may cause the rendition generation to fail. Below are the size limits for file types:
File Type
Size Limit
Excel
2 MB
CSV
1 MB
Word
10 MB
PDF
50 MB
CAD
2 MB
If the file size exceeds the size limit, you will see a notification message in Notifications 
 . The message will show whether the rendition has been generated for printing or not. If the file is ready for printing, you can select  on the
notification and then select 
 Print PDF.
Quick View
PDF renditions of files supported by the view and markup control can be viewed directly in a new browser tab through the following steps:
1. In the Documents feature set, select All Documents.
2. Select the required documents from the All Documents list and select Actions 
 > Quick View 
.
3. In the Select Files to Quick View window, select the required files.
 If only one file is linked to the document, that file will be selected by default.
4. Select OK.
The file will be opened in the browser's default PDF viewer.
Ensure that your browser's pop-up blocker has been disabled to allow files to be opened in new tabs.
Visualization troubleshooting
The following topics will help you troubleshoot issues with viewing, printing, marking up, or navigating (VPMN) files.
 Visualization is controlled by two view controls, the Graphic View Control (GVC) and the view and markup control. The following topics are divided by view control.
Troubleshooting the Graphic View Control (GVC)
The following topics will help you troubleshoot issues with the Graphic View Control (GVC).
Error when the model is spread across a large space
Problem
The viewer displays the following message after opening a large space:
Model has a large range, which limits object visibility
Cause
One or more objects in the model are located considerably away from the main model area. The model may seem to have loaded unsuccessfully, however, the model has actually been zoomed out to fit all its objects in the viewer, with the
overall size in the 3D space reduced drastically.
Solution
Contact Support for assistance to decrease the distance in the model.
Force Refresh to update graphics in a Smart 3D model
You must have the System Administrator role to use this feature.
Problem
Users viewing a Hexagon 3D model streamed from the VDS into the GVC viewer, cannot get the latest data after selecting the 
 Refresh command, or users viewing this error:
# graphic(s) still processing
Cause
The 
 Refresh command only works if an administrator has run Force Refresh.
Solution
The Force Refresh command resets the treeLifespan display setting and updates the 3D model, enabling the 
 Refresh command for your users.For more information about the treeLifespan setting, see display.
To do this:
1. Log on with the System Administrator role.
2. View the applicable 3D View Filter that needs to be refreshed.
3. Select Force Refresh from the menu bar.
 The 
 Refresh command reduces viewing performance since this re-runs the workspace query that creates the graphic tree structure.
Slow performance when loading Hexagon 3D models
Problem
You experience slow performance when loading Hexagon 3D models.
Causes
Here are some common causes for slower performance:
A cached file does not exist.
Browser settings are limiting performance.
Slow network performance and latency.
Not enough memory is available on the host device.
The Windows 10 operating system defaults to a lower power GPU card if the system power profile is set to conserve battery life (Battery Saver mode).
Viewing models with a large number of graphics.
Solutions
For cached file:
You can expect slower performance if this is the first time the model has been opened for viewing or if you are refreshing the model. This is due to required processes on the server. A cached copy of the file will be stored for faster
subsequent views.
To troubleshoot browser settings:
Browser settings can impact viewing performance. Check for these settings:
The cache size is set to the maximum allowable size.
HTTP compression is disabled.
Optimal browser caching policies in both client requests and server responses.
To troubleshoot memory, network performance, and latency issues:
Monitor the CPU usage of the host device. Various platforms have their own proprietary tools for monitoring systems. Consult your infrastructure documentation for detailed information on how to monitor CPU, memory, storage utilization,
and network traffic.
To troubleshoot the GPU card:
To determine the GPU card is using, select Settings > Information > GL Renderer, and then check GL Settings.
If your computer is defaulting to a lower power card, you can set to always run on the higher performance card using that card’s control panel. See your graphics card manufacturer documentation on how to assign an application to a GPU.
When running in a web browser, the browser application you are using must be set to run on the highest performing card. See your graphics card manufacturer documentation on how to assign an application to a GPU card.
To optimize Rendering Settings:
Rendering Settings are set of variables that you can use to refine how the Graphic View Control (GVC) loads models. To create Rendering Settings, have your system administrator contact support for assistance.
Once the Rendering Settings have been created, the system administrator can then apply Rendering Settings to specific documents containing 3D models or to user accounts to maximize a models viewing performance.
How do I apply Rendering Settings to a 3D model?
1. Depending on the dataset your environment uses, log on to HxGN SDx2 as a Business Administrator.
2. Navigate to the System Administration feature set, and select Rendering Settings.
3. Select a Rendering Setting in the results grid, and click Actions 
 > Relationship Builder.
4. In the Relationship Items section, add one or more specific documents containing 3D models to relate to the selected Rendering Settings from the following tabs:
Pins - shows documents previously saved as pins in your Pinboard or newly created documents in New Items.
Existing - shows documents that are already related to the selected Rendering Settings.
Search - search for documents in your system using specific search criteria and Filter options.
5. Select RELATE.
 For more information about the Relationship Builder, see Create and edit a relationship
How do I apply Rendering Settings to a user?
 When Rendering Settings are applied to a user account, it will supersede any other Rendering Settings applied to individual models.
1. Depending on the dataset your environment uses, log on to HxGN SDx2 as a Business Administrator.
2. In the Business Administration feature set, select User Management.
3. In the User tab, find the required user, and select 
 > UPDATE.
4. In the MAIN DETAILS tab, choose one of the Rendering settings.
5. Select UPDATE.
Unable to interact with the GVC on an iPad
Problem
When using Safari to access HxGN SDx2 on an iPad, attempts to interact with a model or drawing in the GVC by zooming or selecting objects have unexpected results such as:
The entire browser window zooming.
The object cannot be selected.
Cause
Safari sometimes displays the full desktop version of a website in a mobile environment, thereby simplifying the user interaction. The simplified user interaction limits certain GVC functionalities. The full desktop version of HxGN SDx2
offers full GVC functionalities.
Solution
In the Safari settings, select Request Desktop Website in the Settings for Websites section, and enable the All Websites toggle.
Troubleshooting the view and markup control
The following topics will help you troubleshoot issues with the view and markup control.
Unable to download a file
Error
The following message displays when trying to download a file:
This file is over the maximum size for downloading. Please consult our documentation for workarounds.
Cause
The file is too large for your browser to download.
Solutions
To download the file without markups:
1. Navigate back to the file or document in the results list.
2. Select Actions 
 > Save Target As.
3. In the Select files to download page, select the files you want to download. The files are displayed in pages based on the total number of files returned.
 Non-viewable files are also displayed, which you can also download as required.
To download the file with markups:
1. Navigate back to the file or document in the results list.
2. Select Actions 
 > Save Alternate Rendition.
An alternate rendition must exist for the file.
Your administrator must configure the method on the GenerateAlternateRendition client API to include markups.
Unable to retrieve the markup rendition
Errors
The following messages display when trying to view a file or compare files:
Unable to retrieve the markup rendition
Created scheduled task to create markup rendition
Cause
You might encounter this problem if a markup rendition does not exist.
Solution
Users can view a file or compare files after the scheduled task completes and a markup rendition is created. When you first try to view a file without a markup rendition you get:
"Unable to retrieve the markup rendition".
When the call to generate the markup rendition completes you either get:
"Generated markup rendition" if successful, or:
"Could not generate markup rendition" if the call fails.
The main scheduler does not need to be enabled to create the markup rendition. If a markup rendition is not found, it will be created asynchronously, without the use of the scheduled task, when the user attempts to view the file.
If the main scheduler is running, a scheduled task is created when the file is attached to the document, and users can check its status. But when users try to view and markup the file, no scheduled task is created, and users will need to
check the server log to view the status.
Display item attribute values do not display in view and markup
A display item for an object method create form does not display the attribute values in view and markup, such as read only. In addition, the parent or dependent display items also do not display the attribute values. For example, this can
happen when you create a change request in the Actions menu for a document in view and markup.
Cause
Your users might encounter this problem in view and markup if the CreateObjectFromSelectedObjects client API is used to make the method for the object create form. The display items in the form do not display the attribute values.
Solution
You must create the method for the object create form for view and markup using the SDACreateClassObjAndRelFromMarkup client API. This client API allows the display item attribute values to be displayed in the object's create form.
For example, read only display item values are displayed in view and markup for a change request form.
Querying for data
With HxGN SDx2, you can:
Search for tags and navigate the 3D model using those tags.
Use advanced search capabilities to find the data you need.
Querying for data
In addition to using the search bar, you can create more complex search queries using 
 Advanced Search, which uses object criteria combined with multiple search operators to locate objects in a single query.
The benefit that an Advanced Search provides over the search bar is that you can create specific search queries that you can save and run again and again. In this way, you can easily find important objects that are used multiple times
throughout the life of the plant or a project.
For more information, see Use the Advanced search.
Use the Advanced search
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Use the Advanced Search (modified in an update).
You can create complex search queries using 
 Advanced Search to search for objects using selected criteria. Each query can then be saved so that the it can run again and the objects quickly found.
Each search query can be built with specific object types, classifications, and criteria that you combine with Boolean search operators to return a targeted set of objects. After you run the query, the items are returned and displayed in the
Results grid.
 You can easily add the returned objects to a Pins list. They are then available for other functionality, such as building relationships to other objects. For more information on Pins, see Using the Pinboard.
Create a query
1. Go to 
 Advanced Search in the side toolbar, and select + New Query.
2. Select the TYPES, CLASSIFICATION, and add search CRITERIA to build the query.
 You can add multiple criteria selections using the AND and OR statements to build the query using Boolean operators, parameters, and terms.
For example, the following query is to locate specific process engineering flow scheme documents that have names that start with the prefix, 1011-ENG-PID:
3. Select SEARCH to run the query.
 You can save the query for future use or once the query has returned the correct data. This is especially useful if the query is complex and you need to refine the query to return specific objects. For more information, see Save a
query.
Save a query
1. Create a query, and select SAVE in the main toolbar.
2. In the Save dialog box, add the name and description for the query, and select SAVE to save the query in the Saved Queries menu.
If you run a query without saving it, select ß Back in the side toolbar to reopen the query details, and then select SAVE.
You can select SAVE AS in the toolbar to save the query as a new query that you can refine.
You can select RENAME to rename an existing query.
Filter the saved queries
1. You can filter the saved queries in the list by selecting 
 Filter in the Saved Queries menu.
2. In the filter box, enter the name or part of the name of the query. For example, Vessel. Only queries containing the name Vessel are displayed in the list.
The Filter icon changes to 
 to indicate that the list has been filtered.
The filtering process only uses the Contains search operator for searching the query list.
Sort the saved queries
You can sort the list of queries in the Saved Queries menu by selecting the 
 Sort by option, and selecting one of the following:
Select this
To do this
 A TO Z
Sort the queries in ascending A to Z order.
 Z TO A
Sort the queries in descending Z to A order.
 OLD TO NEW
Sort the queries by the latest updated date with oldest queries first.
 NEW TO OLD
Sort the queries by the latest updated date with newest queries first.
Edit a query
1. In the Saved Queries menu, select a query to edit, and select 
 More actions.
2. Select 
 EDIT QUERY, and edit the query parameters as required.
 You can select SAVE AS to save the edited query as a new query or select RENAME to rename the existing query with a new name and description.
Delete a query
You can delete a single query or multiple queries from the Saved Queries menu.
1. In the Saved Queries menu, select a single query or select ALL to select multiple queries.
2. Select 
 Delete Queries.
 You can also go to 
 More actions on a single query, and select 
 DELETE QUERY.
View search results
All search and query results are displayed in rows in the Results grid. When you select a row, it is highlighted to keep it in focus. Select the object name to open the Object Details page.
The search results do not contain what I'm looking for. Why?
If the search results do not contain what you are searching for, it might be because of one of the following reasons:
You do not have permission to view the items. Change your user role to view the items.
The items do not exist in the current scope. Change your query scope to match the scope that contains the objects.
The wildcards used are not supported. Contact your administrator for further information.
 If a pinned item has properties that contain a URL, selecting the property opens the URL in a new browser window.
If the query scope includes more than one configuration, the results are shown on separate configuration tabs arranged in alphabetical order. The create scope configuration tab results always appear on the first tab.
Select an object row Select this item box to open the Actions menu. If you select multiple object rows and open the Actions menu, only actions applicable to all the objects are displayed.
The property columns and the order in which they are displayed are determined by the property column set and the column items. However, a column set’s column items can be configured to conditionally display on the same form
when selected from different plants.
Some classified objects return the property column set defined by the class definition as Advanced Search does not use a client API configured column set.
If the object names are configured to open a specific view method, when you select the hyperlink any attached files are displayed using the corresponding application.
Results table options
The following commands are available in the results table.
Select this
To do this
ADD TO PINBOARD
Add the selected object(s) to Pins.
EXPORT ALL TO EXCEL
Export all the results to a Microsoft Excel spreadsheet.
 The results open directly as a Microsoft Excel XLSX file, or you can open the results from your designated
save folder.
ALL
Select all the objects displayed in a single page in the results table.
Open in new tab 
Open the action results in a new browser tab.
 Logging out in one tab automatically logs you out in all other open tabs. You will lose any unsaved
changes.
Refresh 
Refresh the Results table with updated items, such as your To Do List tasks.
We do not recommend you export large data sets using the Export All to Excel command. You should instead use reports as they are specifically developed to handle large amounts of complex data.
Only the action shortcuts applicable to a specific object are displayed in the Actions menu.
The order and type of property columns displayed is determined by the property column set.
Filter or sort the results
At the top of the required column, select Column Settings 
, and then select one of the following:
Select this
To do this
Sort Ascending
Sort the results in ascending order.
Sort Descending
Sort the results in descending order.
Columns
Select the columns displayed in the table.
Filter
Filter the table using standard parameters, such as Contains.
How do you use the Filter settings?
You can select different Filter options in each column to refine the results to a specific set of objects. Filtered columns display a filter flag 
.
Select the Filter option, and enter the required value to filter the column results by that value.
The following table shows the main filter options:
Filter option
Description
Is equal to
Use the Is equal to operator to filter for objects that equal a value in the column item.
For example, you filter the State column to see only objects where State is set to Current.
Contains
Use the Contains operator to filter for objects that contain a specific value in the column item.
For example, you filter the Title column for objects that contain the value Gas.
Starts with
Use the Starts with operator to filter for objects that start with a value found in the column item.
For example, you filter the Title column so only objects where the title starts with Gas.
Ends with
Use the Ends with operator to filter for objects that end with a value in the column item.
For example, you filter the Title column for objects where the title ends with Sheet.
Is empty
Use the Is empty operator to filter for objects that have an empty value in the column item.
For example, you filter the Title column for objects that have an empty value set in the title.
 The Is not empty operator filters for objects that don't have an empty value in the column item.
Is null
Use the Is null operator to filter for objects that have a NULL value in the column item.
For example, you filter the Criticality Rating column for objects that have a NULL value in the column.
 The Is not null operator filters for objects that don't have a NULL value in the column item.
Has no value
Use the Has no value operator to filter for objects that have either an empty value or a NULL value in the column
item.
For example, you filter the Criticality Rating column for objects that either have no value set or have a NULL value
in the column.
 The Has value operator filters for objects that don't have an empty value or a NULL value in the column
item.
Is greater than
Use the Is greater than operator to filter for objects that have a value greater than the value provided.
For example, you filter the Version column for objects that have a value greater than 1 set as their version.
Is less than
Use the Is less than operator to filter for objects that have a value less than the value provided.
For example, you filter the Version column for objects that have a value less than 3 set as their version.
The list of filter options depends on the configuration of the column set. Not every filter is available for each column item in the list.
Depending on the column item selected, some filter options can show a list of existing values in the Select Value list.
You can use the And or Or syntax to narrow results to include a range of values, especially when there are multiple values in the same column item. For example, by setting Starts with to 100 and Ends with 150, only objects
between those values are displayed.
You can combine multiple filters to display a specific set of data. For example, using the Contains and Ends with filters, you can filter the Name column using Contains 1011, an AND, and Ends with HSE. Only objects with names
that contain 1011 and end with HSE are displayed.
You can use wildcards to further target results. For example, if you use *(all) with 01 to filter for objects, (01*), you will get every object with 01, such as 01, 01A, 01B, 01C, and so on
Execute saved reports
Reports provide a quick way to view project or plant information, perform complex queries, and view information across a structure of data. A few saved reports are delivered with HxGN SDx2. Reports are related to the relevant feature
sets. For example, a document revision report is available in the Documents feature set. Users who have access to reports can view them from the related feature sets.
What reports are delivered with the software?
The following saved reports are delivered with HxGN SDx2:
Class Definitions
Methods Report
The following output formats are supported by reports in HxGN SDx2:
XHTML - The results are displayed in a new browser tab.
LISTVIEW - The results are displayed in a new window.
CSV - The results can be downloaded as a CSV formatted text file, one row per data item.
CSV REPEATED - The results can be downloaded as a CSV formatted text file with rows repeated for each primary and related object.
EXCEL (OPENXML) - The results can be downloaded as an OpenXML Excel file. There is an option to use a template Excel file that contains a macro, which can be used to further manipulate the data as required. The macro must
be specified to run when the file is opened, as Open XML does not support the running of macros.
WORD (OPENXML) - The results can be downloaded as an OpenXML Word file. You can either use an existing template or make changes to an existing template and save it with a new name to create a new template.
EXCEL EXTENDED (OPENXML) - The results can be downloaded as a Microsoft Excel file. This style provides a more detailed report format than the EXCEL style. In this output style, the relationships to the first object are listed on
separate rows. There is an option to use the template Excel files that contain a macro, which can be used to further manipulate the data as required. Two templates are delivered by default. You can either use one of the existing
templates or create a new template based on your requirements.
The example below shows the output of the Method Access report. Displaying the two access groups in different rows clarifies the separate relationships between the Method and the Access Group.
EXCEL REPEATED (OPENXML) - This style displays the output in a more compact format compared to other Excel output styles. In this output style:
If an object has a one-to-one relationship with a second object, both objects are displayed in the same row of the spreadsheet. The second object is displayed as a property of the first object.
If the relationship is one-to-many, the related objects are displayed in separate rows.
Properties on relationships are also listed on the same row.
For example, in the table below, the Method, Client API, and Access Group are all listed in the same row. The relationship between the Method and the Client API is one-to-one, so they are displayed in the same row. However, the
relationship between the Method and the Access Group is one-to-many, so they are displayed in separate rows.
Depending on the number of one-to-one relationships, this output style can result in a much smaller Microsoft Excel file compared to other Excel output styles
Icons and execution methods of reports
When you select a report, it either opens the data in a grid view or exports the data in different formats depending on the configuration. Based on the configuration, some reports allow you to add additional filters to get specific data in the
results, and this type of report is commonly referred to as a report with a prompt. 
Each report configured to be displayed appears with an icon that indicates how the results are returned.
Icons
Report execution and accessing results
Run - Grid
view
The report data is displayed in a grid view. You can save the report to a file using the EXPORT ALL TO EXCEL option.
Download -
File based
The report is configured to display the result in a file. The supported file formats are CSV, CSV REPEATED, EXCEL EXTENDED (OPENXML), EXCEL REPEATED (OPENXML), EXCEL (OPENXML), and
WORD (OPENXML).
It is executed in the background, and a notification message is generated. When it is complete, you can download the report result from Notifications 
.
Prompt
The report allows you to add run-time criteria on top of the pre-configured report criteria. 
Launch - New
tab
When you select the XHTML output style, the report is executed in the background, and a notification message is generated. When it is complete, you can download the report result from Notifications 
.
The report result is displayed in a new browser tab.
File download
Along with the report result in a file, this report also downloads files attached to objects in the report based on configuration.
The result is displayed as a CSV, CSV REPEATED, EXCEL EXTENDED (OPENXML), EXCEL REPEATED (OPENXML), EXCEL (OPENXML), or XHTML file, depending on the format configured in the
report.
It is executed in the background, and a notification message is generated. When it is complete, you can download the report result along with the attached files from Notifications 
.
Ignore Config
The report gathers all the data configured at the site level without considering the configurations, such as plants or projects.
The result is displayed as a CSV or CSV REPEATED file, depending on the format configured in the report.
It is executed in the background, and a notification message is generated. When it is complete, you can download the report result in a single file from Notifications 
.
 Executing reports using query scopes returns the data based on the query scopes set. The results are displayed in tabs in an alphabetical order that corresponds to each selected configuration. For more information on query
scopes, see Understanding scopes.
How do I access reports?
By default, reports are created and configured by a system administrator. Your assigned access groups determine what reports you can find and execute in HxGN SDx2.
 If you want to create or update a report, request the necessary access from your system administrator.
Administrators relate reports to their appropriate feature sets. For example, a document revision report may be made available in the Documents feature set.
You can access and run a report by selecting it within the feature set it is related to.
 You can access the report only if one of your roles is associated with an access group that matches the access group assigned to the report. For example, if a report is created with the Engineer access group, only users with roles
associated with the Engineer access group can see the report. For more information, see Run a report.
Run a report
1. Select the feature set where the report you want to run is related.
2. Search for the report you want to run and select it. A notification message is displayed.
 Reports with the LISTVIEW output style are displayed in a new window.
3. When the report has run successfully, download it from Notifications 
. For more information, see View notifications of reports.
 You can also view the status of the report in Notifications. When the report is running, the status is IN PROGRESS. When the report has run successfully, the status is COMPLETED.
Run a report with a prompt
You can provide search criteria for a few reports before they are run, which allows you to view different data in a report without creating multiple reports.
1. Search for the report you want to run and select it.
2. Provide criteria by entering the appropriate parameters and values in the Operator and Value boxes to return a targeted set of data in the result.
3. Select RUN REPORT.
4. When the report has run successfully, download it from Notifications 
. For more information, see View notifications of reports.
 You can also view the status of the report in Notifications. When the report is running, the status is IN PROGRESS. When the report has run successfully, the status is COMPLETED.
Select 
 to add a row to provide multiple criteria for a property.
Select AND or OR when you want to provide multiple criteria for a property in a report.
You can run a report without providing any input, if you do not want to filter the result.
What are the operators I can use to modify the query?
The OPERATOR option allows you to specify search operators to refine your search to a specific set of objects. There are two sets of operators you can use, equals and like, depending on the property type selected.
If the property is a value in a list available by default or if you use the words Null or Blank as the value, only the equals operator is available. The operator automatically changes to equals when you enter Null or Blank.
You can use the wildcards to define search criteria for a string property that matches one or more characters using the like or equals operator.
The following table shows the operators for strings, doubles, integers, small integers, and select lists:
Parameter
Function
equals 
Includes items where this property value matches what you entered in the VALUE box, including wildcards.
not equal to 
Includes items where the value of this property does not match what you entered in the VALUE box.
less than 
Includes items where this property value is less than what you entered in the VALUE box.
greater than 
Includes items where this property value is greater than what you entered in the VALUE box.
less than or equal to 
Includes items where this property value is equal to or less than what you entered in the VALUE box.
greater than or equal to 
Includes items where this property value is equal to or greater than what you entered in the VALUE box.
like 
Includes items where this property value matches what you entered in the VALUE box, including wildcards.
 The behavior of the equals and like operators is identical. You can use either of these when you are defining the search criteria using wildcards.
An asterisk (*) matches zero or more characters in your search. For example, if you specify pa*, the software returns objects where that property value contains words that start with the characters pa, such as pan, park, part,
and so on.
You can use an underscore (_) or a question mark (?) to match a single character. For example, if you specify pi_ or pi?, the software returns pid, pit, and pip, but not pipe.
To search for objects where the property value includes an underscore, question mark, or asterisk character, enclose them in square brackets. For example, use 100[*] to return 100*, but not 100abc.
You can search for objects with a property that contains a single character in a specific range of characters by enclosing them in square brackets. For example, using [a-f] returns only objects where the value of the property
includes a to f in a set of characters. So, if you specify pi[a-p], the software returns objects where the property value is pid and pip, but not pit, as 't' is outside the range.
You can use a caret (^) character to search for objects where a property value does not include a character in a range or set. For example, if you specify pi[^a-p], the software returns objects where the property value is pit, but
not pid or pip, as 'd' and 'p' are in the range a-p.
For example, you can filter the report result by entering parameters for the following properties, as shown in the image below:
Run a background report
Administrators can configure reports to run in the background. Background reports allow you to continue working while the report is being generated. For more information, see Configure a report to run as a scheduled task.
 Ensure that the Main Scheduler and Report Scheduler have been started before running a background report.
1. In the relevant feature set, select the required report.
2. Set time requirements:
To run the report immediately:
i. Ensure that the toggle is turned on in the Run Report Now row.
ii. Select RUN REPORT.
To run the report at a specific time:
i. Turn off the toggle in the Run Report Now row.
ii. In the Schedule Time calendar, select the date and time for the report.
iii. Select SAVE.
 Administrators can check the processed reports and configure them to run as recurring scheduled tasks. For more information, see Configure a report to run as a scheduled task.
3. When the report has run successfully, download it from Notifications 
. For more information, see View notifications of reports.
 You can also view the status of the report in Notifications. When the report is running, the status is IN PROGRESS. When the report has run successfully, the status is COMPLETED.
View notifications of reports
You can view, download, and delete report results in Notifications.
 You cannot view, download, and delete LISTVIEW format reports in Notifications. They are displayed in a new window.
1. In the side bar, select Notifications 
.
2. Select View Notification 
 to view the complete details of a report notification.
Notification features
Feature
Description
Report status
IN PROGRESS: The report is running in the background and has not been completed.
COMPLETED: The report has run successfully and can be downloaded.
FAILED: The report has failed. The error details appear in the notification.
Download Report 
Downloads the report.
 Supported download formats: CSV, CSV REPEATED, EXCEL EXTENDED (OPENXML), EXCEL (OPENXML), EXCEL REPEATED
(OPENXML), WORD (OPENXML), and XHTML.
Delete Notification 
Deletes the report notification.
How are the notifications managed for visual intelligence and list view reports?
A polling mechanism for Visual Intelligence Reports (VIR) and list view reports runs a query to find and display the notifications related to the successfully executed reports. The report results are displayed in a list format. The polling
interval time is 10 milliseconds (ms), and the maximum time to complete a poll is 300 ms.
Using ENS
The Engineering Numbering System (ENS) provides automated object identification numbering that allows you to apply a specific identification format section to create forms as you create objects and documents in the system. You can
also reserve a sequence of ENS numbers that can be allocated to objects in the future for a specific project. The ENS identifying format that you can use is defined in an ENS definition.
Can you make ENS display items mandatory on forms?
The ENS fields in display items for ENS Definitions are mandatory by default on the create or copy forms to ensure that the ENS is correctly applied to data as it is created. However, for some ENS definitions, you can stop this by clearing
the Mandatory on the form option as you create an ENS definition.
You can preview the ENS items added to the ENS definition in the ENS summary. This includes any ENS items that contain a classification. For more information, see ENS preview summary.
You can control the display of ENS in the object create forms using the Relationship Builder. For more information, see Control ENS display in create forms.
Create multiple objects with ENS
When an ENS definition has been configured, you can create multiple objects using the ENS fields dynamically displayed in the Main details section in the create or copy forms. You enter the number of objects you want to create in the
following fields:
ENS Start Sequence - allows you to select the number from which you want to start the ENS numbering.
Number to Create - indicates the number of ENS numbers to create.
 As you add the details in the create or copy form, the ENS definition identifiers are displayed in the dynamic section.
For example, the following image displays a ENS definition configured with four ENS items for the Create Data Book form. The first ENS item is configured with the Name property set as a Constant, the second and third ENS items are
configured with Related Items set to different classification relationship definition UIDs, and the last ENS item is configured with Function set to one as the ENS Sequence start number.
When you have completed the ENS object details in the create or copy form wizard, you can preview the sequence of any created ENS numbers in the ENS Summary page. For more information, see ENS preview summary.
If you are creating more than one document, you cannot upload any file attachments in this process.
Only numbers are supported as pad characters for sequence type ENS items.
ENS preview summary
You can preview the created ENS definition numbered objects in the Summary page, which shows:
The total number of ENS objects created, starting with the first identifier in the ENS sequence.
The total number of ENS objects that cannot be created due to conflicts.
The current status of the ENS objects.
This following image shows the ENS for five data books with related ENS items set with the relationship definition UIDs for two different classifications.
Every object with a conflicted ENS number is listed with the reason why it was not created, such as the number already exists, it is locked, or the number was previously reserved.
If a start number is not selected, the sequence will start at the first available number in the system.
If the sequence includes a section of numbers already allocated, the summary will indicate those and continue after those allocated numbers.
Using the Info Map
You can use the Info Map to dynamically visualize objects and their relationships to other objects, all displayed in an easy to view graphical representation. When you select an object, the Info Map displays it as a central node surrounded
by any related objects displayed as separate nodes. When you select another object node, you make that object the focus of the Info Map so it is now displayed as the central node surrounded by its own related objects.
How can I interact with the Info Map?
Drag and reposition individual object nodes in the window using the mouse or touch screen control.
Zoom and resize objects in the display using the mouse wheel or touch screen control.
You interact with the Info Map and change how objects are presented using the following tool-bar commands:
Name
Icon
Description
Single Mode
Displays a single node surrounded by its related objects and relationships.
Follow Mode
Displays multiple nodes each surrounded by their related objects and relationships, which allows you to follow the
expansion and grow a relationship tree.
 The follow mode is disabled when you view the Info Map from the object details form.
Reset
Resets the node positions back to their original position.
Clear History
Clears the Info Map expansion history and returns the display the selected node and its related objects.
Hide Node Names
Hides the names of each node displayed in the window.
Show Node Names
Shows the names of each node displayed in the window.
Zoom to Fit
Fits all the displayed object nodes into the window.
Undo
Undoes the last selected node expansion.
Redo
Redoes the last selected node expansion.
What are the node commands?
When you hover over any object node, the following commands display around the selected node:
Name
Icon
Description
Actions
Opens the Actions menu for the selected object node.
Details
Displays the Object Details form for the selected object node.
View and Markup
Displays any files attached to the selected object node.
 Due to a limitation in Microsoft Internet Explorer, these commands can become hidden behind other nodes. Drag the node clear of any nodes to see them.
 A number count value on a node indicates that the selected node has multiple related objects. By default, when a node displays a count value greater than three (>3), selecting the node displays the related objects in a results grid.
The value to trigger this action is not configurable.
Create a relationship tree
Select Follow Mode to expand multiple nodes and their related objects to create a relationship tree.
Each time you select another object node, its related objects and relationships are also expanded out in addition to the previous expanded nodes. In this way, you can create a relationship tree, and expand a visual representation of
relationships from one object to another.
Limitations using Info Map
There are variations in functionality using Info Map due to differences in the operating software and the device being used, such as Apple iPads or Android tablets. This means you interact differently with the UI depending on the device
you use. For example:
Apple devices require two clicks to select a node, a click to open the node options, and a click to select an option.
Android devices require you to select and hold a node to open the node options, and a click to select an option.
Node commands can be obscured if you use Microsoft Internet Explorer (IE). This is due to a technical limitation imposed by Microsoft Internet Explorer. Drag the selected node clear of other elements to see the node commands.
 All created sites use the expansion values SPFRelExpand1to2 and SPFRelExpand2to1 to display items in the Info Map. Any user changes made to the Info Map values are retained on upgrade.
Using workflows
Workflows in HxGN SDx2 are ready for you to use. Each workflow is a set of steps that represents business processes to help you manage tasks that must be completed and focus on workflow steps assigned to you.
Make workflows work for you
Workflows are ready for you to use straight away.
Configure automatic reassignment of workflow steps for out-of-office or disabled users
You can configure the automatic reassignment of workflow steps to take place when the user is away from the organization, temporarily or permanently, by assigning a deputy for the user.
When a user is away for one or more days, temporary automatic reassignment of workflow steps and review objects takes place. For a disabled user, permanent automatic reassignment of workflow steps and review objects takes place.
In both cases, a scheduler task processes the pending workflow steps and the corresponding review objects, and then reassigns these to the selected deputy. All the supporting information, comments, and actions related to review objects
are also reassigned to the selected deputy.
 The same process is applicable for consolidation steps.
Reassignment of workflow steps to an existing reviewer
If a workflow step is reassigned to a user who is already a reviewer and that reviewer has not completed their review, the user becomes the deputy, but continues working through their original review. Any actions or review comments from
the user that is out-of-office or disabled are transferred to the deputy. The workflow step for the out-of-office or disabled user is terminated, and the review comments and actions of the user will not appear in any reports.
If a workflow step is reassigned to a user who is already a reviewer and that reviewer has completed their review, then the workflow step from the out-of-office user is not transferred to the deputy, and actions and comments are not
transferred.
Reassignment of workflow steps to the consolidator
If a workflow step is reassigned to a user who is the consolidator, the consolidation step takes precedence over the review step. Any actions or review comments from the user that is out of office or disabled are transferred to the
consolidator so that they can review them and act upon them. The workflow step for the out-of-office or disabled user is terminated, and the review comments and actions of the user will not appear in any reports.
Reassignment of a consolidation step to a reviewer
If a consolidation workflow step is reassigned to a user who is already a reviewer and that reviewer has not completed their review, all actions and review comments from reviewers are transferred and the deputy becomes the consolidator
of all actions and comments from other reviewers. If the reviewer has completed their review, his review is left intact, and he performs the consolidation of all actions and review comments.
For more information, see Allow reassignment of workflow steps.
Allow reassignment of workflow steps
The Allow workflow step auto reassignment option in the system options enables workflow steps to be reassigned to deputies. Scheduler tasks process the steps and move them to the deputy's inbox.
In the system options, in the Workflow options details section, select the Allow workflow step auto reassignment option..
 When the option is cleared, workflow steps cannot be reassigned automatically to the specified deputies.
In the system options, in the Workflow options details section, select the Allow workflow step auto reassignment option.
For more information on the system options, see Configure the system options.
Business administrators can transfer items to a new owner either permanently or temporarily. For more information, see Reassign tasks owned by one user to another user.
Understand role criteria for filtering deputies
The following role criteria are used to filter the deputies that can be selected when a workflow step is reassigned. The options are SAMEROLE, MANAGEDBYROLE, MANAGEROLE, and ANY. You can select multiple criteria, for example,
SAMEROLE and MANAGEROLE, to filter available deputies.
SAMEROLE
In this scenario, the software filters deputies based on those users who have the same roles or more roles. User2 is a valid deputy because it has the same roles plus more than User1. User3 is not a valid deputy because it only matches
fewer roles than User1.
MANAGEDBYROLE
In this scenario, the software filters deputies based on those users who manage the user's role. User1 is related to Role1 which is managed, through a relationship, by Role3. User2 is not directly managed by Role3, while User3 is directly
