The files and reference objects, such as actions, added as information to support the review.
7
Displays the recipients along with the review progress information. Reviews that are in progress are shown as In
Review and those not started are shown as Review not started. The consolidator and reviewers are distinguished by
distinct icons.
What are the life cycle statuses of document reviews?
The following review statuses are found in the document review user interface.
Review status
Visible to
Description
CLOSED AUTOMATICALLY
Originating organization
The document review is closed automatically because the review process is
overdue or by the consolidation process.
COMPLETED
Originating organization
The document review is complete and finished.
DRAFT
Creator in originating organization
The document review has been created as a draft, but it has not been
completed.
WAIVED
Originating organization
The document review recipient chooses to ignore the review.
 Document reviews are not visible to contractors.
Create actions within a QA review
What happens when an action is rejected in the QA Review process?
The QA Action Rejection workflow handles the rejected actions during the document review process. The workflow identifies the originator of the action and sends a rejection notification.
Add actions from the Document Review page
1. Select 
 Create Action on the QA Document Review page.
2. Provide other details as needed.
3. Select Next and browse to any external files you want to relate to the action.
You can select EDIT to update your action, and add more actions for each review until you are finished, and select COMPLETE.
Attached image files and documents appear as links, which are launched in View and Markup.
You can view the details, manage layers, update, or terminate an action by selecting Options 
.
Access to object hyperlinks and options can be restricted by system security rules. For more information, contact your administrator.
Add comments and reference information
1. Select EDIT to add your review comment for the document named in the Document Summary.
2. Expand Supporting Information, and select ATTACH FILES 
 to add any files as supporting information for the review.
3. Select MANAGE RELATED ITEMS to relate documents and objects as supporting information for the review.
4. Select SAVE to save your review. You can edit your review until you are finished.
For more information, see Create and edit a relationship.
If you want to create a new comment on the review, select Actions 
 > New Comment. You can add more comments for each review until you are finished.
If you want to update or terminate a comment on the review, select Options 
 and select Update or Terminate.
You can add hyperlinks to the Comments box. Access to object hyperlinks and options can be restricted by system security rules. For more information, contact your administrator.
Comments from reviewers outside of the review process are read-only.
Attached files appear as links so you can view and mark them up using View and Markup.
Consolidate reviews and prepare to issue
Before sending document reviews back to contractors, you consolidate the document reviews.
What is a document review consolidation, and when should it occur?
A consolidator can complete the consolidation step even without any review completed. The consolidator compiles all the document reviews, comments, and actions as part of the document review consolidation workflow step.
The consolidator responds to each document review, either with approval or by creating a sub-review as an internal technical query asking for further information. These appear in the document reviewers To Do List for rectification. The
consolidator can reject or terminate any duplicate or inappropriate actions.
The consolidator creates the consolidated review, which contains a marked-up PDF rendition file with all applicable actions. Finally, the consolidator sets the return code on the document before selecting COMPLETE to finish, which places
the consolidated document on a workflow to the recipient contractor.
Consolidate the document reviews
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Consolidate The Review Comments <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/64c1401df68938110956dc2e> .
What can a consolidator do?
The consolidator can:
Determine how a review proceeds by either:
Rejecting the review and sending any rejected actions back to the reviewer for rework.
For any rejected actions, the state of the review and actions are set to DRAFT REJECTED. If the rejected actions are associated with a markup layer and that layer is selected to return to the contractor, warnings are given that
the selected layer contains unapproved actions. The consolidator cannot complete the process to issue the document reviews to the contractor until the action is resolved, deleted, or moved to a markup layer that is not selected
to return to the contractor.
Completing the consolidation of reviews, actions and comments and issuing them to the contractor.
Add one or more users as document reviewers during the review process.
When a consolidator adds a user, who has a document receipt notification step, as a reviewer, the document receipt notification step is replaced by a review step.
The new review step is assigned the same target date as that of the original step. If the review is already overdue, the added reviewer will receive an overdue step.
View and edit any reviews, actions, and comments in a DRAFT or RESERVED state, prior to submission.
View all supporting documentation for every review, action, or comment.
View the markup layers and select which markup layers you want to associate with a specific action.
Make changes to the reviews, actions, or comments and then reconsolidate if a document review is rejected.
Send technical queries if more information is required on an action or comment.
What happens when a consolidator requests additional reviewers for a document that is in review?
The QA Append Reviewer workflow handles the request from a consolidator to add one or more reviewers to a document that is in review. The workflow creates a review object for the appended reviewer and adds a task in the user's To
Do list.
1. In your To Do List, select a consolidation review task, and select Actions 
 > Perform QA Review Consolidation.
 The consolidator cannot edit any reviews.
a. In your To Do List, select a document review task for the Perform QA Review Consolidation step.
b. Select Actions 
 > Add Reviewer.
c. In the Add Recipients page, select the Role and User, and then select Finish.
2. On the Reviews tab
a. Review all the markups, actions, and comments.
 You can select specific markup layers to attach an action.
b. Add consolidation review comments.
3. Accept or reject the review and any actions from the review.
 Additional comments or markups may be available from other contributors outside of the review. Select Show contributor markups to see them.
4. On the toolbar, select Document Return Code for the consolidated review. This notifies the originating company of the return status that the consolidator has set for the review, such as Acknowledged, Approved, Rejected,
Reviewed, or Reviewed with Comments.
 Select CREATE INTERNAL TECHNICAL QUERY to request more information from an internal resource.
5. Select COMPLETE. The consolidated review is marked as complete and is removed from your To Do List.
 The Validate Review Supporting Information step is executed when you complete the consolidation step to check if the recipient organization has access to all the reference documents. If the validation fails, you have to
either remove the reference document or update the collaborating organization on the document to complete the step.
How do I identify review progress as a consolidator?
When a QA review is completed or waived, it reflects in the review progress column as a percentage. The percentage of the progress depends on the number of reviewers to the document. For example, If a document has two reviewers
assigned:
Reviewer action
Review progress
Review is started but not completed by any of the reviewers
The review progress is 0.
One of the reviewers completes or waives off the review
The review progress is 50%.
You add a new reviewer
The review progress is 33.33%. (Now the number of reviewers are three and only one of them has completed the
review)
You reassign a reviewer who has already completed the reviews
The review progress is calculated accordingly.
All the reviewers complete the review task
The review progress is completed and is shown as 100%.
What are Contributor markups?
Documents can also be marked up by users who are not assigned to a QA review task. The users who create the markups and comments outside the context of QA review are called as contributors.
The reason why a contributor might want to create markups outside of a QA review is to provide general observations that are shared as comments/markups and the markups provided in other context like technical queries or actions
context.
Familiarize yourself with the Document Review Consolidation page 
1
The details of the document under review, where you can select individual tabs to see the reviews, actions, and
comments to help form a consolidated review.
2
Displays the return code selected for the status of the consolidated review. Select CREATE INTERNAL TECHNICAL
QUERY to create an internal technical query in the consolidator's organization to gather more information.
3
The document summary includes the name, description, revision of the document being consolidated, and the latest
issue purposes, and identifies if any markups contain rejected actions.
4
The files and comments attached to the consolidated review along with the PDF renditions, categorized based on the
issue purpose, for the consolidation. Displays the status of reviewers and contributors.
5
Select COMPLETE to finish your consolidated review.
6
The primary and reference actions. Select EDIT to modify an action or select Options 
 to manage layers, update, or
terminate an action. Warnings display if an action or action pin in a markup layer is rejected or reserved.
7
Displays the recipients along with the review progress information. Reviews that are in progress are shown as In
Review and those not started are shown as Review not started. The consolidator and reviewers are distinguished by
distinct icons.
Manage markup layers
While preparing actions or responses, any markup layer created is related to the communication object. This relationship is used to identify the context and preselect the layers created in that context.
For the layers created outside the current scope, the context type does not exist and the layers do not get autoselected.
If a responder or a reviewer waives a review, the association between the markup and the communication object is delinked.
If a consolidator completes a response or review while it is in a draft state, the association between the markup and the communication object is delinked.
Manage document markup layers
In the document review consolidation, you can manage which markup layers you want to associate with a specific document, so only the relevant markup layers are attached to the document. This provides you with control of how the
consolidated rendition PDF file is sent with the document review.
Familiarize yourself with the Manage Document Markup Layers page
1
The number in the gray panel indicates the total number of layers present in the context.
The number in the green panel indicates the number of layers selected.
2
The context type from where the layer was created.
3
The action pin. The pin that is being managed is highlighted in blue.
From a Document Review Consolidation
1. Select Documents Summary 
 > Manage Layers on a document.
2. In Manage Document Markup Layers, select a file name in the left summary pane.
Select this
To do this
Each markup layer
To see more details in the right pane, which displays the context, any text markups created by the user, any
pinned actions, and the rendition relationships generated by the system.
RELATE LAYERS TO ACTIONS check box
To enable the actions raised in reviews using the action pins to use the same rendition as the document. The
RELATE LAYERS TO ACTIONS check box sets the relation between the actions created from the action pins
and the markup.
Clear the RELATE LAYERS TO ACTIONS check box
To manage the markup layers on the pinned actions.
 The system remembers the state of the RELATE LAYERS TO ACTIONS checkbox for only the current review, on exiting the Manage Document Markup Layers dialog box.
You can also manage layers on an individual action to include a subset of all the layers available. Once the review is closed out, the actions are put in a workflow which generates the renditions for the actions.
If the layer has the context from where the layer is created, the context type is displayed for each markup layer.
If the layer is created from the current review, the context type is highlighted in blue and appears on top of the other layers.
A consolidator can select all the context layers created from the current review by selecting the SELECT LAYERS BY CONTEXT check box.
Manage action markup layers
You can manage which markup layers you want to associate with a specific action, so only the relevant markup layers are attached to the action. This provides you with control of how the consolidated rendition PDF file is sent with the
action.
From an individual action
1. Select the Actions tab.
2. Select 
 > Manage Markup Layers on an action.
3. In the Manage Action Markup Layers dialog box, select a file name in the left summary pane.
4. In the middle selection pane, select each markup layer to see the relevant information on the right summary pane. The right pane displays the context, any text markups created by the user, any pinned actions, and the rendition
relationships generated by the system.
If the layer has the context from where the layer is created, the context type is displayed for each markup layer.
If the layer is created from the current review, then the context type is highlighted in blue and appears on top of the other layers.
A consolidator can select all the context layers created from the current review by selecting the SELECT LAYERS BY CONTEXT check box.
Respond to document reviews
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Respond to Document Reviews <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/64eb20613232a68262d3d14f>
Once the document reviews are issued, the contractors respond to document renditions specific to their contractor organization. Contractors only see reviews, actions, and document renditions, including markups, for their organization and
do not see other organization or contractors' responses.
If the contractor decides to respond, they make the requested updates or changes from the owner, resubmit the updates, and the document review process starts again.
If the contractor decides not to respond, they close the document review.
 Renditions are generated when the project communications are issued and are presented to the recipient instead of the original documents. Renditions contain the markup layers identified for the relevant communication during the
consolidation review.
View the status of responses
To view the status of responses to your technical query, select Actions 
 > Communication Management.
Communicate with stakeholders in your project
You can create outgoing, incoming, and internal queries or actions to communicate with your company and contractor project teams. You can also interactively assign tasks to team members.
Technical queries are managed communications that provide a consistent path and tracking to ensure that the query and any responses are completed in a timely manner. For more information about technical queries, see Learn more
about technical queries.
Actions are observations made during a review that might require additional followup.
Track your technical queries and actions
How do the technical query processes work?
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Introduction to Technical Queries <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/65c3411afc7d6ca3853d90d0>
Technical queries are used by companies and their contractors to ask each other questions or to gather more information.
What does the technical query status mean?
Status
Description
RESERVED
The technical query has been created (draft).
ISSUED
The technical query has been sent to the receiver.
RESPONDED
The technical query has been answered.
REJECTED
The technical query has been rejected.
RESPONSE REJECTED
The technical query's response has been rejected.
RECEIPT ACCEPTED
The technical query has been accepted.
 This status is not applicable for the internal technical query.
INITIATED
The technical query has been submitted, but not yet issued.
 This status is not applicable for the internal technical query.
COMPLETED
The technical query has been closed.
Manage Incoming/Outgoing technical query
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Incoming/outgoing Technical Queries <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/67dd4daf3e379c93c931bfc3>
An incoming technical query is initiated by the contractor to request information from the owner.
An outgoing technical query is initiated by the owner to request information from the contractor.
 Your system administrator may modify the workflow so that the steps differ from those shown. The steps are still carried out using the procedures described below.
Manage internal technical queries
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Internal Technical Queries <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/6633729483ad7fd9d82136e5>
An internal technical query is initiated within an organization to ask questions of other disciplines or departments.
 Your system administrator may modify the workflow so that the steps differ from those shown. The steps are still carried out using the procedures described below.
Learn more about technical queries
Technical queries are managed communications between appropriate stakeholders that provide a consistent path and tracking to ensure that the query and any responses are completed in a timely manner.
Technical queries between stakeholders:
Require a response within a contractually-specified timeframe
May be related to one or more documents or tags
May have responses from multiple responders
A technical query is a report raised on a document, process, or equipment by an engineer or any other authorized user to report an item for further action, such as gathering information, notification of a change of design, maintenance,
discussion, and so on. The technical query is attached to a defined workflow definition asynchronously to distribute the query details to contractors and their technical experts or internally to technical experts within the organization.
Each technical query contains all the relevant information, images, or documents for the item or process being reported, which can be opened directly from within the technical query. When you create a technical query, PDF renditions are
generated and issued to the recipient or contractor instead of the original documents. The technical query can also be configured so that the recipients must respond within a designated time frame to meet any contractual obligations.
How does the action process work?
Actions are used by companies and their contractors to define and track observations made during a review that require additional followup.
What does the action status mean?
The following statuses apply to outgoing and internal actions.
Status
Description
RESERVED
The action has been created (draft).
ISSUED
The action has been sent to the receiver.
RESPONDED
The action has been responded.
REJECTED
The action has been rejected.
RESPONSE REJECTED
The action's response has been rejected.
COMPLETED
The action has been closed.
Manage internal actions
Internal actions are initiated by a user to other users of disciplines or departments within an organization to perform an action, such as update or validate engineering documents or tags.
Steps
Description
Create an internal action
Select Create Internal Action from the Internal Communication feature set.
Add content, markup and comments
This step appears in the To Do List of the user that created the action.
Select the step and then select Actions > Prepare Content to complete and issue the action.
Perform action and respond
The selected responders receive the request in their To Do List.
Select Respond to action.
Review the response
The author reviews the response. If all is OK, they close the action. If more information is required, the action is automatically revised, then the author is
asked to prepare and re-issue the action.
Select Review action response.
Manage outgoing actions
Outgoing actions are initiated by the originating company to the contractor, to perform an action, such as update or validate engineering documents. An outgoing action can be created as part of the Quality Assurance Review process from
the project communication feature set.
Steps
Description
Create an outgoing action
Create the outgoing action from the project communication feature set.
 You can also create an outgoing action from the Document Review page.
Add content, markup and comments
This step appears in the To Do List of the user that created the action.
Select the step and then select Actions > Prepare Content to complete and issue the action.
Perform compliance review
This is an optional step and is controlled by an option on the Response Control tab when you create the action.
Select Perform Compliance Review.
You can reject the action during the review and send it back to the author if it requires rework.
You can reassign this step, if necessary.
Perform action and respond
The selected responders receive the request in their To Do List.
Select Respond to action.
Review the response
The author reviews the response. If all is OK, they close the action. If more information is required, the action is automatically revised , then the author is
asked to prepare and re-issue the action.
Select Review action response.
Learn more about actions
Actions are observations made during a review that might require additional follow-up. These actions have a standard workflow which allows users to assign, manage, and track actions with ease.
What do you want to do?
Create a technical query or an action
Export technical query contents
Add content, markups, and comments
Review for contractual compliance in originating organization
Initial review (applicable for technical queries only)
Respond to technical queries or actions
Consolidate responses (applicable for technical queries only)
Review the response for contractual compliance (applicable for technical queries only)
Rework the response (applicable for technical queries only)
Review the response
Cancel a communication object
Create a technical query or an action
Create a technical query
What types of technical queries can I send and receive?
The three types of technical queries are:
Incoming Technical Query – Initiated by the contractor to request information from the originating company.
Outgoing Technical Query – Initiated by the originating company to request information from the contractor.
Internal Technical Query – Initiated within the organization, company, or contractor to request information across departments or disciplines.
In a project communication feature set, select the type of technical query you want to send.
1. Select the Contract, To organization, Security Code, and either the To role or To user.
2. Select Next and browse to any external files you want to relate to the technical query.
 The Is contractual compliance review required? option (selected by default for outgoing and incoming technical queries) in the Response Control tab sends the technical query for a compliance review before issuing the
technical query to the contractor.
Create an action
What happens when an action is created? 
The action is automatically attached to the Action workflow, and a notification is sent to the recipient's To Do List.
1. Select the required communication feature set, and select Create Action.
 You can also select an object, and select Actions 
 > Create [Type] Action.
2. Provide other details as needed.
3. Select Next and browse to any external files you want to relate to the action.
Selecting the Is contractual compliance review required? option in the Response Control tab sends the outgoing action for a compliance review before issuing the action to the contractor.
Selecting the Follow up required option in the Response Control tab sends a Review responses step to the originator.
 You can update an action when it is in the RESERVED state by selecting Actions 
 > Update.
Update a technical query or an action
1. Select Actions 
 > Update.
 From the Prepare Content page, select the Update button.
2. Update the information for the technical query or the action, as required.
You can update a technical query or an action when it is in the RESERVED state.
The due date is defined by the contract on outgoing actions.
Export technical query contents
Additional workflow steps generate a summary report when a technical query is exported and then generate the report as a PDF. This is attached to the workflow before the technical query is submitted, typically, to contractors who want to
share the information outside of the system.
Locate a reserved technical query and use the Technical Query Letter Preview action to view the letter.
The Export technical query contents command generates a Zip file that includes a similar Microsoft Excel report. This Excel report lists, in a separate row, each attached document, the attached files, and the location of the separate
folders for each document. The separate folders contain the files attached to the documents.
Add content, markups, and comments
To add content, markups, and comments, select the Add content, markup, and comments step in your To Do List.
Manage related information
1. Select Actions 
 > Prepare Content.
2. In the Prepare Content dialog box, select Manage Reference Documents or Manage Reference Tags.
3. In the Results page, select one or more documents (or) tags.
4. Select RELATE.
Relate markups
1. Select Actions 
 > Prepare Content.
2. In the Prepare Content dialog box, select Manage Markup Layers.
3. In the Manage Action Markup Layers dialog box, select a file name in the left summary pane.
 The 
 icon indicates that the file cannot be viewed, either because the web file has not yet been generated or because the base Hexagon file is not available. Contact your administrator if the base file is unavailable.
4. In the middle selection pane, select each markup layer you want to relate. The right pane displays the context, any text markups created by the user, any pinned actions, and the rendition relationships generated by the system.
Add comments and issue
1. Select Actions 
 > Prepare Content.
2. In the Prepare Content dialog box, select Update, and add your comments and message for the recipient in the DETAILS section. The issue state becomes ISSUED.
Review for contractual compliance in originating organization
What is a contractual compliance review, and what does it involve?
A contractual compliance review can be configured to review any changes to contractual deliverables and actions that extend outside of the company. This ensures that all communications and requests that are submitted to the contractor
are within the scope of the contract terms and can be issued to the supplier or contractor for implementation.
The consolidator creates a communication with a concise set of actions that can be sent to the contractor, and the contractual compliance reviewer checks the communication contents, and actions for compliance with the contract. The
contractual compliance reviewer receives the communication in their To Do List and reviews each of the related changes, markups and actions. The contractual compliance reviewer can reject any of the communication contents, actions
and may make additional actions and markups identifying any changes.
Any rejected communications, actions are returned to the consolidator using a dedicated rejection workflow. The consolidator can alter or request alterations to any rejected communications, actions before reconsolidating the actions and
markups.
1. In your To Do List, select the action to check for compliance against the contract before being issued.
2. Select Actions 
> Perform Compliance Review.
3. Review the documents and files for contractual compliance.
 You can select the file hyperlink under ALL FILES tab to open the file in its native format. If you want to view the markups associated with that file:
i. Select Manage Markup Layers next to the communication object.
ii. Select VIEW MARKUP to open the file in the View and Markup window.
 If the contractual compliance review is approved, the action issue state is set to ISSUED. If not approved, the state is set to REJECTED.
Initial review
After the technical queries are issued, they appear in the initial recipient's To Do List of the target organization.
As an initial recipient, you can:
Open the technical query and initiate the response.
Identify other responders and a consolidator. However, if you are the only one to respond, you are also the consolidator.
Identify and manage the markup layers of the marked up technical query renditions and any reference documents.
Reject the technical query. The rejected technical query is sent back to the originator.
Perform the initial response
1. Select the Initial review step in your To Do List.
2. Select Actions 
> Perform Initial Response.
Identify additional responders
As the initial recipient of the technical query, select additional responders and a consolidator for the technical query.
Select Identify Responders on the Perform Initial Response page.
 Select Is contractual compliance review required for response? option to send the technical query response for a contractual compliance review, before sending the technical query back to the originating organization.
Respond to technical queries or actions
Respond to a technical query
What are the main responsibilities for each responder?
The following table shows the standard responsibilities by role for a technical query response.
Initial recipient
Responder
Consolidator
Open Communication Management
Open Communication Management
Open Communication Management
Assign responders and consolidator
Edit, attach, and save a response
Edit, attach, and save a response
Edit, attach, and save a consolidated response
View completed responses
View and manage all responses
Manage markup layers for all responses
View all previous revisions of the technical query
View all previous revisions of the technical query
View all previous revisions of the technical query
Reject a response
Waive a response
Suppress a response
Complete a response
Complete a response
Complete all responses
Consolidate the responses
Reopen a consolidated response
Reject a technical query back to originating organization with a message
What is the difference between primary and reference documents?
You can distinguish between the different documents that appear in a communication review or response:
Primary documents are the main subject of a review or response and require action, comment, or query.
Reference documents are included for reference or provide additional information to support a review or response, action, comment, or query.
What does a contractual compliance review involve for a technical query response?
A contractual compliance review, when configured, can review the technical query responses that may have changes to contractual deliverables or actions that extend outside of the company. This ensures that all technical queries are
completed within the scope of the contract terms and can be returned to the originator.
The contractual compliance reviewer receives the technical query in their To Do List and checks the content of the technical query for compliance with the contract. The contractual compliance reviewer can reject a technical query. Any
rejected technical query is returned to the consolidator to make additional edits or comments to the documentation that require changes to the response.
Get to know the technical query response user interface
1
The details of the technical query, showing the responders, supplier's organization, current issue status, and due date.
2
The files, objects, and any previous technical queries added as related information to the technical query.
3
The responders selected to respond to the technical query.
4
The details of one of the responses already completed, including any markups, actions, and comments.
5
The files and primary reference documents added as information to support the technical query response.
 Select 
 next to a document to show the associated files.
After the Initial review step:
Responders can comment, update, or mark up document renditions specific to their organization.
If a responder does not want to respond or no longer needs to respond, they can waive the technical query response. For example, a responder might waive the technical query response if another responder has already made the
same comment.
Each responder can save their technical query response and add supporting information as files or documents before completing the response.
The response consolidator reviews the actions, comments, and related files from the responders and submits the consolidated technical query response containing the renditions back to the originator.
The response consolidator can complete and reject any technical query or response and return back the response to each responder or the originating organization with a message for rework.
When the originating organization receives the consolidated technical query response, they can either close the technical query or reject the response.
If the originating organization rejects the technical query response, the technical query can be revised with any additional information or renditions and resubmitted.
If a contractual compliance review is configured, a rejected response is returned to the consolidator.
Renditions are generated when the project communications are issued and are presented to the recipient instead of the original documents. Renditions contain the markup layers identified for the relevant communication during the
consolidation review.
If the rendition does not generate correctly, contact your system administrator to fix the problem.
To add new markups, select on file hyperlink to launch the View and Markup page.
Create a response
As a responder, create a technical query response.
Select 
 > EDIT in the response page.
View the status of responses
As a responder or a consolidator, view the status of responses to the technical query.
Select Actions 
 > Communication Management.
Manage markup layers
As a responder or a consolidator, identify and manage the markup layers in an object attached to the technical query.
Select 
 > Manage Markup Layers.
Manage response markup layers
As a consolidator, identify and manage the markup layers of the marked up technical query renditions and any reference documents on the responses. The consolidated response contains all comments, reference documents, and files
from other responders.
Relate documents to the response
As a responder, you can relate documents and files as information to support your technical query response.
1. Expand Supporting Information, and select 
 ATTACH FILES to add any files as supporting information for the review.
2. Select MANAGE RELATED ITEMS to relate documents and objects as supporting information for the review.
 You can also select a technical query, and select Actions 
 > Relationship Builder.
Show responders
As a responder, view the other responders that are assigned to the technical query.
Select Actions 
 > Show Responders.
Reassign technical query to another responder
As a responder, reassign the technical query to another responder.
Select Actions 
 > Reassign.
All responders can view previous revisions of the technical query in the revision box by selecting the INITIAL version.
All responders can return to the previous object in a results grid by selecting PARENT SCREEN.
View a brief view of the workflow by selecting Actions 
 > Brief View Workflow.
 Responders can only respond to the technical query by selecting the LATEST revision.
Complete a response
As a responder or a consolidator, select Actions 
 > Respond to TQ to complete the responses.
 Select _ next to a document to show the associated files. The _ icon indicates that the file cannot be viewed, either because the web file has not yet been generated or because the base Hexagon file is not available. See Manually
generate or regenerate WebGL viewer files for Hexagon 2D and 3D files for details of how to generate the web file. Contact your administrator if the base file is unavailable.
Respond to an action
1. Select the Perform action and respond step in your To Do List.
2. Select Actions 
 > Prepare Response.
Create a response
As a responder, create an action response.
Select 
 > EDIT in the response page.
View the status of response
As a responder, view the status of responses to the action.
Select Actions 
 > Communication management details.
Reassign action to another responder
As a responder, reassign the action to another responder.
Select Actions 
 > Reassign.
Complete a response
As a responder, select Actions 
 > Respond to Action to complete the response.
To add new markups, select on file hyperlink to launch the View and Markup page.
For information on how to manage the markup layers and relate documents to the response, see Respond to a technical query above.
You can reject an action but must provide a reason for the rejection. When the originator is notified that the action is rejected, they must decide to either update the action or cancel it.
Consolidate responses
1. Select the Consolidate responses step in your To Do List.
2. Click Actions 
 > Consolidate Responses.
The consolidator can consolidate all the reference documents and attached files provided by other responders using Add to Consolidate in the Consolidate Responses dialog box. Any response comments provided by other
responders have to be manually copied to the consolidated response by the consolidator.
The consolidator can remove any reference documents and directly attached files provided by other responders from the consolidated response using Remove from Consolidate in the Consolidate Responses dialog box.
All the markups created by other responders on the reference documents and directly attached files are selected by default in the consolidated response. The consolidator has the option to deselect any of these markups from the
consolidated response and can also create new markups.
The consolidator can reassign the Consolidate responses step to another user.
Click Actions 
 > Reassign.
The consolidator has to rework the technical query response if the compliance reviewer rejects the consolidated response. For more information, see Rework the response.
What happens when a consolidator requests additional responders?
The Append Responder workflow handles the request from a consolidator to add one or more responders to the current technical query workflow. The workflow creates a review object for the appended reviewer and adds a task in the
user's To Do list.
1. Select the Consolidate responses step in your To Do List.
2. Click Actions 
 > Add Responder.
3. In the Add Recipients page, select the Role and User, and then click Finish.
How to identify step progress as a consolidator?
When a Prepare response step is completed, it reflects in the step progress column as a percentage. The percentage of the progress depends on the number of responders to the technical query. For example, If a technical query has two
responders assigned:
Responder action
Step progress
Response is started but not completed by any of the responders
The step progress is 0.
One of the responders completes the response
The step progress is 50%.
You add a new responder
The step progress is 33.33%. (Now the number of responders is three and only one of them has
completed the response)
You reassign the Prepare Response step to a responder who has already completed the technical query
The step progress of incoming or outgoing technical query is calculated accordingly.
All the responders complete the prepare response task
The step progress is completed and is shown as 100%.
How to know the status of documents ready to be consolidated?
As a consolidator, when in your To Do List page, you can now check the status of each document and its progress.
Review the response for contractual compliance
1. In your To Do List, select the technical query to check for compliance against the contract.
2. Click Actions 
 > Perform Compliance Response Review.
3. Review the response for contractual compliance.
 You can click the file hyperlink under ALL FILES tab to open the file in its native format. If you want to view the markups associated with that file:
i. Click Manage Response Markup Layers next to the response object.
ii. Click VIEW MARKUP to open the file in the View and Markup Response window.
 If the contractual compliance review is approved, the technical query response state is set to ISSUED. If not approved, the state is set to REJECTED.
Rework the response
The consolidator has to rework the technical query response if the compliance reviewer rejects the consolidated response.
1. Select the Rework responses step in your To Do List.
2. Select Actions 
 > Rework Responses.
What happens when a consolidator requests additional responders?
The Append Responder workflow handles the request from a consolidator to add one or more responders to the current technical query workflow. The workflow creates a review object for the appended reviewer and adds a task in the
user's To Do list.
1. Select the Consolidate responses step in your To Do List.
2. Click Actions 
 > Add Responder.
3. In the Add Recipients page, select the Role and User, and then click Finish.
How to identify step progress as a consolidator?
When a Prepare response step is completed, it reflects in the step progress column as a percentage. The percentage of the progress depends on the number of responders to the technical query. For example, If a technical query has two
responders assigned:
Responder action
Step progress
Response is started but not completed by any of the responders
The step progress is 0.
One of the responders completes the response
The step progress is 50%.
You add a new responder
The step progress is 33.33%. (Now the number of responders is three and only one of them has
completed the response)
You reassign the Prepare Response step to a responder who has already completed the technical query
The step progress of incoming or outgoing technical query is calculated accordingly.
All the responders complete the prepare response task
The step progress is completed and is shown as 100%.
Review the response
The originator gets a step in their To Do List to review the response once the compliance reviewer approves the response.
1. Select the Review responses step in your To Do List.
2. Select Actions 
 > Review Responses.
If the originator approves the response, the item is completed.
If the originator rejects the response, the originator gets a Re-issue Required step in the To Do List to prepare the content again and re-issue the item.
Cancel a communication object
Cancel a technical query
1. Click Actions 
 > Edit > Cancel Technical Query.
2. Enter the reason for canceling the technical query in the Cancel Object(s) dialog box.
Cancel an action
What happens when an action is canceled?
When you cancel an action object, the action is set to the Canceled state and attached to a cancel workflow. The receiver and sender of the action are notified that the action is canceled.
1. Click Actions 
 > Edit > Cancel Action.
2. Enter the reason for cancelling the action in the Cancel Object(s) dialog box.
 Any user (except Business Administrator and System Administrator) from the originating organization can cancel an action when it is in the ISSUED state.
Terminate an action
When you terminate an action object, the action gets deleted from the database and cannot be queried.
1. Click Actions 
 > Terminate.
2. Enter the reason for terminating the action in the Terminate Object(s) dialog box.
 Any user (except Business Administrator and System Administrator) from the originating organization can terminate an action when it is in the RESERVED or DRAFT REJECTED state.
Manage internal reviews
As an engineer working on a document, you can send it to other engineers within the organization for review using the internal review workflow. There can be one or more reviewers but only one approver. Once the review process is
complete, the working documents will be signed off before sending it to other organizations.
You can create an internal review action from the Internal Communication feature set or from the Actions menu of one or more working documents. Once the action is created, it gets attached to the Internal Review workflow.
Step
Description
Branch based on condition
In the New Internal Review Action,
Select SAVE AS DRAFT to attach the documents later.
or
Select FINISH to send the documents for review.
Add content, markups, and comments
Select Manage related files or Manage Documents to add files or documents. For more information, see Managing documents and files.
Select Options 
 to
Use the EDIT FILES option to edit the files on your computer. For more information, see Edit files.
Select the file hyperlink to add markups or comments.
This step is completed by the submitter of the internal review workflow.
 The workflow step response date is calculated based on the due date entered. For example, if the due date is 5 days from the current date, the system
adds 5 days to the current date and sets the result as the workflow step response date, excluding any non-working days. If the Due date box value is blank, then the
current date is set as the workflow step response date.
Set milestone on documents
The system creates a document milestone and sets the milestone status to IN REVIEW.
 When an internal review action is in progress, the Edit Files option is hidden from the document actions list. This prevents unauthorized modifications to
the document while it is under review. Once the review action is completed, the Edit Files option is available. However, if an internal review is rejected, a document
cannot be edited until the internal review action is revised. In the rejection state, the internal review action is revised, and the submitter receives a Review Rejection
and Re-Issue Required workflow steps. In a Re-Issue Required workflow step, the documents can be edited once again. You can then make any necessary
changes to the document.
Review the document
The reviewer receives the request in the To Do List. Like in the Add content, markups, and comments step, the reviewer reviews the document and gives feedback.
Approve the changes
The approver reviews the documents and other review comments. If satisfied, the approver consolidates the changes, approves this step, and completes the
workflow. The system sets the milestone status to REVIEW COMPLETED.
If the approver rejects this step, a Review Rejection step is sent to the submitter. The system sets the milestone status to REVIEW REJECTED.
PDF renditions of the documents are generated, including markups. If a PDF rendition fails to generate, the system directs the workflow to the Correct failure step.
For more information on PDF rendition errors, see Correct PDF rendition generation failures.
Review Rejection
If Reissue is selected, the system revises the review action. Any documents, tags, files, markups on the existing object are copied on to the new object. Based on
the feedback, the submitter then updates the content as mentioned in the Add content, markups, and comments step above.
If Close is selected, the system exits the workflow.
Complete the workflow
The workflow ends and the working document is signed off. The submitter receives a completion notice (information step) in the To Do List.
Details of the internal review workflow steps:
1. In the New Internal Review Action:
Type or select the required information.
Navigate to the next page and search for the working documents.
 The current configuration-related documents are returned in the search results, if the Query by selected configurations only option is enabled.
Select one or more working documents that you want to be reviewed, and select ADD.
 You can edit the value of the issue purpose for each selected document. However, the value cannot be blank.
If the submitter selects SAVE AS DRAFT, the system sends an Add content, markups, and comments step to the submitter's To Do List.
If the submitter selects FINISH, the system sends a review step to the selected recipients.
 The system validates that the submitter(s) are not reviewer(s). However, submitter(s) and reviewer(s) can both be approver(s).
2. When the submitter selects FINISH in the New Internal Review Action or completes the Add content step:
The system sets the recipient to the Reviewer and Approver specified in the create dialog.
The system sets the workflow step target date to the Due date specified in the create dialog.
3. The reviewer gets a step in the To Do List. If the reviewer updates the document, the approver sees those changes in the Approver step.
If there are multiple reviewers, the approver must consolidate the changes and can suppress the comments.
Select WAIVE to decline the document review.
 This is an optional step. Reviewer selection is not mandatory in the internal review workflow process.
4. The approver reviews the document. The approver can see any changes made to the document by the reviewers.
If the approver approves the document, it is signed off and the internal review process ends.
 If the approver completes the step, the system closes any pending review steps in the reviewers' To Do List.
If the approver rejects the step, the system sends a Review Rejection step to the submitter.
5. In the Review Rejection step:
If the submitter selects Reissue, the system sends a Revise Request step to the submitter's To Do List. The workflow continues from step 2.
If the submitter selects Close, the system closes the internal review workflow process.
What does the internal review action status mean?
Review status
Icon
Description
RESERVED/DRAFT
Review action has been created as a draft, but it is not ready for review.
ISSUED
Review action was issued to the internal user.
REJECTED
Review action was rejected by the internal user.
COMPLETED
Review action was completed.
SUPERSEDED
Review action was revised.
See how to create an Internal Review Action
See how the Internal Review Action is reviewed and approved
See how the submitter acknowledges and completes the Internal Review Action
What happens when an approver requests additional reviewers for a document that is in review?
The Append Internal Reviewer workflow handles the request from an approver to add one or more reviewers to a document that is in review. The workflow creates a review object for the appended reviewer and adds a task in the user's To
Do list.
Add an Internal Reviewer
1. In your To Do List, select a document review task for Approve the Review step.
2. Select Actions 
 > Add Internal Reviewer.
3. In the Add Recipients page, select the Role and User, and then select Finish.
How to identify step progress as a Approver?
When a Perform Internal Review step is completed, it reflects in the step progress column as a percentage. The percentage of the progress depends on the number of reviewers to the internal review action. For example, If an internal
review action has two reviewers assigned:
Reviewer action
Step progress
Review is started but not completed by any of the reviewers
The review progress is 0.
One of the reviewers completes or waives off the review
The review progress is 50%.
You add a new reviewer
The review progress is 33.33%. (Now the number of reviewers are three and only one of them has completed
the review)
You reassign a reviewer who has already completed the reviews
The review progress is calculated accordingly.
All the reviewers complete the review task
The review progress is completed and is shown as 100%.
Create distribution lists
As a company or a contractor user, you can define a configuration-controlled distribution list with a list of recipients who are to be assigned a workflow step by the system or receive notifications. These lists come in handy while creating
technical queries, actions, or transmittals.
To create an internal distribution list (From CC users):
1. Select the Internal Communication feature set, and select Create Internal Distribution List.
2. In the New Internal Distribution List screen, type a name and description, and select the users based on the organization.
To create an external distribution list (To CC users):
1. Select the Outgoing Communication feature set, and select Create External Distribution List.
2. In the New External Distribution List screen, type a name and description, and select the users based on the organization.
Any user with a Business Administrator role can view all the distribution lists related to a project.
A user who created the distribution list can update or terminate the list. Any user with a Business Administrator or Global Document Control role can update or terminate a distribution list created by other users.
Access issues
The following issues can occur when accessing HxGN SDx2 in a web browser.
HxGN SDx2 continually tries to authenticate
Problem
If your browser is configured to block third-party cookies, HxGN SDx2 tries to authenticate repeatedly.
Solution
Most browsers have a configuration setting that you can select that blocks any third-party cookies. For example, in Microsoft Edge® it is located in Settings > Site permissions > Cookies and site data. This setting should be switched off
to allow the reading and saving of all third-party cookies from a tenant.
You can also add the tenant as an allowed domain, to allow the reading and saving of the cookies from that tenant. In this case, you should add the OAuth authentication server domain address (auth.intergraphsmartcloud.com) to the
list of allowed tenants.
HxGN SDx2 is blocked by your company firewall
Problem
Your company firewall blocks HxGN SDx2 traffic and you cannot connect to HxGN SDx2.
Solution
Your company network administrator needs to add the following as allowed domains in order for cookies to be read and saved:
auth.intergraphsmartcloud.com
<customer_org_name>.sdr.hxgnsmartcloud.com
If you are also going to be using Smart Cloud hosted design tools or other applications then the following will also need to be added as an allowed domain:
<estate-product>.intergraphsmartcloud.com

How do I get started creating my digital twin in HxGN SDx2?
Before you can have a digital twin of your facility, you need to follow the steps to Create your digital twin.
You can find guidance for configuring your data model and extending the user interface at How do I extend my system?.
Create your digital twin
You can create your digital twin in two ways:
If you are creating a digital twin with integrated tools, many plants, or multiple tenants, we recommend that you Use the SDx2 Starter Pack to create your digital twin.
If you are creating a digital twin within an existing HxGN SDx2 environment with integrated tools, you can Create your digital twin interactively.
For some steps of creating your digital twin, either you must have skilled developers who are comfortable with XML and OData web APIs, or you must use the SDx2 Starter Pack to generate your XML files and Hexagon’s Services for any
third-party tool integrations.
How do I install integrated tools?
You can find detailed instructions for how to install your integrated Intergraph Smart® design tools in the Help for each design tool:
Smart 3D Help <https://docs.hexagonali.com/p/S3DV>
Smart Electrical Help <https://docs.hexagonali.com/p/SEL>
Smart Instrumentation Help <https://docs.hexagonali.com/p/SI>
Smart P&ID Help <https://docs.hexagonali.com/p/SPID>
Here are the basic steps for setting up HxGN SDx2 with integrated design tools:
1. Install the integrated design tool.
Smart 3D Installation instructions
Smart Electrical installation instructions
Smart Instrumentation installation instructions
Smart P&ID Installation instructions
2. Use the starter pack to prepare your digital twin.
3. Register your design tools.
Use the starter pack to prepare your digital twin
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx2 | Using the Starter Pack to create your digital twin <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/6797e9b5fb7a476c3cbcba5d>
The SDx2 Starter Pack is a macro-enabled Excel® spreadsheet that you can use to quickly create the basic configuration information for your organization.
It allows you to modify or add information according to your requirements and generate XML load files. You can then load the XML files into your tenant to create the basic configuration.
We strongly recommend that you use the SDx2_Starter Pack.xlsm file in the SDx2 Starter Pack. However, you can perform many of these tasks within HxGN SDx2 itself. For more information about interactively setting up a tenant, a
plant, or a project, see Prepare your digital twin interactively.
Plan your HxGN SDx2 environment
To create the base configuration of your HxGN SDx2 environment, some information specific to your company is helpful.
HxGN SDx2 provides a powerful digital twin that integrates with your design tools, contextual data, and data-centric work processes. Underlying that digital twin is the Hexagon Common Schema, which represents industry-standard types
of documents and tags while keeping them flexible enough that you can match these industry-standard types to the specific uses and versions that your company uses. The data model uses the Hexagon Plant Breakdown Structure (PBS),
which is a flexible logical hierarchy for the material and process components of your large capital project.
Download the starter pack
1. Log on to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> .
2. Select Downloads.
3. Select SDx2.
4. Select Accept to agree to the legal notice.
5. Select Content.
6. In Supporting Software, select SDx2 Starter Pack to download the SDx2.StarterPack.Sample.zip file.
7. Unzip the SDx2.StarterPack.Sample.zip folder using your preferred compression software.
 Make sure that you unzip the downloaded file in a local folder. Otherwise, the macros will not work properly.
8. In the unzipped SDx2.StarterPack.Sample folder, open SDx2_Starter Pack.xlsm.
 New versions of the SDx2 Starter Pack are available on an as-needed basis. Any changes are described in the SDx2SamplesAndUtilities_StarterPack_Readme.txt file delivered with the starter pack.
Tell me more about the SDx2 Starter Pack
The SDx2_Starter Pack.xlsm is a macro-enabled Excel® spreadsheet that contains the following sheets:
Sheet name
Description
Available interactively in HxGN SDx2
For more information
Info
An information sheet with brief
instructions and the Generate
Load Files macro-enabled button.
N/A

For creating the top level entities
for your PBS (Config).
Yes

Areas
For creating the second level
entities for your PBS.
Yes
Areas sheet
Units
For creating the lowest level
entities for your PBS.
Yes
Units sheet
Document Classifications
For creating your document
classifications.
No
Document Classifications sheet
Document Classification
Mapping
For mapping your document
classifications to the standardized
document types available in the
Common Schema.
No
Document Classification Mapping sheet

For creating knowledge and skill
disciplines to further categorize
your users.
Yes

classifications.
No

for them.
