Regular Expression Language Overview <https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/base-types/regular-expression-language-quick-reference> .
AllAreSetB, AnyAreSetB, NoneAreSetB, and AreSet functions
AllAreSetB, AnyAreSetB, NoneAreSetB, and AreSet test whether the properties in the list of arguments have been set for the object being processed.
The AllAreSetB function returns True if every property for the object identified within the set of arguments for the method has been defined. Otherwise, AllAreSetB returns False.
AnyAreSetB returns True if any of the properties within the set of arguments for this method is set. AnyAreSetB returns False only if none of the properties in the list has been defined.
NoneAreSetB returns True only if none of the properties in the list has been set. If any of the properties has been set, NoneAreSetB returns False.
AreSet returns an integer for the number of the properties in the list that have been set.
Count function
Count returns a count (integer) of the Rels returned when expanding a RelDef from the current object. Count can be used in a logical expression as well. For example,
Count(Obj->Realizes_12)
returns the number of interface definitions realized by the class definition of the current object.
For an example of Count in a logical expression,
Count(Obj->OwnsNozzles_21->OwnsEquimentComponents_21) > 1
returns a Boolean value (True or False) depending on whether expanding OwnsNozzles_21->OwnsEquipmentComponents_21 returns a count that is greater than 1. Count supports Hexagon Common syntax.
EndNamedRecord and EndNamedRecordB functions
EndNamedRecord and EndNamedRecordB are used to test for the existence of a named object (object with a non-blank Name) at the end of the specified path from the current object being processed.
EndNamedRecordB returns True if the object at the end of the specified path has a non-blank Name. If the object at the end of the path has a blank Name or if no object is found at the end of the path, EndNamedRecordB returns False.
For example,
EndNamedRecordB(Obj->CommonRunEnd1Port_12)
returns True only if an object is found after traversing the CommonRunEnd1Port_12 edge definition and that object has a non-blank Name.
EndNamedRecord returns the Name of the object along the specified path if that object exists and has non-blank Name. For example,
EndNamedRecord(Obj->CommonRunEnd1Port_12)
returns the Name of the object found after traversing the CommonRunEnd1Port_12 edge definition only if an object exists and has a non-blank Name. Otherwise, EndNamedRecord returns an empty string.
GetCorrelationID
GetCorrelationID returns the CorrelationID of a RelDef. GetCorrelationID has a single argument - the relationship - and supports Hexagon Common syntax. For example,
GetCorrelationID(Obj->OwnsSupports_21)
returns the CorrelationID of the first object related to the current object on the OwnsSupports RelDef.
GetCountOfRelatedDesignComponents function
The GetCountOfRelatedDesignComponents function returns the number of design components related to the given class definition. For example,
GetCountOfRelatedDesignComponents(SDx_Component)
returns the number of design components related to the class, excluding the SDx_Component.
GetEnumNumber function
The GetEnumNumber function returns the enum number for the given enum value. For example,
GetEnumNumber(Obj.TagClass)
returns the number associated with the enumerated list entry of the class of the object.
HasUniqueProperty function
The HasUniqueProperty functions checks the uniqueness of the object under the specified property. It returns True if it is object is unique; otherwise, it returns False:
When HasUniqueProperty returns False and the count of duplicate objects is less than or equal to 50, the OBIDs of all duplicate objects are also returned.
When HasUniqueProperty returns False and the count of duplicate objects is greater than 50, no additional information is returned.
For example,
HasUniqueProperty(Obj.ControlName)
returns True if the property is unique across the current config and tag class.
This functions supports up to two parameters:
the property checked for uniqueness (mandatory).
a relationship definition, used to determine if the property is unique under that expanded relationship definition (optional). For example, a unit name does not have to be unique across all configurations, only across an area, so the
SDAAreaUnits relationship definitions is specified as the second parameter.
IsContextUnique function
The Cumulate topics establish the definition of the SharedGroup container (versus the extensions container). The IsContextUnique method checks whether the specified string within IsContextUnique is unique for this object within the
set of all objects within the SharedGroup container. For example,
IsContextUnique(Obj.FlowDirection & "/" & Obj.NominalDiameter)
checks whether the string formed by combining the flow direction with the nominal diameter is unique within the SharedGroup container.
IsOtherEndObjectExistingB function
IsOtherEndObjectExistingB returns True if the object at the other end of the specified path exists. IsOtherEndObjectExistingB has a single argument - the Rel expansion - and uses the Hexagon Common syntax. For example,
IsOtherEndObjectExistingB(Obj->OwnsInstruments_21)
returns True if an object exists at the other end of the OwnsInstruments relationship from the current object.
IsRelatedTo and IsRelatedToB functions
IsRelatedTo and IsRelatedToB are used to test for the existence of an object along a given path that realizes a given interface.
IsRelatedToB returns True if one or more objects along the given path realize the given interface. For example,
IsRelatedToB(Obj->ScopedBy_12, "IUoMListType")
This function tests whether, for the current object, navigation along the ScopedBy_12 edge locates one or more objects that realize IUoMListType and, if so, returns True. Otherwise, it returns False.
IsRelatedTo returns the Name of the first object along the specified path that realizes the given interface. For example,
IsRelatedTo(Obj->ScopedBy_12, "IUoMListType")
returns the Name of the first object along the ScopedBy_12 edge from the current object that realizes the IUoMlistType interface. If no objects along the ScopedBy_12 edge realize IUoMListType, IsRelatedTo returns an empty string.
IsSetB function
IsSetB is used to test the value is set on a particular property. For example,
IsSetB(Obj.ControlName)
returns true if the current value of the ControlName for the object is set, otherwise, it returns false.
RegexMatchb function
The RegexMatchb function returns a Boolean value (True or False) when a property value matches a regular expression. RegexMatchb calls the Regex.IsMatch method. RegexMatchb has two comma-separated arguments with no
spaces after the commas:
Argument 1 is the property value. This argument accepts Hexagon Common syntax.
Argument 2 is a regular expression that does not support data model query expansions. For more information about regular expressions, see Microsoft's Regular Expression Language Overview <https://learn.microsoft.com/en-
us/dotnet/standard/base-types/regular-expression-language-quick-reference> .
For example,
RegexMatchb(Obj.Name,(\w\w)\-(\w)\-(\d\d)(\d+)(\w?))
returns True if the name of the current object matches the pattern in regular expression syntax, (\w\w)\-(\w)\-(\d\d)(\d+)(\w?), and False if it does not match.
RegExReplace function
The RegExReplace function performs the regular expression operation on the property value for the given condition. RegExReplace calls the Regex.Replace method. RegExReplace has three arguments, comma-separated without
spaces between the commas:
Argument 1 is the property value. This argument accepts Hexagon Common syntax.
Argument 2 identifies the pattern to be replaced. This is a regular expression that does not support data model query expansions. For more information about regular expressions, see Microsoft's Regular Expression Language
Overview <https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/base-types/regular-expression-language-quick-reference> .
Argument 3 is the replacement string. This argument can return either the entire result of the regular expression query pattern, or it can consist of regular expression groups and a new string pattern construction.
For example,
RegExReplace(Obj.TagClass,[a-z],’’)
removes all the lowercase letters in the TagClass of the current object. Argument 1 identifies Object.TagClass as the property value, argument 2 identifies lowercase letters, and argument 3 sets the replacement character to null. In effect,
this example changes Equipment to E or PipeNozzle to PN.
For another example, if you want to remove the unit name from a tag name when building the ControlName, assuming that the pattern of the unit name is A1U1 - and that your tag is named A1U3 -CV - 100 NW778, you can use a
RegExReplace call in your naming rule.
 If the regular expression includes a comma, you need to escape it using a backslash (\). For example, a comma (,) should be written as (\,). In the example below, commas in the regex quantifiers such as {1,3} are written as
{1\,3} to avoid parsing issues.
<NamingRuleProp>
<IObject UID="979A89DC-7D2D-449B-BA28-D4D01589B474" Name="NormalizeInstrumentNameProp" Description="Remove Whitespace and Unit from Instrument Name"/>
<ISchemaObj SchemaRevVer="2.2.3.3"/>
<INamingRuleProp NamingFormat="RegExReplace(Obj.Name,((A?[1-9]?U?[1-9]?)(\s+)?(-?)(\S{1\,3})(\s{0\,3})?(-)(\s{0\,4})?(\d{1\,5})),$5$7$9)" NamedPropertyDef="ControlName"/>
</NamingRuleProp>
This example changes A1U3 -CV - 100 NW778 to a ControlName of CV-100 NW778.
Another example to consider, if you have a string like U1-FT- 1423 and want to remove the space between FT and 1423, you can use the following regular expression function:
RegExReplace(Obj.Name,^([a-zA-Z0-9]+-[a-zA-Z]+)\s-([0-9]+)$,$1-$2)
This example changes U1-FT- 1423 to U1-FT-1423.
Troubleshoot the Integration Engine
Restart a stuck scheduler task
When data is submitted to the Integration Engine, the scheduler task can become stuck. You can troubleshoot this issue.
 You must have Business Administrator or System Administrator role to view the Scheduled Tasks.
First, confirm the scheduler task is stuck
The SDxiHubAutoIntegrationSchedulerTask or the SDxiHubPublishDocLoadSchedulerTask can become stuck.
To check if the SDxiHubAutoIntegrationSchedulerTask is stuck:
1. Set your Integration Data Filters to Consolidated.
2. In the Design Tool Integration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks.
3. Select the SDxiHubAutoIntegrationSchedulerTask, and select Actions 
 > Details.
4. In the Scheduled task details section, find the Last processing ping time. If this value is older than two hours, the scheduled task may be stuck.
5. In the Admin Integration Engine 3D Objects Progress dashboard, find the Shared Group Object Details widget.
6. Expand 
 the Shared Group Object Details widget.
7. Sort the Last Updated Date column so that the most recent time is first. If the most recent time is older than one hour, the scheduled task may be stuck.
 The scheduled task is stuck if both values are older than the expected values.
To check if the SDxiHubPublishDocLoadSchedulerTask is stuck:
1. Set your Integration Data Filters to the appropriate design component for your document.
2. In the Documents feature set, select Active Smart Documents.
3. Find the Last Updated Date column for documents with a Load Status of Processing. If your document has a Last Updated Date older than three hours, the scheduled task may be stuck.
Second, restart the scheduled task using an XML load file
To troubleshoot a stuck SDxiHubAutoIntegrationSchedulerTask, you must import a load file to reset the scheduled task and restart it:
1. Set your Integration Data Filters to Consolidated.
2. In the Design Tool Integration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks.
3. Select SDxiHubAutoIntegrationSchedulerTask, and select Actions 
 > Export Data.
4. Select 
 to save the XML to a file.
5. Using a text editor, such as Notepad, on your local machine, open the XML file that you downloaded.
6. Make the following edits in the XML file:
Change the SPFSchTskStartDate value to a time earlier than the current time (UTC).
Change the SPFSchTskLocked value to False.
Change the SPFSchTskStatus value to e1SchdReady.
7. Save the XML file.
8. Import the load file. For more information about loading an XML file, see Import a load file.
9. In HxGN SDx2, in the Design Tool Integration feature set, select Integration Scheduler.
10. Select Integration Scheduler, and select Actions 
 > Scheduler > Restart.
To troubleshoot a stuck SDxiHubPublishDocLoadSchedulerTask, you must import load files to reset the scheduled task and the document status and restart the scheduled task.
1. Set your Integration Data Filters to Consolidated.
2. In the Design Tool Integration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks.
3. Select SDxiHubPublishDocLoadSchedulerTask, and select Actions 
 > Export Data.
4. Select 
 to save the XML to a file.
5. Using a text editor on your local machine, such as Notepad, open the XML file that you downloaded.
6. Make the following edits in the XML file:
Change the SPFSchTskStartDate value to a time earlier than the current time (UTC).
Change the SPFSchTskLocked value to False.
Change the SPFSchTskStatus value to e1SchdReady.
7. Save the XML file.
8. In HxGN SDx2, import the load file. For more information about loading an XML file, see Import a load file.
9. Set your Integration Data Filters to the appropriate design component for your document.
10. In the Documents feature set, select Active Smart Documents.
11. Select the stuck document, and select Actions 
 > Export Data.
12. Select 
 to save the XML to a file.
13. Using a text editor on your local machine, such as Notepad, open the XML file that you downloaded.
14. Change the value of SDxIHubDocumentLoadStatus to e1SDxiHubReady.
15. Save the XML file.
16. In HxGN SDx2, import the load file. For more information about loading an XML file, see Import a load file.
17. In the Design Tool Integration feature set, select Integration Scheduler.
18. Select Integration Scheduler, and select Actions 
 > Scheduler > Restart.
SPFEnumEnumObjClass relationship does not exist
Problem
When sharing a document, the following message is displayed:
SPFEnumEnumObjClass rel does not exist for Common Schema DocType ‘Your document type’ and its DocumentClass ‘Your document class’.
Cause
The document classification for your document is not mapped.
 The starter pack includes a limited set of default classifications. Additional classifications must be configured to meet your business needs.
Solution
The Common Schema document type needs a document classification, and the document classification must be mapped. To correct this, you must have the System Administration role.
1. In the SDx2 Starter Pack, find your document type in the EnumEnum column of the Document Classification Mapping sheet.
2. From there, work backwards to find whether it is missing the classification, the mapping, or both.
 When using the starter pack, the value in the Document Classification column of the Document Classification Mapping sheet must match a value in one of the Level 1, Level 2, Level 3, or Level 4 columns of the
Document Classifications sheet.
For more information about document classifications and mapping document classifications, see Document Classifications sheet and Document Classification Mapping sheet.
Troubleshoot Identity Register exceptions
Problem
Cause
Solution
Duplicate tags with property ControlName and value of the property EC_P-
28_C1 are found for TagClass = EquipmentComponent.
Objects with the same ControlName value exist under the same
class.
Check that the ControlName value for the object is unique for a tag class.
Parent for this this tag does not exist and its corresponding relation is
OwnsNozzles.
The parent of the object or the corresponding end object of a
relation is not specified.
This exception appears when the RelPathToGroup expansion
results in empty. The relationship to expand comes from
RelPathToGroup, which identifies an object that is higher in the
hierarchy for creating group objects. For example, an incoming
nozzle does not have equipment that owns it.
Push the group head (the parent object in the hierarchy) from the tool.
Parent of this tag did not get correlated, and its relation is OwnsNozzles.
The parent or the corresponding higher level end object of the
specified relation did not get correlated.
This exception appears in several circumstances: The specified
ControlName does not exist, multiple objects have the same
ControlName that is identified in the relation, the object is
missing its mandatory Rel, or the parent of the object was not
correlated.
Resolve the issue with the parent object correlation by checking the Rel identified in
the message.
Property ControlName not found on the object with <<No Name>> (UID: EQ_P-
134).
The object has no name, so no ControlName can be generated
for it.
Check the naming rules for the matching type to find a reason for the missing
ControlName. For example, if a pattern match condition exists on the NamingRule,
the ControlName might be missing.
Rel cardinality for this tag is not satisfied for the relation PBSHierarchy.
The object is missing a Rel that is identified as mandatory.
Add the missing mandatory Rel for the object.
No RuleDef found.
No correlation RuleDef is available for this TagType.
Configure the RuleDef for the appropriate TagType to apply the correlation rules.
Error handling for project-level naming rules
Problem
Loading process may fail if naming rules are defined at the project level with the following error message:
Cannot load the naming rule at the project level
Cause
Naming rules defined at the project level are no longer supported.
Solution
You must reshare any tool objects that were previously configured with project-level naming rules so that the appropriate plant-level naming rules can be applied. For more information, see Add naming rule relationships.
View and address Integration Engine exceptions
Exceptions can occur when the Integration Engine processes shared data. You can view these exceptions and resend the objects for processing. Some exception types are automatically reprocessed by the
SDxiHubAutoIntegrationSchedulerTask until the auto-retry count is reached.
Examples of exception types that can be automatically retried
SocketNotFoundException
ConcurrencyException
TimeOutException
MemoryOutOfBoundsException
QueryException
InternalServiceUnavailable
InternalServiceException
TransactionDeadlockException
DuplicateKeyException
GeneralException
PartialConsolidateException
However, if the exception type is a NonAutoRetriableException, the Integration Engine does not automatically reprocess the SharedGroup object, and you need to manually trigger the process by doing the following steps.
1. From the Design Tool Integration feature set, select Integration Exceptions.
2. Select one or more of the exceptions.
3. Select Actions 
 > Resubmit for Integration.
You must have the System Administrator role to use this feature.
You can also increase the auto-retry count, if necessary. For more information, see Processing tool data using SDxiHubAutoIntegrationSchedulerTask.
Submit failed processes for reload
When the Integration Scheduler is running, the SDxiHubAutoIntegrationSchedulerTask searches every design component and configuration for ChangeSet or SharedGroup objects that were updated more than three hours before. The
ChangeSet and SharedGroup groups are created by the Integration Engine, used to process the data for objects, and then deleted by the Integration Engine once the process is completed successfully. Therefore, any of these objects
found represent a data load that failed to be processed by the Integration Engine.
The SDxiHubAutoIntegrationSchedulerTask resubmits these ChangeSet or SharedGroup objects for reprocessing.
This task runs automatically every three hours as long as the Integration Scheduler is running.
The scheduler task makes up to three tries to process the data. After those three tries, you must use the steps above to manually submit the data for processing.
You must have either the System Administrator or Business Administrator role to start, stop, or restart the Integration Scheduler.
Configure automatic reprocessing of objects by design component or configuration
When sending objects that failed to be processed by the Integration Engine back to be processed again, you can specify what objects you resubmit. By configuring the scheduled task, you can choose which specific objects are resubmitted
by selecting their plants (configurations) and their specific design components.
 You must have the System Administrator role to make this update.
1. From the System Administration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks.
2. Select SDxiHubAutoIntegrationSchedulerTask.
3. Select Actions 
 > Update.
4. In the Design component domains to retry box, enter the design components that shared the objects you want to resubmit.
 You can specify more than one design component by entering a comma-separated list. For example, DC3D,DCPID.
5. From the Configurations to retry box, enter the plants to which the objects that you want to resubmit were shared.
 You can specify more than one configuration by entering the UID as a comma-separated list. For example, PR_Proj1,PR_PlantA.
Only the objects with configurations and design components selected in these boxes can be resubmitted for reprocessing.
You can use the asterisk "*" wildcard character in the applicable box to select all configurations or design components. If you don't select any configurations or design components, nothing is resubmitted.
For more information about reprocessing objects that failed the Integration process, see View and address Integration Engine exceptions.
Enable or disable the automatic reprocessing of objects that failed the Integration process
 You must have the System Administrator role to make this update.
1. From the System Administration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks.
2. Select SDxiHubAutoIntegrationSchedulerTask.
3. Select Actions 
 > Update.
4. Select the Enabled checkbox to enable or disable this scheduler task.
 When this checkbox is disabled, objects that fail while being processed by the Integration Engine are not resubmitted.
 Other options on this dialog box allow you to customize this scheduler task. For example, you can specify the maximum number of times an object will be resubmitted for processing in the Maximum reintegration attempt
count box.
For more information about reprocessing objects that failed the Integration process, see View and address Integration Engine exceptions.
Extend the administration model
Administration is delivered in the same layer structure as schema.
You can:
Write your own XML files.
Copy one from the core and modify it.
Perform configuration interactively, and then export it as described in Export data.
Export data
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx, HxGN SDx2 | Exporting Data <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/663ced07f6dfe3c12d9362de>
For individual objects or sets of closely related objects, the export data option extracts a full definition of the objects to a file in XML format. You can then modify the file as required, and import the extended model.
The data available for exporting is managed by security rules. Access to the different object types is managed using access groups on individual class definitions, where the access group must have the APIRead property set to Grant. This
ensures that you can only export data that you have permission to access.
 Out of the box, the option to export data is made available only to users with the System Administrator or Business Administrator role, and we recommend it is kept restricted to administrators.
1. Search for the objects for which you want to export the data, and select Actions 
 > Export Data.
2. Select the boxes next to the objects that you want to include in the export.
 Selecting XML options 
 allows you to add Containers IDs and Object IDs to the XML output. Switching these on or off will clear any previously selected objects.
 If you select a relationship with a link interface that also has relationships, the link interface relationships are automatically added to allow you to export them.
3. Click 
 to save the XML to a file.
 You can also click 
to copy the XML to your clipboard.
Import data
You can use Extensible Markup Language (XML) load files to import data, relationships, and configuration information into the database.
How do I view the import history?
You view the files that have been imported successfully in the History tab.
Files that were imported in the last 30 days are listed. The files are grouped by date, and the user that carried out the import.
Create a load file
A load file is an XML file that allows you to import data, relationships, and configuration information into HxGN SDx2.
You can either create a load file manually, using an XML editor or a text editor such as Notepad, or you can export data from HxGN SDx2 to an XML file, and then modify the data as required to create your load file. For more information on
exporting data to an XML file, see Export data.
The format of the load file is described in XML format, and examples are given in Load file examples.
 If a load file contains any non-English characters, you must save the file in the UTF-8 character encoding in order to be able load the file. For more information, refer to the documentation for the specific editor you are using.
Import a load file
1. In the Business Administration feature set, select Import Data.
2. In the Import tab, either drag your files to the tab, or browse for the files to add.
 If you select a .xmlldr file, you must also select all of the .xml files referenced in the .xmlldr file in order for them to be imported.
 For any file that shows an error, you must remove the file before you can continue with the import. To do this, select 
 > Remove file. Once you resolve the error, you can add the file again.
Why can't I import my file?
When you attach your files to import, they are checked to ensure that the following rules are met. If your files do not meet these rules, you cannot continue with the import.
The format of the .xml or .xmlldr file must be valid.
The file size must be below the maximum allowed (1 MB).
The number of update, delete, or insert instructions in the container must be below the maximum allowed (50).
The file must contain supported instructions only. For a list of the instructions supported in HxGN SDx2, see XML instructions.
In addition, the files are also checked to ensure that the following rules are met. If your files do not meet these rules, you can still continue with the import if required.
You do not have the same file selected twice. The system checks the file names and sizes. It highlights any that appear to be the same.
The number of files to be uploaded is below the maximum allowed (10). If you select more than the maximum number, you can continue with the import, but only the first 10 files are added to the queue for importing.
3. To change the order in which the files are processed, select 
, and drag the files into the required order.
4. Select Import.
 If a file fails to load, you can view the error messages returned as described in View load errors. By default, after a failure, the system continues to import the next file.
Any load file that contains non-English characters must be saved in the UTF-8 character encoding before it can be processed.
The History tab shows the recently imported files.
How do I view the import history?
You view the files that have been imported successfully in the History tab.
Files that were imported in the last 30 days are listed. The files are grouped by date, and the user that carried out the import.
View load errors
If a file fails to import, view the reasons for the failure by clicking on the file containing the errors.
The File error window shows the errors on the left and the contents of the file on the right. You can expand each error to show additional information.
XML format
XML files are used to create and update object and relationship data and to perform certain instructions.
The source of the model is defined in XML format files. These files contain a header identifying the scope, component schema, and container ID of its contents. It uses standard XML format for defining objects, properties, and relationships.
For example:
<Container Scope="Data" ContainerID="Plant.Admin" DesignComponent="PBSManager_Component">
<FunctionalUnit>
<IObject UID="UNT_PlantA_U101" Name="U101" Description="Unit 101" />
<IFunctionalUnit />
<IPBSItemCollection />
<IPBSItem />
<IPlannedFacility />
</FunctionalUnit>
<Rel>
<IObject UID="UNT_PlantA_U101-DDCM_PlantA_C056-KV-P-XC-2002-01" />
<IRel DefUID="FunctionalUnitDeliverable" UID2="DDCM_PlantA_C056-KV-P-XC-2002-01-42" OrderValue="0" UID1="UNT_PlantA_U101" IsRequired="False" />
</Rel>
</Container>
XML instructions
In addition to being able to create and update object and relationship data, the XML can be constructed to perform the following supported instructions:
DeleteInstruction
InsertInstruction
UpdateInstruction
SPFAttachWorkflowInstruction
SPFReclassifyInstruction
SPFReviseInstruction
SPFSignOffInstruction
An example of each instruction follows.
 The number of update, delete, or insert instructions in the container must be below the maximum allowed (50).
Delete instruction
<DeleteInstruction>
<IObject UID="{f3edefd3-3a00-4360-a434-b19c4a825d5a}" Name="Delete_SPFAuthSignature" />
<IRefObject RefClass="PropertyDef" RefUID="SPFAuthSignature" RefName="SPFAuthSignature" />
<IDeleteInstruction DeleteTransition="Deleted" />
<IInstruction />
</DeleteInstruction>
 A delete instruction can also be used for terminate by setting the DeleteTransition parameter to Terminated.
<DeleteInstruction>
<IObject UID="{f3edefd3-3a00-4360-a434-b19c4a825d5a}" Name="Delete_SPFAuthSignature" />
<IRefObject RefClass="PropertyDef" RefUID="SPFAuthSignature" RefName="SPFAuthSignature" />
<IDeleteInstruction DeleteTransition="Terminated" />
<IInstruction />
</DeleteInstruction>
Insert instruction
<InsertInstruction>
<IObject UID="{349c5957-e5a5-42b1-959f-8313155ca0dc}" Name="Insert_ISPFFTRItem" />
<IRefObject RefClass="InterfaceDef" RefUID="ISPFFTRItem" RefName="ISPFFTRItem" />
<IPropValues PropValues="IObject\%UID\%ISPFFTRItem\%IObject\%Name\%ISPFFTRItem\%IObject\%Description\%Interface to support FTR\%ISchemaObj\%DisplayName\%ISPFFTRItem" 
AddedInterfaces="IObject~IInterfaceDef~IInterfaceRole~IPropCategory~IEnumMetadata~ISchemaObj~IInterfaceDefExt" />
<IInsertInstruction />
<IInstruction />
</InsertInstruction>
Update instruction
<UpdateInstruction>
<IObject UID="Update_UID" Name="Update_UID" />
<IRefObject RefClass="PropertyDef" RefUID="UID" RefName="UID" />
<IPropValues PropValues="IPropertyDefExt\%HistoryNotRetained\%True"
AddedInterfaces="IPropertyDefExt" />
<IInstruction />
<IUpdateInstruction />
</UpdateInstruction>
 Update instructions must follow the schema evolution rules. For more information about the schema evolution rules, see Data model configuration rules.
Revise instruction
<SPFReviseInstruction>
<IObject UID="Revise_001" Name="Revise_001" />
<IRefObject RefClass="SPFDesignDocRevision" RefUID="{68Y69856458GGKH}" RefName="DocABC" />
<ISPFReviseInstruction SPFReviseInstRevScheme="01A" SPFReviseInstMajorRevision="01" SPFReviseInstMinorRevision="B" SPFReviseInstFilesToCopy="False"/>
<IInstruction />
</SPFReviseInstruction>
 To copy all existing files from the existing document to the new revision, provide "True" for the SPFReviseInstFilesToCopy property. "False" will create the new revision without copying any files attached to the existing revision.
SignOff instruction
<SPFSignOffInstruction>
<IObject UID="SignOff_001" Name="SignOff_001" />
<IRefObject RefClass="SPFDesignDocRevision"
RefUID="{68Y69856458GGKH}" RefName="DocABC" />
<ISPFSignOffInstruction
SignOffInstructionComment="All is OK"
SignOffInstructionAllowWorkingRefs="True" />
<IInstruction />
</SPFSignOffInstruction>
Reclassify instruction
<SPFReclassifyInstruction>
<IObject UID="Reclassify_FLD-001" Name="Reclassify_FLD-001" />
<IRefObject RefClass="SPFFolder"
RefUID="FLD-001"
RefName="ISPFAdhocReport.MTH_CopyIfOwner" />
<ISPFReclassifyInstruction SPFNewClassificationUID="MyFolder" />
<IInstruction />
</SPFReclassifyInstruction>
Attach workflow instruction
<SPFAttachWorkflowInstruction>
<IObject UID="AWF_Tag001" Name="AWF_Tag001" />
<IRefObject RefClass="DEVTag" RefUID="{4687889FDT98760L}" RefName="Tag001" />
<ISPFAttachWorkflowInstruction SPFWorkflowTemplateUID="TagWorkflow001" />
<IInstruction />
</SPFAttachWorkflowInstruction>
Transaction handling
To allow for data to be loaded in discrete transactions, we can specify the data in containers and push all the contents to the server to be processed in one transaction. If any part of the container fails to load, the whole container is rolled
back.
<Containers>
<Container Scope="Data" ContainerID="PBS.Data" DesignComponent="PBSManager_Component">
<FunctionalUnit>
<IObject Name="U101" />
<IFunctionalUnit />
</FunctionalUnit>
<FunctionalUnit>
<IObject Name="U102" />
<IFunctionalUnit />
</FunctionalUnit>
</Container>
<Container Scope="Data" ContainerID="PBS.Data" DesignComponent="PBSManager_Component">
<FunctionalUnit>
<IObject Name="U103" />
<IFunctionalUnit />
</FunctionalUnit>
</Container>
</Containers>
Date time handling
Two types of date/time formats are supported, Server and Local.
Server format is: yyyy/mm/dd-hh:mm:ss:mmm. The Server format supports this format whether the time is specified or not. If a time is specified, then the time is adjusted using regional time settings. If no time is specified, then the
string 00:00:00 (indicating midnight local time) is appended. This time string is then converted to GMT time using regional settings.
Local format is different because the date is entered based on the local date format specified in the Control Panel Settings. For example, May 31, 2012 could be entered as 05/31/2012 or 31/05/2012 depending on your location. The
local time format is: hh:mm:ss and does not support milliseconds. If a date and time are specified, the "-" separator is used as shown in the Server format. If no time is specified, then the local date is converted to the server format
and the time string 00:00:00 (indicating midnight local time) is appended. This time string is then converted to GMT time using regional settings. All conversions are done before sending the date and time to the server.
CreateConfig setting
It is possible to override the Create configuration that is set in the client when loading load files. If this is not set, then the data is loaded using the client's current Create configuration. Configuration independent classes ignore this setting.
<Container CompSchema='' IObject_config="EFPLANT-SC1">
</Container>
Design component setting
When loading PBS data, you must ensure that the data is imported into the PBSManager design component. This is done using the DesignComponent tag.
<Container DesignComponent="PBSManager_Component">
 The DesignComponent tag cannot be used to specify any other design component. It is only used for importing data into the PBSManager design component.
Mode for load files
You can import files in three modes: Create, Update, and Merge. The mode can be set once per load file and should be the first active statement in the load file.
Create mode creates new objects. If any data collisions are detected and an error occurs, you are informed that the object is not loaded.
Update mode updates existing objects. If any objects to be updated are not found and an error occurs, you are informed that the object is not loaded.
Merge mode updates an existing object or creates the object if it does not already exist.
The following are example syntaxes:
Type='Create' specifies that only new objects are created. If they exist, an error is raised and the transaction is rolled
back.
<Container CompSchema='SPFComponent' config='PlantA' Type = 'Create'>
Type='Update' specifies that this container can only update existing objects. If any objects to be updated are not
found, an error is raised and the transaction is rolled back.
<Container CompSchema='SPFComponent' config='PlantA' Type = 'Update'>
If you do not specify a type, the 'Merge' mode is assumed. If an object exists, update it. If the object does not exist,
create it.
<Container CompSchema='SPFComponent' config='PlantA' >
ContainerID
The containerID tag allows all objects that are created to be stamped with the value defined in argument 1.
<Container CompSchema="SPFComponent" IObject_Config="PlantA" Type = 'Create' ContainerID="MyTestData">
Load file examples
This section contains several example load files for creating data and performing instructions.
These load files are intended as samples only.
The configuration information in these samples is based on the default model delivered. If you intend to copy and paste these examples to load data into your installation, you must make sure that the configuration information in your
load files matches your actual configuration information.
Data creation
The following is a sample XML load file used to create data:
<Container Scope="Data" ContainerID="Plant.Admin" DesignComponent="PBSManager_Component">
<FunctionalUnit>
<IObject UID="UNT_PlantA_U101" Name="U101" Description="Unit 101" />
<IFunctionalUnit />
<IPBSItemCollection />
<IPBSItem />
<IPlannedFacility />
</FunctionalUnit>
<Rel>
<IObject UID="UNT_PlantA_U101-DDCM_PlantA_C056-KV-P-XC-2002-01" />
<IRel DefUID="FunctionalUnitDeliverable" UID2="DDCM_PlantA_C056-KV-P-XC-2002-01-42"
             
             OrderValue="0" UID1="UNT_PlantA_U101" IsRequired="False" />
</Rel>
</Container>
Perform instructions
Delete instruction
<DeleteInstruction>
<IObject UID="{f3edefd3-3a00-4360-a434-b19c4a825d5a}" Name="Delete_SPFAuthSignature" />
<IRefObject RefClass="PropertyDef" RefUID="SPFAuthSignature" RefName="SPFAuthSignature" />
<IDeleteInstruction DeleteTransition="Deleted" />
<IInstruction />
</DeleteInstruction>
 A delete instruction can also be used to terminate by substituting the DeleteTransition parameter with the value Terminated.
<DeleteInstruction>
<IObject UID="{f3edefd3-3a00-4360-a434-b19c4a825d5a}" Name="Delete_SPFAuthSignature" />
<IRefObject RefClass="PropertyDef" RefUID="SPFAuthSignature" RefName="SPFAuthSignature" />
<IDeleteInstruction DeleteTransition="Terminated" />
<IInstruction />
</DeleteInstruction>
Insert instruction
<InsertInstruction>
<IObject UID="{349c5957-e5a5-42b1-959f-8313155ca0dc}" Name="Insert_ISPFFTRItem" />
<IRefObject RefClass="InterfaceDef" RefUID="ISPFFTRItem" RefName="ISPFFTRItem" />
<IPropValues PropValues="IObject\%UID\%ISPFFTRItem\%IObject\%Name\%ISPFFTRItem\%IObject\%Description\%Interface
             
             to support FTR\%ISchemaObj\%DisplayName\%ISPFFTRItem" AddedInterfaces=
             
             "IObject~IInterfaceDef~IInterfaceRole~IPropCategory~IEnumMetadata~ISchemaObj~IInterfaceDefExt" />
<IInsertInstruction />
<IInstruction />
</InsertInstruction>
Update instruction
<UpdateInstruction>
<IObject UID="Update_UID" Name="Update_UID" />
<IRefObject RefClass="PropertyDef" RefUID="UID" RefName="UID" />
<IPropValues PropValues="IPropertyDefExt\%HistoryNotRetained\%True"
AddedInterfaces="IPropertyDefExt" />
<IInstruction />
<IUpdateInstruction />
</UpdateInstruction>
 Update instructions must follow the schema evolution rules. For more information about the schema evolution rules, see Data model configuration rules.
Revise instruction
<SPFReviseInstruction>
<IObject UID="Revise_001" Name="Revise_001" />
<IRefObject RefClass="SPFDesignDocRevision"
RefUID="{68Y69856458GGKH}" RefName="DocABC" />
<ISPFReviseInstruction SPFReviseInstRevScheme="01A"
SPFReviseInstMajorRevision="01"
SPFReviseInstMinorRevision="B" etc ..../>
<IInstruction />
</SPFReviseInstruction>
SignOff instruction
<SPFSignOffInstruction>
<IObject UID="SignOff_001" Name="SignOff_001" />
<IRefObject RefClass="SPFDesignDocRevision"
RefUID="{68Y69856458GGKH}" RefName="DocABC" />
<ISPFSignOffInstruction
SignOffInstructionComment="All is OK"
SignOffInstructionAllowWorkingRefs="True" />
<IInstruction />
</SPFSignOffInstruction>
Reclassify instruction
<SPFReclassifyInstruction>
<IObject UID="Reclassify_FLD-001" Name="Reclassify_FLD-001" />
<IRefObject RefClass="SPFFolder"
RefUID="FLD-001"
RefName="ISPFAdhocReport.MTH_CopyIfOwner" />
<ISPFReclassifyInstruction SPFNewClassificationUID="MyFolder" />
<IInstruction />
</SPFReclassifyInstruction>
Attach workflow instruction
<SPFAttachWorkflowInstruction>
<IObject UID="AWF_Tag001" Name="AWF_Tag001" />
<IRefObject RefClass="DEVTag" RefUID="{4687889FDT98760L}" RefName="Tag001" />
<ISPFAttachWorkflowInstruction SPFWorkflowTemplateUID="TagWorkflow001" />
<IInstruction />
</SPFAttachWorkflowInstruction>
Relationship examples
Creating a relationship to an existing relationship
The following is an example of using a fully qualified XML-based load file with this operation.
To relate the access group to the RelDef, do the following:
  <Rel>
    <IObject UID="SPFDocRevisionLabel.DocumentUpdate" Name="" Description="" ContainerID="Core.DocMgt" />
    <IRel UID2="MAG_DocumentUpdate" DefUID="SPFRelDefAccessGroup" UID1="SPFDocRevisionLabel" OrderValue="0" IsRequired="False" />
    <ISPFRelDefAccessGroup SPFRelTerminate="True" SPFRelDragDrop2on1="True" />
  </Rel>
To create the relationship between the RelDef-access group relationship and the condition, do the following:
  <Rel>
    <IObject UID="SPFDocRevisionLabel.DocumentUpdate.CND_IsObj1WORKING" Name="" Description="" ContainerID="" />
    <IRel UID2="CND_IsObj1WORKING" DefUID="SPFRelDefAccessGroupDDCondition" UID1="SPFDocRevisionLabel.DocumentUpdate" OrderValue="0" IsRequired="False" />
  </Rel>
Terminating a relationship to relationship
The following is an example of using a fully qualified XML-based load file with this operation.
  <DeleteInstruction>
    <IObject UID="Delete_SPFLLALineListRecordDocumentVersion.ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentVersion" Name="Delete_SPFLLALineListRecordDocumentVersion.ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentVersion" />
    <IRefObject RefClass="" RefUID="SPFLLALineListRecordDocumentVersion.ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentVersion" RefName="" />
    <IInstruction />
    <IDeleteInstruction DeleteTransition="Terminated" />
  </DeleteInstruction>
Regenerating the Common Schema
When you’ve made changes to the Common Schema and applied them to the database, the system compares the database structure to the information in the version of the Common Schema document stored in HxGN SDx2.
Regenerating this document and storing the updated version in HxGN SDx2 ensures that an updated version of the Common Schema is used when authoring tools share or synchronize.
1. In the Design Tool Integration feature set, select Common Schema Document.
2. Select Actions 
 > Regenerate Common Schema.
3. Select Yes to regenerate the schema.
 If any changes were made to the schema since the last time the Common Schema document was regenerated, it is regenerated again to include those changes. If the system does not find any changes to apply, you receive a
message that the Common Schema is already up to date.
Identification properties and their use
The following properties can be used to identify any modifications when you create or modify any objects and relationships in the system.
Property
Database Build
Update Release
Description
ContainerID
Not used by product
Not used by product
Free for customer use and can be set on an object.
SDxExternalVersion
Not instantiated
Not instantiated
Free for customer use and can be set on an object.
SDxByCustomer
Not instantiated
Not instantiated
Protected field
No update access
Displays the property as Modified when an object
delivered by a product release or update is changed
by a customer. When a new object is created by a
customer, the property is set to Added.
SDxInternalVersion
Set on all configurations
Set to first delivered version only
Set on out-of-the-box delivered new, updated, terminated objects and
relationships
Protected field
No update access
Displays the introduced version applicable to OOTB
Schema and Admin objects only.
SDxSource
Set to the same layers as used in the xmlsrc to manage upgrades
Set to same layers as used in the xmlsrc to manage upgrades
Protected field
No update access
Displays the source from which the object originated.
Set when the database is built, or upgrade is
delivered.
Where a customer makes a modification to an out-of-the-box object and subsequently a product release updates the same object, the SDxByCustomer property is set to Modified.
When a relationship is changed, it changes the structure of the object, so the parent object is also marked as changed. For example, when adding or changing the display of a column item, the column set is considered to be the
parent and also marked as changed.
Customer modifications and relationships
As a customer makes changes to an object in a project, the changes can be identified by the SDxByCustomer property being set to Modified on the object and its relationships. You can search for and filter on the object column set to find
any modified properties using the SDxByCustomer property.
However, in some cases, the change can also affect the parent object and be identified the same way. For example, if a new column item is added to an existing column set, the column set is also considered to have been changed, so both
the column item and the column set will have the SDxByCustomer property set to Modified.
Relationships are identified by the SDxInternalVersion property displaying the product configuration version applicable when the SDxByCustomer was set to Modified. The target object on the owning end of the relationship will also
have the SDxByCustomer property set to Modified.
Optional interfaces on created objects
As a customer creates a new object in a project, any optional interfaces are instantiated on the new object when the object classification type uses the SPFClassificationOptionalInterface relationship.
If the selected classification does not have the SPFClassificationOptionalInterface relationship, the system will use the SPFObjClassEnumEnum relationship to instantiate optional interfaces.
If the classification does not have the SPFObjClassEnumEnum relationship, the system looks for enums related to the object classification at the same level to instantiate optional interfaces.
If there are no enums found at the object classification level, the software navigates up the hierarchy until the SPFObjClassEnumEnum relationship is found. When the SPFObjClassEnumEnum relationship is expanded, the
EnumMetadata relationship expands the enum hierarchy at all levels up to the top. Any interfaces that are found by this expansion will be instantiated on the classified object.
When you change the classification for an object, any optional interfaces related to the new classification type will be instantiated on the existing object. Any relationship to the previous optional interfaces will be terminated.
Configure the user interface
The user interface can be navigated using a group of feature set tiles on the feature set home page. Each feature set contains a set of related options for creating and searching for items associated with a work process. These feature sets
are enhanced from release to release so there is limited scope for change.
 An alternate way of navigating the user interface is to use a home screen dashboard. These provide users with easy access to their tasks, and a role-based view of their assets and critical information. For more information, see
Configure an InsightView dashboard.
The following changes are permitted within the rules listed below:
Feature sets and their content cannot be modified, only removed by removing user access. For example, you can remove a search option.
New feature sets can be created and populated with new commands. For example, you can add feature sets for new data structure creation or for integrations with third-party applications.
Forms can be extended, for example, to expose additional display items and properties.
Core form sections and display items cannot be modified.
List view and object column items can be managed, for example, to modify or change the column items displayed in a user-created column set.
What are the rules for changing the user interface?
The following rules must be followed when changing the user interface.
Feature Sets
Delivered feature sets, including their existing methods and API entry points, must not be changed. You must also not change the display text, content order, groupings, or icon.
Feature sets can be reordered on the home page, and you can add custom API entry points.
Custom feature sets can be created and populated with methods and API entry points, as well as integrated with third-party applications using Web extensibility.
Methods and API entry points must not be disconnected from feature sets. These can only be removed from the feature set by removing user access to the command.
Delivered and custom methods can be added to a custom feature set.
The method conditions cannot be modified as the condition may be used in many places. Instead, it should be copied and replaced as needed.
You can modify the methods associated with the SPFStepClassMethod, SPFClassDefDefaultMethod, and SPFInterfaceDefMethod relationship definition.
You cannot modify the methods associated with the SPFClientAPIMethods relationship definition.
Custom commands and API entry points can be added to a feature set.
Delivered reports can be added to a feature set.
You can terminate relationships between feature sets and reports.
API Entry Points
API entry points filtering must not be changed.
Custom API entry points can be created, updated, and have indexing enabled.
Existing API entry points cannot be updated, deleted, or terminated, but can have indexing enabled.
All document or tag API entry points have indexing enabled by default, which cannot be cancelled.
Existing API entry points can be added to groupings and custom feature sets.
Column sets can be added to entry points by terminating the existing relationship and creating a new one.
API Entry Point Groups
Delivered entry point groups cannot be deleted.
Delivered relationships cannot be deleted.
Relationships can be created to a new SchemaItem entry point group.
Forms
Sections can be added to, or removed from any form tab.
Sections can be reordered in a form.
Custom access groups can be added to the delivered sections.
Custom forms can be created. They can be used to replace delivered forms by terminating the existing relationship between the delivered form and the class definition, and creating a new relationship for the new form.
Existing display items can be added to new sections.
Default values on relationship display items can be altered on specific display items. For example, to set the default security code.
Existing form purpose relationships cannot be modified.
Existing form purposes cannot be modified for the existing sections.
Column Sets
Existing column sets must not be altered, including the content and order of the columns, because it could change the behavior of the system. Column sets must be copied and modified in order to be managed.
Many column sets contain items that are not exposed in the list views but instead dynamically build display items on tabs on the forms, so care is required.
Dynamic display items from column items are an efficient way to extend forms.
Do not delete or terminate any existing column set or column items.
Do not terminate the following relationship definitions related to the column sets: SPFColumnSetColumnItemConfig, SPFColumnSetObjClass, SPFColumnSetColumnItem.
You may relate column sets to the entry points,
You may add or remove the column sets from the following relationship definitions: SPFPinnedUser, SPFDisplayItemFilterColumnSet, SPFRelDefEnd1ColumnSet and SPFRelDefEnd2ColumnSet.
Delivered column items can be related to new column sets.
Display Items
You may copy the display items related to the following relationship definitions: SPFDisplayItemDependentItem, SPFDisplayItemSecondaryDependentItem and SPFDisplayItemFilterColumnset.
Configure the interface
You can configure various items to control how objects are displayed.
Configure feature sets
You can quickly create and configure new feature sets and add their method functions for display in HxGN SDx2.
Create a feature set
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Create Feature Set to open the Create Feature Set form.
2. In the Main details section, enter the name and description.
3. In the GUI display details section, select the Display as box to enter the name of the feature set to appear in the user interface.
4. In the Feature Set details section, select the Feature set image URL box to enter the name of the feature set image to be used.
 You can set the order number in which the feature set displays in the home page in the Sort Order on Home Page box. The default order value is NULL (zero), so that the feature set appears first in any sort order. Feature
sets that share the same number are sorted alphabetically.
Modify a feature set
You can easily modify the description, image, or sort order of an existing feature set.
1. Select the feature set you want to update, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. In the Main details section, select the Description box to change the description for the feature set.
3. In the GUI display details section, select the Display as box to change the display name.
 You cannot change the display name for an existing feature set.
4. In the Feature Set details section, select the Feature set image URL box to enter or update the feature set image name.
5. Select the Sort Order on Home Page box to enter or update the order number in which the feature set displays.
 The default value is NULL (zero) so that the feature set appears first in any sort order. Feature sets that share the same number are sorted alphabetically.
Relate a method to a feature set
Use the Relationship Builder to create relationships between methods and feature sets, so that the methods display as a function tiles in a feature set.
1. Choose a method, and select Actions 
 > Relationship Builder.
 You can select a new feature set, a pinned feature set, or search for existing feature sets.
2. Select Relate.
 To modify how existing functions created from methods are displayed, see Modify how functions display.
 You can configure and maintain features sets and their related objects using the Manage Related Items functionality. For more information, see Manage feature sets.
Manage feature sets
You can manage how feature sets and their function tiles are displayed.
 Function tiles display functionality that is created from an applicable method. For more information on configuring methods, see Configure methods.
Group function tiles in a feature set
You can group method function tiles so they are combined together in a sub-group in a feature set.
1. Search and select a feature set, and select Actions 
 > Manage Methods.
2. Enter the same group name in the Cascade column item for each required method.
For example, the feature set Workflow Management shows all the workflow functions together. By adding the FSGroup 10 group name in the Cascade column for each workflow create method, you can display them in a separate
function group.
The next time you expand the feature set, the three selected create workflow function tiles will display in a separate sub-group.
Control the display of functions
You can control the appearance of function tiles in a feature set so they do not display.
1. Search for a method, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. Select the Suppress in Web Client option.
 By default, the suppress option is not selected so existing methods are displayed and unaffected by an upgrade.
Modify how functions display
You can modify the display of functions created from methods using the Manage Related Items functionality.
Sort the display order of functions in a feature set
1. Go to the feature set in a results grid, and select Actions 
 > Manage Methods.
2. In the Manage Related Items dialog box, enter a value in the Order Value column for each method that you want to specify the order in which the function tile is displayed in the feature set.
 The order in which sub-groups of functions are displayed is controlled by the lowest Order Value. In this way you can control the order in which each method function tile appears in the feature set. For example, if a function
tile is set to an order value of 1, it is displayed before a function tile with an order value of 2.
Group the functions in a feature set
1. Go to the feature set in a results grid, and select Actions 
 > Manage Methods.
2. In the Manage Related Items dialog box, enter a name in the Cascade column for each method you want to group together. In this way, function tiles with the same name are grouped together as a sub-group when displayed in the
feature set.
For more information on managing related items, see Manage related items.
The Cascade column value does not display in HxGN SDx2.
Method functions and API Entry Point functions with the same sub-group name display in separate sub-groups in the same feature set.
You can suppress the display of a method. For more information, see Suppress the display of methods.
Manage method access groups
You can search for a method and use the Update Methods wizard to edit and modify the access groups in the Access Control tab.
Go to a method in the results grid, and select Actions 
 > Update Method.
For more information on configuring the properties for methods, see Configure methods.
Alternatively, you can manage the access to methods that appear as a function tiles using the Manage Access Groups.
1. Go to the method in a results grid, and select Actions 
 > Manage Access Groups.
2. In the Manage Access Groups dialog, select the access group in the left Library pane or right Related Items pane that you want to either add or remove access to the method.
 For more information on managing related items, see Manage related items.
Configure the form used on create methods
When you configure methods to create objects, you can configure separate methods for the same class of object that can each use different forms. This can be useful in certain situations when you want optional interfaces to be
instantiated along with their display items in different create forms for each method configured from the same object class.
You can specify the Create form UID when you a configure a create method from a client API. This ability is supported only for the following client APIs:
CreateObj
CreateClassObj
CreateClassObjAndRel
CreateObjAndRel
CreateClassObjAndLoad
Configure the default method action for object types
You can configure the default method action used for an object type by relating the object type class definition to the selected method. For example, you can change what the object name URL action displays in the Results grid, see View
files default action.
 If a method is not configured as the default for the object type, or the user does not have access to the method, the object details form is displayed by default.
Suppress the display of methods
You can control the appearance of methods, such as actions menu items, by suppressing the method from display. In this way, any method exposed as an actions menu item or function tile in a feature set can be hidden from the interface,
but can still be found using a search.
1. Go to the method you want to suppress, and select Actions 
 > Update Method.
2. Select the Suppress in Web Client option.
By default, the suppress option is not set so existing methods are displayed and unaffected by an upgrade.
If a method is configured to appear as a button on a screen other than the feature set screen, when selected, it is disabled. An information message informs you that the method is not available because it has been suppressed.
View files default action
You can configure the object type default action to view any attached files and open them with the correct view method in the Results grid in the order of precedence or as a node in the Info Map.
You can do this by relating the ViewFileDefault method to the object type class definition and ensuring that the method is configured with the appropriate access permissions so that the files can be viewed.
The ViewFileDefault method uses the ViewFileWrapper client API to select the correct view method for the attached files in the following order of precedence:
View Native
View Alternate
View and Markup Hexagon File
View and Markup Non-Hexagon file
View Laser Scans
 For example, you can view files attached to a document object from the object name URL in the Results grid by relating the ViewFileDefault method to the FDWDocumentVersion class definition.
Configure API Entry Points
HxGN SDx2 uses API Entry Points to expose system objects so they appear in the user interface when called, such as class definitions or methods.
As a system administrator, you expose the system objects using an API Entry Point by configuring the details displayed on the create or update forms. Any exposed system object automatically exposes any relationship and edge
definitions and requires no further configuration.
 You can control the visibility of objects by relating them to a specific design component. Select the Integration Data Filter on the API Entry Point form to create a relationship with a specific design component. For more information,
see Relate design components to forms.
You have access to the System Administration feature set, which details the following system objects that you can use to create API Entry Points and add them to a feature set:
Class definitions
Interface definitions
Methods
Quick Find methods
API Entry Points
 When you create or update a custom entry point, you can select the Enable Indexing option to index the object property terms into an indexed datastore to help improve search performance over systems with large data sets. For
more information, see Configure an entry point for indexing.
Existing API Entry Points delivered with a system cannot be updated, except for selecting the Enable Indexing option.
You can modify how an API Entry Point is displayed as you configure the details of an API Entry Point. For more information, see Configure API Entry Points to display as functions in a feature set.
You can select an Integration Data Filter in each API Entry Point form to create a relationship with an integrated design component. This ensures the API Entry Point objects are only visible to the end user based on the selected
design component. For more information, see Relate design components to forms.
Create an API Entry Point from a class or interface definition
1. Select a class or interface definition in a results list or select an existing class or interface definition node in an Info Map display.
2. Select Actions 
 > Create API Entry Point.
3. On the Create API Entry Point form, enter the name and description in Main details.
4. On API Entry Point details, enter the options required. See a list of the options in Update an API Entry Point.
5. Relate the new API Entry Point to a feature set using the Relationship Builder so that it appears as a function tile on the feature set. For more information, see Configure API Entry Point relationships.
 When creating an API Entry Point from a class definition or interface definition, the system relates the existing object type column set to the API Entry Point for the display of the properties. For more information, see Relating a
column set to an API Entry Point.
Create an API Entry Point from a Quick Find method
1. Select a Quick Find method, and click Actions 
 > Create API Entry Point.
2. On the Create API Entry Point form, enter the name and description in Main details.
3. Relate the new API Entry Point to the selected feature set using the Relationship Builder so that it appears as a function tile on the feature set. See Configure API Entry Point relationships.
 The form is pre-populated with the configuration details from the selected method.
Update an API Entry Point
 Only custom API Entry Points added to the system can be updated. API Entry Points delivered with the system cannot be updated, but can have indexing enabled.
1. Select an existing Entry Point, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. In the Indexing Configuration section, select the Enable Indexing option if you want to index the Entry Point objects for faster searches.
 All document and tag entry points have the indexing option selected by default, which cannot be cancelled.
3. In the Related items section
Under ENTRY POINT GROUPS, search for the entry point groups to relate to the Entry Point.
Under INTEGRATION DATA FILTERS, search for the design components to relate to the Entry Point.
Under REQUIRED ACCESS GROUPS, search for the Access Groups to relate to the Entry Point.
You must terminate related items to completely remove their relationship to a custom Entry Point.
For more information on integration data filters, see Filter data by design component.
For more information about indexing, see Configure indexing for fast searches.
 If you remove all relationships between an Entry Point and any access groups, the Entry Point will then be available to all users.
Create an API Entry Point group
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Create API Entry Point Group.
2. In Main Details, enter a name for the group.
3. Search for the API entry points that you want to add to the group.
4. Search for any API server procedures to add to the group.
5. Select FINISH to create the API Entry Point Group.
Configure API Entry Point relationships
You can configure relationship definitions for an existing API Entry Point.
1. Search for an existing API Entry Point, and select Actions 
 > Relationship Builder.
2. You can relate objects to the entry point saved in the Pins tab, search for object data types to relate in the Search tab, and see any existing relationships in the Existing tab.
To modify how existing functions created from API Entry Points are displayed, see Modify the display of API Entry Point functions in a feature set.
If you remove the relationships between the API Entry Point and any access groups, the API Entry Point will become available to all users.
Relating a column set to an API Entry Point
When creating an API Entry Point from a class definition or interface definition, the system relates the existing object type column set to the API Entry Point to display the properties. However, if a column set does not exist for an API Entry
Point object type, the system automatically selects the most appropriate column set based on the object type, such as the class definition or class definition's primary interface definition.
 By default, the system will always attempt to expose the document version and its related column set for any document API Entry Point to display the document types, even if you configure an API Entry Point to expose the revision
or master levels.
Edit property comparison criteria
You can use the Property Comparison box to configure additional criteria in an API Entry Point to expose a specific object type using property comparison syntax.
1. Find the API Entry Point of a search menu item, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. Edit the Property Comparison box by adding additional property comparison syntax criteria.
For example, you can create a query to return document revisions that match various states that are not undergoing change using the following syntax.
ISPFDocumentRevision~SPFRevState~=~e1WORKING|e1CURRENT|e1CANCELLED|e1RESERVED ISPFDocumentRevision~SPFRevUnderChangeInSameConfig~=~False
 For more information and examples of property comparison criteria, see Property comparisons.
Configure API Entry Points to display as functions in a feature set
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Feature Sets.
2. Select the feature set in which you want to display the API Entry Point as a function, and select Actions 
 > Manage Entry Points.
3. In the Manage Entry Points dialog box, select Search, and choose the API Entry Point from the left Library pane.
4. Drag and drop the selected API Entry Point onto the right pane or select Relate Selected Item 
to relate the API Entry Point to the feature set.
5. Use the Up or Down arrows to position the API Entry Point where you require in the Related Items pane.
6. Enter the name of a sub-group in the Home Page Group column for the API Entry Point. In this way, API Entry Points with the same name are displayed together as a sub-group of functions in a feature set.
7. Enter a value in the Order Value column to specify the order in which the API Entry Point function displays in the feature set. The order is controlled by the lowest Order Value. For example, if an API Entry Point function is set with an
order value of 1, it appears before a function with the order value of 2.
For more information on managing related items, see Manage related items.
API Entry Points and method functions with the same sub-group name display in separate groups in the same feature set.
For more information on modifying functions in a feature set, see Modify the display of API Entry Point functions in a feature set.
Modify the display of API Entry Point functions in a feature set
You can modify how existing functions are displayed in a feature set.
1. Search for the API Entry Point that you want to modify how the function displays in a feature set, and select Update.
 You can only update an API Entry Point that has been created by your administrator.
2. In the Entrypoint for APIs UI details
Select Hide API entry point from list in the UI to hide the API Entry Point type from displaying in the search menu.
Select Hide from Home Page to hide the API Entry Point function from display in a feature set.
Select Home Page Group, and enter the name of a sub-group in which you want to display the API Entry Point function.
 The sub-group names are managed in the Home Page Group column in the Manage Entry Points dialog box. For more information, see Configure API Entry Points to display as functions in a feature set.
 To completely remove an API Entry Point from the display, it must be terminated. If you only remove the relationships between the API Entry Point and its access groups, the API Entry Point becomes available to all users.
Manage access groups for an API Entry Point
You can relate access groups to an API Entry Point for the access group to see a menu items or functions.
1. Go to the API Entry Point in a results grid, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. In the Required Access Groups tab, add criteria or wildcards to the search bar to find the access group, and select Search 
.
3. In the Access Groups results list, choose the access group from the available list, and select Add.
Users must have an access group that allows you to update API Entry Points.
If you remove the relationships between the API Entry Point and its access groups, the API Entry Point becomes available to all users.
To terminate an access group from the API Entry Point, in the Required Access Groups tab, choose the access group from the list, and select Terminate.
You can also use the Manage Related Items dialog to relate access groups to an API Entry Point. For more information on managing related items, see Manage related items.
Use the Info Map to configure an API Entry Point
You can use the Info Map to configure API Entry Point objects by navigating around the visible relationships in the context of other related objects. You can also see who can view the relationships and what data is presented.
Choose an API Entry Point in the results grid, and select Actions 
 > Configure Entry Point. The API Entry Point objects are displayed in the Info Map on the Configure Entry Point page.
 You must have administration role access to configure API Entry Points using the Info Map.
You update the API Entry Point by selecting an object node. The configuration actions available to you depend on the type of node selected:
Node type
What can you do?
Central Node
Configures the central node using actions displayed on the Actions 
 menu.
Interface Definition
Expands the interface definition to view the available edge and relationship definition nodes.
Edge Definition
Opens the Edit EdgeDef dialog box, so you can select the column set, the access groups, and choose to hide the objects from the user interface or
the Info Map.
Relationship Definition
Opens the Edit RelDef dialog box, so you can select the End 1 and End 2 column sets, the roles, the access groups, and choose to hide the objects
from the user interface or the Info Map using the End 1 and End 2 attributes.
If the Info Map End 1 and End 2 attributes are not set, the existing non-Info Map specific End 1 and End 2 attributes are used.
You can control the visibility of object navigations by selecting the Info Map Navigation option.
Show all navigations
You can override any security restrictions on the displayed objects on the Configure Entry Point page by selecting the Show All option. This allows you to see all the object expansions and configured navigations in the Info Map normally
hidden from the selected role due to access restrictions.
View visible navigations
You can choose to hide or display the object navigations by selecting the Info Map Navigation option in the Configure Entry Point page. You control what can be seen by selecting the attribute options when you edit a relationship or
edge definition. For example, you might want an attribute navigation to appear on a context or action menu, but not display in the Info Map.
Resolve which column set to use to display items
HxGN SDx2 uses two mechanisms to resolve which column set to use when displaying items:
When you query for data using an API Entry Point, HxGN SDx2 selects the column set configured for the API Entry Point form.
When you query for data by expanding a relationship, HxGN SDx2 can select the column set in two ways:
The server selects the column set for the expansion using the SPFRelDefEnd1ColumnSet, SPFRelDefEnd2ColumnSet, or SPFEdgeDefColumnSet relationships.
The server selects a column set directly linked to the end interface of the relationship definition or edge definition using the SPFInterfaceDefColumnSet relationship.
Configure search menu items
You can select an object category on the Search menu to run specific searches for each object type in the database. The object categories automatically return the exposed objects and displays them in the Results grid.
Exposing API Entry Points
API Entry Points are used to expose object types and display them in a results grid. Each configured Entry Point is displayed as a category which is automatically exposed in the Search menu.
Hiding API Entry Points
If you want to hide an Entry Point to prevent the item from appearing as an object category in a search menu or on the Home page, use the following steps:
1. Search for the Entry Point you want to hide, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. In the Main Details tab, locate the API Entry Point details section:
a. Select Hide API entry point from list in UI to hide the Entry Point type from the Search menu.
b. Select Hide from Home Page to hide the Entry Point function tile from display in a feature set.
 Setting these options does not affect relationship navigation. For example, if you hide the DEVTag entry point, you can still navigate the relationships to the areas.
 If you want to completely remove an Entry Point from display, you can only do this by terminating it. If you remove the relationship just from the Entry Point to any access groups, the Entry Point will then become available to be
seen by all users.
Configure the default search bar criteria
You can configure the default criteria that appears in the search bar for each Entry Point object type selected in the search menu. This allows you to set different default search criteria for each object type to help search performance.
You can change the default search criteria using the objects Entry Point as follows:
1. Search for the Entry Point in a results grid, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. In the Default Search Criteria box, specify your search criteria. This can be any defining parameters or wildcards that you want to appear in the search box by default, such as a specific name or prefix.
3. Select the Apply Default Search Criteria option to apply criteria to the search bar.
You must select the Redirect To Search Bar option in order for the Entry Point object type to appear in the search bar menu before any default search criteria can be applied.
If you leave the Default search criteria box blank, and select the Apply default search criteria option, no search criteria is applied in the search bar.
If you do not select the Apply default search criteria option, your criteria is not applied and the existing default * all objects is applied.
You must be an administrator to access the Entry Points found in the System Administrator feature set.
Configure peek search request value
As you enter characters in the Search bar, the system invokes a peek search request into the database after a set number of characters have been typed. The peek search menu displays the number and examples of objects returned that
match the entered characters. The main Search is disabled until the set number of peek characters has been typed.
You can improve search performance for objects with long prefixes by refining the peek search request value to a higher value. This reduces the number of peek search requests before you run the main search to return objects in a results
grid.
For example, the default character limit is set to three (3), but you need to type in six (6) characters to match your Entry Point object type. Increasing the peek search request value to six (6) avoids unnecessary peek search requests being
sent to the database after you type three, four, or five characters for that object type.
You configure the number of characters before a peek search request is invoked as follows:
1. Search for the Entry Point you want to edit, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. Enter the number of characters for the peek search in the Number of characters for peek search request box.
The default minimum peek search request value is set at three (3), and the maximum value that you can set is nine (9).
The peek search request value set for the number of characters is specific to each Entry Point. Increasing the peek search request value for one Entry Point does not affect any other Entry Points.
The peek search value only applies to the main Search 
 bar and Relationship Builder search bar.
The Search 
 is disabled until the set number of peek characters has been typed.
Disable peek search
You can disable the peek search functionality for individual Entry Points, even if the number of characters for peek search has been configured. This can help system performance by avoiding multiple peek search requests that could have
a significant impact on the server.
To disable the peek search functionality for an Entry Point:
1. Locate the Entry Point in a results grid, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. Select the Disable peek option.
Change the default search operator
