Only relationships that are not already displayed on the Main Details tab or on the Update tab of the dialog box are displayed in a relationship tab.
Relationship tabs are configured by default on the Details dialog box of the following administration items:
Access groups
Roles
Column sets
Relationship tabs on Update forms
On the Update dialog box, each relationship tab can have two buttons, Maintain Relationships and List Edit. The List Edit button is only available if list editing is configured for the related item.
The Maintain Relationships button opens the Maintain Relationships dialog box where users can relate items to the object or remove related items.
The Apply to similar items option on the Maintain Relationships dialog box allows users to make changes to one link interface and apply the changes to all the link interfaces.
If there are any items related to the object with a link interface, users can select the Maintain Attributes button to edit the link interfaces and any attributes on the link interfaces.
If List Edit is configured for the related item, the List Edit button is available on the Relationship tab on the Update dialog box.
You can use List Edit to create and relate items to selected objects. For more information on List Edit, see Using List Edit.
Configure display items for related objects in create and update forms
You can configure a display item with a relationship definition so that any related objects display in a grid or display as chips in the object Create and Update forms.
You can do this by expanding the list in the Display Item Display Type option in the Display Item form and selecting
Relationship Maintenance display type, which displays any related items in a grid at the bottom of the form
Relationship Filter display type, which displays the added results as individual chips.
When multiple display items are configured in this way, the different object types are shown in separate tabs at the bottom of the form.
 This only displays related objects in the object create and update forms. Other display types listed in the Display Item Display Type box display relationships differently depending on the type of display required, such as a list box
or combo box, and so on.
Relationship maintenance
1: The display item type creates tabs on the dialog boxes to display the relationships.
2: The search box uses the target object's name as the default filter to populate the values. Click Advanced Search 
 to open the advanced search window as shown below.
3: The added results are displayed in a grid layout. Each row in the grid can be managed independently. The columns on the grid are derived from SPFRelDefEnd1ColumnSet, SPFRelDefEnd2ColumnSet, or SPFEdgeDefColumnSet
related to the display item.
 To review and update the column set defined on the above relationships:
a. In HxGN SDx2, right-click the relationship definition or the edge definition.
b. Select Update.
c. Review or update
RelDef End1 ColumnSet property for SPFRelDefEnd1ColumnSet
RelDef End2 ColumnSet property for SPFRelDefEnd2ColumnSet
EdgeDef Display ColumnSet property for SPFEdgeDefColumnSet
4: FILTER - Using the filters available, you can further refine the search results. By default, Name and Description filters along with the column items configured on the relationship definition or edge definition column set
(SPFRelDefEnd1ColumnSet, SPFRelDefEnd2ColumnSet, or SPFEdgeDefColumnSet) appear in the filters.
 To review and update the filter column set defined on the display item:
a. In HxGN SDx2, right-click the display item.
b. Select Manage Filter Column Set.
Relationship filter
1: The search box uses the target object's name as the default filter to populate the values. Click Advanced Search 
 to open the advanced search window as shown below.
2: The added results are displayed as chips, which can be managed independently.
3: The context information can be viewed by clicking on the object (chip) and is configurable through the class definition display column set. To configure the column items, you need to relate the column set to the object with
SPFClassdefDisplayColumnSet.
4: FILTER - Using the filters available, you can further refine the search results. By default, Name and Description filters along with the column items configured on the relationship definition or edge definition column set
(SPFRelDefEnd1ColumnSet, SPFRelDefEnd2ColumnSet, or SPFEdgeDefColumnSet) appear in the filters.
 To review and update the filter column set defined on the display item:
a. In HxGN SDx2, right-click the display item.
b. Select Manage Filter Column Set.
Picture mask types
Picture masks types are format strings and characters that can format input data used for display items. The picture property on a display item is used for the masking in all cases, except for those properties that are date formats.
Set date and time format
You can combine picture mask format strings to format the date and time input data. The date strings must be prefixed with DATE. You can construct picture masks using format strings and characters.
To display the date and time as 06/01/2001 12:00 PM, you enter DATEdd'/'MM'/'yyyy hh':'mm tt.
To display string literals that contain date and time separators or format strings, you must enclose the substring in single quotation marks ('). To display the date as June 06 at 3:00 PM, you would enter DATEMMMM dd 'at' h:mm tt.
 If the 'at' substring is not separated with single quotation marks, as shown, the result is June 06 aP 3:00PM. This is because the character 't' is read as the one letter AM/PM abbreviation in the input format string.
Format strings
This table lists all the valid format strings that the data and time input mask can contain.
Format string
Description
Null String
No mask. The display item acts like a standard text box. This setting is the default.
d
One or two digit day.
dd
Two-digit day. Single digit values are preceded by a zero.
ddd
Three-character day of the week abbreviation.
dddd
Full day of the week name.
h
One or two digit hour using the 12-hour format.
hh
Two-digit hour using the 12-hour format. Single digit values are preceded by a zero.
H
One or two digit hour using the 24-hour format.
HH
Two-digit hour using the 24-hour format. Single digit values are preceded by a zero.
m
One or two digit minute.
mm
Two-digit minute. Single digit values are preceded by a zero.
M
One or two digit month number.
MM
Two-digit month number. Single digit values are preceded by a zero.
MMM
Three-character month abbreviation.
MMMM
Full month name.
s
One or two digit seconds.
ss
Two digit seconds. Single digit values are preceded by a zero.
t
One letter AM/PM abbreviation (AM is displayed as A).
tt
Two letter AM/PM abbreviation (AM is displayed as AM).
y
One digit year (2001 is displayed as 1).
yy
Last two digits of the year (2001 is displayed as 01).
yyyy
Full year (2001 is displayed as 2001).
Characters
This table lists all the characters that the data and time format input mask can contain:
Character
Description
U
Forces the entry to uppercase.
#
Digit placeholder.
.
Decimal placeholder. The actual character used is the one specified as the decimal placeholder in your international settings.
This character is treated as a literal for masking purposes.
,
Thousands separator. The actual character used is the one specified as the thousands separator in your international settings.
This character is treated as a literal for masking purposes.
A
Alphanumeric character placeholder (entry required). For example: a – z, A – Z, or 0 – 9.
a
Alphanumeric character placeholder (entry optional).
9
Digit placeholder (entry optional). For example: 0 – 9.
Literal
All other symbols are displayed as literal.
Validate dates
Picture properties can be used for validation rather than forcing formatting. This allows administrators to prevent users from inserting dates that are invalid, such as dates in the past.
The following constants are supported and the logic is performed. For example, if you enter FUTUREONLY in the picture mask, and the user enters a date that is before todays date, an error message appears.
Case "TODAY"
If ldatLocalDate.Date <> System.DateTime.Today Then
lstrReturnError = SPF.Client.Resources.SPFResources.GetStringWithParameters(9302,
New String() {Picture})
End If
Case "FUTUREONLY"
If ldatLocalDate.Date <= System.DateTime.Today Then
lstrReturnError = SPF.Client.Resources.SPFResources.GetStringWithParameters(9302,
New String() {Picture})
End If
Case "PASTONLY"
If ldatLocalDate.Date >= System.DateTime.Today Then
lstrReturnError = SPF.Client.Resources.SPFResources.GetStringWithParameters(9302,
New String() {Picture})
End If
Case "NOTFUTURE" ' = today and past
If ldatLocalDate.Date > System.DateTime.Today Then
lstrReturnError = SPF.Client.Resources.SPFResources.GetStringWithParameters(9302,
New String() {Picture})
End If
Case "NOTPAST" ' = today and future
If ldatLocalDate.Date > System.DateTime.Today Then
lstrReturnError = SPF.Client.Resources.SPFResources.GetStringWithParameters(9302,
New String() {Picture})
Use password masks on forms
You can use the picture properties to mask the password that is entered on forms. To do this:
1. Search for the property definition you want to modify.
2. Choose the property definition, and select Actions 
 > Show Display Items.
3. Choose the display item name, and select Actions 
 > Update to modify the display item.
4. In the Update Display Item dialog box, type PASSWORD in the Property picture box.
 This setting supports lower and upper case format and will mask the password in all the forms that include the display item. For more information on display items, see Configure display items.
5. Select Finish to update the display item.
Use regular expressions
The regular expression processor in Microsoft .NET is used to process picture masks with character or numeric placeholders. To use these placeholders, you must prefix the picture mask with the word NEW.
The software validates two formats of expressions for the NEW (VB.NET) format and the previous old format. We identify those using the new format by prefixing the picture with NEW. Any in the old format are converted into the new
format and then re-evaluated.
The following is replaced:
lstrRegExp = pstrPicture.Replace("#", "[0-9]")
lstrRegExp = lstrRegExp.Replace("?", ".")
lstrRegExp = lstrRegExp.Replace("*", ".*")
lstrRegExp = lstrRegExp.Replace("[!", "[^")
lstrRegExp = "^" & lstrRegExp & "$"
The following table lists some examples of valid picture masks.
Picture mask
Description
NEW^[A-z][0-9]?$
The input must be 1 alphabetical character plus an optional single numeric character.
NEW^[A-z]$
The input must only be alphabetical characters.
NEW^[A-z]{3}$
The input must be exactly 3 alphabetical characters.
For more information about regular expressions, see Microsoft's Regular Expression Language Overview <https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/base-types/regular-expression-language-quick-reference> .
Configure template documents
There are many cases in an engineering workflow where the creation of a new document requires the starting point to be based on a template document that has a predefined set of files already attached, such as a project or engineering
design document.
You configure template documents based on a selected classification type. So when you create a new document, the method used automatically attaches a set of template files to the new document that match the selected classification
type. The files are added without the need for any manual user intervention.
Since the template document used is specified by classification, the same files from the template can be applied to all documents created using that method. Therefore, if you do not want the document template to be used for different
classifications, you must configure a separate create method for each classification type.
You can also configure a document create method to display the Template Documents tab in the attach files part of the create form, where you can select the templates from a list. This allows you to manually seed and attach a defined
set of files to a new document. The attached files can also be renamed from their original file name using an option that adds the prefix name of its document parent to the file name.
 If the document classification is configuration-controlled, you can create the same method that uses that classification in different configurations and attach different seed files to the template documents for each configuration without
changing the method.
Create a template document
1. In the Business Administration feature set, select Create Template Documents.
2. On the New Template Document form, select a document classification type from the list.
 When assigning classification to the object, you can use the Classification search bar to search and filter for the appropriate classifications based on their Name and Description.
3. In the Main Details section, add the name and description for the new template document, as well as any details and values as required.
4. In New Files pane, add files to the template from the LOCAL FILE SYSTEM tab by selecting CHOOSE FILES to locate and attach files or drop the files onto the indicated area.
5. Select FINISH to create the Template Document.
The files selected in the template are automatically added to any newly-created document by the create method configured with the same classification.
The template document Status is set to RESERVED and the revision Status is set to WORKING by default.
You can only select a single owning group to relate to a template document.
If the document class is configuration-controlled, then it is possible to provide different seed files per configuration based on the scope without changing the method. For example, you could create the same template document for
different configurations so that each configuration could have its own seed files when you create a document.
You can see the revision and version of a template document in a results grid and when you create a document to ensure you choose the correct template document.
For more information on creating documents, see Create a document.
Configure a method to display the templates
By default, files from a template document are added automatically to a newly-created document in the background. However, you can configure a create method for new documents so that a template documents for a specific
classification appear in the Template Documents tab. When you select a template document, any attached files are displayed in the Template Files pane.
You can display the Template Documents tab by relating the selected create method to either the CreateClassObj client API or the CreateWizardClassObj client API, and then adding the template document classification syntax to the
selected method option. The syntax is based on the client API that created the method. The Template documents tab will then appear in the attach files part of the create document form.
What makes up the template document syntax?
The template document syntax to enable the display of the Template Documents tab is constructed using the following string:
TemplateclassificationUID~IsSingleSelection~IsRenameChkBoxEnabled.
Where:
TemplateclassificationUID is the UID classification type.
IsSingleSelection is for the a single document.
IsRenameChkBoxEnabled gives you the option to rename the files using the new document suffix. Selecting true means the option is selected by default. Selecting false means the option is not selected by default.
The syntax string is then added to the following arguments:
Argument 18 for methods created using the CreateClassObj client API.
Argument 9 for methods created using the CreateWizardClassObj client API.
Example syntax string
The FDWCreateDocument method is configured by default to display the Template Documents tab using the following string:
DC_Template_Documents_1~true~false.
This syntax was added to the method Argument 18, as the method was created using the CreateClassObj client API.
To display the Template Documents tab in the New Document form:
1. Search for and select a create document type method.
2. Define the template document syntax for the specific classification type.
3. Add the syntax string to the applicable method property as follows:
SeedFileConfiguration property for methods created using the CreateClassObj client API (Parameter 18).
SeedFileConfiguration property for methods created using the CreateWizardClassObj client API (Parameter 9).
By default, methods created using either the create client APIs have the method property left blank by design, so they do not show the Template Documents tab in the New Files page.
The Template Documents tab can be only be displayed by methods that use client APIs that support the File attachment configuration property (Parameter 6).
Edit and check in a template document
When you edit a template document, it is checked out and set as reserved for edit, and then downloaded to your device for editing. After a document template has been edited, you check in the document template as the latest revision.
1. Select Actions 
 > Edit Template Document.
2. Edit the template document locally.
3. To check in the updated template document, choose the template document, and select Actions 
 > Check In.
4. On the Select Template Document(s) to Check In window, select a template document you want to check in, and select FINISH.
The modified template document must be checked in with its original name.
You can see the check in details in the History viewer, which lists the template document history details..
When you edit a template document, a new WORKING version is created based on the selected revision scheme. The previous template document is set to Superseded.
Undo edit in a template document
 This is administration command for system administrators only.
If the files attached to a WORKING version are being edited,
1. Select Actions > Undo Edit Template Document.
2. Select Yes to revert the version and its attached files back to the previous version iteration.
You do not see the Undo Edit Files command for any document revision with only one version, as this action is controlled by a condition in the method so that the system will not delete the revision.
You can change the behavior of the Undo Edit Files command by configuring the UndoEditFiles client API. For more information, see UndoEditFiles client API.
 You can view the related files by selecting a document from a results grid, and then selecting Actions > Show All Files.
Configure a property value to be set on merged objects
You can apply a property value to all claimed objects being merged from a project or lower configuration, which can be seen in a results table. You can do this by entering a property value in the format <Interface>~<Property>~<Value> in
the Property to be set after successful merge parameter in the MergeConfig, MergeObj, and MergeStructure client APIs.
For example, IFDWTaggedItem~FDWTagStatus~e1FDWTagStatus_AsBuilt
This configured value on the IFDWTaggedItem interface definition sets the FDWTagStatus property definition to the enumerated value UID, e1FDWTagStatus_AsBuilt. This applies the AsBuilt value to the objects. However, you can also set
string properties.
If the interface and property defined in the Property to be set after successful merge parameter is not applicable to a specific merged object type, the merged object does not have the property value applied on merge.
If you want to set multiple values on merged objects, you can use commas to separate the values. For example: Interface1~Property1~Value1,Interface2~Property2~Value2.
If an object has an existing property value applied that is different to the value configured for the Property to be set after successful merge parameter, the existing property value is replaced by the configured value.
This functionality is intended for administration type properties and the values set are not applicable to relationship definitions.
For more information on the Property to be set after successful merge parameter in the MergeConfig, MergeObj, and MergeStructure client APIs, see Client APIs.
Configure viewing page titles
The SPFDocumentVersionTreeDisplay column set controls what properties are displayed in the following viewing page titles:
View and Markup
View Native
View Alternate
Edit Files
Save Target As
View and Markup with Outgoing Action
Column sets are composed of column items. Each column item represents a property or relationship value for the item. You can configure what column items are displayed using the Manage Column items dialog box. The following
column sets are included by default:
Name
Revision
Version
Workflow Status
 For more information on how column sets work, see Configure column sets.
Configure the audit functionality on methods
The audit functionality tracks actions performed by users over a period of time, like download, view, or update of any objects or files in HxGN SDx2. You can also run a report to analyze who has viewed or updated the information in HxGN
SDx2, and when.
 Before configuring, you must select Enable Auditing in the system options. For more information, see Configure the system options. To ensure the changes are implemented, refresh your web browser.
In the System Administration feature set, any user with an Audit role must perform the following tasks to configure the audit functionality on a method:
1. Select the method, and select Actions 
 > Enable/Disable Audit.
2. Select the Is Audit Required On Method option.
3. Select UPDATE.
4. To ensure the changes are implemented, refresh your web browser.
In the Business Administration feature set, any user with the Audit role can execute the following reports:
Audit history - to view who has used a method configured for auditing, at what time, and for which object in the system.
Methods for audit - to view all the methods configured for auditing.
Disabling auditing stops new data collection and storage, but existing audit data remains and can still be reported. Re-enabling auditing resumes logging of data, but a gap exists in the audit trail for the disabled period. 
Configure column sets
The column set functionality provides the user with the ability to configure column sets to control the display of properties and their order when displayed in a results grid. As an administrator, you can create column sets and relate the
column set to an object's class definition, a method, a relationship, or an interface definition to display a defined set of properties.
Column sets
Column sets control what properties are displayed in columns when an object is displayed. Each column set is composed of column items that determine the appearance and order of the properties. Each type of object can have its own
column set to determine which properties you want to be displayed. The column items that are created are automatically related to the column set.
Column items
A column set is composed of a number of column items that each represent the properties or relationship values for the object type. You can control the order in which the column items display in a column set, as well as the column width
and other display attributes by setting the values on the column items.
How do column sets work?
Results grid and List Edit
Column sets support list views in the results grid and when using List Edit, and help identify and display an object's properties and relationships. You can also identify properties on related objects.
Manage related items
Column sets support list views and help identify properties and objects expanded from relationships when the Dynamic Item option is set.
How does the system know which column set to use?
When HxGN SDx2 displays data in a results grid, the system chooses which column set to use. This choice is based on:
The object type that is being displayed.
The system configuration, where a column set is specified on a method argument or an object type.
When the column set selected is based on the object type being displayed, the server works out the best column set to use by:
Looking for a column set for the first object returned.
Looking for a column set based on the object’s classification.
Looking for a column set linked to the interfaces on the object.
If a column set does not exist for an object type, the system automatically selects the most appropriate column set based on the object type, such as the class definition or class definition's primary interface definition.
When additional properties are required to display in a column set, you can add the column items by selecting a column set and clicking Manage column items. For more information on managing column items, see Manage related
items.
You can create a relationship between a column set and an interface definition so that any object that is instantiated with the interface definition has its properties displayed using the related column set.
Create a column set
You can create a new column set to define a custom view for an object to display a defined set of properties when displayed in the results grid. Once the column set has been created, existing column items can be added to it.
1. Locate the Business Administration feature set, and select Create Column Set.
2. In the New Column Set form, enter a name and description for the column set based on the object type to which the column set is to be related, such as a class definition.
 If you want to use the column set with List Edit, select the Column set has been enabled for list editing option.
Create a column item
You can create new column items to include custom properties for objects when displayed in the results grid. To create a column set item:
1. Locate the Business Administration feature set, and select Create Column Item.
2. In the New Column Item form, enter a name and description for the column item based on the column item type required.
3. Enter the details and information in the Column item details as required. For example, you can add the UID ot the property definition.
The following properties are required for creating or managing related items.
Property
Description
Edge definition
The relationship or edge to be navigated. This is an optional property.
 Ensure you use the edge definition for all column items that have the same relationship definition path.
Property definition UID
The UID of the property definition that the column item will display.
Property edge Alias
Alias used for the relationship between the property definition and the edge definition. This property is used for reporting and is not
required for regular column items.
Dynamic item
Select this option when the column item is created from a relationship definition or property definition which is expanded from a
linked interface.
Graph edge name
The name of the Graph edge definition the column item will display. This property is used for reporting and is not required for
regular column items.
Dynamic form section
The form section dynamically generated from the column items configured for the object.
Column item phase
The phase to which this column item belongs.
Display item help text
The text that appears when the cursor pauses over the column item.
Dynamic form sequence
The unique sequence number to control the display order on dynamic forms.
Some options are mutually exclusive to the form.
You select a column set and use the Manage column items command to configure new column items. For more information, see Manage related items.
Relate an interface to an column set
You can relate an interface definition to a column set.
Search and locate the column set, and select Actions 
 > Manage Interfaces
When you create a relationship between a column set and an interface definition, any object that has instantiated that interface is displayed using the related column set.
You can also relate a classification to a column set using Actions 
 Manage Classifications.
Managing column items
When you need to edit or add additional properties to a column set:
1. Locate the required object column set,and select Manage column items.
2. Add additional column item properties or edit the existing column items using Manage related items as required.
 For more information on managing column items, see Manage related items.
Manage column items for Edit
Column set items can be managed for use in dynamic create forms and, with the correct permissions, you can edit objects and their property values in the table rows using the List Edit functionality. For more information, see Using List
Edit.
Locate the column set for an object, and select Actions 
 > Manage Column Items for Edit.
 For more information on managing column items, see Manage related items.
Configure automatic sorting of column items
You can configure the automatic sorting of column item properties into a selected order when they are displayed in the results grid using the Manage Column Items command.
The two property options available for automatic sorting are:
Sort Sequence - determines the numerical priority order of the column items to sort on.
Sort Order - determines whether the column items are sorted in ascending or descending order.
For example, to sort the sequence for a class definition by name first and then sort by ascending order:
1. Set the Sort Sequence property for the Name column item to 1.
 Add numbers to other column items so they display in a set numerical order, as required.
2. Set the Sort Order column item to Ascending.
 For more information, see Manage column items.
Configure column width
You edit the column item values Display As and Size by selecting them directly in the column row.
For example, the Description column size for the following SPFObject is set to 32.
 For more information, see Manage column items.
Example configurations
Mange column items on an object
Property name on the object - SPFObject column set.
Manage column items on a relationship
Property expand on relationship SPFRelDefAccessGroup - SPFRelDefMAG column set.
Manage column items on a related object using a relationship definition
Property client API name on related object SPFClientAPIMethods - SPFMethodColumnSet column set.
 The edge definition entry in Edge must be directional (+or -)
Manage related items
The Manage Related Items functionality in HxGN SDx2 provides the user with the ability to configure and manage related items for a specified object relationship. The related items are presented in a tabulated view, so you can easily add
or remove existing related items for a selected object type.
 You can also configure some display properties in the column items for specific object types.
The following are some examples of the objects that you can configure using the Manage Related Items dialog:
Functions in a feature set
Classification in a column set
Interface definition in a column set
Column set in a classification
Column set in an interface definition
Column items in a column set
Form sections in a form
Display items in a form section
Access groups for a form
Access Groups for a role
Roles for an access group
You can manage each of these and others by locating the type of object, and selecting the appropriate Manage <related item> action displayed in the object's Actions menu.
 You must regenerate the metadata for any related item changes to be applied to the selected object in the system.
Access to the manage related items method functionality is controlled by your role and system permissions. For more information, contact your system administrator.
For more information about the SDxManageRelatedItems client API that methods use to display specific object types, see SDXManageRelatedItems client API.
Use the manage related items dialog
You view the related items for a selected object in the Manage Related Items dialog, which displays all the related items in a tabulated grid. In the dialog, you can search for any valid items that can be related to the selected parent object
or edit existing related items and their properties directly in the columns.
For example, the following image shows the related items for the ClassDefColumnSet by selecting the action Manage Column Items.
1 - The Library pane on the left displays valid items in a list view that can be related to the selected object type. Use the search to find any valid items in the system, as highlighted by the example, IsCfgControlled.
2 - The Related Items pane on the right displays all the related items that have an existing relationship to the selected object. You can select a cell in a column to edit the property for that related item.
3 - Select the Relate Item 
 or Remove Item 
 arrows or drag an item to create or remove a relationship for a selected object type.
4 - Select the MOVE UP or MOVE DOWN buttons or drag an item to change the order of the related item in the object's column set.
What are the commands used in Manage Related Items?
The following commands are available in the Manage Related Items dialog:
Select this
To do this
Search
Add any criteria, parameters, or wildcards to search for items in the system.
Relate Item 
Create a relationship from the selected item to the selected object.
Remove Item 
Remove the relationship from the related item to the selected object.
MOVE UP
Move the selected item up in ascending order.
MOVE DOWN
Move the selected item down in descending order.
To view examples of how to relate items to specific object types, see Configure the related items for a specific object type.
The Manage Related Items dialog automatically changes the viewing aspect of the panes, depending on the device being used.
 Replacing or removing the Name column item can cause instability. You can change the display name for the Name column item by changing the Display as field in the Manage Column Items screen. The launch URL
objectName requires the Name column item.
Search for an item to relate
1. In the Search bar, enter the search criteria, along with any wildcards.
2. Select Search 
, and the valid items are displayed in the Library pane.
For example, if you entered *IsCfg* in the search bar, the IsCfgControlled item is displayed in the Library pane.
 You can only search for the name or part of the name of an item in the Search field.
Relate an item
1. Select a valid item in the Library pane, and either:
Select Relate Item 
 to move the item into the Related Items pane.
or
Drag the selected item onto the Related Items pane.
2. Select SAVE. The relationship is created between the selected item and the specific object type.
Remove a related item
1. Select the related item in the right Related Items pane, and either:
Select Remove Item 
 to remove the item from the Related Items pane.
Or
Drag the item onto the left Library pane.
2. Select SAVE.
 Only the relationship between the related item and the parent object is removed. It does not delete any relationships the item may have with any other objects.
Edit an existing related item or property
1. Select the related item in the Related Items pane, and make changes to the related item directly in the property column cell.
 Not all column cells are editable. A property column cell may contain a selectable list.
2. Select SAVE, to apply the related item change to the selected object.
Change the display order of related items
1. Select the related item row in the right Related Item pane, and either:
Select the MOVE UP or MOVE DOWN buttons to change the row order.
Or
Drag the selected item row to a new position in the Related Item pane.
2. Select SAVE.
View related items in the object's details form
You can view any related items for specific objects directly in the Details form.
For example, the following image shows the related column items for the FDWTag column set.
 You must regenerate the metadata so that any changes made to the order or sequence of any related items using Manage Related Items is applied to the object. They then will display in the object's MAIN DETAILS tab.
Configure the related items for a specific object type
The following examples show how you can use Manage Related Items to configure items that are related to specific object types, such as column items for a column set. For details on how to use the Manage Related Items, see Use the
manage related items dialog.
The examples only show a small set of object types that can be configured using Manage Related Items. What objects you see and what you can edit will vary according to how your system, your roles, and your access is
configured.
Some related items shown in the examples can be located by expanding an object's Relationships in the Actions menu.
For more information on configuring access groups, see Access group configuration.
For details on how to use the Manage Related Items, see Use the manage related items dialog.
Manage access groups
Access groups govern your access to commands for relationship creation and navigation. Access groups are modeled to represent different levels of functional access to allow roles to have access to the different components of HxGN
SDx2.
You can control the access to the functionality by relating a role to an access group or relating the method that provides the functionality to an access group. When access to functionality is restricted to specific access groups, the
application functionality is hidden from other access groups.
For more information on creating access groups, see Access group configuration.
Manage the access groups for a role
Roles are related to a set of access groups with domains and owning groups optionally configured on these relationships.
1. Search for a role, and select Actions 
 > Manage Access Groups.
2. Add or remove the access groups as required.
3. Select the owning groups or domains to configure for the relationship.
Why would I want to restrict method access by object ownership?
The access granted by any access group in a role can be restricted to a set of owning groups by identifying them on the role or access group relationship. This enables the same access group to be used in different roles on different sets of
data identified by their owning group.
The most typical use of this is to restrict access to documents and data by their department or discipline by using the owning groups to represent a department or discipline.
The domains accessible for query are all the domains configured on the role-to-access group relationships for the user and all their dependent domains.
At least one domain must be configured on the role to access group relationships, otherwise, the user cannot access any domain information.
You can restrict method access based on the ownership of an object, select the owning groups that you want to restrict the access to in the Owning Group column.
For more information, see Manage roles for an access group and Use the manage related items dialog.
You can also create new roles for an access group, see Create a role.
Manage roles for an access group
Roles are related to a set of access groups with domains and owning groups optionally configured on these relationships, so your users can access data on your system. You can assign different roles to each access group.
1. Search for the access group, and select Actions 
 > Manage Roles.
2. Search and locate the roles to assign to the access group in the left Library pane.
3. Select Relate selected items 
 to relate the selected role to the access group.
 You can also drag a role to the right Related Items pane to relate it to the access group.
At least one domain must be configured on the role or access group relationships; otherwise, the user cannot access any domain information.
You can restrict the access of a role to methods using owning groups. For more information. see Restrict access to methods using owning groups.
For more information on managing related items, see Manage related items.
Manage methods for an access group
Methods are exposed as function tiles in a feature set, actions, action menus, toolbars, and search options. Methods are displayed to users through their relationships with specific roles. When an access group is associated with a role, the
methods that the access group exposes are available to all the users assigned to that role.
The relationships between methods and access groups can be configured using Manage Methods command, which allows you to add methods to an access group and edit the relationship properties.
Search for an access group, and select Actions 
 > Manage Methods.
You can also use the Manage Access Groups action from the method.
You can restrict access to methods using owning groups, see Restrict access to methods in an access group and Further ways to restrict access to methods.
For more information on creating, updating, and managing methods, see Configure methods.
For more information on managing related items, see Manage related items.
Restrict access to methods in an access group
The access granted by any access group can be restricted to a set of owning groups by identifying them on the access group relationship. This enables the same access group to be used in different roles on data identified by their owning
group. In this way, you can restrict access to methods based on the ownership.
You can restrict method access based on the ownership of an object so that the user must be either the owner of the object or related to the owning group found on the access group. When an owning group is set on a role, all the method
functionality granted by that access group can be restricted to only the objects within that owning group. Using this approach, the role of a user can grant them permission to update objects that belong to one owning group, but not update
the objects that belong to other owning groups.
 To restrict access to methods using owning groups, see Restrict access to methods using owning groups.
At least one domain must be configured on the role or access group relationships; otherwise, the user cannot access any domain information.
For more information on managing owning groups, see Configure owning groups.
For more information on managing related items, see Manage related items.
Manage relationship definitions for an access group
You can update a relationship from a relationship definition to an access group when the role of the user has an access group related to the relationship definition.
Search for an access group, and select Actions 
 > RelDef Access.
When a relationship is being created between two objects, the creation in each direction is controlled separately.
The system relates the object at end 1 onto the object at end 2 based on the interfaces on the object compared with the interfaces at end 1 and end 2 of the relationship definition.
For more information on relationship definitions, see Relationship and edge definitions.
For more information on managing related items, see Manage related items.
Manage access groups for an API Entry Point
You can relate access groups to an API Entry Point for the access group to see a menu items or functions.
1. Go to the API Entry Point in a results grid, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. In the Required Access Groups tab, add criteria or wildcards to the search bar to find the access group, and select Search 
.
3. In the Access Groups results list, choose the access group from the available list, and select Add.
Users must have an access group that allows you to update API Entry Points.
If you remove the relationships between the API Entry Point and its access groups, the API Entry Point becomes available to all users.
To terminate an access group from the API Entry Point, in the Required Access Groups tab, choose the access group from the list, and select Terminate.
You can also use the Manage Related Items dialog to relate access groups to an API Entry Point. For more information on managing related items, see Manage related items.
Manage the relationship definitions for an entry point
You can manage the relationship definitions for an entry point that are related to an access group.
1. Search for an entry point, and select Actions 
 > Show Access Groups.
2. Select an access group, and select Actions 
 > Manage RelDef Access.
3. In the Manage RelDef Access dialog, add or remove the relationship definitions to configure the access group for the entry point as required.
Manage column sets
The details, relationships, and properties of each object are displayed in a set of columns. Each column set is composed of column items that represent a relationship or property value for a selected object. You can control the display of
properties in a column set and other display attributes by setting the values on the column items.
 For more information, see Manage related items.
Manage classifications for a column set
You can manage the classifications related to a column set.
1. Search for a column set, and select Actions 
 > Manage Classifications.
2. In the Manage Classifications dialog, add or remove the classifications for the selected column set as required.
 When you create a relationship between a column set and classification, objects with those classifications are displayed using the related column set.
Manage interfaces for a column set
You can manage the interface definitions related to a column set.
1. Search for a column set, and select Actions 
 > Manage Interfaces.
2. In the Manage Interfaces dialog, add or remove the interface definitions for the selected column set as required.
 When you create a relationship between interface definitions and a column set, any object that has instantiated that interface is displayed using the related column set.
Manage column items for a column set
You can manage column items in an object's column set. Each column item represents a property or relationship value in a column set for the specific object.
1. Search for an API Entry Point, and select Actions 
 > Show Column Set.
2. Select the column set, and select Actions 
 > Manage Column Items.
3. In the Manage Column Items dialog, configure the column items in the column set as required.
What columns are found in Manage column Items?
The following columns are found in the Manage Column Items dialog.
Column
Description
Name
Column item name.
Description
Description of column item.
Display As
The column item display name.
Size
Width of column.
Order Value
Sequence in which the item appears.
Property
The property definition that the column item displays.
Edge
The edge definition that the column item displays.
Be sure to use the edge definition for all column items that use the same relationship definition path.
Graph/Edge Alias
The alias used for the relationship between the property definition and the edge definition.
Dynamix
This property should be set to True when navigating to relationship properties or objects.
Configs
The configuration in which the column item is set to conditionally display when used in a dynamic form section.
Graph/Edge Name
Name of the edge definition the column item displays.
 Where applicable, values can be edited directly in a column cell, such as Display As. In others, you can select the value from a list,such as Size, and Order Value.
Manage column items for dynamic forms
You can manage column set items for use in dynamic forms or the List Edit control.
1. Search for an API Entry Point, and select Actions 
 > Show Column Set.
2. Select the column set, and select Actions 
 > Manage Column Items for Edit.
3. In the Manage Column Items for Edit dialog, configure the column items in the column set as required.
What columns are found in the Manage related items for Edit?
The following additional columns are found in the related items pane when you use Manage related items for Edit.
Column
Description
Disable View
Hide this column when viewing data.
Hide on Dynamic Form
This property can be set to True to hide a column item from showing on any dynamic forms.
Disable Edit
Hide this column when using column set for list edit.
Readonly
Ensure cell is read-only.
Mandatory
Ensure cell is mandatory.
Display Override
Used to change the type of control used when editing.
Default UOM
If the property is scoped by a UOM, values default to this UOM.
Default Create
For new rows, default properties and relationships.
Picture
Used for validating cell entry.
Allow Create
For relationship cells, allow creation of a new related item.
Min Value
Set a minimum value for integer and double property types.
Max Value
Set a maximum value for integer and double property types.
 Where applicable, values can be edited directly in a column cell, such as Display As. In others, you can select the value from a list, such as Size and Order Value.
 You can hide an attribute column item from any dynamic form by selecting Manage Column Items for Edit on the specific column set and setting the Hide on Dynamic Form property to True.
Configure automatic sorting of column items
You can configure the automatic sorting of column item properties into a selected order when they are displayed in the results grid using the Manage Column Items command.
The two property options available for automatic sorting are:
Sort Sequence - determines the numerical priority order of the column items to sort on.
Sort Order - determines whether the column items are sorted in ascending or descending order.
For example, to sort the sequence for a class definition by name first and then sort by ascending order:
1. Set the Sort Sequence property for the Name column item to 1.
 Add numbers to other column items so they display in a set numerical order, as required.
2. Set the Sort Order column item to Ascending.
 For more information, see Manage column items.
Configure column width
You edit the column item values Display As and Size by selecting them directly in the column row.
For example, the Description column size for the following SPFObject is set to 32.
 For more information, see Manage column items.
Manage column items by configuration
You can configure property attribute column items to conditionally display in the same dynamic form section based on the selected configuration or plant.
This functionality allows you to choose to configure the property attributes for a specific column set to display in a form based on the selected configuration.
1. Search for an entry point, and select Actions 
 > Show Column Set.
2. Select the column set, and select Actions 
 > Manage Column Items.
3. In the Configs column item for each selected property row, select the configurations from a list.
For example, in the FDWTag classification, the column item Description will be displayed only in the first three configurations to conditionally display in a dynamic form.
Configure an InsightView dashboard
An InsightView dashboard displays real-time data from various sources (including third-party data sources) in a single page within HxGN SDx2. You can create customized dashboards for each role in a facility. There are three types of
InsightView dashboard:
Dashboards
These display data related to a selected object, allowing you to easily visualize plant information relevant to that object. For example, a dashboard can be opened from a tag's Actions 
 menu and can display information related to
that tag.
 These dashboards can be displayed as a pop-up window or as its own page.
Home Screens
These can be used as a user's default home page instead of the feature sets, providing users with easy access to their tasks and a role-based view of their assets and critical information.
 The user selects between feature sets and a home screen dashboard in their user options. For more information, see Set a home screen dashboard as your home page.
Context Panels
These display data related to a selected object, in a split screen view. For example, you can select a tag and a context panel displays its properties and any other data, alongside the Results page. For more information, see Using a
Context Panel dashboard.
A dashboard is made up of a web page containing a number of widgets. Each widget can display data from a different data source. A widget can be used in multiple web pages if required.
 To see what dashboards a widget is used in, search for the widget and select Actions 
 > Show Web Page.
Before you can connect to another data source, you need to set up the authorization server which will authorize requests for data to a data source. You also need to create Web APIs to request the data from the data sources. Dashboards
use the Open Data Protocol (OData) version 4, and Web API v1 and v2 to access data from other systems.
 For more information on OData, see Using OData in Web API v1 or v2.
Create an authorization server
1. In the Dashboards feature set, select Create Authorization Server.
2. Type a name for the server.
3. In Authorization Server URL, type the URL to connect to the authorization server.
4. If required, search for the request header to use with this authorization server.
Create a Web API
1. In the Dashboards feature set, select Create Web API.
2. Select whether this is a local or remote Web API.
When assigning classification to the object, you can use the Classification search bar to search and filter for the appropriate classifications based on their Name and Description.
A Web API is already provided for connecting to your local server. This is HEX_Local_SDx_V2_DASH.
3. Type a name and description for the Web API.
4. Type the required URLs in the Base URL and Metadata URL fields.
 You can use the environment variable {{LOCAL_SERVER_URL}} as part of the URL to connect to your local server. For example, the Base URL would be {{LOCAL_SERVER_URL}}api/v2/DASH/, where api/v2/DASH/ is
the API entry point group specifically for dashboard requests.
5. For a remote Web API, select the authorization server to use, and type the other authorization details required.
6. If required, search for the request header to use with this Web API.
Create a request header
Request headers are used to provide additional information to an HTTP request. For example, you could use it to specify a specific configuration to limit the results returned. Request headers can be related to authorization servers for use
when authenticating, Web APIs when retrieving meta data, and widgets when executing a request.
1. In the Dashboards feature set, select Create Request Header.
2. Type a name for the request header.
3. Type the required key and the value to use.
In addition to standard HTTP request headers, OData headers are also supported. For more information, see OData headers in Web API v1 or v2.
When using an environment variable, the request header is not sent with the request to resolve the environment variable.
Example request headers
Request header
Description
Key: SPFQueryConfigUID
Value: <Config UID>
When applied to a widget, this sets the scope of the widget to the configuration identified by <Config
UID>.
Key: SPFQuerySelectedConfigOnly
Value: True
When applied to a widget, this overrides the Query by selected configurations only option in the
user settings and sets it to True.
Key: SPFEffectiveDate
Value: <Date>
When applied to a widget, this returns historical data using the specified date.
An example <Date> is 2021/03/25-12:12:00:000.
Create a dashboard
1. In the Dashboards feature set, select Create Dashboard.
2. Type the name of the dashboard and a title. The Title is displayed in HxGN SDx2 when the dashboard is opened.
 You can also enter a description to help identify this dashboard.
3. Select the type of dashboard required.
What are the different types of dashboard available?
The following types of dashboard can be created. For more information, see Configure an InsightView dashboard.
Option
Description
Home Screen
Creates a full-page home screen dashboard that can be made available from either the user's home page or their Dashboards button.
Actions Menu
Creates a full-page dashboard that will be available from the Actions menu of a selected object.
Context Panel
Creates a split-screen view dashboard that displays data related to a selected object.
Popup Modal
Creates a pop-up dashboard that will be available from the Actions menu of a selected object.
4. To make the dashboard available to other users, select Published. You can leave this option cleared until you have finished making your changes and select it at a later time, as described in Update a dashboard.
 The Published flag provides a quick and simple option to control whether the dashboard is available to end users without modifying the access groups.
5. Select the template to use to layout the new dashboard.
 For a context panel dashboard, the most appropriate templates to use are indicated with a star 
.
6. Click Next to move to the next page of the wizard.
7. Select at least one interface definition for the dashboard. This option is not applicable to home screen dashboards as they aren't related to a specific object.
8. Select the access groups who will be able to access the dashboard.
9. Click Finish to create the dashboard.
 You can update the dashboard title and access control as described in Update a dashboard.
10. Configure the widgets to display on the dashboard. For each widget, click 
, and either:
Create a widget or
Select an existing widget to display.
 If you are creating a home screen dashboard, do not include a widget that uses the CURRENT_OBJECT_ID variable, as this variable is not set in a home screen dashboard.
 Click 
 to update a widget on the dashboard.
11. Check your widgets are returning the expected results as described in Check the status of a widget.
 You can select 
 > Preview Page to see a preview of the dashboard. You can also preview an individual widget by hovering over it and selecting 
. When previewing widgets, they are displayed as view-only and you can't
interact with them. Widgets aren't previewed if they rely on an object to be selected first.
Create a widget
Widgets are created as part of the procedure for creating a new dashboard. For more information, see Create a dashboard. Once you have created a widget, you can preview the widget by hovering over it and selecting 
. Widgets aren't
previewed if they rely on an object to be selected first.
 Some example widgets are also provided and can be added to a dashboard as described in Create a dashboard.
What types of widget can be created?
Widget type
Description
Chart
A chart widget displays returned data in a chart view. A chart can be configured so that selecting a section of the
chart displays the related data in a grid in a second page.
Count
A count widget displays a count of the number of results returned, with the details in a grid in a second page.
Form    
A form widget displays returned data in a form.
 You can change a form widget to show the data in a list or in a tile. You can also sort and filter the data
displayed.
Grid    
A grid widget displays returned data in columns similar to the Results page.
IFrame    
An iframe widget displays another web page.
 A user name and password might be required to log on to the external web page when an iframe widget is
viewed in a dashboard.
Property Inspector
A property inspector widget displays the names and values of the properties associated with a selected object. By
default, it shows all populated properties for the object apart from those that are only useful to an administrator.
 You can view the additional administration properties for the object by selecting 
 > Show Administration
Properties. These administration properties can include DomainUID, SDxByCustomer, OBID, UniqueKey, UID,
ClassDef, and LastUpdatedDate.
 A property inspector widget cannot be used on a home screen dashboard.
QuickAccess3DFilter
A 3D Quick Access widget displays a list of the filters that you have access to. Select  > View and Markup next to
a filter to open the related 3D model.
Spotlight
A spotlight widget displays one item of returned data scaled to fit the widget.
Tag Existence
A tag existence widget displays the Tag Existence Dashboard in a widget. For more information on the Tag
Existence Dashboard, see View the Tag Existence Dashboard.
To Do List
A To Do List widget displays a summary of your To Do List. You can switch between displaying tasks by
configuration or object type, at the bottom of the widget.
How do I create a widget using a new API entry point group?
You may want to create a widget using a new API entry point group as it allows you finite control over columns sets, entry points, and relationships.
1. Create a new API entry point group with the required API entry points. For more information, see Create an API Entry Point group.
2. Create a web API to use the API entry point group. For more information, see Create a Web API.
3. Create a widget that uses the new web API.
 You can have only one To Do List widget, one 3D Quick Access widget, and one Property Inspector widget created in the system at a time. If a To Do List widget, a 3D Quick Access widget, or a Property Inspector widget already
exists, you cannot create another one.
Widgets are created in the Create Widget wizard, which displays the items used to configure the widget in separate tabs.
1. Complete the tabs to create the widget as follows:
 The tabs displayed vary depending on the type of widget you are creating.
a. Properties
Type a name and title for the widget. The Title is displayed at the top of the widget box.
 You can't modify the widget name once the widget is created. The widget name can't contain spaces. If you plan to link to this widget from another widget, or you are creating a chart widget with related data displayed in
a second page, you can use only alphanumeric characters (and underscores) in the widget name.
 You can also enter a description to help identify this widget.
b. Control
Select the type of widget to create.
 The tabs that are available in the remainder of the wizard will change to suit the type of widget selected.
c. Form Properties
This tab is only available if you are creating a form widget.
Select whether to initially display the information in a tile or a list layout.
Tile layout example
   
List layout example
   
d. API
1. Select the required Web API.
2. If a local Web API is used, and you want the user to be able to change the scope for this widget, select Allow SDx scope selection to be changed. The default scope will be the user's create scope in a home screen, or
the object's scope in a dashboard.
 You can force a widget to use a specific configuration using a request header. For an example, see Example request headers. We recommend using a request header to control the configuration when connecting to
a remote site.
e. Headers
If required, search for the request header to use with this widget.
For information about request headers, see Create a request header.
f. Custom Variables
If required, use the Build URL using variables control to use a variable to build the URL.
You have two options:
Define a variable to use in a URL
Retrieve a URL from a property (available for iframe widgets only)
With the exception of CURRENT_OBJECT_ID, HxGN SDx2 environment variables are not supported in widgets.
Variables should always use upper case.
Define a variable to use in a URL
 Variables defined in this way can be used only in the widget they are defined in. Variables are always defined against the local data source and not the Web API selected in the widget.
1. Select Define a variable to use in a URL.
2. Type a name for the variable in Variable alias. For example, TAGNAME.
 You cannot include WIDGET_ in your variable name as this is reserved for use in widget dependencies.
3. Type the value to assign to the variable in Variable definition. For example, Tags({{CURRENT_OBJECT_ID}})?$select=Name.
4. Use the Variable alias in the URL to return the required data. For example, Tags?$filter=Name eq '{{TAGNAME}}'.
For more information on the parameters you can use in this URL, see Supported URL parameters.
Retrieve a URL from a property
 This option is available only for iframe widgets.
1. Select Retrieve a URL from a property.
2. Type the location of the property that will return the required URL.
For more information on the parameters you can use for this, see Supported URL parameters.
g. URL
Either type the suffix for the URL to return the required data, or click 
 to use a wizard to complete the URL. For more information on the wizard, see Complete the URL using a wizard. If required, you can reference
another widget on the dashboard in the URL, see Make a widget dependent on another widget. For more information on the parameters you can use in the URL, see Supported URL parameters.
With the exception of CURRENT_OBJECT_ID, HxGN SDx2 environment variables are not supported in widgets.
Variables should always use upper case.
For a count or grid widget using a local Web API, the Actions menu will be available if the Name, Config, and ID are all returned, and the widget is displayed in a full page dashboard (rather than a pop-up window). The ID
column is hidden by default in the results.
If you want to filter using an enumerated property, for example the document type, use the Name of the property and not the Display Name in the URL.
As the URL is completed, it is checked to ensure it is syntactically valid. A warning is shown if there are any issues. You can still finish creating the widget as the error may be due to permissions issues, which will not affect
the widget when it is used. However, you cannot finish creating the widget if the URL is blank, groupby is not set for a chart widget, or the defined custom variable has not been used.
h. Todo List
This tab is only available if you are creating a To Do List widget.
Select the tab to show when the widget is first displayed. The default is Configuration.
i. Chart Options
This tab is only available if you are creating a chart widget.
1. If you are creating a chart that groups data by a date or YMD property:
a. Use Aggregate by to group the data by day, month, or year.
b. If you have selected to aggregate the data, use Line Type to add a line to your chart. Choose from:
Cumulative Line to add a line to your chart showing the total number of objects in each group.
Combined Line to add a line to your chart showing the total number of objects.
2. Select the type of chart to use when the widget is first displayed. The available chart types depend on the options selected:
Chart option selected
Chart types available
Aggregate by
Line and Bar
Line Type set to Cumulative Line
Bar
Line Type set to Combined Line
Line and Bar
None
Doughnut, Line, and Bar
 Unless Line Type is set to Cumulative Line, the user can switch between the available chart types when they are viewing the widget.
j. Drill Down URL
This tab is only available if you are creating a chart widget.
To configure the chart widget so that clicking on a section of the chart displays the related data in a grid in a second page, select Configure a drill down URL.
 You can click 
 to use a wizard to modify the URL. For more information on the wizard, see Complete the URL using a wizard.
 Additional tabs may be displayed for some widget types. These tabs will expose fields based on the related client API. For more information about client APIs and their arguments, see Working with Client APIs.
2. On the Summary tab, check the details selected in the widget.
 To update a setting, click on the applicable tab and update the setting, as necessary.
3. Click Finish to save the widget.
Complete the URL using a wizard
The URL Builder wizard allows you to complete the URL for a chart, count, form, grid, or spotlight widget, without having detailed knowledge of the syntax required. As you make selections in the wizard, you can see the URL being
completed at the bottom of the form. A warning is shown if there are any issues. You must have permission to access the data that you want to configure in the wizard.
 If you are configuring a drill down URL for a chart, only the Property Selection and Choose how the results are ordered sections can be modified.
 UOM fields are not currently supported by the wizard. You can manually add UOM fields to the URL once you have completed the wizard.
1. In Entity, select the type of data you want the URL to return.
2. To return data related to a specific object, use Single Entity to select the object. This can be your variable, the widget on the dashboard to select the data from, or the current object.
 Only widgets that do not already depend on another widget can be selected. Currently, you cannot select a chart widget.
 If available for the type of data selected, you can also select a navigation property.
3. If you are creating a chart widget, select how you want to group the data in the chart. For each required property, click 
 and drag it to the Selected Properties list.
 You can change the order in which the grouping is applied by clicking 
 and dragging the selected properties up and down.
4. If you want to configure the URL further, click 
 and use the buttons to configure the URL as described below. You can:
Select the properties to include
1. Click Select specific properties 
.
2. For each property that you want to display in the widget, click 
 and drag it to the Selected Properties list.
 You can change the order in which the selected properties are displayed by clicking 
 and dragging them up and down.
Select the properties to order the data displayed
1. Click Order the results 
.
2. Click 
 and drag the properties that you want to use to order your results to the Selected Properties list.
3. Click 
 or 
 to order the property in either ascending or descending order.
 You can change the order in which the selected properties are used by clicking 
 and dragging them up and down.
Create filters
1. Click Configure a filter 
 to display the filter options.
2. Complete the boxes for the first property in this filter.
 You can also select to use your variable, a widget on the dashboard, or the current object as the value of the filter.
3. Click 
 to add additional properties to the filter. You can select:
Add a filter 
 to add another property to the filter.
Add a filter group 
 to add a filter group to the filter. This adds properties to the filter within brackets.
4. Select whether to make this an AND or OR filter, and complete the boxes for the new property.
5. Continue to add properties to the filter as required.
Set the top and skip values
Click Select top and skip 
, and set the values for each.
 For dashboards, top is used to limit the number of items displayed in a page, rather than limiting the total number of items returned.
 You can collapse and expand each section within the URL as required.
5. Click Save when you have finished configuring the URL.
 If the URL shows a warning, you can still complete the wizard. This is because the error may be caused by permissions issues, which will not affect the widget when it is used. However, you cannot complete the wizard if the URL is
blank, if there is no groupby set for a chart widget, or if the defined custom variable has not been used.
Make a widget dependent on another widget
When designing your dashboard, you may want to include a widget that displays information related to an object selected in another grid or count widget in the dashboard. For example, the widget TagList displays a list of tags and, when a
tag in that list is selected, the widget CRList updates to display any ongoing change requests related to the selected tag.
To do this, when creating the widget that depends on another widget, use a variable called WIDGET_<WIDGET NAME>_<PROPERTY TO RETURN> in the URL field to represent the object selected in another widget. For example, the
variable WIDGET_TAGLIST_ID is the Id of the selected object in the widget with the name TagList.
For example, to configure the widget CRList as described above, type the following in the URL field:
Tags({{WIDGET_TAGLIST_ID}})/SDATagsChangeRequest?$filter=Status eq 'IN PROGRESS' or
              
              Status eq 'OPEN' or Status eq 'APPROVED'&$select=Name,Description,Status
 To create a widget that displays the related data from a section of a chart widget, you can use the Drill down URL in the URL field.
When a widget is dependent on another widget, 
 is displayed at the bottom of the widget in the dashboard.
Variables should always use upper case.
Supported URL parameters
Dashboards use the Open Data Protocol (OData) and Web API v2 to access data from other systems.
Only GET requests used with filter, select, count, top, skip, orderby, and groupby parameters are supported when defining the URL to return the data. Any supported keyword can be used when using a variable to build the URL.
For dashboards, top is used to limit the number of items displayed in a page, rather than limiting the total number of items returned.
The groupby parameter can be used with chart widgets only.
For example:
When the current object is a tag, the following returns the name and description of the tag:
Tags({{CURRENT_OBJECT_ID}})?$select=Name,Description
The following returns the name and description of all tags where the name starts with 230-AF:
Tags?$select=Name,Description&$filter=startswith(Name,'230-AF')
For a chart widget, the following returns all tags grouped by status, and a count of the number of tags in each group:
Tags?$apply=groupby((Tag_Status),aggregate(Id with countdistinct as Count))
 For more information on OData, see Using OData in Web API v1 or v2.
Check the status of a widget
When viewing the dashboard, either from the home screen dashboard or from the Actions 
 menu of the related object, select 
 to see the status of a widget.
If any of the widgets do not display correctly, possible solutions can be found in InsightView dashboard issues.
You can also preview a widget when creating or updating a dashboard. To do this, hover over the widget and select 
.
The status details returned depend on the type of widget, and can include the entries shown in the following table.
Name
Displays
API
The Web API used to access the data.
Timer
The time taken to return the results.
Records
The number of items returned for the widget.
Status
Whether or not the widget successfully returned data.
Query
The selected scope for the widget.
Query by selected configurations only
Whether this option is set to restrict the scope to just that selected. For more information, see Query by selected configurations only.
Error
An explanation for the error, if one occurred.
Information on why the widget may not display its contents correctly can be found in the troubleshooting topic Widget content is not
displayed.
 indicates that an error has occurred.
A user name and password might be required to log on to an external web page in an iframe widget. If the widget contents don't display, and you aren't prompted for these details, select 
 to log on to the external web page in a new
window.
Select 
 to expand the widget. Select 
 to return it to the original size.
You can also double-click the widget title to expand and collapse it. On an Apple iPad, you can double-tap to expand or collapse the widget and to zoom in.
Select 
 to reload the data in the widget.
Update a dashboard
1. In the Dashboards feature set, select Dashboards, and search for the dashboard to update.
2. Select Actions 
 > Update.
3. Select 
 > Configure Page to modify the dashboard title, description, access control, whether or not the dashboard is published for other users to see, and how it is displayed.
 If a dashboard is created as either a home screen or a context panel dashboard, this cannot be changed. However, a dashboard that opens from an object's Action 
 menu can be changed between displaying as its own
page and displaying in a pop-up window.
 In the Access Control section, click 
 to display only the selected Interfaces or Access Groups. Interfaces are not applicable to home screen dashboards.
4. Update the widgets on the dashboard:
Select 
 to open the Configure Widget wizard, and update the details of the widget as described in Create a widget.
Hover over the widget, and select 
 to remove the widget from the dashboard and delete it from the system.
Hover over the widget, and select 
 to unrelate the widget from the dashboard. The widget will still be available for use on other dashboards.
You can also update a widget when viewing widgets in the results grid by selecting Actions 
 > Update.
You can select 
 > Preview Page to see a preview of the dashboard. When previewing a dashboard, it is displayed as view-only, and you can't interact with any of the widgets. Widgets aren't previewed if they rely on an
object to be selected first.
5. If you remove a widget from the dashboard, click 
, and either:
Create a widget or
Select an existing widget to display.
View the widget logs
When a widget requests data, any performance issues will cause the widget to be disabled in the dashboard the next time the dashboard is loaded. The widget will remain disabled, until manually enabled by the user. However, if it
continues to have issues, then it will be disabled again when the dashboard is next loaded. This ensures that slow running widgets do not impact the performance of the system.
Details of any errors and performance issues are recorded in widget logs. There are two types of log:
Error - This logs when a widget does not load correctly for whatever reason.
Performance - This logs when a widget request returned more than the allowed number of results or ran for longer than the allowed number of seconds. These issues cause the widget to be disabled the next time the dashboard is
loaded.
For each widget, the log records the user, the dashboard, and details of what caused the log to be created. You can configure the widget logs in the system options. For more information, see Configure the system options.
To view the widget logs, search for the required widget, and select Actions 
 > Show Widget Logs.
 Widget logs are automatically cleared by the system after a set number of days. By default, error logs are removed after 14 days, and performance logs after 7 days.
InsightView dashboard issues
Issues when configuring an InsightView dashboard
The following problems can occur when you configure an InsightView dashboard.
My tablet keyboard is preventing me from creating a widget
Problem
When using Chrome on a small tablet, the displayed keyboard prevents you from viewing the page to create a widget.
Common cause
When the keyboard is displayed, it reduces the amount of space available to display the page used to create the widget.
Solution
Change your keyboard to be a floating keyboard so that it is displayed on top of the page instead of reducing the amount of space available.
The dashboard is currently unavailable
Problem
When trying to update a dashboard, the following message is displayed:
This dashboard is currently unavailable. The method related to this dashboard has been deleted. Delete this dashboard and create a new one.
 Users will also see the following message when trying to view the same dashboard:
This dashboard is currently unavailable. Contact your administrator.
Reason
The method related to the dashboard has been deleted.
Solution
To resolve this issue, the dashboard must be deleted and recreated.
Issues when displaying an InsightView dashboard
The following problems can occur when you display an InsightView dashboard.
Cannot embed a website in an iframe
Problem
You cannot embed a website in HxGN SDx2 as it is being prevented from being embedded in an iframe.
Reason
