Some websites use specific HTTP headers, such as X-Frame-options and Content Security Policy (CSP), to prevent the website from being embedded in an iframe. These headers are important, as they are used to stop the website from
an outside attack called click-jacking. For example, the website www.google.com prevents it from being embedded in an iframe.
Solution
Some applications will not allow a report to be embedded in an iframe by default, such as Power BI. However, the Power BI application and others offer an option allowing you to generate a link, which can be used to embed the content in
an iframe. If you have access to modify the HTTP headers of a website, you can embed the website in an iframe for a specific domain.
 If you modify the website so it can be embedded in an iframe for multiple domains, it will not be as secure.
Site is shown as Not Secure
Problem
When accessing an InsightView containing an iframe widget, your site is shown as Not Secure. The Not Secure banner remains when you leave the page.
Common cause
A site displayed in an iframe widget has an invalid certificate.
Solution
Your Web Client site remains secure even when the banner is displayed. Restart your browser to remove the Not Secure banner.
The Actions menu isn't available for items in a grid or count widget
Problem
When displaying a dashboard, the results shown in a grid or count widget do not have an Actions menu.
Common causes
The dashboard is shown in a pop-up window.
The widget is expanded.
The widget is not returning the Name, Config, and Id columns.
The widget is displaying results from an external system.
 The object icons are also not displayed if the Name, Config, and Id columns are not returned, or if the results are from an external system.
Solutions
If the dashboard is displayed in a pop-up window, ask your administrator to change it to a full page display.
If the widget is expanded, return it to its original size.
Ask your administrator to check the widget is returning the Name, Config, and Id columns. The Id column is not displayed by default, even when it is returned.
 The Actions menu is applicable only to local systems.
The dashboard is slow to load on my iPad
Problem
When using Safari to access HxGN SDx2, InsightView dashboards are very slow to load data.
Common cause
By default, Safari shows websites as if on a desktop computer. Disabling this setting improves the performance of the dashboard.
Solution
In your Safari settings, select Request Desktop Website in the Settings for Websites section, and disable the All Websites toggle.
Widget content is not displayed
Problem
When accessing an InsightView, a widget is empty or displays an error.
For information on specific errors, see When viewing a widget, the error "Resource not found for the segment <Name>" is returned.
Common causes
1. A user name and password are required to log on to the external web page in an iframe widget.
2. The widget is linked to another widget which is in a different scope.
3. The widget is disabled as, the last time the dashboard was displayed, the widget request either took too long to return the results, or there were too many results returned.
Solution 1
Click 
 to see the status of a widget, and then click 
 to log on to the external web page in a new window.
Solution 2
If a widget is linked to another widget (indicated by 
), it will only display results if the two widgets are showing the same scope.
Solution 3
Manually enable the widget by clicking 
. However, when the dashboard reloads, the widget will be disabled again if it continues to run slowly or return too many results. Ask your administrator to look into the issue if this happens.
When viewing a widget, the error "Resource not found for the segment <Name>" is returned
Problem
When accessing an InsightView, the contents of the widget are not displayed. The error Failed to parse the OData url: 'Resource not found for the segment <Name>.' is returned.
Common cause
You do not have the necessary permissions to view the widget content.
Solution
Discuss with your administrator whether or not you should be able to view the widget content. Your administrator may then assign you the necessary permissions.
Configure launch URLs
You can create URLs in HxGN SDx2 that are composed to consistently open a path to pre-determined views of objects and documents. For example, you can create a URL to open a specific object or set of objects in the results grid.
When a URL to a specific object is launched, the system determines if you need to change scope to access the object depending on your access permissions.
If you have the necessary role and scope permissions set, you can then make an informed choice to switch scopes to the correct query or create scope so you can access the objects. The URL navigates straight to the selected objects.
You can launch the URL within different parts of HxGN SDx2 to view objects and documents, such as:
Results Grid
Info Map
Details
View Item in Drawing and View Item in Model
Any method supported in the Web Client
Visual Intelligence
 If you create URLs based on workflow steps, the steps need to be referenced by OBID and not by the name. This ensures that the workflow steps are correctly identified.
Launch URL examples
You can use the following syntax examples to compose a URL to launch your specified objects directly in different areas of HxGN SDx2. You launch a URL using the launch path created with your parameters. You combine the various
parameters depending on the view being launched and the object(s) being identified. The key parameters you can use to compose the URL are:
config - the UID of the Plant or Project in which the requested object exists.
methodName - the name of the method that the URL will invoke, such as ViewNavigateFiles.
objectName - the name of the requested object.
objectObid - the OBID of the requested object.
objectUid - the UID of the requested object.
setName - the set name derived from the appropriate API entry point.
 The setName parameter can be found in the Set Name column of an API entry point displayed in a results grid.
data - an optional parameter to expand a method.
The setName parameter is required if you use the objectName parameter instead of the objectObid parameter.
Parameters that contain special characters must be encoded before being added to the URL, such as # must be encoded to %23. This is because some characters are used in URL routing, and if put in the name, they will break the
URL. For more information on encoding, see https://www.tutorialspoint.com/html/html_url_encoding.htm <https://www.tutorialspoint.com/html/html_url_encoding.htm> .
If any parameter contains the %, *, or ? special characters, they act as wildcards and must be escaped before being encoded and added to the URL. For example, % must be escaped to \%. This is then encoded to %5C%25.
However, encoding being escaped is not always guaranteed to work.
Replacing or removing the Name column item can cause instability. You can change the display name for the Name column item by changing the Display as field in the Manage Column Items screen. The launch URL objectName
requires the Name column item.
The following syntax are examples only and show how you can compose a URL to return the specified objects:
Using objectName to launch a document in the results grid
Where the syntax uses the results, config, setName, and objectName.
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/results;config=[UID];setName=[setname];objectName=[objectname]
Using objectName to launch a document in the Info Map
Where the syntax uses infomap, config, objectName, and setName..
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/infomap;config=[UID];objectName=[objectname];setName=[setname]
Using objectName to launch an object details
Where the syntax uses details, config, objectName, and setName.
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/details;config=[UID];objectName=[objectname];setName=[setname]
Using objectObid to launch a document in results grid
Where the syntax uses results, config, and objectObid.
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/results;config=[UID];setName=[setname];objectObid=[objectOBID]
Using objectObid to launch an object in the Info Map
Where the syntax uses infomap, config, and objectObid.
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/infomap;config=[UID];objectObid=[objectOBID]
Using objectObid to launch an object details
Where the syntax uses details, config, and objectObid.
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/details;config=[UID];objectObid=[objectOBID]
Using objectUid to launch a document in the Results grid
 The objectUid must be combined with the setName parameter in order to work in the Results grid. This is the same as the objectName URLs.
Where the syntax uses results, config, setName, and objectUid.
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/results;config=[UID];setName=[setname];objectUid=[objectUID]
 UIDs that contain special characters must be encoded before being put in the URL, such as # must be encoded to %23. This is because some characters are used in URL routing and will break the URL if put in UID or name.
Using methodName to launch a document
There may be times when you need to launch a view controlled by a method to invoke the URL path. When launching a method, you combine the use of launch with the run parameter.
When the method needs to point to an object context, such as DocumentCopy, it must be provided using the following arguments:
config - the UID of the Plant or Project in which the requested object exists
methodName - the name of the method that the URL will invoke, for example, ViewNavigateFiles
objectName, objectUid, objectObid - the object can be identified by its Name or UID if the OBID is not known
setName - needed to ensure data of the correct type is retrieved when identifying by Name or UID
For example:
When launching a method to view a file on a document, you can combine the use of launch and run parameters to invoke the path along with the config, methodName, objectName, and SetName arguments:
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/run;config=[UID];methodName=[methodname];objectName=[objectname];setName=[setname]
For example:
When launching a method that does not need object context, such as Create or To Do List, you combine the use of launch and run parameters to invoke the path along with the config and methodName arguments. Therefore, to launch a
method to the To Do List:
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/run;config=[UID];methodName=WFInBox
Using data to view one or more objects in a document or file
When you need to view one or more objects in a model or drawing using the ViewNavigateFiles or FileViewNavigate methods, you must provide one of the following arguments:
ITEMSBYUID - the UID of the object
For example:
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/run;config=[UID];objectObid=[objectOBID];methodName=[methodname];data={"ITEMSBYUID":["UID1", "UID2"]}
ITEMSBYOBID - the OBID of the object
For example:
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/run;config=[UID];objectObid=[objectOBID];methodName=[methodname];data={"ITEMSBYOBID":["OBID1", "OBID2"]}
ITEMSBYNAME - the name of the object
For example:
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/run;config=[UID];objectObid=[objectOBID];methodName=[methodname];data={"ITEMSBYNAME":["Name1", "N1*"]}
Use a comma separated list for multiple values. Brackets are used to enclose multiple items to view one or more objects in a model or drawing.
You can use wildcard characters for the ITEMSBYNAME argument. The % wildcard character must be encoded as %25.
You must use JSON escape characters when using special characters for the ITEMSBYNAME argument.
The launch URL functionality is available only for data shared with HxGN SDx2 using integration or data-capture and requires both tags and representation object relationships for the model or drawing.
Using data to launch a visual intelligence report
When you need to launch a specified visual intelligence report using the ViewNavigateFiles method, you must provide the following arguments:
config - the UID of the Plant or Project in which the requested object exists
methodName - the name of the method that the URL will invoke, such as ViewNavigateFiles
objectName, objectUid, or objectObid - the object can be identified by its Name or UID if the OBID is not known
setName - needed to ensure data of the correct type is retrieved when identifying by Name or UID
data - the JSON parameters used to execute a Visual Intelligence report (VIR)
For example:
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/run;config=[UID];objectName=[name];setName=[setname]
;methodName=ViewNavigateFiles;data="VIR":["OBID"]
For example:
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/run;config=[UID];objectName=[name];setName=[setname]
;methodName=ViewNavigateFiles;data={"VIR":["OBID"]}
For example:
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/run;config=[UID];objectName=[name];setName=[setname]
;methodName=ViewNavigateFiles;data=%7B%22VIR%22:[%22OBID%22]%7D
Using data to launch a visual intelligence search
When you need to launch a specified visual intelligence search using the ViewNavigateFiles method, you must provide the following arguments:
config - the UID of the Plant or Project in which the requested object exists
objectName, objectUid, or objectObid - the object can be identified by its Name or UID if the OBID is not known
setName - needed to ensure data of the correct type is retrieved when identifying by Name or UID
methodName - the name of the method that the URL will invoke
data - the JSON parameters used to execute a Visual Intelligence search (VIS)
For example:
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/run;config=[UID];objectName=[name]
;setName=[setname];methodName=ViewNavigateFiles;data="VIS":["searchterm1","searchterm2","searchterm3"]
For example:
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/run;config=[UID];objectName=[name]
;setName=[setname];methodName=ViewNavigateFiles;data={"VIS":["searchterm1","searchterm2","searchterm3"]}
For example:
https://[localhost]/[webclient]/#/launch/run;config=[UID];objectName=[name]
;setName=[setname];methodName=ViewNavigateFiles;data=%7B%22VIS%22:[%22searchterm1%22,%22searchterm2%22,%22searchterm3%22]%7D
 The % wildcard character must be encoded as %25.
Configure file types
The out of the box actions are described in Working with documents. You can configure additional actions, for example, to create new major or minor revisions on revise.
 If you have created your own custom document classes, you will need to configure the new methods to work with your documents and their interfaces.
Configure Save Target As action to filter file types
You can configure the Save Target As action to filter the file types attached to a document so you can choose which ones the users can download. For example, you may have multiple file types attached to a document but you want to
restrict users to only view and download the PDF or Word files.
1. Find the FileDocRevCopyToLocal method.
2. Select Actions 
 > Update.
3. In the Argument 5 (FileType Filter) box, type the file type extensions in a comma-separated list. For example: pdf,doc.
If this argument is left blank, all file types can be seen and are available for download.
Only files attached to the document that match the file type filter list can be viewed. If no files match the file type filter list, a no files found message is displayed.
If a document has two file types attached but only one file type can be viewed, the download automatically proceeds for that file type.
Configure object actions to restrict file types
You can filter out any specific files so that new objects are created only with the wanted files when you use object actions. You can do this by specifying the UID of the unwanted file type in the RestrictedFileTypeUIDs parameter in the
EditFiles, Checkout, ReplaceFiles, and Revise client APIs. Any methods using these client APIs will restrict the action from attaching the file types to the new object. For more information on each specific client API, see Client APIs.
For example, you can ensure any outdated .vecz or .binz files attached to the parent file are not downloaded or copied during the Checkout or Revise operations. When you use the Replace Files methods, it will not display the .vecz or
.binz files in the attached files section.
Configure the display of missing and duplicate reference files
You can configure the display of missing and duplicate reference files information when you perform the following actions in HxGN SDx2:
Create a document
Update a document
Check-in a document
Revise a document
Replace file for a document
Enable or Disable the display of missing or duplicate reference files information in Reference Details window
1. Find the method to configure the client API document action.
2. Select Actions 
 > Update.
3. In the Method details section, update the parameter by assigning the required value.
Based on the type of document action, you can configure as per the details given in the table below:
Document Action
Client API
Method to update
Parameter to update
Default Value
Create
CreateClassObj
CreateDocument
Argument 6 (File Attachment Configuration)
PROMPT
Update
ManageFiles
DocManageFiles
Argument 2 (Reference file not found action)
PROMPT
Check-in
CheckIn
VersionCheckIn
Argument 2 (Reference file not found action)
PROMPT
Revise
Revise
RevisionRevise
Argument 4 (File Attachment Configuration)
PROMPT
Replace
ReplaceFiles
ReplaceWorkingDocFiles
Argument 2 (File configuration)
PROMPT
UpRevCurrentDocReplacesFilesAndSignoff
Client API parameter values
You can assign any of the following values to update the client API method parameters, as required:
Parameter value
Purpose
PROMPT
Display both missing and duplicate reference files in the Reference Files Details
window and continue with the file attachment process.
CONTINUE
Continue with the file attachment process without displaying the missing reference
files information.
ABORT
Abort the file attachment process if software detects that reference files are
missing.
For more information about the client APIs and their parameters, see Client APIs.
Configure PDF generation with custom rendition options
 This feature is available only to the System Administration and Business Administrator roles. The customized rendition options cannot be applied to Hexagon file formats.
In HxGN SDx2, you can customize the PDF rendition settings for supported file formats to generate a PDF. The customized rendition settings are applied to documents or file types when you generate their PDF renditions.
To do so, you must complete the following steps:
1. In the Business Administration feature set, select Create Rendition Options.
2. On the New Rendition Options form, do the following:
a. In the Main details section, enter the Name for the rendition option.
 You can enter the Description for the rendition option.
b. In the CAD options section, select the visual details as required.
Show CAD options
Parameter
Description
Color mode
Select to generate the PDF in Color, Grayscale, or Monochrome.
Layouts to include
Select the layouts to include in the PDF from the following options:
Only Model
Only Layouts
Only First Layout
All
Layers to configure
Select the CAD file layers to be visible in the PDF using the following options:
All Layers: Include all the layers in the PDF rendition, regardless of being turned on or off in the CAD file.
Visible Layers: Include the layers that are turned on in the CAD file.
None: Exclude all the layers, even if they are turned on.
Allow thin lines
Select to export all elements (such as lines and text) with line weight as zero. If this option is not selected, the actual
width of the elements is exported.
If you select this option, the lines appear as shown in the example below:
If you do not select this option, the lines appear as shown in the example below:
Page auto-rotation
Select to set the page orientation (landscape or portrait) for the drawing content automatically.
 This option is applicable only when a page size is specified.
If you select this option for a page size of 297 mm x 420 mm (A4 portrait), the drawing content appears as shown in
the example below.
If you do not select this option for a page size of 297 mm x 420 mm (A4 portrait), the drawing content appears as
shown in the example below.
c. In the Excel options section, select Apply page breaks to insert page breaks in the Excel file.
d. In the General options section, select the necessary property details and enter the values wherever required.
Show General options
Parameter
Description
Color mode
Select to generate the PDF in Color, Grayscale, or Monochrome.
Allow thin lines
Select to show thin lines when generating the PDF.
Markups as comments
Select to use markups as comments. If this parameter is not selected, the markups are embedded in the PDF content.
PDF/A compliant
Select to validate the rendered PDF document for PDF/A compliance, and convert it to PDF/A format.
Width and Height
Enter the width and height for the PDF.
e. In the Use section, select the condition for the automatic configuration of the rendition option when creating a new document. For more information, see Configure conditions.
 The condition applies to new documents.
f. In the Related Items section, search and select a document or file type for which the PDF needs to be generated. For example, you can enter * to view the list of supported documents or file types, as shown in the image given
below.
Show the Related Items section
The rendition option related to the document will be applied when generating PDF renditions. If a rendition option is not related to the document, the rendition option related to the file type will be applied. If there are no
rendition options related to the document or the file type, the default rendition option will be applied.
By default, Default File Type is selected as the file type for PDF generation. This setting will be automatically chosen if no file type is selected or if the selected file type does not have a file type to PDF relationship.
g. In the Attach Plot Style File (.tbl/.ctb) section, attach the relevant TBL and CTB files to enhance the appearance of certain visual elements such as the colors and lines in the PDF rendition of CAD files.
 You can attach:
CTB files to DWG and DXF files.
TBL files to DGN files.
3. Select FINISH.
View rendition options
In the Business Administration feature set, select Rendition Options to display the Rendition Options Results page.
Update rendition options
You can update a rendition option by performing the following steps:
For documents with an existing PDF rendition:
1. In the Rendition Options Results page, select the rendition option from the list, and then select Actions 
 > Update.
2. In the Update page, update the rendition options as required.
3. Select UPDATE.
4. In the Re-generate Renditions dialog:
a. Select YES to regenerate the PDF rendition of the attached document using the latest rendition options.
b. Select NO to use the existing PDF rendition of the attached document.
For documents without an existing PDF rendition:
1. In the Rendition Options Results page, select the rendition option from the list, and then select Actions 
 > Update.
2. In the Update page, update the rendition options as required.
3. Select UPDATE.
4. In the Re-generate Renditions dialog:
a. Select YES to generate a PDF rendition of the attached document with the latest rendition options.
b. Select NO to generate a PDF rendition of the attached document with the latest rendition options (same as the step above).
 The latest rendition options are displayed irrespective of the option selected in the Re-generate Renditions dialog.
Configuring custom file types
You can configure custom file types that are preconfigured for PDF generation by using the Native file extension parameter and assigning custom file extensions to the newly created file types. For example, .dgn is the native file
extension for isometric file types with extensions .i01, .i02, and so on. Similarly, .tif is the native file extension for file types with extensions .t01, .t02, and so on.
You can configure custom file types by performing the following steps:
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Create File Type to open the Create New File Type form.
2. In the Main details section, enter the Name and Description.
3. In the File type details section, select the Viewable checkbox and enter the File extension and Native file extension.
 You can only enter file types supported by the View and Markup Control as values for the SPFNativeFileExtension property.
Configure markup renditions
What file types require a markup rendition?
Markup renditions (PDF) are required to mark up or compare the following file formats:
File format
Native application
File extension
CAD
AutoCAD Drawing
.dwg

.dxf
MicroStation Design File
.dgn
Hexagon 2D Drawings
SmartSketch
.igr
.sma
Smart P&ID
.pid
Smart Orthographic Drawings
.sha
Smart Electrical
.spe
Aspen Basic Engineering
.zyq
Hexagon 3D Models
3D WebGL Rendition
.binz
Smart Review
.vue
Smart 3D
.zvf
Images
Joint Photographic Experts Group
.jpg
Portable Network Graphic

The markup rendition is generated when:
The file is attached to a document.
A user attempts to compare or mark up a file that does not have a markup rendition. In this case, the user sees the following messages: "Unable to retrieve the markup rendition" and "Created scheduled task to create markup
rendition." For more information, see Unable to retrieve the markup rendition.
Confirm the following to ensure that markup renditions are automatically generated:
The Auto-generate Markup Rendition flag on the SPFFileTypeFileType relationship definition between the file type objects and the markup rendition file type object is required.
The MainScheduler must be set to Start.
Configure locking and unlocking of markup layers
To filter locking and unlocking of markup layers by owning group, you must:
1. Set the condition on the LockMarkupFile method to IsMarkupUnlockedAndUserInOwningGroup (it is set to IsMarkupLocked by default).
2. Create and set the following condition on the UnlockMarkupFile method:
Name - IsMarkupLockedAndUserInOwningGroup
Description - Markup Locked
Condition definition - Obj.SPFIsMarkupLocked=True and (Count(Obj->SPFItemOwningGroup_12)=0 or (instr (Env.OwningGroupsForUserInQueryConfig, Obj->SPFItemOwningGroup_12.Name) > 0))
Configure the view and markup control
A view and markup control is used for viewing, marking up, and comparing certain file formats in your system. The same view control is also responsible for generating PDFs and markup renditions of files. You must configure the following
items to start using the view and markup control.
What file types are supported in the view and markup control?
The following file types are supported in this view control:
File format
Native application
File extension
Image
JPEG File Interchange
.jpg
Microsoft Windows Bitmap
.bmp
Portable Network Graphics
.png
Tagged Image File
.tiff
.tif

.pptx

Text

Grant permission to print and download
You can grant permission to specific users or groups to print or download files in the View and Markup Control by configuring the access to the AllowMarkupPrint and AllowMarkupDownload methods. For more information, see Manage
methods for an access group or Manage the access groups for a method.
To grant permission, you must complete the following steps:
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Methods.
2. Search for the AllowMarkupPrint method (for printing) or the AllowMarkupDownload method (for downloading).
3. Select Actions 
 > Manage Access Groups... for each method.
4. In the Manage Access Groups... dialog box, relate the relevant access groups to the method.
Configure custom UI assets
You can introduce custom UI assets to visually customize the display of your graphical objects in HxGN SDx2 with their own custom UI asset.
Each custom UI asset must be:
Created using a graphic design tool, such as Adobe Illustrator.
Saved in SVG file format, which allows the UI asset to be proportionately scaled by the software to the required size.
These custom UI assets can be added as file attachments when you use the standard create and update actions for classified objects or when you update the file attachments for an existing classified object.
For example:
You can create two new widgets and relate each widget to a different class definition object so that you can view the objects with their own related UI icons displayed in each widget panel.
You can define a custom UI asset for an object so that any method displays the custom icon when added to the shortcut ribbon.
You cannot attach a new custom UI asset to an object if the object already has an existing UI asset of the same type.
You must delete an existing UI asset from a classified object in order to replace it with a new custom UI asset when updating an object.
The maximum number of custom UI assets you can attach to an object during file management is one.
An interface definition is created for each custom UI asset that represents the object, such as a symbol..
Retrieve custom UI assets
You can retrieve a list of the custom UI assets contained in a tenant directory by performing a GET request to return each specific asset type. Each viewer asset type is listed by their name, size, and last modification date and time.
For example, the following GET returns a list of the assets by type for the tenant named sdx2_tenant.
GET
<ACCESS URL>/sdx2_tenant/sdxservice/assets/stamps
<ACCESS URL>/sdx2_tenant/sdxservice/assets/symbols
Each GET returns the assets in a list as shown in the following example for symbols.
You can also retrieve a UI asset by performing a GET to return a single named UI asset contained in the tenant directory. For example:
<ACCESS URL>/sdx2_tenant/sdxservice/assets/symbols/Angle Solenoid2.svg
Bulk upload custom UI assets
You can bulk upload custom UI asset types for a tenant using Postman. The bulk upload service process can be used for all defined UI asset types and is a two stage process:
1. A call issued to BulkUIAssetCreate to upload the content describing the assets along with the files associated with the UI assets.
2. A call issued to GenerateUIAssets to generate the UI assets from the object instances created in the BulkUIAssetCreate call.
Each UI asset type is uploaded in a single ZIP file with a form-data payload containing an assets.json file with the UI asset types listed.
Bulk upload the assets
You can bulk upload the UI assets in the ZIP file using the following example post request:
POST
<ACCESS URL>/sdx2_tenant/sdxservice/api/v2/SDA/BulkUIAssetCreate
1. Enter the URL as above as the POST.
2. Set the Body content to form-data.
3. Set the Key name to File as the single value type.
4. Select the Zip file in the Value type field that contains the asset information. For example, assets.zip.
Generate the assets
After running the BulkUIAssetCreate request, you must perform the GenerateUIAssets request using the following post:
POST
<ACCESS URL>/sdx2_tenant/sdxservice/api/v2/SDA/GenerateUIAssets
 There is no Body content required for this request.
Contents of the Zip file
When creating the ZIP file, the contents describe the UI assets to be created and their related asset file types. For example, the file assets.zip contains the following files and folders:
assets.json
fonts
CA_Font-Bold.ttf
icons
icons\wrench.svg
Format of the assets.json file contents
The assets.json file describes the UI assets to be created. For example:
[
{
"Name": "ca_icon_bulk_1",
"Description": "A wrench icon",
"Type": "icon"
"Filename": "ca_font_bulk_1.ttf",
"AssetFile": "icons/wrench.svg"
}
{
"Name": "ca_icon_bulk_1",
"Type": "Font
"Filename": "ca_font_bulk_1.ttf",
"AssetFile": "CA_Font-Bold.ttf".
"Category": "Electrical"
}
]
 An example for the Category of the UI asset would be Electrical.
Format of the assets.json file
The assets.json file should be in the following example format:
{
"$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-04/schema#",
"type": "array",
"items": [
{
"type": "object",
"properties": {
"Name": {
"description": "The name of the asset"
"type": "string"
},
"Description": {
"description": "A description of the asset"
"type": "string"
},
"Type": {
"description": "The asset type"
"type": "string"
"string": [ "Icon", "Font", "Symbol", "Stamp", "ActionPin"]
},
"Filename": {
"description": "The filename used to access the asset"
"type": "string"
},
"AssetFile": {
"description": "The asset file contained in the Zip archive"
"type": "string"
},
"Category": {
"description": "The category of the asset"
"type": "string"
}
},
"required": [ "Name", "Type", "Filename", "AssetFile", "Category"]
]
}
]
}
The Category field is only mandatory when you are uploading Symbols, so they can all be in the same group.
An example of the UI asset file contained in the Zip archive would be icons/wrench.svg.
An example of the filename to be used to access the UI asset would be ca_icon_wrench.svg.
Bulk download custom UI assets
You can bulk download custom UI assets defined by the asset type using a GET request. Each UI asset type is downloaded in an individual ZIP file archive appended with the name of the UI asset type, so symbols are downloaded in a
symbols.zip.
 This functionality is not applicable to icons or fonts.
For example, the following GET requests return the UI assets for these specific types:
GET
<ACCESS URL>/sdx2_customer/sdxservice/assets/stamps/stamps.zip
<ACCESS URL>/sdx2_customer/sdxservice/assets/symbols/symbols.zip
Configuration level controlled assets
When configuration control is applied at a configuration level, you can include the corresponding configuration level assets in the ZIP download by specifying the correct configuration level header in the GET request, such as
SPFConfigUID or SPFQueryConfigUID.
For example:
GET
<ACCESS URL>/sdx2_tenant/sdxservice/assets/symbols/symbols.zip
SPFConfigUID: PL_PlantA
Category defined assets
When an UI asset is defined to a category, the asset is listed in a sub-directory of the ZIP archive. So an asset defined to the category New is downloaded in the sub-directory New of the downloaded ZIP archive.
Configure a UI icon
Delivered UI icons visually represent each asset type in the Home page, Results grid, Actions menu, hover menu, or the Info Map.
To create a new icon:
 A System Administration role is required to perform these steps.
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Create Icon.
2. Enter the name and description for the icon.
 Ensure the Enabled checkbox is selected to control the display of the UI icons.
3. On the next page, choose and attach the new icon file.
Click here to learn how to configure UI icons for custom objects
UI icons for a specific object type must be:
Created using a graphic design tool, such as Adobe® Illustrator®.
Named to match the class definition of the object type.
Saved in SVG file format, which allows the image to be scaled by the software to the required size.
 You cannot replace a delivered icon for an existing class definition.
UI icons are created at configuration top and are not configuration controlled so they can be applied to the objects in every configuration in a system.
If a UI icon shares the same name as a delivered icon, the delivered one will be used automatically.
To display UI icons in a feature set, see Create a feature set.
To display UI method icons on the Actions menu, see Display a method icon on the Actions menu.
Configure reports
Reports provide a quick way to view project or plant information, perform complex queries, and view information across a data structure. A few saved reports are delivered with HxGN SDx2.
What reports are delivered with the software?
The following saved reports are delivered with HxGN SDx2:
Class Definitions
Methods Report
Reports are related to the relevant feature sets. For example, a document revision report is available in the Documents feature set. Administrators who have access to reports can also view them from the Reports feature set. The
Reports feature set allows you to create new reports and configure them based on your requirements.
The Reports feature set is displayed by default with the following options:
Show Reports: It displays all the reports that are available in the database.
Create Report: It allows you to create a new report definition.
 These entry points are available for users who have a role related to the SystemAdmin access group. A system administrator can assign access to any role.
An overview and default layout of a report
The content and format of each report are configured using report definitions. You can save these report definitions as saved reports.
Report definitions determine the type of object that is searched for and the properties of those objects that are returned. For example, you can create a report definition to search for tags based on their properties and return not only
properties particular to the tags but also properties of related objects, such as the names of related units.
View definitions form the basis of reports, providing the definition of the structure to be queried and the data to be displayed.
Access groups provide access control to the reports.
Different formatting options control the output of the data. This can be in XHTML, CSV, CSV REPEATED, LISTVIEW, WORD (OPENXML), EXCEL (OPENXML), EXCEL EXTENDED (OPENXML), or EXCEL REPEATED (OPENXML)
format.
A few report templates are delivered with the core model, and additional templates can be configured.
Reports are based on view definitions, which are in turn based on graph definitions. The graph definition is a collection of edge definitions that represent a path through the data model.
The view definition sits on top of the graph definition and acts as a filter. Otherwise, a user will be overwhelmed with too many details when they request a report. When the user requests a report, the view definition picks the required
properties that the user wants to see from the objects found. Basically, reporting is simply a process of putting user-defined criteria into a graph definition via the view definition at the point of executing the report.
The following diagram shows the default layout of a report.
Create a report
Define criteria for a report
Configure a report to run as a scheduled task
Relate a report to a feature set
Existing reports
Learn more about report output formats
How do I access reports?
The Reports feature set allows you to create and update reports. You can access the Reports feature set if you have a role related to the SystemAdmin access group. A system administrator can assign access to any role as needed.
Your assigned access groups determine what options are available in the Reports feature set. If you have access to all the report options, you will see the following:
Create Report - Select to create a new report. For more information, see Create a report.
Show Reports - Select to view the list of reports and edit existing reports.
Find a report
You can view a list of all the reports available in the database by selecting Show Reports in the Reports feature set. This option is only visible if you have a role related to the SystemAdmin access group. For more information, see Find a
report.
Administrators can also relate reports to their appropriate feature sets. For example, a document revision report may be made available in the Documents feature set.
You can then access a report from the relevant feature set if one of your roles is associated with an access group that matches the access group assigned to the report. For example, if a report is created with the Engineer access group,
only users with roles associated with the Engineer access group can see the report.
Run a report
You can run a report by selecting it within the feature set it is related to. To access the report from the relevant feature set, one of your access groups must match the access group assigned to the report. For more information, see Run a
report.
You can also run a report by searching for it and selecting Actions 
 > Run Report. You must either be a system administrator or have roles associated with the SDABasicViewer and VIEWONLY access groups to access the Run
Report method. For more information on how to run a report, see Run a report to review the result set.
Edit a report
You can update the details of some options that were used when the report was created and add new properties by searching for it and selecting Actions 
 > Edit Report Definition. You must either be a system administrator or have
roles associated with the SDABasicViewer and VIEWONLY access groups to access the Edit Report Definition method. For more information on how to edit a report, see Edit a report.
Create a report
Follow the steps to create a new report:
1. In the Reports feature set, select Create Report.
2. In the Create Report wizard, enter the required values, and select the appropriate options as shown in each of the following tabs:
 You can select PREVIOUS to go back to review a previous tab or simply select a tab to review and amend the properties as required.
Report Details
1. In the Report Details tab, enter the name for the report in the Name box.
You can enter a description in the Description box.
You can hide a report from all the feature sets it is related to by selecting the Make Private checkbox. This option is useful when an administrator is updating a report configuration and wants to hide the report from its
users. However, the report will still be visible in the Show Reports entry point under the Reports feature set.
When you clear the Make Private checkbox, the report becomes visible to users under the respective feature sets.
2. In the Owner box, select an owner for the report. Otherwise, you will be the owner of the report by default.
3. In the Access Groups box, select one or more access groups that will have permission to view and execute the report. For more information on access groups, see Access group configuration.
View Definition
In the View Definition box, select the name of the view definition for the report from the list.
 You must select the view definition to be used as the basis for the report, which displays the properties you want to include in the report.
A few view definitions are delivered with HxGN SDx2. You can view them in the Data Model Administration feature set by selecting Data Model Administration > View Definitions.
If you need additional view definitions based on your system requirements, you can import them by using the Import Data option. For more information, see Import data.
A detailed sample file is available on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For information on how to download sample files from Smart Community, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
Report Builder
1. Select the type of report output style from the Report Output Style list and a template from the Report Template list.
You can export the report output to CSV, CSV REPEATED, EXCEL EXTENDED (OPENXML), EXCEL (OPENXML), EXCEL REPEATED (OPENXML), LISTVIEW, WORD (OPENXML), or XHTML format.
The Report Template option is displayed based on the report output style you selected.
2. In Properties, choose the appropriate options and enter the required values as shown below:
Select the checkboxes for the properties that you want to include in the report.
The list of properties available varies according to the view definition selected.
You can change the order in which the selected properties are displayed by selecting 
 and dragging it up and down.
The Caption column helps in configuring the headers for the report. Update the names in the Caption column as needed.
Set the sorting for each property as necessary.
You can set the report to sort properties in ascending or descending order by clicking in the Sort column.
You can also set a value for each property by entering a value in the Sort Order column so that properties are sorted in the order you have defined.
 You can also change the values using a spin box.
You can define search criteria for each property by selecting 
 in the Criteria column. For more information, see Define criteria for a report.
You can allow users to specify new search criteria for one or more properties each time the report is run. Find the row with the property on which you want to filter the results, and then turn on the toggle in the Prompt
column. Repeat this step for each property you want to filter on.
 You cannot define both criteria and a prompt on the same property of the report.
Select the following checkboxes as necessary:
Context object required
This indicates that the report can only be run from the Actions menu of the contextual object and will not appear in any feature sets. When you select the LISTVIEW output style, the report results are displayed in a
new tab. For the remaining output styles, the report results are executed in the background, and a notification is displayed.
When you select this checkbox, the Prompt column is not displayed.
This checkbox is not available if the report output style is WORD (OPENXML).
Include intermediate nodes
This includes all the intermediate nodes in an expansion are included from the top down. This includes the parent nodes even when any child items are missing or are excluded from the report criteria.
 This checkbox is not available if the report output style is WORD (OPENXML).
Terminated workflows report
This allows you to generate a report that extracts information on terminated workflows for a set of objects.
 This checkbox is not available if the selected view definition does not have any associated workflows or if the report output style is WORD (OPENXML).
When selected, this option searches for properties related to workflows and extracts information on both active and terminated workflows. By default, the report is generated based on the workflow name. If the
workflow name is not available in the selected properties, the report is generated based on the workflow description.
You can also define criteria in the Criteria column for the Termination Date property if you want the report to include only terminated workflows.
The Doc Revision Terminated Workflow Report view definition is delivered by default. This is visible only when you have the Document Control role. You can also configure the view definition based on your
requirements and load it into the system using the Import Data option. For more information on how to import files, see Import data.
Ignore Config
This allows you to run a report that extracts information from all the configurations (plants or projects) irrespective of access permissions and displays the result in a single file.
 This checkbox is only available when you select the CSV or CSV REPEATED report output style.
3. To download the physical files associated with objects:
a. In Downloads, select the Download Files checkbox.
b. In the Properties section, find a row that represents the files to be downloaded and turn on the toggle in the Download Files column.
When you run the report, the files are downloaded as a zip file.
When downloading the report, you will be prompted to enter or select a folder path to save the downloaded files based on your browser settings. For example, in Google Chrome, go to Settings > Downloads  and
turn on the Ask where to save each file before downloading option to enable the report to prompt you to select the folder path. Otherwise, the files are downloaded to the Downloads folder on the local computer.
Be aware that this option is not available if the report output style is LISTVIEW or WORD (OPENXML), or when you select the Ignore Config checkbox.
4. Select NEXT to go to the Relate tab or select SAVE & CLOSE to save and close the report.
 If the Context object required option is selected, you must not relate the report to any feature sets.
Relate
Use this page to relate the report to feature sets and Integration Data Filters (IDFs) so that the report is available in those objects.
1. Select 
 to relate the report to a feature set.
2. In the Select Feature Set list, select the feature set to relate to the report.
The report will appear as a tile in that feature set.
When the report is not related to any feature sets, it will be accessible from the search menu.
3. In the Group Name list, select the group name under which you want the report to appear.
4. In the Order Value list, select a value to specify the order in which each report appears in the selected group.
If you want to relate the report to multiple feature sets, add a new row by selecting 
.
You can also directly relate a report to a feature set from the Actions menu, but you cannot select the group name or order when following this method. For more information, see Relate a report to a feature set.
5. Select one or more Integration Data Filters to relate them to the report so that the report is available in those IDFs.
If the report is not related to a specific IDF, it will be available only in the Consolidated IDF and return consolidated data.
By relating a report to an IDF, you can filter report results based on IDFs. For more information on filtering data using IDFs, see Filter data by design component.
6. Select NEXT to go to the Summary tab or select SAVE & CLOSE to save and close the report.
Summary
1. The Summary tab displays all the selected configuration attributes you have selected for the report.
 You can go back and select each tab to review the properties selected for the report and amend the selections as required.
2. Select FINISH to create the report.
How do I use the filter options?
You can apply the below filters to refine the results:
1: Use this option to search for a particular object name or to refine the values. The search box uses the object's name as the default filter.
2: Use this option to toggle between the selected items and all items.
3: Select 
 to change how the filter works, and select 
 to choose the item the search includes, such as name, class definition, or property definition.
4: Indicates the number of items selected.
Define criteria for a report
You can filter the report result set by setting a criterion on the properties. Report criteria help in selecting a property value using which the report result set can be filtered. You can specify criteria for any number of properties.
1. In the Reports feature set, select Show Reports.
2. Search for the report, and select Actions 
 > Edit Report Definition.
3. On the Report Builder tab, in the Selected Properties column, find a property for which you want to define the criteria.
4. In the Criteria column, select 
 in the row that corresponds with the property to open the Define Criteria dialog.
5. In the Define Criteria dialog, use the Operator and Value boxes to define the criteria. The Criteria Output section displays the criteria you defined.
You can add rows for defining additional criteria by selecting 
.
You can remove unnecessary rows by selecting 
.
What are the operators I can use to modify the query?
The OPERATOR option allows you to specify search operators to refine your search to a specific set of objects. There are two sets of operators you can use, equals and like, depending on the property type selected.
If the property is a value in a list available by default or if you use the words Null or Blank as the value, only the equals operator is available. The operator automatically changes to equals when you enter Null or Blank.
You can use the wildcards to define search criteria for a string property that matches one or more characters using the like or equals operator.
The following table shows the operators for strings, doubles, integers, small integers, and select lists:
Parameter
Function
equals 
Includes items where this property value matches what you entered in the VALUE box, including wildcards.
not equal to 
Includes items where the value of this property does not match what you entered in the VALUE box.
less than 
Includes items where this property value is less than what you entered in the VALUE box.
greater than 
Includes items where this property value is greater than what you entered in the VALUE box.
less than or equal to 
Includes items where this property value is equal to or less than what you entered in the VALUE box.
greater than or equal to 
Includes items where this property value is equal to or greater than what you entered in the VALUE box.
like 
Includes items where this property value matches what you entered in the VALUE box, including wildcards.
 The behavior of the equals and like operators is identical. You can use either of these when you are defining the search criteria using wildcards.
An asterisk (*) matches zero or more characters in your search. For example, if you specify pa*, the software returns objects where that property value contains words that start with the characters pa, such as pan, park, part,
and so on.
You can use an underscore (_) or a question mark (?) to match a single character. For example, if you specify pi_ or pi?, the software returns pid, pit, and pip, but not pipe.
To search for objects where the property value includes an underscore, question mark, or asterisk character, enclose them in square brackets. For example, use 100[*] to return 100*, but not 100abc.
You can search for objects with a property that contains a single character in a specific range of characters by enclosing them in square brackets. For example, using [a-f] returns only objects where the value of the property
includes a to f in a set of characters. So, if you specify pi[a-p], the software returns objects where the property value is pid and pip, but not pit, as 't' is outside the range.
You can use a caret (^) character to search for objects where a property value does not include a character in a range or set. For example, if you specify pi[^a-p], the software returns objects where the property value is pit, but
not pid or pip, as 'd' and 'p' are in the range a-p.
6. If necessary, select either AND or OR, and define additional criteria.
You can define filter criteria for multiple properties on the same report.
You cannot define both criteria and a prompt on the same property of the report.
7. Select OK to return to the Report Builder tab. The Criteria column displays the criteria you defined in the appropriate row.
Configure a report to run as a scheduled task
You can allow users to choose between running a report immediately or at a specific time using the steps below:
1. In the Reports feature set, select Show Reports.
2. Select the report, and then select Actions 
 > Edit Report Definition.
3. In the Report Builder tab, select the Run in Background checkbox.
 When you select this option, ensure that the report is related to at least one feature set.
4. Select SAVE & CLOSE.
When you select the Run in Background checkbox, the Prompt and Context object required options become unavailable.
If the LISTVIEW output style is selected, the Run in Background checkbox is unavailable.
Users can choose whether the report is executed immediately or at a specific time when running the report. For more information, see Run a background report.
How can I check if a scheduled report was run successfully?
You can check the processed scheduled reports using the Scheduled Tasks entry point. Follow these steps after the scheduled date and time for the report to run have passed:
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks.
2. Search for the report on the list, and check the Status column.
 If the report is failed for any reason, you can run the report again by selecting Actions 
 > Task > Retry Task.
Configure a report to run as a recurring scheduled task
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks.
2. Select the report, and then select Actions 
 > Update.
3. In the Resubmission count box, enter the number of times you want the report to run. For example, enter 5 to run the report five times and then stop.
 Enter -1 to continue running the report until you cancel the scheduled task.
4. If necessary, edit the start date or time for when the report will start running.
5. In the Resubmission interval box, update the polling interval in milliseconds.
What is the polling interval?
The Scheduler polling interval is the regular time the database verifies if any tasks need to be gathered and processed. You can set the Schedulers to poll the server sites at specified intervals that you can configure when you create
or update a Scheduler. The polling interval default value can vary according to the system to eliminate excessive processing time. If the polling interval affects your system performance, you can set the Scheduler to poll at less
frequent intervals by increasing the polling value to help minimize any performance impact that polling is having on your system. Administrators can set the polling value for each individual Scheduler on the Update form.
The minimum polling interval you can set for a Scheduler is 10 minutes (600,000 ms). When a Scheduler is free and reaches the set polling interval, the maximum number of tasks that can be processed at the same time is
limited to two.
If the scheduler is polling at maximum capacity, any new tasks are not processed until the polling interval is reached and the Scheduler becomes free.
Schedulers and their default polling intervals
Schedulers
Default polling interval times
Main Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Report Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Upgrade Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
User Management Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Termination Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Job Cleanup Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Change Request Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Integration Scheduler
60 minutes (3,600,000 ms)
Export Task Scheduler
10 minutes (600,000 ms)
PBS Sync Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Sync Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Recovery Scheduler
60 minutes (3,600,000 ms)
Indexing Scheduler
10 minutes (600,000 ms)
Relate a report to a feature set
You can relate a report that you have created to any feature set from the Show Reports tile by following the procedure below:
1. In the Reports feature set, select Show Reports.
2. Select the report you want to relate to, and select Actions 
 > Manage Feature Sets.
3. In the Manage Feature Sets dialog, search for the feature set that you want and select Relate Item 
 to relate it to the report. For more information, see Manage related items.
Existing reports
You can update or edit an existing report from the Actions menu of the report.
Find a report
Run a report to review the result set
Update the access group of a report
Edit a report
Terminate a report
Find a report
1. In the Reports feature set, select Show Reports to find all the reports in the system.
2. Find the report, and use the Actions menu methods like Run Report, Edit report definition, and Terminate to test the result set, edit the existing report definition, and terminate the report, respectively.
Run a report to review the result set
1. Search for the report that you want to run and select Actions 
 > Run Report. A notification message appears.
 The Specify values for report dialog may appear depending the configured report result filters. It is not mandatory to provide filter criteria during the report generation. If you did not provide filter criteria, the report will
display all the data available in the database. For more information, see Run a report with a prompt.
 Reports with the LISTVIEW output style are displayed in a new window.
2. When the report has run successfully, download it from Notifications 
. For more information, see View notifications of reports.
 You can also view the status of the report in Notifications. When the report is running, the status is IN PROGRESS. When the report has run successfully, the status is COMPLETED.
 If a report is related to a feature set, you can run the report by selecting its feature set.
Update the access group of a report
 You must have a role related to the SystemAdmin access group to view this option. System administrators can assign this option to any access group to grant viewing permissions for associated roles.
1. In the Reports feature set, select Show Reports.
2. Select a report from the list, and select Actions 
 > Change Report Access.
3. In the Change Report Access dialog, select the required access group or groups from the list, and then select FINISH.
Edit a report
You can edit a report based on the access groups that are related to your role. HxGN SDx2 allows you to modify some of the options that were selected when creating the report and add new properties. Follow the following steps to update
the report:
1. In the Reports feature set, select Show Reports.
2. Select the report in the list, and select Actions 
 > Edit Report Definition.
3. In the Edit Report Definition wizard, make the necessary changes to the report. For more information, see Create a report.
The procedure for editing a report is almost the same as the procedure for creating a report.
The reports that are set to be contextual can also be edited from this entry point.
Terminate a report
 Terminating an object changes its status to Terminated without removing it from the database. Terminating objects instead of deleting them allows you to continue to see the history of the object after termination. You can view the
history of a terminated object by changing the effective date of the database to a time before the object was terminated.
 You must have access to the SystemAdmin access group to terminate a report.
1. Search for the report in the list, and select Actions 
 > Terminate.
2. Confirm that you want to terminate the report.
Learn more about report output formats
The following output formats are supported by reports in HxGN SDx2:
XHTML - The results are displayed in a new browser tab.
LISTVIEW - The results are displayed in a new window.
CSV - The results can be downloaded as a CSV formatted text file, with one row per data item. Use the CSV output style to output tabular data in a plain text form. A CSV report has records separated by a character or string, most
commonly using a comma or tab. A settings option allows you to select the CSV report format to include properties or UoM value delimiters, date, time, and encoder formats.
CSV REPEATED - The results can be downloaded as a CSV formatted text file with rows repeated for each primary and related object.
Use the CSV REPEATED output style to output tabular data that supports multiple related fields. If you have multiple relationships between two objects, each relationship will be on a separate row. For example, if a tag has two
disciplines and you use the CSV REPEATED output style, the tag will have two rows, one for each discipline. Using the existing CSV format only produces one line for the tag with both disciplines comma-separated.
EXCEL (OPENXML) - The results can be downloaded as an OpenXML Excel file. There is an option to use a template Excel file that contains a macro, which can be used to further manipulate the data as required. The macro must
be specified to run when the file is opened, as Open XML does not support the running of macros.
WORD (OPENXML) - The results can be downloaded as an OpenXML Word file. You can either use an existing template or make changes to an existing template and save it with a new name to create a new template.
EXCEL EXTENDED (OPENXML) - The results can be downloaded as a Microsoft Excel file. This style provides a more detailed report format than the EXCEL style. In this output style, the relationships to the first object are listed on
separate rows. There is an option to use the template Excel files that contain a macro, which can be used to further manipulate the data as required. Two templates are delivered by default. You can either use one of the existing
templates or create a new template based on your requirements.
The example below shows the output of the Method Access report. Displaying the two access groups in different rows clarifies the separate relationships between the Method and the Access Group.
EXCEL REPEATED (OPENXML) - This style displays the output in a more compact format compared to other Excel output styles. In this output style:
If an object has a one-to-one relationship with a second object, both objects are displayed in the same row of the spreadsheet. The second object is displayed as a property of the first object.
If the relationship is one-to-many, the related objects are displayed in separate rows.
Properties on relationships are also listed on the same row.
For example, in the table below, the Method, Client API, and Access Group are all listed in the same row. The relationship between the Method and the Client API is one-to-one, so they are displayed in the same row. However, the
relationship between the Method and the Access Group is one-to-many, so they are displayed in separate rows.
Depending on the number of one-to-one relationships, this output style can result in a much smaller Microsoft Excel file compared to other Excel output styles
Report output styles and templates
Output style
Template requirement
XHTML
A few templates are delivered with the software. You can either use any of them or create a new template based on your requirements.
However, this report style also works without a template.
LISTVIEW
No template is required.
CSV
No template is required.
CSV REPEATED
No template is required.
EXCEL (OPENXML)
Two templates are delivered with the software. You can either use any of them or create a new template based on your requirements.
However, this report style also works without a template.
EXCEL EXTENDED (OPENXML)
Two templates are delivered with the software. You can either use any of them or create a new template based on your requirements.
However, this report style also works without a template.
EXCEL REPEATED (OPENXML)
Two templates are delivered with the software. You can either use any of them or create a new template based on your requirements.
However, this report style also works without a template.
WORD (OPENXML)
A few templates are delivered with the software. You can either use any of them or create a new template based on your requirements.
This output style does not work if a template is not selected.
Generate OpenXML Word report for an object
You can generate an OpenXML Word report for an object of any class definition. The Word Open XML Template classification has been added to the Template Documents to support the creation of OpenXML Word report templates.
You can create a custom report template based on the Word Open XML template classification. After creating a custom report template, you then must create a template file (.docx) and a control file (.xml), and attach them to the custom
report template. The template file (.docx) contains the layout of the OpenXML Word report, and the control file (.xml) contains mapping information that links fields in the template file to properties in view definitions.
After configuring a custom report template, you can create a new method using the OpenXMLWordReport client API to generate a report based on that custom template.
For more information about creating custom OpenXML Word reports, see Create custom OpenXML Word reports.
Five OpenXML Word report templates are delivered with the software: DocumentTemplate, External Transmittal Word OpenXML Template, Internal Transmittal Word OpenXml Template, Cancelled External Transmittal Word
OpenXML Template, and Cancelled Internal Transmittal Word OpenXML Template. DocumentTemplate is used to generate OpenXML Word reports for classified objects, such as documents. The other four templates are used
to generate OpenXML Word reports for transmittals, which are non-classified objects.
OpenXMLWordReport client API
The OpenXMLWordReport client API is delivered in the Core.Core_Admin container and provides the ability to generate an open XML Word report for a selected object. This client API is used by the following delivered methods:
DocumentRevisionOpenXMLWordReport
SCLBTechnicalQueryReportPreview
SDAXmtlReportPreview
The DocumentRevisionOpenXMLWordReport method is used to generate an OpenXML word report for classified objects, such as design documents. The other methods are used to generate OpenXML word reports for
transmittals.
For more information about how the software filters the template documents for classified objects when a report category is specified, see Filter template documents to generate OpenXML Word reports for classified objects.
Create custom OpenXML Word reports
You can create a custom OpenXML Word report for an object of any class definition.
 Before you begin creating a custom OpenXML Word report, change your Create scope to Configuration Top so that the template can be used in all plants in the tenant; otherwise, you can use it only to create custom
reports in the plant under which it was created.
The process for creating a custom OpenXML Word report includes the following tasks:
Create a template (.docx) file
Create a control (.xml) file
Create a template document for OpenXML Word report
Create a method using the OpenXMLWordReport client API
Create a template (.docx) file
A template file is a Microsoft Word document that contains the layout of the OpenXML Word report. You can define the layout of the report by adding controls, such as labels, text boxes, check boxes, list boxes, and so on. A template file
can contain both tabular data and non-tabular data.
A sample template file delivered for the transmittals object is shown in the following image:
1
Labels for non-tabular data.
2
Labels for tabular data.
3
Static text.
The following procedure describes how to create and configure a template file.
1. Open Microsoft Word.
2. Select the DEVELOPER menu to switch to the Developer mode.
 If the DEVELOPER menu is not visible, select File > Options > Customize Ribbon to enable the Developer mode.
3. To add controls, such as a label, text box, and so on, to the template file, select the appropriate control in the ribbon, as shown below:
4. After adding the required controls, select a control, and select Properties in the ribbon. The Content Control Properties dialog box appears.
5. Enter a Title and Tag for a control. Each control must have a unique tag. This tag is linked in the control file to the view definition and property that contains the data for the control.
 After defining all the tags, select Design View on the ribbon to view all the tags.
6. Specify other properties for controls, as needed.
7. Save the template file.
A sample Design View for the template file delivered for transmittals is shown in the following image:
1
Tags for non-tabular data
2
Tags for tabular data
When specifying a tag name for a date control in the Content Control Properties dialog box, always begin the tag name with “dat”, for example, datCurrentdate.
When specifying a tag name for a check box in the Content Control Properties dialog box, always begin the tag with “chk”, for example, chkSpaces.
Create a control (.xml) file
The control file is an xml file where you map the controls defined in the template file to specific properties in view definitions in the database.
A sample control file delivered for transmittals is shown in the following image:
The following procedure describes how to create and configure a control file:
1. Open Notepad or any XML editor.
2. Add the root tag as <Control Mapping>.
3. Add the <ViewDefs> tag.
 To include properties from multiple view definitions in the control file, list all the view definitions here. For example:
<ViewDef Name ="ExternalXmtlReportViewDef"/>
<ViewDef Name ="ExternalOpenXMLReportViewDef"/>
4. Create <Controls> tag to define non-tabular data. A Controls tag includes a source and target that link a control in the template file to its matching property in a view definition. For example, the following syntax links a text box with the
unique tag txtName to the matching Name property in the ExternalXmtlReportViewDef view definition:
<Controls source ="ExternalXmtlReportViewDef.Name" target ="txtName"/>
Create a separate control tag for each non-tabular control defined in the template file.
You can also specify multiple properties in a single control by separating them with a comma. For example:
<Controls source = "ExternalXmtlReportViewDef.Name,ExternalXmtlReportViewDef.Description" target ="txtName"/>
5. If the report includes data in a table, add a <Tables> tag.
6. For each table in the report, specify the view definition name as: <Table ViewDef="View definition name">. For example, <Table ViewDef="ExternalXmtlReportViewDef">.
7. Beneath each Table tag, map the property for each column using a Column tag. The Column tag includes a source and target that link a column in the table to its matching property in the view definition. For example, the following
syntax links a text box with the unique tag txtRevision to the matching Document Revision property in the view definitions defined in the preceding Table tag:
<Column source = "Document Revision" target="txtRevision"/>
Create a separate column tag for each tabular control defined in the template file.
Close all the tags.
8. Save the file as a .xml file when all controls are mapped.
Create a template document for OpenXML Word report
1. In the Business Administration feature set, select Create Template Document.
2. In the New Template Document dialog box, select Word OpenXML Template.
3. Enter the required details.
4. In the Word OpenXML Report Details section, enter the following optional information if you will be configuring different templates for use based on the classification or condition of the objects:
Report category - A label that represents a group of report templates that are available to certain types of objects. For this report to be available to an object, the report category value here must match the value in the Report
Category for Classified Objects box for the method.
Object Classifications - Any classification for the classified object that uses this template document for OpenXML Word reports. For example, P&ID, 3D Documents, Electrical, Civil, and so on.
Object Condition - An additional level of condition, where you can associate different templates for different classified objects. For example, you might want to have separate report templates for current and working documents.
 If you have only one template for objects using this method, leave these fields blank.
5. Attach the template file and control file to the template document.
6. Select Finish.
Create a method based on the OpenXMLWordReport client API
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Create Method.
2. In the Create Method page, enter the required information:
a. Details
1. Enter Name, Description and Display as details for the method.
2. In the Method Details section, choose a Condition.
3. Select Client API > OpenXMLWordReport.
4. Select Next.
b. Properties
1. In the Template Document Name box, enter the name of the template document to be used when creating a report with this method.
 If you are creating a method for classified objects, and if you want the software to return a different template based on the classification of the object or a condition met by the object, then leave the Template
Document Name box empty.
2. Select the Truncate Time check box to specify that dates included in the report show only the date, and not the date and time stored in the database.
3. In the Report Category for Classified Objects box, enter the report category that you specified on the template document that will be available using this method.
 The software uses the report category value to return a set of matching templates for the selected object classification.
c. Access Control
1. In the Access Control tab, select at least one Access Groups.
d. Summary
1. Review all the information summarized for the method.
2. Select Finish.
 For classified objects, if the custom method includes a value in the Template Document Name box, this template document is always used to generate an OpenXML Word report with the custom method. Any value specified
in the Report Category for Classified Objects box is ignored.
 For information on displaying a report method in a feature set, see Relate a method to a feature set.
Filter template documents to generate OpenXML Word reports for classified objects
If you provide a value for Report Category for Classified Objects on the custom method, then when that method is called, the software searches for template documents with the same value in the Report Category property and the
Object Classification. If one or more templates are found with the above combination, the software considers the object condition specified on each of the template documents and finds the condition that matches the object that the
method was called from. The template document with the matching category and condition is used to generate the OpenXML Word report.
If the object that the method was called from does not meet the conditions set on any of the template documents with matching report category and object classification values, the software looks for a matching template document with that
classification. This process continues until a template is found that matches both the report category and condition of the object or until it reaches the root classification.
This behavior is explained using the following example.
You create two template documents with the following properties:
Name
Report category
Object classification
Object condition
Template 1
DocumentRevisions
3D Documents
IsReserved
Template 2
DocumentRevisions
3D Documents
IsIssued
You also create the following objects:
A method that has DocumentRevisions as the value for the Report Category for Classified Objects
Documents with a subtype of Civil Details in both the Reserved and Issued states
When you generate an OpenXML Word report for any of these design documents, the software looks for the matching template document with the Object Classification of Civil Details and Report Category of DocumentRevisions.
Because there is no template document with this combination, the software navigates up the classification tree to the parent classification of Civil Details that is Civil, and looks for a template document that has an Object Classification of
Civil and a Report Category of DocumentRevisions.
If the software does not find a template document with this combination of values, it navigates up another level to the parent classification of Civil that is 3D Documents, and looks for a template with the Object Classification of 3D
Documents and Report Category of DocumentRevisions.
The software finds two template documents with a matching combination of values, so it evaluates the conditions of each template document and uses the template with the condition that matches the object the method was called from.
 The software ignores the filtering process (described above) if the custom method includes a value in the Template Document Name box, this template document is always used to generate an OpenXML Word report with
the custom method. Any value specified in the Report Category for Classified Objects box is ignored.
Create a VDS connection
By default, HxGN SDx2 delivers an All Objects Default View Work Area to display a tag in a 3D model with the View Item in Model command. If you require a different Default View Work Area, you must create a VDS connection. The
following steps are required to create a VDS connection:
1. Log on to HxGN SDx2 as a user with the Business Administrator role.
