2. Navigate to the System Administration feature set, and select Create VDS Connection.
3. On the Create VDS Connection form, enter the required information in the Name and Description fields for the connection object.
4. Complete the URL field, entering a fully qualified domain name to the VDS Web Server end point.
Example: https://servername.domain.com/tenantname
5. Select a Default View Work Area. For more information about view work areas, see Create a view work area.
6. Select a Configuration item, and select Finish.
Configure a client application
Client applications are used to support a variety of features in HxGN SDx2, such as for integration and data take on. You can create a client application object using the following steps:
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Create Client Application.
2. In the Create Client Application form:
Enter the Name and the Client application ID.
 The Client application ID must match the Client ID registered for your tenant on your authorization server or set up in Okta authorization.
Select Allow impersonation of all users or relate individual users to control who can use the client application.
3. Select Finish.
 Before you start running any new client application, you must ensure that it is registered for your tenant. The client application ID is used to identify the client.application and is set by the system administrator. Contact your system
administrator for more information.
Create the client application in Postman
You can create a client application by using Postman to make a POST request: For example:
POST
<ACCESS URL>/sdx2_customer/sdxservice/api/v2/SDA/Objects
Add the payload as follows:
{
"Class":"SPFClientApplication",
"Name":"TestClientApp",
"Description":"TestClientApp",
"ClientApplicationID":"clientID",
"AllowImpersonationOfAllUsers":"True"
}
 The name of the HxGN SDx2 tenant is sdx2_customer in the example URL. You can also configure access to control who can use the client application.
Configure the system options
1. In the System Administration feature set, select System Options.
2. Select Actions 
 > Update.
For details about the options in this dialog box, find the section below:
Options details
For more information about the options in this section, see Options details.
Actions Menu Behavior - Allows you to automatically collapse or expand the methods and relationships in the Actions menu based on the selected value. The default value is ExpandActionsCollapseRelationships, which expands the
Actions node and collapses the Relationships node on the initial load of the Actions menu. The other values are ExpandActionsAndRelationships, CollapseActionsAndRelationships, and CollapseActionsExpandRelationships.
Default notificationdef - Allows you to specify the notification definition used to create the notification sent to users who have registered interest in an object when that object is changed.
 The title of the delivered default notification definition is "Modified: name of the object", and the body of the message is "The class of the object object called name of the object was modified by name of the user who made changes
to the object."
Default calendar - Allows you to specify a default calendar that can be used for reports.
Number of polls between scheduler last polled update - Allows you to set the number of polls after which the last polled time should be updated for the scheduler. By default, the number of polls is set to 0. For example, if the Last
polled time value is 22/04/13 11:44:38 and the Number of polls between scheduler last polled update value is set to 2, with the polling interval set to 60 seconds, then the Last polled time is updated every 2 minutes (calculated as
2*60 seconds = 2 minutes).
Time Zone Format - Allows you to pick how you want to see the offset of your local time with GMT/UTC time. By default, you will see the time in your local time zone. Use zzz as a suffix to view the time difference of your local time to
universal time with the one of the formats listed below:
User local time
Configure this
To view this
Result (let's say time in India is 15:30:29)
India - IST
Empty
hh:mm:ss (Default time of your
local system).
15:30:29
India - IST
zzz
hh:mm:ss +hh:mm
15:30:29 +05:30
India - IST
{UTCzzz}
hh:mm:ss {UTC+hh:mm}
15:30:29 {UTC+05:30}
Dubai - GST
(GMTzzz)
hh:mm:ss (GMT+hh:mm)
14:00:29 (GMT+04:00)
 You can use any kind of parentheses or brackets, like (), {}, or <>.
Enable Auditing - Allows you to track actions performed by users over a period of time, like download, view, or update of any objects or files in HxGN SDx2. You can also run a report to analyze who has viewed or updated the information
in HxGN SDx2, and when.
Login user display options details
For more information about the options in this section, see Login user display options details.
Login user display option - Allows you to select how you want login user names to appear in tables and other places in the user interface. You can choose one of the following:
Name, for example, "Authoring Administrator"
Login Name, for example, "authoringadmin"
Both, for example, "Authoring Administrator (authoringadmin)"
By default, tree views always display both the login name and the name for users. You can change the display by modifying the SPFLoginNameTreeDisplay column set.
To change how list views display names and login names, you can modify the SPFLoginUserColumnSet column set.
Schema access control option details
For more information about the options in this section, see Schema access control option details.
Enable interface definition access control - When this option is selected, the software supports a fine-grained interface definition access control relating to owning groups. This has a performance overhead for operations, such as on a
left-click, filling the properties grid, and evaluating methods on a right-click. This option is disabled by default to improve performance.
Configuration options details
For more information about the options in this section, see Configuration options details.
Use Concurrent Engineering Icons - When selected, concurrent engineering icons are used across configurations and projects.
Suppress multiple views of the same object when querying across multiple query configurations – When selected, the number of duplicate objects displayed in a query is reduced when querying across multiple configurations.
File management options details
For more information about the options in this section, see File management options details.
Nested reference file level - Limits how many relationship levels are created when a master document with reference files is uploaded, as they may also reference other files. For example, if a master document is uploaded with four
reference files, only two levels of relationships are created if this option is set to two.
Cyclic references allowed - Prevents the accidental creation of cyclic references. For example, cyclic references exist when FileA references FileB and FileB references FileA. Some CAD systems support cyclic references, but for those
that do not, this option should be turned off.
Auto generate alternate renditions - When this option is selected, the software generates an alternate rendition automatically for every file created. The alternate rendition is attached to the file from which it was generated and is stored
in the database.
 The Auto generate alternate renditions option is disabled by default.
Auto generate thumbnails - When this option is selected, the software creates a thumbnail image (PNG) for all viewable files when loaded into the system, assuming that the file type has a relationship to the PNG file type (FT_png). The
thumbnail file is attached to the file from which it was generated and is stored in the database.
 The Auto generate thumbnails option is disabled by default.
File share default expiry days - Allows you to set the number of working days that the link for an offline transmittal will remain active. This option adds a layer of security, as the link to download the attached documents in a transmittal
expires, based on the value provided. By default, the value is set to 15.
Transmittal default file type - If selected, the system sets the value as the default file type to be attached to the transmittal.
Workflow options details
For more information about the options in this section, see Workflow options details.
Recalculate target dates on reassign - Recalculates the target date of a workflow step when you reassign that step to another user or role or when you add one or more users to that step. If you do not select this check box, the step
reassignment will have no effect on the target date of that step. This option is enabled by default when the product is upgraded to version 2019 and is disabled by default when version 2019 of the product is installed the first time.
Merge process steps on completion - When a workflow is completed or terminated all the consecutive process steps are merged into a single process step and the redundant steps are deleted. This can help reduce the database size.
Display workflow information steps - Displays any information workflow steps in your To Do List. If you do not select this check box, the information steps are signed off automatically.
Allowable number of days to wait between e-mail - Allows you to set the number of days to wait between sending workflow step overdue reminder e-mails.
Allowable number of days overdue before expiration - Allows you to set the number of days a workflow step can exceed its target date before the step is considered overdue. This number is used for the overdue steps report and
controls the color of the To Do List icon. When a workflow step action is overdue (the target date is reached), the To Do List icon turns red. Only steps meeting the criteria entered here are considered in expiration checks.
Allowable number of days overdue before reassignment - Allows you to set the number of days a workflow step can be overdue before it is reassigned.
Create workflow expiration check task - Creates a scheduler workflow expiration check task to determine which are the expired overdue workflow steps, check dates, and automatically sends out email reminders. When the number of
days overdue before expiration has been reached, the expired workflow step is reassigned back to the participant.
 If the workflow expiration check task option is cleared, any scheduler tasks that are in the Ready or Processed states are deleted.
Suppress all e-mail - Suppresses all e-mail notifications from workflow to recipients.
Reject e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to recipients when a step is rejected.
Action e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to the recipients when a step action is required.
Started e-mail definition - Sets the default e-mail message sent to the recipients of a step when that step is started. The delivery of this e-mail is optional.
Reassign to e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to the recipient to whom the step is reassigned.
Reassign from e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to the recipient from whom the step is reassigned.
First assignee e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to the first assignee.
Completion e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to the workflow submitter when the workflow is completed. The delivery of this e-mail is optional.
Sign off e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to recipients when a step is signed off.
Workflow off hold e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to recipients when the workflow is taken off hold.
Overdue e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to recipients when a step is overdue.
Workflow on hold e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to recipients when the workflow is put on hold.
Information e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to recipients when an information step is assigned to them.
Step claimed e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message sent to the recipients when a step is claimed.
Future actions e-mail definition - Sets the e-mail message that is sent to all recipients of all future steps in a workflow when the first step has been completed.
Initial workflow status - Allows you to assign a status to a change action after the change action is added to a workflow.
Completion workflow status - Allows you to assign a status to a change action after it is completed.
Information step definition - Allows you to select the step that is displayed in the inbox when a user is assigned as an Info Only recipient on an Assignment step.
Workflow calendar - Defines the default calendar to be used for workflows. For more information, see Configure and maintain the system calendar.
Allow workflow step auto reassignment - Enables the automatic reassignment function. For more information, see Allow reassignment of workflow steps.
Workflow viewer column set - Allows you to select the workflow properties to be displayed in the Workflow step details window.
Delete unclaimed steps on completion - Deletes any unclaimed parallel steps when an interactive step is assigned to a role. This can help reduce the database size. By default, this option is disabled.
SDx only option details
For more information about the options in this section, see SDx only options details.
Allow workflow step to auto delete markup(s) - Enables the automatic removal of markups at the end of the workflow.
Global options for Attribute Management details
For more information about the options in this section, see Global options for Attribute Management details.
Use Form Sequence Override - Allows you to override the sequence order of column items in a dynamic section tab when the sequence order was previously defined when you managed column sets. This prevents you having to change
the sequence of each column set individually.
Dashboards options details
For more information about the options in this section, see Dashboards options details.
Seconds before a widget request in progress will be cancelled - The number of seconds that a widget request can run for before it times out and is cancelled. When this timeout is hit, an entry is created in the widget log. The default is
100 seconds.
Maximum number of results to be returned from a widget - The maximum number of results that a widget request can return. When the number of results is exceeded, an entry is created in the widget log, and the widget will be
disabled the next time the dashboard is loaded. The default is 1000.
Maximum seconds a widget request will run for - If a widget request takes longer than the specified number of seconds to finish running, an entry is created in the widget log, and the next time the same dashboard is displayed, the
widget will be disabled. The default is 60 seconds.
Configure indexing for fast searches
The indexing service uses automatic indexing technology combined with a performance orientated search engine that is designed to make fast and scalable searches across large datasets, especially found in large multi-tenant systems.
This functionality uses a separate indexed datastore to store column item property terms found in the column set for an object entry point. When indexing is enabled on an entry point, the related search engine queries just for the relevant
property terms in the read datastore, which displays the results significantly faster than the standard as-built search functionality.
What are the advantages of using an indexed datastore?
HxGN SDx2 document queries can be broad, especially as documents are highly complex objects. A separately indexed datastore provides the following advantages:
Provides significant performance improvements over the standard as-built search functionality for multi-tenant systems that use large data sets .
Enables the indexing of existing entry points in tenants that have a significantly large number of objects.
Efficiently indexes property data into a faster and smaller read-only indexed datastore used by the search.
Works asynchronously in the background to index entry point data into a read-only datastore.
Synchronization of the property terms between the read indexed datastore and the write datastore.
Only indexes data in a system when the Integration Data Filter (IDF) is set to consolidated.
 If you select any other IDF filters, the search is directed to the write datastore (database).
Indexes objects through the column items configured in the entry point, which:
Indexes the property terms that are only exposed in the results grid.
Searches for objects that are only active and current in the consolidated design component.
Applies specific security rules when a querying the indexed datastore.
Indexes Tags using four key text fields that can be searched, Token, Name, TagClass, and TagType.
 The standard as-built search must return data from the write datastore for various document versions, revisions, masters and their related objects, and so is significantly slower than the search engine used in the indexed
datastore.
What is an indexed search?
An indexed search is one that specifically handles queries for large volumes of data. This type of search uses a scalable search and analytics engine that uses an inverted index to keep track of files or documents by the terms they contain.
The search works by looking up the property terms in the indexed datastore that returns the relevant documents and objects that contain those terms much faster than the standard as-built search functionality.
Indexing can be used for full text searches where high availability and scale are required. It also provides results to search queries at low latency for a real-time search. The indexing service stores the data and indices in a separate
datastore so it can quickly search through large data sets and types of data.
An indexed search provides:
Open-source search engine architecture.
A separate read indexed datastore to store data and analytics information.
Synchronization between the read indexed datastore and the write datastore (database).
Support for querying large volumes of data separately from the write datastore.
Indexes of the objects from entry points using property terms as accessible data in the indexed datastore.
Fast and efficient functionality to handle large complex data searches
Search results with the most relevant objects shown in a hierarchal order.
A vastly improved search experience for users over the standard as-built search service when running queries over large datasets.
How does the indexing service work?
The indexing service uses two separate datastores:
The system write datastore.
The indexed read datastore.
The indexed read datastore provides the following advantages:
Search queries are significantly faster to return data from the indexed read datastore.
Separate scalability and flexibility for both the indexed read datastore and the write datastore.
The indexing service works by:
Extracting property terms from the column set in an entry point object and transforming those terms into the read indexed datastore.
Building the alternate persistence read indexed datastore.
Synchronizing the data between the read indexed datastore and the write datastore.
Saving changes to the data in the write datastore into the read indexed datastore.
 Newly created or updated objects are prioritized for indexing to ensure their immediate availability in a search.
Indexing scheduler
As part of the Integration Engine, the Consolidation Service generates large changesets that contain information about the objects being processed by the service.
The indexing service uses the IndexingScheduler, which polls every 10 minutes (600,000ms) to collect changesets older than two hours. It then combines these multiple changesets into a single batch of 10,000 objects, before raising an
event to start the indexing process.
Once the indexing process is complete, another batch of 10,000 objects can be raised. This helps reduce the number of indexing events and enhances performance for systems with multiple tenants during high system loads.
How does the search service work?
The search service uses a performance search engine that works by:
Using the entry point column set to search for the property terms displayed in the column items.
Transforming the OData queries into a language that the search engine can understand.
Authenticating and authorizing each search query.
Using rules to filter and refine the search query.
Serializing objects to EDM objects that are then used to pass the query results back to SDx2.
Managing the various search service processes through the REDIS cache.
Indexing of system data provides:
Significant performance improvement when searching large volumes of data, which takes the load off the system datastore.
An effective index of content found in a write datastore into a separate indexed read datastore.
Faster searches of entry point data in the indexed datastore compared to the standard as-built search functionality.
Easy indexing of the object properties for selected API entry points.
Scalability for both the indexed datastore and the system datastore (database).
Ability to report on the indexed datastore using a dedicated dashboard in an integrated system.
Why is there a difference in the sort order of the indexed search results?
 The sort order of objects resulting from an indexed search cannot match the sort order of an SQL Server search.
There is a difference in the sort order of returned objects displayed in an indexed datastore than those returned by an SQL Server. This is because the search engine sorts the order of named objects lexographically, based on the
alphabetical order of the Unicode characters. An SQL Server uses specific collation settings to determine how the string comparison is performed. This affects the sort order as it takes into account other factors, such as case
sensitivity and character accents. The following example explains the sort order difference between a search using an indexed datastore and a search in an SQL server.
An SQL server search sorts the returned documents in the following order:
TESTDOC-01
T-RT-01
In the SQL server, the sort order is affected by the collation setting behavior in SQL. The first characters are the same, but the second character – comes later than E in the above string, so an SQL search returns the documents in
this sort order.
A search using an indexed datastore sorts the returned documents in the following order:
T-RT-01
TESTDOC-01
In the indexed data, both documents have the same first character, so the second character is used to determine the sort order. In Unicode, the second character - has a value of 45 and the second character E has a value of 69.
Therefore, as T-TR-01 has a lower value for the second character, it is sorted to display before TESTDOC-01.
Configure an entry point for indexing
 The indexing functionality is automatically enabled by default for document and tag entry points delivered with a new system and cannot be disabled. It is also enabled by default for any custom document or tag entry points
and also cannot be disabled. For other entry points, the indexing functionality can be enabled when you create or update an entry point.
You can configure indexing for any class definition or interface definition entry point object. The indexing and search service is especially useful when search performance is critical to your tenants and you have a large amount of objects in
the system write datastore.
New objects are prioritized for indexing to ensure the immediate availability of their indexed results in any subsequent search. The indexing service reads the property terms used in the column set configured for the entry point objects.
Once the indexing option is set on an entry point, the column item property terms are automatically indexed into the separate read-only indexed datastore.
To create an index of entry point property terms in the indexed datastore.
1. Choose an entry point, and select Actions 
 > Update API Entry Point.
2. In the update form, select the Enable Indexing option.
The Enable Indexing option when selected appears seamless to users as it works asynchronously in the background.
The document and tag entry points delivered out-of-the-box automatically have the Enable Indexing option selected by default, which cannot be turned off.
If you create a custom document or consolidated tag entry point, the Enable Indexing option is also selected by default and cannot be turned off.
Entry points that do not enable the indexing option carry on working as normal and use the original as-built standard search functionality to read data from the write datastore (database).
The indexing service only indexes data that match the consolidated integration design component. The search engine is only used when the Integration Data Filter (IDF) is set to consolidated in scopes.
If you select any other IDF filters, the search is directed to the write datastore (database).
Any updated entry point property terms are automatically indexed into the read-only datastore.
The indexing scheduler uses the indexing service to reduce the load on the system.
All indexing related messages are redirected to the indexing service for processing.
The indexed datastore is created when indexing is enabled and its size is automatically extended as the amount of indexed objects increase.
If a column set is not configured, the indexing service uses the SPFObject column set by default.
Objects that are missing or fail indexing are automatically reindexed. For more information, see .Automatic reindexing of failed or missing objects.
For more information on configuring API entry points, see Configure API Entry Points.
Document and tag entry points
Document and tag entry points are delivered out-of-the-box with the Enable Indexing option turned on by default, so indexing is automatic and cannot be turned off.
If you create any custom document or tag entry points, the Enable Indexing option is also turned on by default and cannot be turned off.
 For all other types of entry point, there is no change in operation, so you can turn on or off the Enable Indexing option when updating or creating an entry point.
Update the cached entry point metadata
You must refresh the metadata in the system using the Regenerate Metadata function after selecting the Enable Indexing option for an entry point. This is needed to update the cached metadata for the entry point.
 If you do not update the metadata cache, the entry point data will not be indexed. For more information, see Regenerate metadata.
Viewing results from the indexed datastore
When the indexed datastore properties are displayed in a results grid, the read data is displayed much faster that from the write datastore. However, some data may be missing in the search results as the index is still being rebuilt. In that
case, you can refresh the view of the indexed results manually to see if any of the missing data is now in the search results.
Select Refresh 
 to reload the view of the indexed results.
If you suspect any indexed data is still missing, you can turn on the Switch to Standard query option in the information bar. This option uses the standard as-built search functionality to view the object data in the write datastore, and so
consequently search performance is much slower to return object data.
 The Switch to standard query option is only displayed if indexing has been enabled for the selected entry point. For more information, see Configure an entry point for indexing.
Reindexing objects
When any objects fail or miss indexing, you can reindex entry point objects to ensure that the indexed datastore is always in synchronization with the system write datastore. The indexed datastore is completely rebuilt during this process
to accommodate the reindexed data.
Objects are reindexed due to updates to the entry point data, which also includes:
Column set updates - When a new column item is added or updated for a column set used by an entry point that has indexing enabled
Security rule updates - When a security rule is added or updated for an entry point that has indexing enabled.
Property comparison updates - When a property comparison is updated on an entry point that has indexing enabled.
Reindexing works asynchronously in the background to help reduce load on the application server.
A notification is sent each time the indexing or reindexing operation fails, so you can correct the issue.
Reindexing works in line with distributed plants and retrieves objects for reindexing from the plant domain tables from each configuration individually.
Automatic reindexing of failed or missing objects
When any objects fail or miss indexing, they are automatically reindexed using the AutoIndexingScheduler to ensure that the indexed datastore is always in synchronization with the system write datastore and helps to keep the data
consistent.
The scheduler runs every hour to reindex objects and only reindexes objects that are older than 1 hour. This is done to avoid multiple reindexing attempts within a short period of time.
The reindex processes up to 100 objects at a time for each entry point by default.
Only one reindexing event is allowed at a time for each entry point.
The number of objects the AutoIndexingScheduler can process is configurable in each entry point using the AutoIndexingScheduler.MaxCountPerEntryPoint.Enabled feature flag option
Manually reindex objects
You can manually reindex objects, irrespective of whether the objects are indexed or not. This is done to reduce the load on the application server by preventing multiple reindexing events being raised on the same entry point.
You start a reindexing event on an entry point by using the following methods in Actions:
Reindex all objects - reindexes all the existing entry point objects
Reindex pending objects - reindexes any objects that were pending indexing after creation.
 If you raise multiple reindexing events, the current event request will continue until completion. Any new reindexing event requests raised will be terminated.
Multiple reindexing events
The reindexing process prevents multiple reindexing events by tracking the indexing operation of an entry point in each tenant. When a reindexing request is received by the indexing service, the reindexing event is recorded.
Only one reindexing event is allowed at a time for each entry point to prevent multiple reindexing events being subsequently raised on the same entry point. This process helps reduce the load on the application server.
If you raise multiple reindexing requests on the same entry point, you are informed that any new reindexing request is terminated to allow the current reindexing event to continue.
When a new column set, security rule, or property comparison is added or updated , a new reindexing event request is automatically triggered. The new reindexing event request takes priority and supersedes the current reindexing
event, which is terminated.
You must restart any terminated reindexing request once the current reindexing event has completed.
Indexed objects dashboard
The status of indexed objects can be displayed as a report using a widget in the Object Indexing dashboard. This report retrieves data from the indexed datastore and displays the indexed totals for each type of object when the entry point
has had the Enable Indexing option selected.
The widget displays a count of the number indexed objects in columns in a bar chart.. The results for each API entry point are displayed in separate columns displayed as the Indexed or Not Indexed categories:i
You can click each column in the bar chart to view the objects in an indexed results grid.
The Object Indexing dashboard is displayed as a home screen dashboard enabled for the Business Administrator role.
If there are no indexed objects in the system, the widget is blank.
Objects that are missing or fail indexing are automatically reindexed. For more information, see .Automatic reindexing of failed or missing objects.
For more information on creating an InsightView Object Indexing dashboard with the Object Indexing widget, see Using an InsightView dashboard.
You can view the objects in the indexed datastore and check the write datastore to see if that the latest indexed objects are available. For more information, see Viewing results from the indexed datastore
Configure email definitions
Email definitions specify the type of email messages sent when certain events occur during a workflow, such as when a step is completed. Email definitions can be defined to apply to step definitions for a workflow step, or at a system-wide
level for specific events. If an email definition is not specified for a workflow step or a relevant event, then no email is sent.
Once an email message object is created, it can be associated with a workflow step or set as a default for use with all workflows based on the event, such as if a step is reassigned or claimed. Any workflow definitions that call the related
step will use this e-mail message template when notifying users of an action.
Several email definitions can be defined for each event allowing a more specialized email definition to be used whenever appropriate. To determine the email definition that is most appropriate, they are sorted by the interface specialization
value that they correspond to.
When a step is started in a workflow, it determines if the current step has an associated email message. If so, it uses that email message to send out a workflow email notification.
If no email message is found related to the step, then the default email message is generated from the email definition related to the object.
How can I configure default email notifications?
The default email notifications sent to the recipients of a step can be configured in the workflow options. These emails are overridden by any email definitions defined for the step class to which the step definition belongs.
To configure the default emails:
1. In the system options, scroll down to the Workflow options details section. For more information on the system options, see Configure the system options.
2. Type in the Allowable number of days to wait between e-mails, Allowable number of days overdue before expiration, and Allowable number of days overdue before reassignment as required.
3. If you want to stop all email notifications from going to the workflow recipients, then check the Suppress all e-mail check box.
4. Select the email definitions from the drop-down lists, as required. See below for a description of each of the definitions.
Email
Description
Reject email definition
The email message sent to recipient from whom a step is rejected or is not completed successfully.
Action email definition
The email message sent to the recipients when the next step requires an action.
Started email definition
The default email message sent to the recipients of a step when that step is started. The delivery of this e-mail is optional.
Reassign to email definition
The email message sent to the recipient to whom a step has been reassigned.
Reassign from email definition
The email message sent to the recipient the step is reassigned from.
First assignee email definition
The email message sent to the first assignee.
Completion email definition
The email message sent to the workflow submitter when the workflow is completed. The delivery of this email is optional.
Sign off email definition
The email message sent to all relevant recipients when a step is signed off.
Workflow off hold email definition
The email message sent to all relevant recipients in whose To Do List has a step waiting to be done when the workflow is taken off hold.
Overdue email definition
The email message sent to recipients in whose To Do List has a step waiting to be done when a previous step is overdue.
Workflow on hold email definition
The email message sent to all relevant recipients in whose To Do List has a step waiting to be done when the workflow is put on hold.
Information email definition
The email message sent to all recipients when an information step is assigned to them.
Step claimed email definition
The email message sent to the all recipients assigned a step when the step is claimed by one recipient.
Future actions email definition
An email message that can be configured to be sent to all recipients of all future steps in a workflow when the first step has been completed. This
provides the recipients with a notification of potential future work.
Create a new email definition
1. In the Business Administration feature set, click Create email definition.
2. In the Create Email Definition window, type the required values, select the appropriate options as shown in each of the following tabs below:
 You can click PREVIOUS or NEXT to navigate between different tabs.
General Details
Type a name and description for the email definition.
Email Details
In the Reply Address box, type the e-mail address of the recipient.
In the Email subject Text box, type a brief description of the e-mail. This appears in the subject line of the email.
Email Body
Type the content of the email in the text editor. This should include anything that needs to be clearly explained to the user.
How can I customize my email message?
HxGN SDx2 allows you to customize your email notification message content in different ways of which few are listed below:
When defining the email message object, you can define URLs that can display a hyperlink in the message text, which can be used to access the related workflow object in their To Do List.
Conditions syntax can be used to implement text substitutions by properties on the object in the workflow. All email message objects start with the workflow object and can navigate relationships to the workflow set. For more
information on how to configure conditions, see Configure conditions.
Example: <WORKFLOW>
If there is no relationship definition, then the syntax only requires a keyword.
Example: &Obj->SPFWorkflowWorkflowStep_21->SPFItemWorkflow_21.Name&
Email Body examples
Example 1:
The following example illustrates on how to compose a message which allows the email recipient to view the workflow item from their inbox:
The workflow for object <&Obj->SPFItemWorkflow_21.Name&> has finished.
To view the item in HxGN SDx2, please click this:
<ahref=&ENV.SDAClientURL&/#/objects;queryFilter=%7B%22filters%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22pageSize%22%3A25%2C%22page%22%3A1%2C%22relatedItemFilters%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22expandFromObjectId
>SPFItemWorkflow_21.OBID&%22%2C%22hasLink%22%3Atrue%7D;config=%5B%22&Obj->SPFItemWorkflow_21.Config&%22%5D/detail/&Obj->SPFItemWorkflow_21.OBID&>link</a>
Example 2:
The following example illustrates on how to compose a message which allows the email recipient to download a file from their inbox:
Transmittal <a href=&ENV.SERVERURL&/api/v2/SDA/DownloadSharedFile?GUID=%27&Obj->SPFFileComposition_21.SPFFileShareID&%27>&Obj.Name& </a> has been shared with you and is
ready to download.<br><br>The link to download the package expires on {{EXPIRYDAYS}}.<br><br>
If a condition syntax in the Email Body text editor results in multiple objects, then the body of the email may be configured to display the object information on a separate line. When the condition returns multiple properties of
the related objects, the objects will be listed on separate lines. However, if the condition returns only one property for each related object, then the results will appear on one line with commas separating each object.
If a property definition is scoped by an Enum, the body of the email returns the Enum's name. However, you can configure the email syntax to return the display name in an email message by adding a suffix of a tilde (~) to a
property definition. This indicates that the Display Name property will be used. If there is no value for the Display Name property, the email will return the value in the Name property instead. For example, &Obj-
>SPFWorkflowWorkflowStep_21->SPFItemWorklow_21.SPFXmtlIssueState~& is ready to be actioned.
You can use different options in the text editor to customize your email text.
You can copy and paste any image or logo directly into the text editor.
You can enable the View Source toggle to display the email body text in HTML format or to compose a custom HTML template as email body.
We recommend you use the launch URL syntax when creating a link to the Web Client. For example, https://domain.com/#/launch/run.
3. Click FINISH to save the changes.
 This feature uses the SPFCreateEmailDefinition method related to the SPFCreateEmailDef client API. For more information, see SPFCreateEmailDef client API.
Learn more about email digests
An email digest is a collection of notification messages that are sent together instead of separately. A user preference can be set in the user Options page to receive digests instead of individual email messages.
This service allows each user to configure a time period over which email will be collected and grouping for consolidation related emails (for example by project). The email digest also supports the attachments sent along with the emails.
For more information on the email digest settings, see Set the user options.
A scheduled task is created for the email digest based on the duration value set and the task automatically sends the emails in the corresponding duration interval. You can view the scheduled tasks in the System Administration
feature set.
The email digest format type can be HTML or text.
Configure and maintain the system calendar
You can find the system calendar using a search, and selecting System Administration > Calendars.
Maintain your calendar
1. Select SystemCalendar, and select Actions > Update.
2. Select the check boxes for any standard non-working days. For example, Saturdays and Sundays.
 Non-working days are displayed in grey.
Add a holiday
Click the date in any month for other non-working days. For example, national holidays.
 Non-working days are displayed in grey.
Add a new year
1. Select SystemCalendar, and select Actions > Update.
2. In the Calendar years box, expand the list, and click NEW CALENDAR YEAR.
3. Type in the number and description for the new year.
Copy a calendar
1. Select SystemCalendar, and select Actions > Copy.
2. Type in the name and description for the new calendar.
Manage workflows
HxGN SDx2 workflows are a set of steps that represent your business processes including distribution, reviews, approval, and notification. Most of the templates are configured out-of-the-box.
However, as an administrator, you can also create custom workflow templates with workflow steps that have defined recipients. These templates can be conditionally made available to manage different classes of objects or modify the
editable workflow templates. For more information, see Manage workflow templates.
What are the delivered workflow templates?
Two versions of each of the workflow templates are delivered for HxGN SDx2.
A master workflow template with the prefix MSR that cannot be used in the system and is supplied only for reference.
An editable copy of the workflow template with the prefix HEX that customers can use and edit in their system.
The name and UID of the workflow template will have the prefix
MSR for the master or non-editable copy
HEX for the editable copy
Master workflow template (MSR)
In order that each master workflow template (MSR) remains intact and unaffected, the ISPFNonEditableCopy interface definition is instantiated on each master workflow template version. The ISPFNonEditableCopy interface is only
realized by elements used in the master workflow template and does not allow those elements to be referenced, edited, updated, or attached. You cannot use the master workflow template in the user interface as selection or update
options for it are disabled. When the master workflow template version is selected in the user interface, the edit option is not displayed. However, you can copy the master workflow template to create an editable copy of the workflow
template that does not instantiate the ISPFNonEditableCopy interface.
 When copying a master workflow template, if a step in the master workflow (M1) includes a success/failure step that comes from another master workflow (M2), then a deep copy of the template is performed. This creates two HEX
copies of the template, one for M1 and one for M2. The steps between these two copies are related.
Editable copy of the workflow template (HEX)
The editable copy of the workflow template (HEX) is supplied so you can customize the workflow to add your own work processes. The editable copy does not contain or reference the elements that realize the ISPFNonEditableCopy
interface. This allows you to edit the copy of the workflow template to meet your custom workflow needs, such as editing step recipients. A new master workflow template can be created by instantiating the ISPFNonEditableCopy interface
onto the editable copy of the workflow template to lockdown the edit option. This is only possible when no parts of the workflow template are related to any other editable template copy.
What are the rules for workflow process remodeling?
Two versions of each workflow template are delivered.
A master workflow template with the prefix MSR that cannot be used in the system and is supplied only for reference.
An editable copy of the workflow template with the prefix HEX that customers can use and edit in their system.
The following rules must be followed when changing the HEX version of the template:
Do not modify any existing step definitions, step classes, step statuses, or their related conditions.
You can copy the step definition, its step class, and related condition.
You can add, remove, and reorder steps in the workflow template.
You can provide your own prefix to the workflow template.
You cannot modify any existing notification definition.
Notification definitions can be copied.
 Workflows are configured with a set of steps that represent your business process. Certain steps in the process are critical to the operation of that work process. These critical steps are indicated in the work process and
should only be altered within the scope described at each step.
 For details on how to configure workflow steps, see Manage workflow templates.
Manage workflow templates
Workflow templates contain the steps necessary to achieve a change management process. A workflow administrator creates workflow templates consisting of the workflow steps with defined recipients. Each step defines an action that a
user or the software must complete for the object to move to the next step in the workflow. Steps can optionally have a list of checklist items that the user must complete and check off before the object moves to the next step.
The order of processing for each step is defined by the Accept and Reject steps. By default, the first step is started when the workflow is attached to the change object. Subsequent steps start when the prerequisite steps are completed.
Parallel steps are configured by specifying a number of steps that start based upon the completion of a particular step.
An object can move from one workflow into another. For example, a document review and sign off workflow can be configured so that a document rejected during a review step moves to a different workflow designed to address the issues
found with the document. Workflow steps manage each stage of the business process including validation, approval, distribution, and notification. Each workflow step has a target completion date that determines when overdue notifications
are sent and workflows expire.
 By default, only 18 workflow templates of HEX and MSR versions are delivered with the product.
The steps in the template workflow are selected from a library of step definitions to ensure a consistent look and feel to all workflows. The following information can be specified on the steps in the workflow template:
Option to provide a condition to filter attachment of object. For example, a document would have to be working to be submitted to an approval/signoff workflow.
Step dependency (allowing for serial or parallel processing).
The recipients of each step (these can be users, roles, or matrices).
The next step for voting steps with a voting percentage that determines a true vote by all recipients.
The object status on step completion.
A rejection route for validation or voting steps.
The step duration (used to set the target completion date when the workflow is in use).
Optional checklists can be configured and attached for work assignment steps..
Create a workflow template
The workflow template consists of a sequence of steps, where each step signifies an action. Moving from one step to another step is determined by set of conditions that are fulfilled by the appropriate step recipient.
 In HxGN SDx2, the software does not support attachment of workflow templates to a object.
What options are displayed on the toolbar in the Create Workflow template page?
The following options are displayed on the toolbar in the Create Workflow template page:
Click
To
List the step statuses and paths by color or icon.
Change the display of the workflow steps.
What are the Filter options?
The Filter 
 options allow you to change the display of the workflow steps.
Filter option
What it allows you to do
Brief View
Change the view to a brief view of only your task
steps after the selected task, or expand all the parallel
workflow paths and steps.
Landscape
Change the workflow orientation between upright and
landscape.
Minimize Process
Hide or expose all process steps in the workflow.
Edit Workflow Template
Update the selected workflow template.
Add New Workflow Step
Add a new step to the workflow template.
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, click Create Workflow template.
2. In the Configure Template Details window, type the required values, and select the appropriate options as shown in each of the following tabs:
 You must click PREVIOUS or NEXT to navigate between different tabs.
General Details
1. Type a name and description for the workflow template.
2. Select the Delete Workflows On Completion option to automatically delete completed or terminated workflow instances from the system. This can help reduce your database growth and maintain your systems performance.
 Depending on your business needs, you can choose to set this option as you create or update a workflow template to safely remove temporary workflow instances from the database. On other workflow templates, you
can choose to keep the completed or abandoned workflow instances, such as if needed for audit or regulatory purposes.
 The Delete Workflows On Completion option setting works asynchronously as a scheduler task across the system regardless of the configuration. However, be aware that intensive database activity could
potentially occur deleting a lot of completed or terminated workflows.
Workflow Details
1. Select an appropriate user from the Re-Assign User list to whom the workflow step needs to be reassigned whenever the default user is not available. This option is considered whenever the workflow is trying to reject, or
cannot reject, a step.
The Submitter represents the person who submitted the object to the workflow.
If multiple recipients are assigned to a step and even one recipient rejects the step, then the object in the workflow is sent back to the user specified in the Reassign to Participant property in the workflow template.
However, if you use a voting step to calculate the number of accept and reject responses, you can set a threshold that, when met, allows the workflow to continue with the correct accept or reject path, rather than being
reassigned to another user.
A step is not reassigned to a participant if it has a single success step and that success step is not a voting step.
2. Select a condition from the Condition (Optional) list that must be met by an object before a copy of the workflow template can be associated with it.
Business Objects
Search from the Workflow Business Objects list and select an appropriate object based on which corresponding interfaces, step definitions, and methods are filtered and displayed to the user.
 The Workflow Business Objects list displays only the objects associated with the workflow.
Optional Details
Select a corresponding interface for the chosen workflow business object from the Interfaces list, based on which step definitions are available for the workflow template.
Summary
This tab displays all the selected options which will be applied when creating a workflow template.
3. Click FINISH to save the changes.
 Once the workflow template is created, you can add the first workflow step by clicking 
 Add New Workflow Step in the Create Workflow template page. For more information, see Create a workflow step.
Create a workflow step
You must have the Business Administrator role to create and update a workflow template.
How can I create a custom workflow step status?
You can create a custom workflow step status which can be later applied to a workflow step when creating or updating the step. The workflow step status allows you to identify the progress of the step.
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, click Create Workflow Step Status.
2. In the Main details section, type the name and description. For example, name as WF-Validated.
3. In the Workflow status details section, type the percentage in the Status percentage box.
4. Click FINISH to save and apply the changes.
1. In the Create Workflow template page, select 
 Add New Workflow Step.
 If the step being created is the first step of the workflow template, you can also select 
.
2. In the New Workflow Step pane, enter the required values, and select appropriate options in each section, as shown below:
a. Step Details
1. Type a name and description for the workflow step.
2. Type and search for the appropriate Step Definition and select ADD. The step definition is added, and the Distribution and Assignment Details section is displayed.
 You can also select the Advanced Search option to search for and display a targeted set of results based on the type of object selected in the Advanced Search window.
You can select 
 to update a corresponding step definition.
You can select CREATE NEW STEPDEF to create a new custom step definition using the Configure Workflow Step Definition window. For more information, see Create a workflow step definition.
The step definition determines the action that the step recipients need to perform on the business object. Based on the type of step definition selected, next corresponding sections in the pane are enabled.
3. Type and search for an appropriate Step Checklist and select to assign it to the workflow step. Checklists are series of items or tasks that a user is supposed to complete before the workflow finishes. However, completion
of checklist can be an optional or mandatory prerequisite for the workflow step completion. Once the checklist is assigned to a workflow step, it is displayed in the To Do List against the workflow step.
If no checklists are displayed or none of them match your requirement, create one by selecting CREATE NEW CHECKLIST and following the steps in Create a workflow step checklist.
You can select 
 to update a corresponding checklist.
You can select the Is Checklist Mandatory option, to ensure that the corresponding checklist is completed before the step is signed off. On selecting this option, the workflow step in the To Do List displays a
warning message to notify about the checklist completion. You can access the checklist from the CHECKLIST tab that is displayed when you select the step in the To Do List.
 You can select FILTER to refine the search results based on the applied filter criteria.
b. Distribution and Assignment Details
1. In the Choose who needs to react section, select either the Submitter, one or more users, or one or more roles to perform the step action.
 You can add any number of users or roles.
From the Users list, search and select the users that will be assigned to perform the step, and select ADD.
From the Roles list, search and select the roles that will be assigned to perform the step, and select ADD.
2. In the Use Distribution Matrix section, type and search for the appropriate distribution matrix in the Matrices box to use it to determine what users or roles are assigned the step.
3. In the Choose who needs to get informed section, select either the Submitter, one or more users, or one or more roles to be informed about this step.
 You can add any number of users or roles.
From the Users list, search for and select the users that will be assigned to perform the step, and select ADD.
From the Roles list, search for and select the roles that will be assigned to perform the step, and select ADD.
 You can also select the Advanced Search option to search for and display a targeted set of results based on the type of object selected in the Advanced Search window.
4. In the Choose who needs to react in group section,
Select an option in the Assignment list to specify how many users must perform a task, when it is assigned to more than one user or role.
Select an option in the Acknowledgement list to specify how many users must respond to a notification, when it is assigned to more than one user or role.
c. Navigation Details
1. Search for and select an appropriate step from the Failure / Reject Step list defined in the current template, and select ADD. The selected step is where the object will be sent if the current step fails or is rejected.
2. Search for and select an appropriate step from the Success / Accept Step list defined in the current template, and select ADD. The selected step is where the object will be sent if the current step is successful or
accepted.
What are accept and reject steps ?
With the exception of the first step in a workflow, each step must have a preceding step.
Accept step – the next step to be performed if the current step is accepted.
Reject step – the next step to be performed if the current step is rejected or fails.
A workflow can be accepted or rejected to another workflow.
When you set an Accept or Reject step, you are setting the next step in the workflow.
Before setting the Accept or Reject steps, ensure that the first step in the workflow is correct. This is the only one that will not have a preceding step.
 If you reject a step in a workflow and the reject path is a future step in the same workflow, a new revision of the workflow is not created. The item continues in the original revision of the workflow, optimizing the use
of the database.
 The Search option displays results only from the current template. If you are adding navigation to the first workflow step in the template, then the Search option does not return any results as there are no other
steps in the current workflow template. However, you can search for and select steps from other templates by selecting the Advanced Search option. The workflow step results from other templates are displayed in blue.
 You can always go back and add reject step and success steps later, after all the steps have been created. This is especially useful if you are creating the first step in the template, as the step you are going to
choose might not have been created yet.
d. Transition Details
1. Search for and select a Success Status that will be applied to an object once it has successfully completed this step.
2. Search for and select a Failure Status that will be applied to an object if it fails to complete this step.
What are the different type of workflow statuses?
A workflow status lets you know that an object is in a workflow, and how far along it is in that workflow. For example, the following default workflow step statuses are delivered:
Status
Description
!WF-C
Associated with one or more workflows that are in progress
WF
In a workflow
WF-A
Was approved in a workflow
WF-C
Has successfully completed a workflow completed
WF-I
Was issued in workflow
WF-R
Was rejected in workflow
WF-V
Was validated in workflow
e. Additional Details
In the Overdue Duration (in days) box, type the number of days that the step overdue notification should be displayed in the HxGN SDx2 To Do List.
3. Select FINISH to save the workflow step and display a graphical representation of the workflow step in the Create Workflow template page.
What options are displayed on the workflow step?
The following options are displayed on the workflow steps. You can use them to perform the specified action:
Click
To
Edit the current workflow step
Delete the current workflow step
Add a new success step
Add a new failure step
Hide the process steps between tasks
Display the process steps between tasks
Step definition
Workflow steps only exist in the context of a workflow template. Every step must be related to a step definition, which is used to describe the behavior of the step, and is selected from a library of step definitions. The following information is
specified on the step definition:
Step sequence
Step status
An accept route for all steps
A rejection route for validation or voting steps
The recipients of each step (can be users or roles)
The step duration (used to set the target date)
Create a workflow step definition
You can create a custom workflow step definition which can be used to define the behavior of the corresponding step.
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, click Create Workflow Step Definition.
2. In the Configure Workflow Step Definition window, type the required values and select the appropriate options as shown in each of the following tabs:
 You can click PREVIOUS or NEXT to navigate between different tabs.
General Details
Type a name and description for the step definition.
Workflow Step Type
Select an appropriate option from the Workflow step type list, based on the displayed for selection.
 Step types are properties of step classes. They determine the behavior and appearance of the step.
What are different types of workflow steps?
The step class controls the detailed behavior of the step. The basic behavior is identified by the step type. The step type dictates how the step is managed by the system; the methods determine the functionality available to the
user in the client.
The step types reflect the internal behavior of the step and cannot be altered without significant customization of the code.
The following default step types are delivered with the software:
Step type
Description
Purpose
I
Information
This step is for information only and is a special work step that
can support actions like Acknowledge Receipt. It contains no
subsequent steps.
W
Working
This step assigns work to the step's recipients.
P
Process
This step is a non-interactive step that performs an operation on
the object in the workflow.
V
Voting
This step is used to process the results of parallel approval
steps.
S
Sign Off
This is a special process step used for electronic document sign
off to indicate that the document has gone through the workflow.
Voting and sign off are forms of process steps.
When you select the Process step type, you can select the Is a merge process step option to combine a set of process steps and execute them in a defined order. For more information, see Create a merge workflow
process step type.
Email Options
Search for and select an appropriate email definition for each email notification type. The email notifications are sent to the recipients of a step when certain events occur during a workflow, such as when a step is completed.
The email notification options vary depending on the workflow step type.
What are different types of email notifications?
Email
Description
Reject email definition
The email message sent to a recipient whose step is rejected or is not completed successfully.
Action email definition
The email message sent to the recipients when the next step requires an action.
Sign off email definition
The email message sent to all relevant recipients when a step is signed off.
Overdue email definition
The email message sent to recipients whose To Do List has a step waiting to be done when a previous step is overdue.
Information email definition
The email message sent to all recipients when an information step is assigned to them.
Claim email definition
The email message sent to all recipients when the step action has been claimed.
 You can also click the Advanced Search option to search for and display a targeted set of results based on the type of object selected in the Advanced Search window.
If an email definition is not specified for a workflow step or a relevant event, then no email is sent.
You can click 
 to update the corresponding email definition.
You can click CREATE NEW EMAIL DEFINITION to create a new custom email definition. For more information, see Create a new email definition.
Todo List Actions
Select one or more Methods associated with the step class. These methods determine the actions that can be performed when the step is added in the Todo list.
3. Click FINISH to save the changes.
Create a workflow step checklist
What are workflow step checklists?
Each step in a workflow can have a checklist attached to it to be completed by the recipients.
Completion of these checklists can be mandatory as indicated by a * or optional.
The checklist items are presented in the To Do List against the step.
Checklists are defined with a name and description.
Each item in the check list is defined as a simple text string.
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, click Create Workflow Step Checklist.
2. In the Create New Checklist window, type the required values and select the appropriate options, as shown below:
Name - Type a name for the workflow checklist.
Description - Type a description for the workflow checklist.
3. Click 
Add New Checklist Item to add the item to the checklist. Type a Name and Description for the checklist item.
4. Click SAVE to save and apply the changes.
You can change the priority of the checklist items by using the drag-and-drop functionality.
You can click 
 to update the corresponding checklist item.
Update a workflow template
 The master workflow template (MSR) version delivered is supplied for reference only and cannot be used or edited.
You must have the Business Administrator role to create and update a workflow template.
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, click Workflow Templates.
2. Search for and select a workflow template to update.
3. Click Actions > Update to edit the workflow template in the Template Details window as described in Create a workflow template.
Copy a workflow template
The Copy Workflow Template feature allows you to copy any workflow template along with its steps to create a new version. You can make any changes to the copied template without effecting the original template definition, which can
be attached to any object.
 You can a create a copy of any workflow template including the MSR version.
1. Search for the workflow template to be copied.
2. Click Actions 
 > Copy Workflow Template.
3. Configure the copied template by following the steps in Create a workflow template.
 By default, the copied workflow template name is prefixed with the word Copy. You can change the name of the workflow template as required.
 This feature uses the SDxCopyWorkflowTemplate method related to the SDxCopyWorkflowTemplate client API. For more information, see SDxCopyWorkflowTemplate client API.
Delete completed workflows
You can manually delete any completed workflow instances for a selected workflow template, including any terminated instances. This can help reduce your database growth and maintain your systems performance.
Choose a workflow template, and select Actions 
 > Delete Completed Workflows.
 The Delete Completed Workflows action works asynchronously as a scheduler task across the system regardless of the configuration. However, be aware that intensive database activity could occur, potentially deleting a lot
of completed or terminated workflows.
 You can schedule the automatic deletion of completed workflows when you create or update a workflow template. For more information, see Manage workflow templates.
Create a merge workflow process step type
You can create a merge workflow process step type using the MergeProcessSteps class to build one step that executes a defined set of process steps in consecutive order.
To create a merge process workflow step type:
1. Create a workflow step definition, and in the Workflow Step Type tab, select the Process workflow step class.
2. Select the Is a merge process step option.
The Process class name box displays the MergeProcessSteps class name by default.
The Process steps box dynamically displays the available process steps you can add to the set. You can also search for other process steps to add.
3. Select the process steps and arrange them in the required order of execution.
 This merge process step type replaces the individual process steps and can help performance by reducing the database size.
Merge completed workflow steps
You have the option to automatically merge all process steps when a workflow completes, and replace them with a single unified step. All the redundant steps are then deleted. The benefit of merging similar process steps into a single
process step are that the database size may be significantly reduced for workflows that are used intensively. You can configure workflow templates in two ways:
Go to an existing workflow template that has yet to complete, and select Actions 
 > Merge process steps on completion option.
Before creating a new workflow template, in the System Options scroll to the Workflow options details section, and check the Merge process steps on completion option.
For more information, see Configure the system options.
Configure workflow step re-authentication
You can configure a workflow step to require password re-authentication to sign off and approve the step before the workflow can continue. This functionality is available for any workflow step when approval is required, such as those found
in the ToDo list, Change Management, Response Management, and Document Review, and so on.
To enable the workflow step re-authentication, set the Password required for signoff option on a workflow step class object. This sets the SPFPasswordReqdForSignoff property to True on the workflow step class object.
When the re-authentication option is enabled, you are prompted to re-enter your password as you try to submit a workflow step. A dialog supplied by the configured authentication provider handles the re-authentication process and allows
the workflow to continue.
If an error occurs, the user is taken back to the workflow point just before the re-authentication dialog was displayed. The step will not progress. For more information, see Workflow step password re-authentication errors.
 The re-authentication window may be displayed as a new tab instead.
Configuration control of workflows
Each workflow instance is related to a configuration so that the workflow processing and its data is separated by each configuration, such as a plant. The separation of workflows by configuration helps enhance the performance of systems
that have large datasets and use multiple project configurations.
Every workflow instance is processed using the workflow template class definition, which uses domain-specific tables to separate the workflow data by configuration or plant. This means that your To Do List only loads the workflow steps
for you to action for the configurations selected in your query scopes. This helps improve your To Do List usability and performance if you have a system with multiple configurations.
What are the details of the workflow templates?
The workflow template class definitions control the workflow processing by separating each workflow instance by the plant. The details of the class definitions, domain tables, and domain groups are shown as follows:
<Rel>
<IObject UID="WT.SPFWorkflowTemplateComponent" />

Workflow step recovery
An automatic recovery scheduler mechanism helps progress workflow process steps that are stuck due to service interruptions. For more information, see Recovery scheduler task.
Configure roles for workflows
You can configure the access to workflows by assigning roles to an object attached to the workflow.
Configure workflow access
You can configure the roles for users that can access and edit workflows using access groups. Access groups allows users to perform basic actions on a workflow or workflow step, such as view, reassign, or complete. All users can view
workflows in the delivered software.
 The WorkflowAdmin access group allows users with administration roles to edit workflows.
Configure roles to appear during reassignment
You can configure the roles to appear in the Reassign dialog box by configuring specific roles as a parameter on the Reassign method.
You can hide the roles panel on the Reassign dialog box.
1. Select the Reassign method, and select Update.
2. Select the Show roles option.
If no roles are configured on the Reassign method parameter, the roles appearing on the Reassign dialog box are those available to the current user in the configuration of the object on the workflow.
You can control the pagination during reassign by setting a page size value that is applied to both the roles and user tabs. For more information, see AddRecipient client API and MultiStepReAssign client API.
Configure roles to appear when adding users as workflow recipients
You can configure the roles to appear in the Add recipients dialog box by configuring specific roles or all roles as parameters on the Add recipients method.
You can hide the roles panel on the Add recipients dialog box.
1. Choose the Add recipients method, and select Update.
2. Clear the Show roles option.
 If no roles are configured on that parameter of the Add recipients method, the roles available on the Add recipients dialog box are those available to the current user in the configuration of the object of the workflow item.
Update role assignments for new users
When a new user is added to a role, you can add the new user to any started workflow so they can immediately begin to participate in the process.
The system automatically creates new workflow steps for any newly created users and sends notifications to their To Do List so they can immediately start to contribute in the specific workflow.
Choose the business object, workflow instance, or workflow template, and select Actions > Update Role Assignments.
If the action is performed on a instantiated workflow, the system only creates new workflow steps for the new users for that workflow.
If the action is performed on a workflow template, the system creates new workflow steps for the new users for all workflows.
If the action is performed on a business object, the system creates new workflow steps for the related workflow.
The Update Role Assignments methods scan through the workflow to find the steps that are assigned to the role and automatically creates new workflow steps for the new users.
Reassign tasks owned by one user to another user
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® HxGN SDx2 | Task Reassignment Configuration for Business Administrators
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/64de58fc64532d35ea2d7e84> .
You can use Task Reassignment Configuration to permanently transfer the ownership of objects owned by one user to another user, such as workflow steps, matrices, subscriptions, and documents.
 You must have the Business Administrator or System Administrator role to carry out task reassignment from one user to another.
For example:
1. In the Business Administration feature set, select Task Reassignment Configuration.
2. Search for the user for whom you want to reassign items.
To permanently transfer tasks, choose the user from the list, and select Permanent Reassignment.
To temporarily transfer tasks, choose the user from the list, and enter the From Date and To Date during which workflow tasks are temporarily assigned to another user.
3. In Assignee Filters, select the role criteria, Same Role, Managed by Role, Manage Role, or Any Role, to filter the list of users to those that can be assigned workflow steps.
4. In Assignee Selection, search for and select the user to which you want to reassign the items using one of the following:
In the Project row, select a user from the Assignee list in the row. This user is assigned the workflow tasks only for that project. You can select different users for each project.
Choose Assignee 
 options, and select a Common Assignee. A Common Assignee is a user that has a common role across a single project or multiple projects, and can be assigned your workflow tasks for all projects based
on that common role.
5. Select FINISH.
You can clear an assignment by choosing the user, and selecting Clear Reassignment.
The status of a reassignment is indicated by the following icons:
 - Permanently Reassigned User
 - Permanently Reassigned and Disabled User
Transfer ownership of items from one user to another
When a user is permanently disabled, such as when they leave the company, you can transfer ownership of items that the user owns, to another user. This can include documents, subscriptions, matrices, and much more. Notifications
should also be sent regarding the change of ownership to other users with that role assignment.
 For more information on how to transfer ownership to another user, see Reassign tasks owned by one user to another user.
To permit this transfer of ownership, the SPFAllowRelReassignment property is exposed on the IRelDef2 Interface, and is set on the following relationships in the HxGN SDx2 data model.
SPFItemOwner
SPFNotifyUser
SPFWorkflowReassignToUser
SPFRecipientUser
The SPFRelReassignmentCondition is a relationship definition between the IRelDef and ISPFCondition, to check that certain conditions exist before items are reassigned. The minimum cardinality is 0 and the maximum is * on the
relationship for End1. The minimum cardinality is 0 and the maximum is 1 on the relationship for End2. The relationship cardinality indicates that 1 or more relationship definitions can have at most one condition.
Control the reassignment pagination
You can control the pagination displayed during reassignment by setting the page size value found on the reassign method.
The page size value is applied to both the roles and user tabs and allows you refine them into manageable lists. This is especially useful if you have a large amount of users and roles.
 For more information on setting the page size value for reassignment pagination, see the AddRecipient client API and MultiStepReAssign client API.
Delivered workflows
HxGN SDx2 delivers several automated workflows. These workflows are described in detail in the following sections, including which steps should not be removed if you choose to copy and modify the workflow templates. For more
information about changes you can make to workflows, see Manage workflows.
Change request workflow
The Plant Change Request workflow is used to execute simple changes to your plant within your company or execute and collaborate on large projects with contractors.
The steps that make up the workflow are described in the following table.
 If you choose to edit the workflow, do not remove any steps marked as mandatory.
Step name
Description
Mandatory?
Comments
SOPSBranchBasedOnSaveAsDraftForBriefChange
Request
Evaluates whether the user selected Prepare Change or
Finish on the Create Change Request page.
The next step is Prepare change request if Prepare
