Cancelled Transmittal Workflow
The following information describes the cancelled transmittal workflow template (SCLBCancelledTransmittalLifeCycle). It explains what can and cannot be changed.
Details of internal process steps
 If the system fails to generate and attach any of the PDF renditions, it directs the workflow to the Correct failure step.
What does the workflow look like?
The steps that make up the workflow are described in the following table.
 If you choose to edit the workflow, do not remove any steps marked as mandatory.
Step name
Description
Mandatory?
Comments
Copy recipients from previous transmittal
Copies the recipients from the transmittal that has to be
canceled to send a notification that the transmittal has been
canceled.
The next step is Set transmittal state to CANCELLED.
Yes
This step is needed to inform the recipients that a
particular transmittal is canceled and would no longer be
accessible.
Set transmittal state to CANCELLED
Changes the status of the transmittal object to
CANCELLED.
If this step is removed, the transmittal object status will not
be modified.
The next step is Run and attach cancelled report.
Yes
This step is needed to clearly indicate that the
transmittal was cancelled. Otherwise, one might assume
the transmittal was issued.
Run and attach cancelled report
Runs the configured report to generate and attach the
transmittal cover letter. If successful, routes the workflow to
the Cancelled Transmittal Issued step. Otherwise, routes
the workflow to the Correct failure step.
No
Cancelled Transmittal Issued
Allows the user to complete the transmittal. This is the last
step of the workflow.
No
Correct failure
Allows the user to fix the errors that failed the report
generation and resubmit the query.
The next step is Run and attach cancelled report.
No
 This step is needed if the Run and attach
cancelled report step exists, as otherwise, the report
generation failure cannot be fixed.
Content extraction workflow
Data Capture uses the Content Extraction Workflow to extract content from the documents attached to a Content Extraction Task (CET). After the CET is created, it is automatically attached to the workflow.
What does the workflow look like?
The steps that make up the workflow are described in the following table.
 If you choose to edit the workflow, do not remove any steps marked as mandatory.
Process step name
Description
Mandatory
Comments
SDVJobConversionStep
Converts an SDx2 object to a Data Take-On job
object. If successful, it goes to the
ExtractContentStep, otherwise it goes to the
UpdateCETJobDetails step.
Yes
This step is needed as the further steps uses the
Data Take-On job to perform tasks.
ExtractContentStep
Initiates a backend service to send the tags,
representations, and relationships between the
following to the staging table:
Document and tag
Document and representation object
Tag and representation object
If successful, it continues to the
ReviewExtractedContentStep, otherwise it goes
to the UpdateCETJobDetails step.
Yes
This step is needed to extract the data and
relationships from the loaded documents.
ReviewExtractedContentStep
Allows the assigned user to review the extracted
content and approve the step. If approved, it
continues to the AlterStagingDataStep, otherwise
it goes to the StageCleanUp step.
No
UpdateCETJobDetails
Updates the CET job status and progress details,
and goes to An Error Has Occurred During
ContentExtraction, Please review Job and
Document Status for more details step.
Yes
This step is needed to keep track of CET job
status and progress.
AlterStagingDataStep
Removes any unclassified tags and relationships
from the staging tables. If successful, goes to the
ImplicitDeleteStep, otherwise it goes to the An
Error Has Occurred During Altering staging
data, Please review Job and Document Status
for more details step.
No
ImplicitDeleteStep
Terminates or deletes any objects and relationships
that were not identified after re-processing the
content. For example, during the initial content
extraction, tags such as Tag 1 and Tag 2 may be
identified. If a subsequent CET fails to recognize
Tag 2, the system will terminate the relationship to
Tag 2. Any OPCs not identified after a re-run of the
CET will be deleted. If this step is successful, the
workflow goes to the SDVExportStep, otherwise it
goes to the UpdateTaskStatusPostImplicitDelete
step.
Yes
This step is needed to terminate or delete any
unidentified tag relationships, OPCs after re-
processing the content.
SDVExportStep
Exports the data to the database. The next step is
the UpdateTaskStatusProcessStep.
Yes
This step is needed to export the extracted
content as DC objects to the database.
UpdateTaskStatusProcessStep
Updates the Content Extraction Task status. If this
step is successful goes to the StageCleanUp step,
otherwise goes to the
UpdateTaskStatusJobProgress step.
Yes
This step is needed to update the status of
content extraction task based on which user
determines further action.
UpdateTaskStatusJobProgress
Updates the CET job status and progress details.
The next step is the CorrectandRe-Export step.
Yes
This step is needed to keep track of CET job
status and progress.
StageCleanUp
Deletes the staging tables for jobs that have
completed with either Passed or Failed status. The
next step is UpdateStageCleanUpJobProgress
step.
No
UpdateStageCleanUpJobProgress
Updates the job status and progress about the
staging tables clean up information.
 This is the last step of the workflow.
No
UpdateTaskStatusPostImplicitDelete
Updates the Content Extraction Task status about
the ImplicitDeleteStep failure. The next step is
UpdateJobProgressPostImplicitDelete step.
Yes
This step is needed to update the status of
content extraction task based on which user
determines further action.
UpdateJobProgressPostImplicitDelete
Updates the CET job progress details based on the
PostImplicitDelete task status. The next step is
TargetCleanUp step.
Yes
This step is needed to keep track of CET job
status and progress.
CorrectandRe-Export
Allows the assigned user to re-validate the data and
approve the step to continue the workflow. If
successful, it goes back to the SDVExportStep,
otherwise it goes to the TargetCleanUp step.
Yes
This step is needed to re-verify the extracted
content if the export was not successful.
TargetCleanUp
Deletes the landing tables for the failed jobs from
the database. The next step is StageCleanUp step.
No
An Error Has Occurred During ContentExtraction, Please review Job and Document
Status for more details
An error notification step that informs the user that
the CET job has failed and the possible reasons for
it.
Yes
This step is needed to know the reasons for
failure.
An Error Has Occurred During Altering staging data, Please review Job and
Document Status for more details
An error notification step that informs the user that
staging data alteration process has failed and the
CET job status has been updated. The next step is
StageCleanUp.
Yes
This step is needed to know the reasons for
failure.
Configure dynamic assignment of recipients
You can configure any workflow step to dynamically assign recipients. Recipients are users or roles that are related to the source object to which the workflow is attached. You can assign recipients based on a property on the current object
or a related object.
When you create the workflow template you:
Add a step definition using the PropagateRecipients process class.
Define the reason for receipt.
Define the notification format for users or roles.
The PropagateRecipients process step class supports multiple arguments with the following conditions:
If an argument relationship definition expansion returns any values, the process step will assign recipients and will not process any additional arguments.
If the relationship definition expansion on all arguments does not return any value, the default recipient for the step is assigned.
Each argument is a combination of RelDefUID with direction (a single relationship definition, multiple relationship definitions, edge definition, or property definition), reason for receipt, recipient format, and workflow information steps
delimited by ~. This set of parameters can be repeated with different values by separating each set with a comma. Each of the four parameters are described below:
Parameter1: Defines the relationship, edge, or property definition that will be used to find the users or roles to assign to the step.
Parameter2: Defines the reason for receipt.
Parameter3: Defines the format in which the recipient receives files associated with the workflow.
Parameter4: Displays workflow information steps to the user if set to True. If the argument is set to False, the workflow information steps are not displayed.
Example 1: PropagateRecipients,OBJ->SPFFolderUser_12~RFR_I~Email,OBJ->SPFRoleFolder_21~RFR_C~1 x A4. This example shows the arguments to assign recipients with configurable reasons for receipt and formats.
Arg1: SPFFolderUser_12~RFR_I~Email
SPFFolderUser_12 - If the object in the workflow is associated with a folder with related users, this argument finds any users related to that folder and automatically assigns recipients based on that relationship.
RFR_I - This value indicates that the users found with the edge definition SPFFolderUser_12 are assigned a Reason for Receipt of Information.
Email - This value indicates that the users found with the edge definition SPFFolderUser_12 will receive the information as an email.
 If the Argument1 relationship definition expansion returns any values, the process step will assign recipients and will not process Argument2.
Arg2: SPFRoleFolder_21~RFR_C~1 x A4
SPFRoleFolder_21 - If the object in the workflow is associated with a folder with related roles, then this argument finds any roles related to that folder and automatically assigns recipients based on that relationship.
RFR_C - This value indicates that the roles found with the edge definition SPFRoleFolder_21 are assigned a Reason for Receipt of Comment.
1 x A4 - This value indicates that the roles found with the edge definition SPFRoleFolder_21 will receive the information in printed format, on size A4 paper.
The users or roles defined using this process class overrides any recipients assigned in the Assigned Recipients dialog box.
If the Reason for Receipt is
Not set, the default for any information step is Information only. For other steps, the default is Comment.
Set to Information, the software checks the system options to determine if the Display workflow information steps option is selected. If this option is selected, then a workflow step is placed in the recipients To Do list. The
SPFWFSRActionInBox property will be set on the recipient based on the system options setting. For more information on the system options, see Configure the system options.
If the format is not set on Parameter3, the value is blank.
Example 2: Propagate recipient to the originating user related to the workflow object
Step class: SCLBPropagateRecipientProjComsFromUser and SCLBSetProjComsFromUser
PropagateRecipients,OBJ->SDAProjectCommunicationFromUser_12
Example 3: Propagate recipient to the targeted user who is related to the workflow object
Step class: SCLBPropagateRecipientProjComsToUser
PropagateRecipients,OBJ->SDAProjectCommunicationToUser_12
The following examples show how you can assign recipients based on a user property on the current object or a related object. The supported arguments are a property on the current object, a property on a related object, or a related
object. By default, the software checks the LoginName property. If you want to propagate recipients based on the long name or another property, you must add the PropertyDefUID to check that the user has access to an object.
Example 4: Assign recipient to the source object
When a technical query object is attached to a workflow, you can use the following user properties to assign the recipient:
PropagateRecipients,OBJ.SCLBProjComsInitiatedBy
Arg: SCLBProjComsInitiated assigns the recipient based on who created the technical query.
PropagateRecipients,OBJ.SCLBProjComsRespondedUser
Arg: SCLBProjComsRespondedUser assigns the recipient based on who responded to the technical query.
Example 5: Assign recipient to the related object
When a document is attached, or related, to a technical query (the source object), you can use the following user properties to assign the recipient.
PropagateRecipients,OBJ->SCLBTQReferenceDocVersions_12->SPFRevisionVersions_21.SDADocRevAuthor(SPFLoginName)
Arg: OBJ->SCLBTQReferenceDocVersions_12->SPFRevisionVersions_21 evaluates the technical query object in the workflow, then navigates from end 1 to end 2 of the SCLBTQReferenceDocVersions relationship to find any
document version attached to the technical query. If it finds a document version, it navigates from end 2 to end 1 of the SPFRevisionVersions relationship to find the document revision.
SDADocRevAuthor(SPFLoginName) finds the author of the document revision and assigns the author as the recipient using the login name, for example, jadoe.
Example 6: Assign recipient to the source object using the long name
PropagateRecipients,OBJ.SCLBProjComsResolvedByUser(Name)
Arg: SCLBProjComsResolvedByUser(Name) uses the user's long name, for example, Jane Doe, to assign the recipient based on who resolved the technical query.
Configure schedulers
 Only administrators have access to the Scheduler menu where they can create, start, stop, restart, update, or terminate a scheduler.
As documents and objects are loaded to the server, they are processed using Schedulers as separate tasks before the data is published to the system. Schedulers are used to manage these tasks and process them through the workflow
corresponding to the type of task.
Task specific information is stored in the server for each task sent to a Scheduler. Each task tells the Scheduler what service or process to run, when to run it, and where to run it. This also contains information for the data to process a task
once, a number of times, or repeatedly on a specified schedule. Tasks are then processed and assigned to appropriate workflow for applicable users to review or action accordingly.
Each time you create a new scheduled task, the related Scheduler is prompted to poll for tasks after a defined polling interval. The Scheduler then gathers a set number of tasks and retrieves that number to start processing. When a set of
tasks is complete, the Scheduler will poll again for more tasks after the set polling interval.
To display the list of Schedulers available in the system:
In the System Administration feature set, select Schedulers.
How does a Scheduler poll for tasks?
Schedulers are used to poll for tasks and manage them in a controlled process. If there are tasks ready for execution, it sends them to the appropriate service for processing. The scheduler processor then picks up the tasks that are ready
to be processed and sends them to any available services that are ready to receive and execute the tasks. The HxGN SDx2 application server processes only the tasks that match the specified criteria.
What are the default Schedulers?
The following Schedulers are exposed by default:
Main Scheduler
Report Scheduler
Upgrade Scheduler
User Management Scheduler
Termination Scheduler
Job Cleanup Scheduler
Change Request Scheduler
Integration Scheduler
Export Task Scheduler
PBS Sync Scheduler
Sync Scheduler
Recovery Scheduler
Indexing Scheduler
Automatic ReIndexing Scheduler
What is the polling interval?
The Scheduler polling interval is the regular time the database verifies if any tasks need to be gathered and processed. You can set the Schedulers to poll the server sites at specified intervals that you can configure when you create or
update a Scheduler. The polling interval default value can vary according to the system to eliminate excessive processing time. If the polling interval affects your system performance, you can set the Scheduler to poll at less frequent
intervals by increasing the polling value to help minimize any performance impact that polling is having on your system. Administrators can set the polling value for each individual Scheduler on the Update form.
The minimum polling interval you can set for a Scheduler is 10 minutes (600,000 ms). When a Scheduler is free and reaches the set polling interval, the maximum number of tasks that can be processed at the same time is limited to
two.
If the scheduler is polling at maximum capacity, any new tasks are not processed until the polling interval is reached and the Scheduler becomes free.
Schedulers and their default polling intervals
Schedulers
Default polling interval times
Main Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Report Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Upgrade Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
User Management Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Termination Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Job Cleanup Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Change Request Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Integration Scheduler
60 minutes (3,600,000 ms)
Export Task Scheduler
10 minutes (600,000 ms)
PBS Sync Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Sync Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Recovery Scheduler
60 minutes (3,600,000 ms)
Indexing Scheduler
10 minutes (600,000 ms)
What is a PBS sync scheduler?
You can use the PBS sync scheduler to replicate the Plant Breakdown Structure of a consolidated design component based on the Common Schema into any required tool design component in HxGN SDx2.
Create a scheduled task to look for changes to the PBS structure in the consolidated design component and update those changes in the required tool design component.
How can I create a scheduled task for the PBS sync scheduler?
You can create your own XML load file using the following sample XML to create a scheduled task for the PBS sync scheduler.
<SPFSchedulerTask>
<IObject UID="SCHDTask_DC1PBSSyncSchedulerTask" Name="DC1PBSSyncSchedulerTask" Description="SIO PBS sync scheduler task for DC1 domain" />
<ISPFSchedulerTaskPrimary />
<ISPFSchedulerTask SPFSchTskFrequency="-1" SPFSchTskEnabled="True" SPFSchTskInterval="1800000" SPFSchTskStartDate="2021/01/01-00:00:00:000" SPFSchTskLocked="False"
SPFSchTskContextRoles="RL_Business_Administrator" SPFSchTskStatus="e1SchdReady" SPFSchTskContextUser="BusinessAdmin" />
<ISPFAdminItem />
<ISDxiHubPBSSyncTask DesignComponentDomainToSync="DC1" />
</SPFSchedulerTask>
 Replace "DC1" in the XML file with your design component domain name for the DesignComponentDomainToSync property.
For more information on how to create XML load files and import them into HxGN SDx2, see Import data.
When the PBS sync scheduler is run for the first time in any design component, it copies the complete PBS structure of the consolidated design component into the tool design component. During subsequent polling, it will copy only
the changes made to any created, modified, and terminated objects in the PBS structure from the consolidated design component.
The default polling interval is set to 30 minutes. You can change the polling interval based on the frequency of changes to your PBS structure.
Ensure that the PBS sync scheduler has synced the PBS changes for the required tool design component before you run any jobs that reference the PBS structure. Otherwise job validation will fail when the Check Exists rule is run
on the PBS objects.
The PBS sync scheduler will eventually be replaced by the Compare Auto Commit scheduler.
Create a scheduler
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Create Scheduler.
2. On the New Scheduler form, select the properties from the different tabs available and enter the values required, such as Name and Description.
3. Select the Scheduler site from the list if you have created a dedicated scheduler site.
4. Set the Polling interval.
What is the polling interval?
The Scheduler polling interval is the regular time the database verifies if any tasks need to be gathered and processed. You can set the Schedulers to poll the server sites at specified intervals that you can configure when you create
or update a Scheduler. The polling interval default value can vary according to the system to eliminate excessive processing time. If the polling interval affects your system performance, you can set the Scheduler to poll at less
frequent intervals by increasing the polling value to help minimize any performance impact that polling is having on your system. Administrators can set the polling value for each individual Scheduler on the Update form.
The minimum polling interval you can set for a Scheduler is 10 minutes (600,000 ms). When a Scheduler is free and reaches the set polling interval, the maximum number of tasks that can be processed at the same time is
limited to two.
If the scheduler is polling at maximum capacity, any new tasks are not processed until the polling interval is reached and the Scheduler becomes free.
Schedulers and their default polling intervals
Schedulers
Default polling interval times
Main Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Report Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Upgrade Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
User Management Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Termination Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Job Cleanup Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Change Request Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Integration Scheduler
60 minutes (3,600,000 ms)
Export Task Scheduler
10 minutes (600,000 ms)
PBS Sync Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Sync Scheduler
30 minutes (1,800,000 ms)
Recovery Scheduler
60 minutes (3,600,000 ms)
Indexing Scheduler
10 minutes (600,000 ms)
5. Select the Enabled check box to activate the Scheduler after creation, and click SAVE.
Start or stop a scheduler
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Schedulers. This displays a list of the schedulers available in the system.
2. Select a Scheduler from the list, and click Actions 
 > Scheduler > Start or Stop.
Restart a scheduler
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Schedulers. This displays a list of the schedulers available in the system.
2. Select a Scheduler from the list that has stopped processing, and click Actions 
 > Scheduler > Restart.
Update a scheduler
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Schedulers. This displays a list of the schedulers available in the system.
2. Select a Scheduler from the list, and click Actions 
 > Scheduler > Update.
 Select the properties from the different tabs available and update the values as required.
Terminate a scheduler
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Schedulers. This displays a list of the schedulers available in the system.
2. Select a scheduler from the list, and click Actions 
 > Scheduler > Terminate.
Query for scheduled tasks
You can view all the scheduled tasks for each scheduler in the database, including tasks in process, waiting, or failed, and so on.
In the System Administration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks. This displays a list of the scheduled tasks available in the system.
Each task result displays information about the task processor, the task status, the dates on which the task was created and will end, frequency, and so on.
Retry a failed task
In the event of a server failure or other unexpected database interruption, any scheduled tasks that were in process are set to a Failed status when the scheduler service is restarted. An exception message displays against each failed task
containing details of the error.
How can I get notified when a task fails?
You can use the SPFSchTskNotifyRoles relationship definition to relate a role to a scheduler task. If that scheduler task fails, users with the related role receive a notification of the failure, including the exception message. The type of
notification received depends on the user's preferences, see Set the user options.
 Create and import a load file to relate roles to scheduler tasks using the relationship definition. For more information, see Import data.
You can retry any failed tasks after the server connection is restored or when the Scheduler is restarted.
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks.
2. Select a failed task to retry in the Scheduled Tasks results, and select Actions 
 > Task > Retry Task
Diagnosis
If you haven't enabled notifications for the failed task, you can diagnose what caused the failure by viewing the exception message for each failed task, which is partially displayed in the Failure Msg column for the task. You can see the full
exception message in the task details:
1. In the Scheduled Tasks results, select the name of the failed task.
2. In the Main Details tab, the full exception message is displayed in the Failed Msg dialog.
 Do not remove failed documents from the Scheduler Tasks results. Documents are queued to the appropriate queue based on their published order. Removing a document from the queue that has a failed task can result in
loss of data or could cause your data to be out of sync with the system.
Update a task
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks. This displays a list of the scheduled tasks available in the system.
2. Select a task from the Scheduled Tasks results, and click Actions 
 > Update.
 Select the properties from the different tabs available and update the values as required.
Terminate a task
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks. This displays a list of the scheduled tasks available in the system.
2. Select a task from the Scheduled Tasks results, and click Actions 
 > Terminate.
 The task is terminated in the Scheduled Task list, but details are retained in the system.
Delete a task
1. In the System Administration feature set, select Scheduled Tasks. This displays a list of the scheduled tasks available in the system.
2. Select a task from the Scheduled Tasks results, and click Actions 
 > Delete.
 The task is deleted from the Scheduled Task list and no longer available in the system.
Recovery scheduler task
An automatic recovery scheduler mechanism is provided with the software that processes steps that are stuck in the Ready to Start (RS) state due to service interruptions from system failures or unexpected conditions. The recovery
scheduler automates the recovery or rejection of any stuck workflow steps to ensure that the workflows progress smoothly without requiring any manual intervention.
The ISDxWFStepRecoverySchedulerTask automatically identifies workflow steps that are stuck in the Ready to Start (RS) state. It queries the workflow steps at intervals based on the frequency configured in the ProcessStep property.
The process either recovers the step or rejects the step, once the maximum number of recovery attempts is reached, and allows the workflow to proceed efficiently.
To support this functionality, four property definitions are defined for the ISPFObjectWorkflowStep interface definition:
SDxWFStepAliveDate
This indicates whether a specific process step is active.
If the value is not set, the recovery scheduler uses the step's start date.
SDxWFStepExecutionTime
Specifies the maximum timeout value for the process execution, measured in minutes.
If the maximum timeout vale is not set, a default timeout of one hour is applied.
StepRecoveryIteration
Tracks the number of times the scheduler attempts to retry a stuck workflow step.
The number increases incrementally with each attempt to retry a step.
MaxRecoveryAttempts
Defines the maximum number of retry attempts allowed for a stuck workflow step.
This limit can be customized for each workflow step to prevent repeated processing of stuck steps.
On processing
The recovery scheduler queries all workflow steps in the Ready to Start (RS) state and compares the SDxWFStepAliveDate value in each step with the time specified in the SDxWFStepExecutionTime property. If a step exceeds the
execution time, the scheduler initiates a recovery attempt.
 For long-running workflows, set an appropriate value for SDxWFStepExecutionTime property to ensure the correct handling of workflows and avoid recovery issues.
On recovery attempt
Each recovery step attempt is recorded as the StepRecoveryIteration value. If the attempts reach the MaxRecoveryAttempts value, the workflow step is bypassed in future recovery runs. This limit helps prevent repeated processing of
permanently stuck workflow steps, improving overall scheduler efficiency, and enables the recovery of other steps that can be reprocessed.
 For long-running workflow steps, it is essential you periodically update the SDxWFStepAliveDate property to reflect activity and prevent premature recovery attempts.
Handling the recovery of AMQP Steps
Once a message is published by the workflow to the HxGN SDx2 AMQP queue, the consumer service sets the SDxWFStepAliveDate property before starting the process class execution. This enables the recovery scheduler task to
detect and process the recovery of messages during outages.
If the recovery scheduler detects an AMQP step that is still stuck in the workflow and exceeds the configured process execution time, the scheduler task will re-publish the message so that the workflow can resume processing.
If the SDxWFStepAliveDate property is not set, any published message in the queue for an extended period of time may be lost.
The SDxAMQPStepRecoveryTime property is set to the default value of seven (7) days.
Recovery Logic for AMQP Steps
During processing, the recovery scheduler identifies the AMQP steps where the SDxAMQPStepRecoveryTime property has exceeded seven (7) days and the SDxWFStepAliveDate property has no set value. This indicates that the
process class execution for those AMQP steps has not started. The AMQP steps identified are then re-published so that the consumer service can process those steps.
If the SDxWFStepAliveDate property value exists, the process class execution has started. However, the recovery depends on the value set for the SDxWFStepExecutionTime property.
If no AMQP step recovery has started, the step is recovered when the time exceeds the SDxAMQPStepRecoveryTime property.
Excluding process steps from recovery
For process steps that do not require recovery because they have already been handled, an ExcludeRecovery property is set in the runtime arguments.
If set to False, the recovery is enabled. This is the default.
If set to True, those steps are excluded from the recovery process, avoiding unnecessary intervention.
 If a recovery is not needed, then the ExcludeRecovery property needs to be called. For example, the following is a step call for an SDVUnzip:
Public Sub New(ByVal RunStepAsync As Boolean, Optional ByVal RecoverStep As Boolean = False, Optional ByVal ExcludeRecovery As Boolean = False)
mblnIsAsync = RunStepAsync
mblnCanRecover = RecoverStep
mblnExcludefromRecovery = ExcludeRecovery
Scheduler task fails with error message
Issue
Any of the scheduler tasks may fail with some of the following error messages. This list of error messages is not exhaustive.
SDxAdmin Concurrency violation error occurred while updating the records
Failed to terminate interface records in the database
Error has occurred during Deferred Cancel Methods
SDxAdmin Failed to insert property records into the database
An error has occurred in the cache service
Solution
You must investigate the reason for failure and retry the failed task. If the issue persists, contact your administrator. For more information, see Retry a failed task.
Configure watermarks
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2® | Watermarks <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/631f33d40cd9ea30adf25fce>
Watermarks are text or images that may be placed on the alternate renditions and printed copies of files. Watermarks can be added to documents when required. You can add an existing image as a watermark and adjust its size, or you
can create a text watermark using the options for font and size. You can also apply dynamic values for the watermarks.
Watermarks can be used in documents for different purposes such as placing a company logo in alternate renditions and printed copies, adding the date of printing on the printed copies, and so forth. Multiple watermarks can be created in
the system and used in different scenarios. You can control the visibility of watermarks in different scenarios using the configurations of watermark options. For more information, see Create a watermark.
Create a watermark
1. In the Business Administration feature set, select Create Watermark.
2. Type a Name and Description for the watermark.
3. Enter the Details, Position and Alignment, and Style.
Watermark options
Option
Description
Type
Select TEXT to create a text watermark using Watermark Text, Font, Size Type, and Font Size or Relative Scale.
Select IMAGE to create an image watermark using File (select the image file saved in your computer) and Relative Scale.
Watermark Text
Enter the watermark text or dynamic values such as:
File name: &Obj.Name&
Creation time: &env.currenttime&
Font
Select a font available on the server machine for the watermark text.
 You can select the All Unicode font to display special characters such as technical symbols and non-Latin alphabets as the
watermark text.
Size Type
Use numerical values representing the absolute font size by selecting Font Size.
Use percentage of the total page size by selecting Relative Scale.
Font Size
Based on the size type, select a numerical value (starting from 0) or a decimal figure (from 0 to 1).
Horizontal Alignment
Select a position among left, center, and right.
Vertical Alignment
Select a position among top, center, and bottom.
Offset X Coordinate
Adjust the position along horizontal alignment.
Offset Y Coordinate
Adjust the position along vertical alignment.
Rotation
Select an angle between 0° and 360°. For values above 360°, the angle will be adjusted by subtracting 360° from the given value.
As Background
Keeps the watermark in the background.
Color
Select a color for the watermark text.
Opacity
Set the opacity of the watermark.
4. Configure the usage of the watermark in the Use section. You have the following options:
Apply the watermark on specific renditions and their thumbnails by selecting Active for Alternate Rendition and the relevant condition.
Apply the watermark on printed files by selecting Active for Print and the relevant condition.
The Condition, configured at the file level, sets the criteria for watermark usage. If unselected, the watermark will always be applied.
5. Select Finish to create the watermark.
Based on the conditions for the actual design object, multiple watermarks can appear on the alternate rendition.
If you want to update an existing watermark, search for it, select Update Watermark, and make the changes.
After changing the Size Type in the Update Watermark screen, you should ensure that valid values are used for the Font Size.
Preview a watermark
You can generate a temporary PDF to preview the configured watermark stamped on it.
1. Click PREVIEW in the New Watermark dialog box to display the Preview watermark on a sample file dialog box.
2. Select an appropriate value for the PDF size.
Size options
Option
Description
Page Size
Select the size (from A0 to A10)
Select the orientation (between Portrait and Landscape)
Custom Size
Enter Width and Height.
Select Units (Millimeters and Inches)
Sample File
Choose a file saved in your system.
 The file size limit is 2 MB.
You can also use the preview watermark functionality in the Update Watermark screen. For more information, see Create a watermark.
You can preview the file formats supported in the PDFTron View Control. For more information, see What file formats are supported?.
Configure bookmarks for the PDF generated from the data books
You can configure the display of bookmarks based on which the PDF generated from the data books will specify the bookmarks. To do this, you must complete the following steps:
1. Find the method GenerateDatabookMergedRendition and select Actions 
 > Update Method.
2. In the Properties tab, in the Specify dynamic expression for bookmark box (Arg3), type an appropriate dynamic expression for bookmark display.
3. Select Finish to update the method.
What are the list of expressions that can be used to customize the bookmark display?
Expression
To display
&Obj->EDG_FileVersionMaster->SDADataChapterMaster_21-
>SDADatabookChapterComposition_12.Name&
Dynamic chapters parent
&Obj->EDG_FileToRevision->SDADataChapterCompositionRevision_21-
>SDADatabookChapterComposition_12.Name&
Static chapters parent
&Obj->EDG_FileVersionMaster->SDADataChapterMaster_21.Name&
Dynamic chapter
&Obj->EDG_FileToRevision->SDADataChapterCompositionRevision_21.Name&

&Obj->EDG_FileVersionMaster.Name&
Document names
Regenerate metadata
You must refresh the metadata following any changes to the Web API Services configuration, such as exposing a class definition or an interface definition.
To regenerate the metadata directly, you can use the regenerate metadata function.
In the System Administration feature set, select Regenerate Metadata.
Regenerate metadata using Web API v2
To regenerate metadata for a schema using Web API v2, you can use a REST client to initiate a metadata regeneration by sending a POST request to the RegenerateMetadata API Services action. For example,
<Access_URL>/api/v2/RegenerateMetadata
Regenerate metadata using Web API v3
To regenerate metadata for a schema using Web API v3, you can only use a REST client to initiate a metadata regeneration by sending a POST request to the RegenerateMetadata API Services action. For example:
<Access_URL>/api/v3/RegenerateMetadata
Set up additional Project features
Once you have set up projects, contracts, and the organizations and users working on these, you need to configure additional options.
Configure issue purposes
Each time a document is created, it can be assigned a specific issue purpose. This designates why the document is being issued, such as a draft for review or design, and so on.
Create an issue purpose
1. Select the Business Administration feature set, and select Create Issue Purpose.
 The issue purpose is always created under Configuration Top, independent of the currently selected scope.
2. On the New Issue Purpose page, provide the information in the Main details and Issue Purpose details sections.
 In the Sequence box, type a value to determine the order of appearance. The lower the number, the higher is the order of appearance.
 To relate new issue purposes to a plant or project, see Manage the plant configuration details and Manage the project configuration details.
View and update issue purposes
To view
Select Issue Purposes in the Business Administration feature set.
To update
1. Search for your issue purpose in the Business Administration feature set.
2. Click Actions 
 > Update.
To terminate
1. Search for your issue purpose in the Business Administration feature set.
2. Click Actions 
 > Terminate.
Configure submittals
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Administration | Submittals <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/paths/6584626c26f700b8401bf67d/>
You configure the mapping for importing submittals using Data Take-On. Data Take-On imports files in comma separated values (CSV) format into the staging area where the validation takes place. Import definitions are used to map the
column headers in the input file to the correct column headers for objects, properties, and relationships in the staging area. The import mapping not only uses the physical column headers that already exist in the input data file, but it also
supports other column headers that are computed or provided by the user during the import process.
Data Take-On validates the quality of the incoming data in the supplied files before they are loaded into the system.
You also configure submittals using the CreateClassObjAndLoad client API, which is used to create classified objects and provide the capability to upload files into the Data Take-On workflow using a CSV control file.
For more information, see CreateClassObjAndLoad client API.
Validation rules for MDR submittals
The following table provides the validation rules applicable on MDR deliverable submittals.
Validation rule
Description
Check Category is not empty
Checks if the category column in the CSV has a value.
Check Document Title is not empty
Checks if the document title column in the CSV has a value.
Check Issue date value is not empty
Checks if the issue date column in the CSV has a value.
Validate IssuePurposes For Config
Checks if one or more issue purposes in the CSV are not related to the current plant or project.
ValidMDR
Checks if a contract is related to an MDR and matches the given MDR
ValidateDocHasContract
Checks if the given document is related to another contract.
Configure management properties on contractor documents
Typically, contractor documents loaded through a submittal process are set to an Issued status and can be updated by the owner operator. After a contractor document is submitted and loaded into a system, you can update security
properties, such as security level and review priority. In addition, you can allow documents from the contractors who are no longer active on the project, to be shared with the project.
The following table explains the privileges a user has on the properties of contractor documents.
A user role with this privilege can
Security Code
Workflow priority (this property can also be set when creating or updating a Technical Query)
WBS reference, discipline, and secondary disciplines
Document editing
 A user role with this privilege
cannot set the collaborating
organization.
View
Set
Update
Document control
Update
Set
Update
Collaborating organization
To update the collaborating organization, do the following:
In the Update document form, manage the organizations in the Collaboration tab.
If the document is signed off, select Actions > Update Collaborating Organizations.
Collaborating organization users can only see the documents which are in current revision state, if created by other organization users. If a document is revised, the collaborating organization user cannot see the new version of the
revised document until that document version is signed off.
How do I operate and maintain a plant?
HxGN SDx2 allows you to capture, organize, and link large volumes of facility information and provide web-based access to everyone who needs it.
Using work packages
Work packages help organize, plan, schedule, and manage your work by grouping data, documents, and tags into manageable data sets. Work packages support the planning workflow for plant maintenance or inspection work packages,
or work orders, created in other plant operations software, such as SAP.
You can mark up documents and drawings with comments or changes about the expected work, then attach them to the work package.
Based on the type of role assigned to you, you can create or query different types of work packages:
Maintenance work package
Inspection work package
Engineering work package
Contract work package.
For information about the functionality available to a role, see the HxGN SDx2 Roles <https://docs.hexagonali.com/v/u/JGCuEhM~7gm9q8wykJGH2A> matrix.
Create a work package
Engineering work package
 You must have the Business Administrator or System Administrator role to create, update, view an engineering work package.
 You can create an engineering work package only when the configuration is set to project scope.
1. In the Work Packages feature set, select any of the following feature based on the type of the work package to be created
Create Engineering Work Package - to create an engineering work package
2. Enter the required information in Description.
A unique name for the work package is auto-generated by the ENS definition.
The work package is created with the Status set to Open.
Inspection and Maintenance Work packages
 You must have a Planner role to create, update, view inspection and maintenance work packages.
1. In the Work Packages feature set, select any of the following feature based on the type of the work package to be created
Create Inspection Work Package - to create an inspection work package
Create Maintenance Work Package - to create maintenance work package
2. Enter the required information in Description.
 You can select an Approver role, and use the Approver user box to search for approvers to add to your workflow.
Contract work package
 You must have the Business Administrator or System Administrator role to create or update a work package.
1. In the Work Packages feature set, select any of the following feature based on the type of the work package to be created
Create Contract Work Package - to create a contract work package
2. Enter the required information in Description.
3. Enter required information in Name, Internal Organizations and External Organizations.
A unique name for the work package is auto-generated by the ENS definition.
The work package is created with the Status set to Open.
What is the impact of setting the default response days for a contract work package?
The following table explains the impact of setting the default response days properties in the New Contract page on the project communication workflows:
 If the default response day property value is empty, the current date is propagated as the target date on the workflow step.
Project communication type
The default response days value is used to calculate
Outgoing technical query
The target date set on the Initial review workflow step.
Incoming technical query
The target date set on the Initial review workflow step.
Outgoing action
The target date set on the Perform action and respond workflow step.
Outgoing transmittal
The target date set on the Transmittal issued workflow step.
 For example, if the default response days is 10, the system adds 10 days to the current date and sets the result as the target date on the workflow step, excluding any non-working days.
Send the work package
Select:
Prepare Content to send the work package to the Prepare Content step. This step allows you to update the work package with related documents, drawings, tags, and comments. For more information, see Prepare the content.
Finish to either send the work package for approval, or to complete the step.
Prepare the content
 You must be assigned a Planner role to complete this step.
The Prepare Content page is automatically displayed if you select Prepare Content when creating a work package. Alternatively, if you have saved a draft of the work package, you can open it from your To Do List 
 by selecting Add
drawings and documents or comments, followed by Actions 
 > Prepare Content.
1. Select:
Manage Documents or Manage Tags to relate documents or tags to the work package. For more information, see Manage related information.
Relationship Builder to relate change requests to the work package. For more information, see Manage related information.
Manage Markup Layers to choose the markup layers to include in the consolidated files of the work package. For more information, see Manage markup layers.
 In the Manage Markup Layers page, any markup layers created for the work package are automatically selected.
Manage related files to attach additional files from your computer to the work package. Attached files are displayed in the All Files tab.
Potential Conflicts or Potential Impacts to Analyze the impact of the work package.
 Select 
 next to a document to show the associated files.
2. Select COMPLETE to generate PDF renditions of documents that have attached files, including markups. The work package is then sent for approval if required. A consolidated PDF is also generated including all the documents
attached to the work package.
You can also select SAVE AS DRAFT to save the work package so that you can continue working on it later.
A watermark showing the current date is added to the PDFs so that it is easy to see when a printed copy is out of date. For more information on configuring the watermark, see Configure watermarks. If you want to create a PDF with
a customized watermark, use the workflow process steps described in Create document PDF renditions and Create a consolidated PDF rendition object.
A PDF is not generated when a work package has no documents.
If a PDF rendition fails to generate and attach, the system directs the workflow to the Correct Failure step. For more information on PDF rendition errors, see Correct PDF rendition generation failures.
Manage related information
Manage documents or tags
1. Select Manage Documents or Manage Tags to display the documents or tags in the Results window.
2. Select the documents or tags to include.
You can also search for documents or tags in the search bar.
You can use the Filter by options to return more targeted search results.
3. Select RELATE.
The related documents and tags are displayed in their respective DOCUMENT VERSIONS and TAGS tabs. You can use these tabs to relate additional documents and tags:
a. In the relevant tab, search for required documents or tags using the search bar.
b. Select the documents or tags in the list of results, and select RELATE.
You can also use FILTER options when searching for documents.
Manage change requests
1. Select Relationship Builder, and use the Search tab to search for change requests to relate.
 A change request can only be related to a work package when it is in the same configuration.
2. Select the items you want to relate, and select Relate.
Analyze the impact of the work package
Work package scoping lets you assess the impacts and conflicts caused by documents and tags related to a work package or a change request.
You must have the Planner role assigned to scope the work package.
You can perform work package scoping when at least one document or tag is related.
The documents and tags associated with the work package must have relationships created for document-tag and tag-document.
If you open the document in the View and Markup page (and it is a published Hexagon model that has been transferred to the Facilities Data Warehouse using Data Take-On), you can select Show repeated results 
 in the
Visual Intelligence panel to group and view tags belonging to more than one work package. For more information, see Using visual intelligence to analyze your data.
Scope a work package
Select Potential Conflicts or Potential Impacts.
The items in the Potential Impacts and Potential Conflicts lists can be filtered so that you can easily find the required items.
You can also scope a work package by selecting the work package followed by Actions 
 > Work Package Scoping.
Add or remove documents or tags
When you select a tag, document, or change request, additional items that could be impacted are displayed. These can be easily added to the work package.
1. Select the tag, document, or change request in the list.
2. In the Potential Impacts list, select the tags and documents to add, and select Apply to relate them to your work package.
To remove a related item from the work package, select Delete 
 next to the item.
 Expand 
 any document to find out more about its status and issue purpose.
Review conflicts
1. Select the tag, document, or change request in the list.
2. In the Potential Conflicts list, review the items that could conflict with your work package.
 Work packages and change requests in the closed state are not listed.
3. Select the item to view the information for the work package.
4. Select Work Package Scoping from the item's 
 Edit menu to view its impact analysis information.
Update work package details
1. Select the work package, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. Update the required information and select Update.
Review content
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx, SDx2 | Reviewing a Work Package <https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/65f3083e519b7d96a3778146>
1. Select the Review work package item in your To Do List 
, and then select Review work package from the Actions 
 menu.
2. Select:
 > Edit to add any comments.
Potential Impacts or Potential Conflicts to view the scope of the work package, see Analyze the impact of the work package.
View Work Package Scope 2D to show the tags related to the work package in a 2D drawing.
View Work Package Scope 3D to show the tags related to the work package in a 3D model.
3. Either Approve or Reject the work package.
 If the Allow workflow step to auto delete markup(s) option is enabled in the System Options, the markups related to the work package are automatically deleted when the work package is approved. For more information, see
Select to automatically delete markups.
View files
1. In the Work Packages feature set, select <Type of Work Package> Work Packages.
2. Select View and Markup 
 to view the consolidated files attached to a work package.
Select to automatically delete markups
If enabled, markups will be automatically removed when the workflow is approved. To enable this option:
In the system options, in the SDx only options details section, select the Allow workflow step to auto delete markup(s) option. For more information on the system options, see Configure the system options.
Copy a work package
1. Select a work package and select Actions 
 > Copy.
2. Enter the required information and select Copy.
Select Pins 
 > New Items to view the copied work package.
Comments, documents, or files related to the original work package are not copied.
The copied work package has the same workflow as the original work package.
Monitoring the progress of loaded data
 Be sure the Integration scheduler is started before sharing data or documents from a design tool.
When the objects are shared by the tool, they are created in the design component of the tool. When a design tool registers with HxGN SDx2, the application creates a basic set of database tables, where each table in the set is named
after the design component (S3D, PID, for example) that registered. Then objects shared by that tool for that design component are stored in those tables.
Graphics are shared along with the corresponding objects, and these are stored in the graphics schema domain. Graphics are parsed and stored in the tables, rather than a view file. The GDS (Graphical Data Services) is a separate
module to parse this information and put it into the tables.
Single object sharing and Batch sharing use different APIs for sharing.
 You can monitor the progress of data that you are loading from design tools as your data passes through the HxGN SDx2 Integration Engine.
Retry loading graphical documents
View and address Integration Engine exceptions
Creating a report of incomplete relationships in the loaded as-built data
Loading shared documents and drawings
 The first shared revision of a document creates a Facility Data Warehouse (FDW) document master in the database. Each FDW document has a mandatory relationship to SPFPrimaryClassification. Complete the mapping
between Common Schema and the HxGN SDx2 schema to identify this classification.
When you share a graphical document, you can check the load status of your graphical document, and, if the system encounters an error loading the document, you can retry the share.
Tell me more about loading shared documents and drawings
After you share a document, either a revisable document or a document for design review, HxGN SDx2 processes the tool data and the tombstone files from the design tool, using the Scheduler Service.
The SDxiHubPublishDocLoadSchedulerTask polls periodically for the files that need to be processed, and then processes them to load the graphical file, and the relationships between the document and the shared files, to the
database.
 HxGN SDx2 uses the Business Admin user to poll for and load documents with the SDxiHubPublishDocLoadSchedulerTask. This user must have Business Administrator role permissions at Configuration Top in the inherited
mode or at the level of the specific plant or project these documents were shared to for them to be processed.
In HxGN SDx2, it is the administrator's job monitor the loading of documents and drawings shared from the authoring tool and take the necessary steps to resolve issues that arise during that process. You can find more information about
that in the Monitoring the progress of loaded data section.
View the load status of shared documents
You must have the Business Administrator role.
Your query scope must be set to the Project with which you associated the shared documents.
Set the Integration Data Filters to the tool from which you are shared the documents. Integration Data Filters is in Settings 
 > Scope.
Documents load in the order that the design tool shares them.
To view the status of documents shared with HxGN SDx2 using continuous sharing:
In the Documents feature set, select Active Smart Documents.
 You can also find the status of shared documents being loaded into HxGN SDx2 by selecting Scheduled Tasks in the Design Tool Integration feature set.
Documents you see here will have one of the following statuses:
Queued - the document has been shared by the design tool.
Preprocessing - the document has been attached to the load queue for processing.
Processing - the load queue has begun the loading the documents.
Failed - the document failed to be loaded. You can view the Load Error Message in the Results grid when viewing the scheduled task.
Successful - the document was successfully loaded by the scheduler task.
For more information, see Retry loading graphical documents.
Retry loading graphical documents
You must have the Business Administrator role.
Your scope must be set to the project with which you associated the graphical documents.
Set the Integration Data Filters to the tool from which you are sharing the graphical documents. Integration Data Filters is in Settings 
 > Scope.
1. In the Documents feature set, select Active Smart Documents.
2. In the Results grid, select the document to retry.
3. Select Actions 
 > Resubmit For Load to queue the document for loading.
View the load status of revisable documents
To view the status of a revisable document being processed by the Integration Engine and loaded into the HxGN SDx2 database:
Select Integration Documents Only from the Design Tool Integration feature set and search for the document you want to check.
The Load Status property for each shared document returned appears in the Results grid.
When you use this command, the system ignores any selections you make in the Integration Data Filter section of the Query and create scope window.
If the document was loaded successfully, the document load status is Successful. If the system encountered any errors loading the document, the load status is Failed, and you can view the Load Error Message in the
Results grid.
Retry loading revisable documents
 You must have the role of either Business Administrator, System Administrator, or Document Controller to see the command in this procedure.
Select the failed document, and select Actions 
 > Resubmit for Load.
View the progress of specific objects in the Integration Engine
While data is being processed by the Integration Engine, you can search for the objects that you have shared and view the Integration Service State.
You must have the Business Administrator role.
You must set your Query scope to the project with which you associated the data, and you must switch on the Create scope.
Set the Integration Data Filters to the tool from which you are sharing the data. Integration Data Filters is in Settings 
 > Scope.
The Integration Engine consolidates only the classes that have a RelPathToGroup attribute for the IClassDef element. For more information, Review the requirements for consolidation. You can view the progress for objects that are
eligible for consolidation.
1. In the Tags, Assets and Models feature set, select the relevant tile to search for the objects you shared.
2. In the Results grid, select an object.
3. Select Actions 
, and select:
Show Changeset to view the changeset the object was shared in to identify the batch or other related items. You can view the Integration Service State on the Changeset Results grid.
Show Group Object to view the group object that is created by the Integration Engine. The group object is a logical group for the existing tool objects. You can view the Integration Service State on the Group Object Results
grid. For more information about Integration Service State, see Tell me more about the Integration Service State statuses.
Show Identity Register Object to view the identity register object, which is a unique identifier, the CorrelationID, used by the Integration Engine to store the exception details.
 You can view tool objects related to a Changeset or Group Object by selecting the changeset or group object in the Results grid, and selecting Actions 
 > Show Related Objects.
View the status of objects from the dashboards
You can track the status of the objects you are loading from different design tools and see the data being processed through the Integration Engine on a home screen dashboard.
You can configure and customize this dashboard as required. For more information, see Configure an InsightView dashboard.
Can I create a custom dashboard to see my shared data?
Yes. You can create your own dashboards to show additional information regarding the object progress in the Integration Engine. For more information, see Create a dashboard.
Can I configure delivered widgets?
Yes. You can configure the delivered widgets for the dashboards or update the dashboards. For more information, see Update the dashboard.
Below is a list of widgets that you can configure to display information about the count and status of the objects shared from different design tools.
This widget
Shows
3D DRAWING ITEMS VIEW
Details about the objects that have the IDrawingItem interface and were shared from 3D.
3D GRAPHICS OBJECT VIEW
Details about the objects that have the IGraphics3D interface and can be used for
graphic hotspotting to data shared from 3D.
CONSOLIDATED DRAWING ITEMS VIEW
Details about the consolidated objects that have IDrawingItem3D interface.
CONSOLIDATED GRAPHICS OBJECT VIEW
Details about the consolidated objects that have the IGraphics3D interface and can be
used for graphic hotspotting.
CONSOLIDATED OBJECT STATUS
Status of the consolidated objects by class definition, such as Equipment, Cable, and so
on.
CONSOLIDATED OBJECTS COUNT
Total count of the consolidated objects.
CORRELATED OBJECT STATUS
Status of the correlated objects by class and design component, such as Area,DC3D.
CORRELATED OBJECTS COUNT
Total count of the identity register objects or the correlated objects.
HANDOVER CHANGESET OVERALL STATUS
Total count of the changeset objects. For more information, see Tell me more about
changeset objects.
PID DRAWING ITEMS VIEW
Details about the objects that have the IDrawingItem interface and were shared from
P&ID.
PID GRAPHICS OBJECT VIEW
Details about the objects that have the IGraphics3D interface which can be used for
graphic hotspotting shared from P&ID.
SHAREDGROUP OVERALL OBJECT STATUS
Total count of the SharedGroup objects.
Open the dashboards
1. Select Dashboards 
 to display the home screen dashboards.
 If home screen dashboards are set as your home page, select Home 
 to display the dashboards you can access.
2. Navigate to any one of the Integration Engine dashboards using the controls at the bottom of the page.
 On a tablet, you can also scroll through your available home screens by swiping left or right.
The status of the objects being processed by the Integration Engine is shown in different widgets. The widgets you see depend upon the design tool the objects were shared from.
You must have the Business Administrator role to access the delivered dashboards.
You can see multiple dashboards based on the different design tools the objects are shared from.
If an object has a RelPathToGroup attribute and is related to multiple design components, it is eligible for processing by the Integration Engine.
A sample XML file, Admin_Integration_Dashboard_Sample.xml, is available on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . This file has the necessary schema for creating dashboard widgets for a custom
design component. For information on how to download sample files from Smart Community, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
 Follow the ReadMe instructions to process and use the XML data effectively.
How does a delivered object progress dashboard look like?
You can see multiple dashboards based on the different design tools the objects are shared from. For example, if the objects are shared form Smart 3D, you can see Admin Integration Engine 3D Objects Progress dashboard.
To filter the data displayed by a property, select Column Settings 
at the top of the relevant column, and then use the available options to sort the result. For more information, see Filter or sort the results.
Select 
 to expand the widget. Select 
 to return it to the original size.
You can also double-click the widget title to expand and collapse it. On an Apple iPad, you can double-tap to expand or collapse the widget and to zoom in.
Select 
 to reload the data in the widget.
What do the different dashboard widgets show?
This widget
Shows you
TOOL TAG TYPE OBJECT DETAILS
All the relevant information about the tag objects pushed from the design tool appearing in the title bar of the
widget.
For example: Name, OBID, Unique ID, and so on.
 The Tag objects are instances of class definitions that realize the ITag interface.
TOOL PORT TYPE OBJECT DETAILS
All the relevant information about the port objects pushed from the design tool named in the title bar of the widget.
For example: Name, OBID, Unique ID, and so on.
 The Port objects are instances of class definitions that realize the IPort interface.
SHARED GROUP OBJECT DETAILS
Details about the types of objects (tags and ports) in the shared group objects to be processed by the Integration
Engine, so that the appropriate correlation rules can be applied later.
 If an object has a RelPathToGroup attribute and is related to multiple design components, it is eligible for
processing by the Integration Engine.
You can check the IntegrationServiceState column in the Shared Group Object Details widget to see the
status of the object. For more information about Integration Service State, see Tell me more about the
Integration Service State statuses.
You can check the Dependent UIDs For Retry Consolidate column for the UID of an object from the group
on which the consolidation should be retried.
You can also see the top level of the object hierarchy after the shared group object creation in the Parent
Object OBID column.
CORRELATED OBJECT DETAILS
Details about the identity register objects with a correlation ID for other services can work with it. A correlation ID is
a string that uniquely identifies an object across the integrated design tools.
If an object is not processed further to the consolidate service, the reason is displayed in the exception
columns in the Correlated Object Details widget.
You can also troubleshoot certain Identity Register exceptions. For more information, see Troubleshoot
Identity Register exceptions.
TAG TYPE CONSOLIDATED OBJECTS
Details about the consolidated tag type objects using the correlation ID created previously. You will also find the
final Consolidated Correlation ID of the consolidated object.
 Since the correlation ID is unique to a specific object, this ID is used to locate the existing tag type object in
the group being consolidated.
PORT TYPE CONSOLIDATED OBJECTS
Details about the consolidated port type objects using the correlation ID created previously. You will also find the
final Consolidated Correlation ID of the consolidated object.
 Since the correlation ID is unique to a specific object, this ID is used to locate the existing port type object
in the group being consolidated.
During this process, if an object is re-shared, the existing shared group object is updated and the process restarts from the cumulate step.
You can also check the status of the widgets. For more information, see Check the status of a widget.
What columns are found in the dashboard widgets?
The table below presents information on each widget, including their respective column names and descriptions.
Column
Description
Tool Tag Type Object Details widget
Name
Name of the specific object.
OBID
Object ID of the object.
UID
Unique ID of the object.
Tag Type
Entries that contain specific information of a particular tag.
Tag Class
Classification of the tag that was shared.
Creation User
User name of the person who created the object.
Created Date
Date when the design object was created.
Last Updated Date
Date when the object was last updated.
Tool Port Type Object Details widget
Description (Port Type)
Description of the port object.
Port Type 1
Entries that contain information of a particular port with specific type 1.
Port Type 2
Entries that contain information of a particular port with specific type 2.
Port Class
Class of the port shared by the design tool.
Shared Group Object Details widget
Group Context OBIDs
OBIDs of the objects in a shared group object created after logical grouping.

Parent object of the hierarchy after the shared group object was created.

Tool domain the objects were shared from.
Integration Service State
Current state of the object being processed by the Integration Engine.
Dependent UIDs For Retry Consolidate
UID of the object from the group on which the consolidation must be retried by the Consolidate service.
Auto Retried Count
Count used to depict the number of retries of the Integration Engine process on a shared group object, usually triggered by the scheduler.
Description

Correlated Object Details widget
Correlation ID
Unique ID generated by the correlate service by applying some correlation rules to the objects. This is used to create and update
consolidated objects in the consolidate design component.

Domain
Design component names for design tools.
Correlate Exception
Displays the exceptions on the Identity Register (IDR) object by correlate service.
IDR Consolidate Exception
Exceptions on the IDR object by the consolidate service.
SDx IdentityRegister Item State
Properties set by the correlate service on the IDR object, which helps the Integration Engine to terminate the IDR object once the
respective consolidated object is terminated.
SDx IdentityRegister Item Previous CorrelationID
Previous correlation ID of the object.
Tag Type or Port Type Consolidated Objects widget
Consolidated Correlation ID
Correlation ID of the consolidated object.

group object by filtering (= CONTAINS) in the Group Context OBID.

Object.

object in the Consolidated Objects widget.
The group objects with CorrelationID enter the consolidate step.
This unique CorrelationID is used to create or update the

How to confirm object correlation and consolidation?
1. In the Admin Integration Engine 3D Objects Progress dashboard, find the Correlated Object Details widget.
 This widget shows identity register objects for all tool design components. For more information, see Identity Register (IDR) objects.

The system generates a count of all objects for which identity register objects exist.

If an object has graphics and is consolidated but does not have a correlation ID in the Correlated Object Details widget, the system uses the identity register object's UID as part of its correlation ID.
One of the reasons objects are not processed through the Integration Engine is that they are listed under the Common Schema Classdefs without a RelPathToGroup widget and are missing identity register entries, graphics, or
required schema.
For more information, see What do you see in the Integration Engine Operational Schema Details dashboard?
In the Integration Engine Operational Schema Details dashboard, you can view the class definitions of the objects with and without RelPathToGroup details.
The Integration Engine consolidates only the classes that have a RelPathToGroup attribute for the ClassDef element. You can also view the progress for objects eligible for consolidation.
You can see only objects that are instances of class definitions that realize the ISIOObject interface and are shared by one or more design tools.

objects consolidated with respect to the ClassDef of the objects.
