SDxViewConsolidatedDataForObject client API
The SDxViewConsolidatedDataForObject client API is used to view the data in the consolidated design component for a consolidated object. This client API uses no parameters.
The SDxViewConsolidatedDataForObject method uses the SDxViewConsolidatedDataForObject client API to display information about a selected consolidated object.
SDxWorkflowTemplateEditor client API
The WorkflowTemplateEditor client API is used for updating the workflow template. This client API uses no parameters.
The SDxWorkflowEdit method uses this API to view or edit the details of a workflow template.
SDxWorkflowEditShortcut is a shortcut menu method that uses this API and is associated with editing the workflow template.
SendToDocumentVersion client API
The SendToDocumentVersion client API is used to create a document version for an active smart drawing within the tool design component or to generate a document version for a FDWDocument in the consolidated design component.
This client API uses no parameters.
The Create Document Version method uses this client API to create a document version for an active smart drawing or for a FDWDocument.
SetObjectCollectionValues client API
The SetObjectCollectionValues client API is used for updating the values of multiple properties for multiple objects in one go. This client API can be related to any method. The corresponding method must be configured with the updated
property and its value. At a time, more than one property value can be updated. They must be entered in the following format, where each property configuration is separated by a semicolon (;). (<Interface1name>~
<Property1Name>~=~<Property1Value>;<Interface2name>~<Property2Name>~=~<Property2Value>)
This configured method can be related to the object type class definition, based on the interface to expose the method, in the Actions menu of the corresponding object.
For example, this configuration ISDASecurityControlledItem~SecurityCodeName~=~Unclassified will modify the property of the object related to the given interface ISDASecurityControlledItem with the value: Unclassified.
Applying the configured method action to a set of objects will modify the specified properties of all chosen objects according to the provided configuration.
The number of given properties must match the number of given values.
If any property type in the method is not applicable to the selected object, it is skipped.
You can update maximum of 20 properties per object and a maximum of 200 objects in single attempt.
To update the values of hierarchical enum entries, you must ensure that the values of enum properties being updated follow the appropriate relationship. For example, for a document object, if you want to update the value of
SDADocSubType, you must ensure that the value being updated belongs to the corresponding parent level DocType as per the relationship hierarchy. If you want to update the SDADocSubType to a value that belongs to a different
parent level DocType, you must ensure to update both parent and child property values to update the relationship. Example: ISDADocument~SDADocType~=~e1FDWDrawings;ISDADocument~SDADocSubType~=~e1FDWCA
ISDADocumentArgument
Description
Parameter 1
Property configuration
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
Property configuration to update values (configuration must be:
Interface~PropertyName~=~PropertyValue;)
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Comments
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
Property name to store comments
Parameter 2 type
String
 No delivered methods use this client API.
SetObjectValues client API
The SetObjectValues client API is used for updating the values of multiple properties for an object in one go. This client API can be related to any method. The corresponding method must be configured with the updated property and its
value. At a time, more than one property value can be updated. They must be entered in the following format, where the entries of each property must be separated by a semicolon (;).
(Interface1name~Property1Name~=~Property1Value;Interface2name~Property2Name~=~Property2Value)
This configured method can be related to the object type class definition, based on the interface to expose the method, in the Actions menu of the corresponding object.
For example, this configuration ISDASecurityControlledItem~SecurityCodeName~=~Unclassified will modify the property of the object related to the given interface ISDASecurityControlledItem with the value: Unclassified.
The number of given properties must match the number of given values.
If any property type in the method is not applicable to the selected object, it is skipped.
The maximum number of properties that can be updated at a time is 20.
To update the values of hierarchical enum entries, you must ensure that the values of enum properties being updated follow the appropriate relationship. For example, for a document object, if you want to update the value of
SDADocSubType, you must ensure that the value being updated belongs to the corresponding parent level DocType as per the relationship hierarchy. If you want to update the SDADocSubType to a value that belongs to a different
parent level DocType, you must ensure to update both parent and child property values to update the relationship. Example: ISDADocument~SDADocType~=~e1FDWDrawings;ISDADocument~SDADocSubType~=~e1FDWCA
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Property configuration
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
Property configuration to update values (configuration must be: Interface~PropertyName~=~PropertyValue;)
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Comments
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
Property name to store comments
Parameter 2 type
String
 No delivered methods use this client API.

The ShareFile method uses this client API to share the zip package of an offline transmittal by URL with other users. So, when you have an offline transmittal to share with others, you can use this feature.
ShareUrl client API
The ShareUrl client API allows you to share a URL path to selected data with other users. This client API uses no parameters.
How does the ShareURL client API work?
The ShareURL client API works by generating a URL to launch a view of your search results. You share the view with other users by sending them the URL so they can directly see the objects in their client.
The ShareURL method uses this client API to share a path with other users to the selected data. When you have objects in a results grid you wish to share with others, you can use this feature.
 The ShareURL client API only works if the URL contains the entity type in the URL path. This is a limitation.

SignOff client API
The SignOff client API is used to issue a document. Sign off changes the status of a document revision from Working to Current, and locks the version to prevent users from checking it out or creating a new version of the associated
revision.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Allow working references
Parameter 1 default
False
Parameter 1 description
When set to True, all files referenced by files attached to this document are automatically signed off, too. When set to False,
the references files are not signed off.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
Parameter 2
Purge superseded versions
Parameter 2 default
False
Parameter 2 description
If set to True, the system will automatically purge earlier versions for this revision that has been superseded. If set to False,
the superseded versions are maintained.
Parameter 2 type
Boolean
Parameter 15
Allow signoff references
Parameter 15 default
True
Parameter 15 description
Allow signoff reference files during signoff operation. By default it is set to True, which means references files will be shown
during signoff
Parameter 15 type
Boolean
The RevisionSignOff method uses the SignOff client API to change the status of a document version.
SignOffWithForm client API
The SignOffWIthForm client API is used to issue a document using a dialog box where additional notes can be added. Sign off changes the status of a document revision from Working to Current, and locks the version to prevent users
from checking it out or creating a new version of the associated revision.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Allow working references
Parameter 1 default
False
Parameter 1 description
When set to True, all files references by files attached to this document are automatically signed off, too. When set to False,
the references files are not signed off.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
Parameter 2
Purge superseded versions
Parameter 2 default
False
Parameter 2 description
If set to True, the system will automatically purge earlier versions for this revision that have been superseded. If set to False,
the superseded versions are maintained.
Parameter 2 type
Boolean
Parameter 15
Allow signoff references
Parameter 15 default
True
Parameter 15 description
Allow signoff reference files during signoffwithform operation. By default it is set to True, which means references files will be
shown during signoffwithform.
Parameter 15 type
Boolean
The SignOffWithForm method uses the SignOffWithForm client API to change the status of a document version using a dialog box where you can add additional notes.
SOPSCommunicationManagement client API
The SOPSCommunicationManagement client API is used to launch the communication management screen from the To Do list.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Title
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The title of the page
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Column Set
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
The UID of the column set used to show the details of the communication item.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Method Used To Launch Markup in Edit Mode, Method Used To Launch Markup in View Mode
Parameter 3 default
None
Parameter 3 description
The UID of the method to launch the file viewer in edit mode and view mode.
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 4
Method Used To Manage Communication Item Markup Layers
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
The UID of the method used to manage the file viewer layers on the communication item.
Parameter 4 type
String
Parameter 5
Method Used To Manage Response Markup Layers
Parameter 5 default
None
Parameter 5 description
The UID of the method used to manage response layers.
Parameter 5 type
String
Parameter 6
Create New Technical Query Method
Parameter 6 default
None
Parameter 6 description
The UID of the method used to create a new technical query.
Parameter 6 type
String
Parameter 7
Response Mode
Parameter 7 default
None
Parameter 7 description
The response mode chosen for the communication
Prepare - The preparation of the response by the recipient
Respond - The response sent by the recipient (waive and complete)
Consolidate - The consolidation of the response by the recipient
Compliance - The contractual compliance of the response by the recipient
InitialResponse - The initial communication response by the recipient
ResponseCompliance - The contractual compliance of the response by the originating company
ReceiveResponse - The receipt of the response by the originating company (complete)
ReceiveResponseReject - The receipt of the response by the originating company (reject)
RespondReject - The response sent by the recipient (reject and complete)
ViewOnly - View the communication as read-only
Parameter 7 type
String
Parameter 8
Response Primary Classification
Parameter 8 default
None
Parameter 8 description
The primary classification to be used when generating responses
Parameter 8 type
String
Parameter 9
Response Relationship Definition
Parameter 9 default
None
Parameter 9 description
The relationship between the communication item and the response
Parameter 9 type
String
Parameter 10
Manage Response Related Items Method
Parameter 10 default
None
Parameter 10 description
UID of the method used to manage related items on the technical query response
Parameter 10 type
String
Parameter 11
Manage Related Items for the Technical Query
Parameter 11 default
None
Parameter 11 description
UID of the method used to manage related items on the technical query
Parameter 11 type
String
Parameter 12
Update Technical Query object method
Parameter 12 default
None
Parameter 12 description
The UID of the method used to update the technical query
Parameter 12 type
String
Parameter 13
Method used to manage Review Markup
Parameter 13 default
None
Parameter 13 description
UID of the method used to manage the review markup layers
Parameter 13 type
String
Parameter 14
Methods used to launch Impact Analysis
Parameter 14 default
None
Parameter 14 description
A comma-separated list of the UIDs of the methods used to launch impact analysis
Parameter 14 type
String
Parameter 15
Method used to identify Responders
Parameter 15 default
None
Parameter 15 description
UID of the method used to identify responders
Parameter 15 type
String
Parameter 16
Method used to resolve conflicts
Parameter 16 default
None
Parameter 16 description
UID of the method used to resolve conflicts
Parameter 16 type
String
Parameter 17
Method to view item in 3D
Parameter 17 default
None
Parameter 17 description
UID of the method used to view the item in 3D
Parameter 17 type
String
Parameter 18
Method to view item in 2D
Parameter 18 default
None
Parameter 18 description
UID of the method used to view the item in 2D
Parameter 18 type
String
The following methods use the SOPSCommunicationManagement client API to launch the communication management screen from the To Do List.
SDAPrepareWorkpackage
SOPSCorrectTempChangeRequestFailure

SCLBCommunicationManagementDetails client API.

SOPSDocChangeActionCommunicationManagementDetails

SPFCopyConfigAccess client API
The SPFCopyConfigAccess client API is used to copy role assignments and security codes for all the users in an existing configuration to the same users in a different configuration.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Configuration role assignment method
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The UID of the configuration role assignment method
Parameter 1 type
String
The SPFCopyConfigAccess method uses the SPFCopyConfigAccess client API to copy users' role assignments and security codes between configurations.
SPFCreateEmailDef client API
The SPFCreateEmailDef client API is used for creating an e-mail definition. This client API uses no parameters.
No method uses this API to allow you to create an e-mail definition.
SPFUpdateEmailDef client API
The SPFUpdateEmailDef client API is used for updating an e-mail definition. This client API uses no parameters.
The SPFUpdateEmailDefinition method uses this API to allow you to update an e-mail definition.
SPXSAPSync client API
The SPXSAPSync client API is used to synchronize data between HxGN SDx2 and SAP. This client API uses no parameters.
The SPXSAPSyncCall method uses this client API to trigger a sync action when performing sync or delete sync operations in the Connectors feature set. For more information, see HxGN SDx2 Connector for Plant Maintenance Help.
StartScheduler client API
The StartScheduler client API is used to activate a selected scheduler. This client API uses no parameters.
The StartScheduler method uses the StartScheduler client API to activate a scheduler.
StepAcceptAction client API
The StepAcceptAction client API provides the functionality behind methods that progress a document or transmittal forward in a workflow. This client API uses no parameters.
The following methods use this client API to indicate that a step is complete or successful and the workflow should progress forward to the next step defined as the Accept Step.
ApproveCorrelations
WFStepAcknowledge
WFStepCompleted
WFStepApprove
WFStepRetry
SCLBResubmitForReview
StepClaim client API
The StepClaim client API provides the functionality that allows one user to claim a workflow step that has been assigned to a role or group of users.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Reset target dates
Parameter 1 description
When set to True, the duration period for the current step should be restarted from the point where the step was claimed and
target dates for the current and future steps should be recalculated accordingly. When set to False, the dates are not reset.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
Parameter 1 default
True
Parameter 2
User rel
Parameter 2 description
User relationship definition
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 2 default
None
The following methods use this client API to claim a step for the user.
WFClaim
SCLBClaimQAConsolidation
SCLBProjComsClaimExternalReview
SCLBProjComsClainExtInitialReview
SCLBProjComsClaimInt
SCLBProjComsWFClaimExtXmtl
SOPSDIcClaimApproveReviewStep
SOPSDIcClaimImplementationStep
StepRejectAction client API
The StepRejectAction client API provides the functionality to indicate that a workflow step failed or has been rejected and that the workflow should continue with the step defined as the Reject Step. This client API uses no parameters.
The following methods use this client API to indicate that a step was rejected or unsuccessful and that the workflow should continue to the step defined as the Reject Step.
WFStepUnableToComplete
WFStepReject
SCLBPProceedWithoutReview
StepUpdateDates client API
The StepUpdateDates client API updates the duration length assigned to a step in a workflow, as well as the target and planned dates. This client API uses no parameters.
The StepUpdateDates method uses the StepUpdateDates client API to update the duration of a step.
StepUpdateDesc client API
The StepUpdateDesc client API updates the description provided for a step in a workflow. This client API uses no parameters.
The StepUpdateDesc method uses the StepUpdateDesc client API to update the description of a step.
StepUpdateRecip client API
The StepUpdateRecip client API updates the recipients associated with a workflow step from that step. This client API uses no parameters.
The StepUpdateRecip method uses the StepUpdateRecip client API to update the recipients associated with the workflow step.
StopScheduler client API
The StopScheduler client API is used to deactivate a selected scheduler. This client API uses no parameters.
The StopScheduler method uses the StopScheduler client API to deactivate a scheduler.
TagExistenceRoute client API
The TagExistenceRoute client API allows you to view the Tag Existence Dashboard.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Tag class names
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
Enter the comma separated tag class names to include. If not specified, all tag class names will be included.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Design components
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
Enter the comma separated design components to include. If not specified, all design components will be included.
Parameter 2 type
String
The Review Tag Existence method uses the TagExistenceRoute client API to allow you to view the Tag Existence Dashboard.
TagExistenceWidget client API
The TagExistenceWidget client API allows you to view the Tag Existence Dashboard in a widget.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Tag class names
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
Enter the comma separated tag class names to include. If not specified, all tag class names will be included.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Design components
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
Enter the comma separated design components to include. If not specified, all design components will be included.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Display mode
Parameter 3 default
None
Parameter 3 description
Enter Chart, Gauge, or Grid to display just that part of the screen in the widget, or leave blank to display all parts of the
screen.
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 4
Show component picker
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
Select whether the design component selector should be displayed. Set this to True to display the selector.
Parameter 4 type
Boolean
The Review Tag Existence method uses the TagExistenceWidget client API to allow you to view the Tag Existence Dashboard in a widget.
TagReview client API
The TagReview client API is used to allow tags to be reviewed in the content extraction workflow. This client API uses no parameters.
The Perform Tag Review and View Extracted Tags methods use the TagReview client API to allow tags to be reviewed in the content extraction workflow.
TaskReassignmentConfiguration client API
The TaskReassignmentConfiguration client API is used to display configurations to which a user has access.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Columnset UID
Parameter 1 description
UID of the column set to be used to display the configurations
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
CS_UserDetails.
Parameter 2
Use default query configurations
Parameter 2 description
If ALL is set, then all configurations are displayed on the Task Reassignment page. If it is blank, only the selected query
configurations are displayed.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 2 default
None
The following method uses the TaskReassignmentConfiguration client API to determine if access needs to be checked across all configurations or selected configurations.
TaskReassignmentConfiguration
TerminateObj client API
The TerminateObj client API terminates the selected object. This client API uses no parameters.
This client API is used by many methods in the system to terminate objects.
TerminateWorkflow client API
The TerminateWorkflow client API is used to terminate the relationship between a document or transmittal and an instance of a workflow definition. All history pertaining to work already completed is retained in the database. However, the
relationship is marked terminated and is therefore no longer valid from the point of termination forward.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Comments mandatory
Parameter 1 default
False
Parameter 1 description
Specifies whether comments must be provided in the dialog box provided to the user before they can complete the terminate
operation.
Parameter 1 type
Boolean
The TerminateWorkflow method uses the TerminateWorkflow client API to terminate the relationship between the selected document or transmittal and an attached instance of a workflow definition.
UnCancelDocument client API
The UnCancelDocument client API is used to revert the document to the state it was in when it was cancelled. This client API uses no parameters.
The UnCancelDocument method uses this client API to reverse the cancellation of the document in a workflow and return it to the previous state.
UndoEditFiles client API
The UndoEditFiles client API is used to revert the editing on the files.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Delete Revision
Parameter 1 description
Options are YES, NO, or PROMPT.
YES – If multiple versions exist for a revision, then it deletes all versions along with the revision.
NO – If multiple versions exist for a revision, then it deletes latest version.
PROMPT – Prompts the user whether to delete the revision or not.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
PROMPT
 You do not see the Undo Edit Files action for any document revision with only one version, as this action is controlled by a condition in the method so the system will not delete the revision.
The following methods use the UndoEditFiles client API to revert the editing on the files.
UndoEditFiles
InvalidUndoEditFiles
UndoSignOff client API
The UndoSignOff client API allows you to change a signed off document back to the Working state. This client API uses no parameters.
The UndoSignOff method uses the client API to revert a signed off document to the Working state.
UnlockUserAccount client API
The UnlockUserAccount client API allows administrators to unlock a temporarily locked user account when it has been locked for a period of time after a number of failed log on attempts. This client API uses no parameters.
The UsrAdmUnlock method uses the UnlockUserAccount client API to unlock the user account.
UpdateMethod client API
The UpdateMethod client API is used to update existing methods.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Update Method UID
Parameter 1 default
True
Parameter 1 description
If set to True, the UpdateMethod UID is used to update an existing method.
Parameter 1 type
String
The UpdateMethod client API is used by the UpdateMethod method to update existing methods. The default is True.
UpdateReferences client API
The UpdateReferences client API is used to manage the version of referenced files.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Column set to present data
Parameter 1 default
CS_UpdateRefsColumnSet
Parameter 1 description
Column set used to display items in the Update References dialog box.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Column item for target references
Parameter 2 default
CI_TargetReferencesRev
Parameter 2 description
The editable column item for selecting the target reference document. Allows you to specify whether you want to maintain a
relationship to the latest working version of a reference file, the current version, or the original version to which the relationship
was initially created.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Skip Max Nested Level Validation
Parameter 3 default
False
Parameter 3 description
When set to True, the maximum nested reference level validation is skipped.
Parameter 3 type
Boolean
The following methods use the UpdateReferences client API to manage what version of references file a document is related to.
FileUpdateReferences
UpdateReferences

Description

Column set to present data

String

Column item for target references

CI_TargetReferencesRev
Parameter 2 description
The editable column item for selecting the target reference document.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Skip Max Nested Level Validation
Parameter 3 default
False
Parameter 3 description
Configuration to skip max nested reference level validation
Parameter 3 type
Boolean
The following methods use the UpdateReferences client API to manage what version of references file a document is related to.

UpdateReferredBy
UpdateRoleAssignments client API
The UpdateRoleAssignments client API is used to include new users in started workflows. This client API uses no parameters.

UpdateRoleAssignmentsForTemplate
UpdateRoleAssignmentsForWFItem
UpdateRoleAssignmentsForWorkflow
UpdateTagNamingSystem client API
The UpdateTagNamingSystem client API is used to update a tag naming rule. This client API uses no parameters.
The Update Tag Naming System method uses the UpdateTagNamingSystem client API to allow an administrator to update a tag naming rule.
UpdateTagNamingSystemPart client API
The UpdateTagNamingSystemPart client API is used to update a naming rule part. This client API uses no parameters.
The Update Tag Naming System Part method uses the UpdateTagNamingSystemPart client API to allow an administrator to update a naming rule part.
UpdateWatermark client API
The UpdateWatermark client API is used to update existing watermarks. This client API uses no parameters.
The UpdateWatermark method uses the UpdateWatermark client API to allow an administrator to update existing watermarks.
UpdateWidget client API
The UpdateWidget client API is used to edit a widget in a wizard dialog. This client API uses no parameters.
The UpdateWidget method uses the UpdateWidget client API to open the widget wizard.
UploadDocument client API
The UploadDocument client API is used to query for the existing document, retrieve the metadata of the selected document, create a new revision of that document, attach any selected files to this new revision, and upload the document
along with the files.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Title
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The title that appears at the top of the dialog box.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Entrypoint set name to query the documents
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
Entrypoint set name to query the documents. For more information on how to configure the entry point, see Configure API
Entry Points.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
File attachment configuration
Parameter 3 default
SPFDesignFile~SPFFileComposition~PROMPT
Parameter 3 description
File attachment configuration (optional) FileClassDef~FileRelDef~ReferenceAction~SeedFileTemplateDocName.
SPFFileComposition is the default relationship definition if none specified and the seed file document is optional. Reference
file not found actions are CONTINUE | PROMPT | ABORT
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 4
RelDef to navigate to the ClassDef
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
RelDef to navigate to the ClassDef - Used to derive the class definition to create from the classification object.
Parameter 4 type
String
Parameter 5
UID of classification
Parameter 5 default
None
Parameter 5 description
UID of classification
Parameter 5 type
String
Parameter 8
Workflow processing option
Parameter 8 default
None
Parameter 8 description
HxGN SDx2 chooses the applicable workflow unless a named workflow is specified. However, if more than one applicable
workflow is found, the software does not attach the object to any workflow. The options include Select, SelectRequired, or a
named workflow.
Parameter 8 type
String
The following method uses the UploadDocument client API to query for the existing document based on the given arguments.
UploadDocument

Description

String

ColumnSet

CS_SPFObject

than one object. If not set, the default is CS_SPFObject.

ViewInDocument client API
The ViewInDocument client API allows you to view a selected object in context in a document.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
EdgeDef or GraphDef to Versions
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The edge or graph definition used to find the document versions that the selected object can be found in.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 2
Graph Objects EdgeDef
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 2 description
The edge definition used to find the graph objects.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 3
Document Interface List
Parameter 3 default
None
Parameter 3 description
A comma-separated list of the interface definitions used to filter the document versions found.
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 4
Use Creation Date Filter?
Parameter 4 default
False
Parameter 4 description
When set to True, document will not be returned unless they were created before the selected object. When set to False, the
documents are not filtered by the creation date and can be returned regardless of if they were create before or after the
selected object.
Parameter 4 type
Boolean
Parameter 5
Document View Markup method
Parameter 5 default
None
Parameter 5 description
Method used to view and markup document
Parameter 5 type
String
Parameter 6
File picker column set

None

The column set to use within the File Picker dialog box.
Parameter 6 type
String
Parameter 7
View in active drawing
Parameter 7 default
False
Parameter 7 description
When set to True, returns the tool documents associated with the consolidated object.
Parameter 7 type
Boolean

VTLStageObjToTargetObjCreate client API
The VTLStageObjToTargetObjCreate client API is used for creating mappings between staging class definitions schema and load class definitions schema for loading objects into the system. This client API uses no parameters.
The VTLStageObjToTargetObjCreate method uses this client API to create the stage to load object mappings.
VTLStageObjToTargetObjUpdate client API
The VTLStageObjToTargetObjUpdate client API is used for updating mappings between staging class definitions schema and load class definitions schema for loading objects into the system. This client API uses no parameters.
The VTLStageObjToTargetObjUpdate method uses this client API to update the stage to load object mappings.
VTLStagePropToTargetPropCreate client API
The VTLStagePropToTargetPropCreate client API is used for creating mappings between the staging properties definitions and load properties definitions schema for loading properties into the system. This client API uses no
parameters.
The VTLStagePropToTargetPropCreate method uses this client API to create the stage to load property mappings.
VTLStagePropToTargetPropUpdate client API
The VTLStagePropToTargetPropUpdate client API is used for updating the mappings between the staging properties definitions and load properties definitions schema for loading properties into the system. This client API uses no
parameters.
The VTLStagePropToTargetPropUpdate method uses this client API to update the stage to load property mappings.
VTLStageRelPropToTargetRelPropCreate client API
The VTLStageRelPropToTargetRelPropCreate client API is used for creating mappings between the staging relationship properties definitions and load relationship properties definitions schema for loading relationship properties into the
system. This client API uses no parameters.
The VTLStageRelPropToTargetRelPropCreate method uses this client API to create the stage to load relationship property mappings.
VTLStageRelPropToTargetRelPropUpdate client API
The VTLStageRelPropToTargetRelPropUpdate client API is used for updating mappings between the staging relationship properties definitions and load relationship properties definitions schema for loading relationship properties into
the system. This client API uses no parameters.
The VTLStageRelPropToTargetRelPropUpdate method uses this client API to update the stage to load relationship property mappings.
VTLStageRelToTargetRelCreate client API
The VTLStageRelPropToTargetRelCreate client API is used for creating mappings between the staging relationship definitions and load relationship definitions schema for loading relationship into the system. This client API uses no
parameters.
The VTLStageRelPropToTargetRelCreate method uses this client API to create the stage to load relationship mappings.
VTLStageRelToTargetRelUpdate client API
The VTLStageRelPropToTargetRelUpdate client API is used for updating the mappings between the staging relationship definitions and load relationship definitions schema for loading relationships into the system. This client API uses no
parameters.
The VTLStageRelPropToTargetRelUpdate method uses this client API to update the stage to load relationship mappings.
VTLSystemOptions client API
The VTLSystemOptions client API is used to update a system option object. This client API uses no parameters.
The VTLSystemOptions method uses this client API to update a system option in the Data Take-On feature set.
WebClientExtensibility client API
The WebClientExtensibility client API is used to invoke custom pages in HxGN SDx2.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
URL to custom page
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The full URL to a custom page. May be on a separate or remote server
Parameter 1 type
String
 You can also use the WebClientExtensibilityBaseURL environment variable instead of the base URL path for parameter 1. For example, https://SubDomain.FullDomain.com/WebClientExt/#/area-example can be written as
~env.WebClientExtensibilityBaseURL~/#/area-example.
The following methods use this client API to display j5 applications in HxGN SDx2:
SOPSShowPermits displays the j5 Control of Work application.
SOPSShowShiftlogbook displays the j5 Operations Logbook application.
WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client API
The WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client API is used to perform an extensibility action against multiple objects.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
URL to multiple objects
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 1 description
The full URL to multiple objects. May be on a separate or remote server
Parameter 1 type
String
 You can also use the WebClientExtensibilityBaseURL environment variable instead of the base URL path for parameter 1. For example, https://SubDomain.FullDomain.com/WebClientExt/#/area-example can be written as
~env.WebClientExtensibilityBaseURL~/#/area-example.
No delivered methods use the WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client API.
WorkflowDetailsReport client API
The WorkflowDetailsReport client API allows you to create methods that will run a report to display information about each step in a workflow.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
ViewDef name
Parameter 1 description
Specifies the name of the view definition.
Parameter 1 type
String
Parameter 1 default
None
Parameter 2
Output type
Parameter 2 description
Specifies the format in which the results of the report will be displayed. The only available option is XHTML.
Parameter 2 type
String
Parameter 2 default
None
Parameter 3
Template name
Parameter 3 description
Specifies the name of the template file, stored in HxGN SDx2, where the results must be loaded.
Parameter 3 type
String
Parameter 3 default
None
The WorkflowDetailsReport method uses this client API to run a report with detailed information about each step of the workflow attached to the selected object.
When updating the WorkflowDetailsReport method, ensure that the following input parameters remain unchanged in these tabs:
Details
Condition: HasWorkflowHistory
Properties
ViewDef name: The value must be empty.
Output type: XHTML
Template name: Workflow Details Report Template
XmtlCancel client API
The XmtlCancel client API is used to cancel the transmittals attached to the workflow.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Not used
Parameter 2
Not used
Parameter 3
Not used
Parameter 4
Name of cancel workflow
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
Name of the workflow the cancelled transmittal is attached to.
Parameter 4 type
String
Parameter 5
Issue states to cancel and revise
Parameter 5 default
None
Parameter 5 description
This argument takes list of transmittal issue states to cancel and revise with a new workflow. Multiple issue states can be
specified by comma separated values, such as ISSUED,COMPLETED. Transmittals with other issue states that are not
specified will only be cancelled.
Parameter 5 type
String
Parameter 6
Workflow Step Definition Name
Parameter 6 default
None
Parameter 6 description
Specifies the workflow step definition to identify the workflow step in the current workflow. The canceled workflow is added to
this workflow step.
Parameter 6 type
String
The following methods use the XmtlCancel client API to cancel the workflow an object is attached to.
XmtlCancelExt
XmtlCancelInt
SCLBCancelIntAction
SCLBCancelAction
SCLBCancelTQ
SDACancelXmtl
XmtlUndoCancel client API
The XmtlUndoCancel client API is used to revert the transmittal to the state it was in before it was canceled.
Argument
Description
Parameter 1
Not used
Parameter 2
Not used
Parameter 3
Not used
Parameter 4
Name of the Undo Cancel workflow
Parameter 4 default
None
Parameter 4 description
Name of the workflow the previously canceled transmittal is attached to when the cancel is undone.
Parameter 4 type
String
The following methods use the XmtlUndoCancel client API to undo the cancellation of objects in a workflow. The commands are available from the Edit submenu for an object, but do not use any parameters of the client API.
SCLBUndoCancelAction
SCLBUndoCancelTQ
SDAUndoCancelXmtl
Workflow process step types
The following process step types are supplied with the core model for use with the HxGN SDx2 workflows.
Are items mergeable
This process class name checks across configurations to determine whether the items are mergeable or not and outputs which items are not mergeable and why.
Process class name: AreItemsMergeable
Syntax: AreItemsMergeable,Arg1
Examples:
AreItemsMergeable,+SCLBActionPrimaryDocVersion,SDAProjectComsReferenceData_12,-SPFFileComposition,EDG_SPFViewableFiles,
Where:
Arg1: Defines the relationship definitions, or edge definitions, used to navigate from the starting object, such as an Action, to any object that needs to be checked for mergeablity, including documents and files. For relationships, the
direction to navigate needs to be included with the UID in the format of + or _12 for end 1 to end 2 navigations, and – or _21 for end 2 to end 1 navigations.
Attach workflow to an object
This process step finds the objects related to the item already in a workflow and then associates those objects with another workflow. It is used in the workflow for collaboration, or project communication.
Process class name: AttachWorkflowToObj
Syntax: AttachWorkflowToObj, Arg1, Arg2
Example: AttachWorkflowToObj,EDG_SCLBDocRevisionRejectedActions,WD_SCLBQAActionRejection
Where:
Arg1: Edge definition or relationship definition between the objects in the workflow and the target objects.
Arg2: Specifies the UID of the workflow to be attached.
Change contract and revise related documents
Process step class
This process step class can be used to change the contract and revise all related documents. The arguments define what the workflow does when a workflow step is initiated to transfer the contract.
Process class name: Contract Transfer Request
Syntax: SDAChangeContractAndReviseRelatedDocuments,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3
Example:
SDAChangeContractAndReviseRelatedDocuments,SDAContractTransferRequestDocVersion,SDASDADocContract,SDAProjectComsContract
Where:
Arg1: Relationship to get the document versions related to the contract transfer request.
Arg2: Relates the document master(s) to the contract.
Arg3: Relationship to get the contract related to contract transfer request object
The following example shows the SDAChangeContractAndReviseRelatedDocuments process step:
<SPFStepDef>
<IObject UID="STP_SDAChangeContractAndReviseRelatedDocuments" Name="SDAChangeContractAndReviseRelatedDocuments" Description="Change contract and revise related documents" />
<ISPFAdminItem />
<ISPFStepDef />
</SPFStepDef>
<SPFStepDefClass>
<IObject UID="WSC_SDAChangeContractAndReviseRelatedDocuments" Name="SDAChangeContractAndReviseRelatedDocuments" Description="Change contract and revise related documents"/>
<ISPFAdminItem />
<ISPFStepDefClass
SPFProcessClassName="SDAChangeContractAndReviseRelatedDocuments,SDAContractTransferRequestDocVersion,SDADocContract,SDAProjectComsContract" >
</SPFStepDefClass>
Claim your change request to project scope
Why should I claim the change request to project scope?
Claim to project scope to implement the requirements of change request at the project level.
What happens when I assign a project to the change request?
When a project is assigned, the workflow will claim the tags and revise the documents to the assigned project automatically.
 If the changes are not necessarily to be implemented at project scope, you can opt out of assigning the project and continue at the plant scope.
Process step class
This process step class can be used to claim the change request to project scope. The arguments define what the workflow does when a workflow step is initiated to claim the change request to project scope.
Process class name: Claim to project
Syntax: ClaimAndReviseRelatedDocuments,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3,Arg4,Arg5,Arg6,Arg7,Arg8, Arg9
Example:
ClaimAndReviseRelatedDocuments,,SDAContractTransferDocVersion,True,,,,,False, FDWDocFDWTag;FDWDocRevAsset
Where:
Arguments
Definitions
Arg1
Relates the change request to a project. (Optional)
Arg2
Relates the change request to a document version. (Optional)
Arg3
Transfers the workflow to the project scope. (Optional)
Arg4
Relationship definition between the workflow item and the claimed objects (; separated). (Optional)
Arg5
Claims the document before the revision. Ignores if already claimed. (Optional)
Arg6
Ignores the system exclusive claim. (Optional)
Arg7
Lists the properties and values, colon separated, that need to be updated on the higher configuration workflow item.
(Optional)
Arg8
Specifies whether to increment the minor (True) or major (False) revision. The default is set to False. (Optional)
Arg9
Copies and assigns the existing document to asset and document to tag relationships to the document revision.
(Optional)
Can the system automatically inherit existing document relationships when creating a new document revision?
Yes, by following the steps given below:
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, select Workflow Step Definitions.
2. Search for Revise Document Versions, and select Actions 
 > Update.
3. In the Revise Document Versions step definition dialog, select the Workflow Step Type tab, and update the Process class name by appending the name with one or more unique ids of the relationship definitions of the document
where each value should be semicolon-separated.
Example:
ClaimAndReviseRelatedDocuments,,SDAContractTransferDocVersion,True,,,,,False, FDWDocFDWTag;FDWDocRevAsset
Where:
the unique ids FDWDocFDWTag and FDWDocRevAsset tells the software to copy and assign the existing document to asset and document to tag relationships to the document revision.
 Ensure that the End1 parameter in the FDWDocFDWTag and FDWDocRevAsset relationship definitions is correctly configured to replicate and assign the existing relationships (document to tag and document to asset) to the
new document revision. To update, do the following:
1. Search and select the corresponding relationship definition.
2. Select Actions 
 > Update.
3. In the End1 details section, for Copy behavior when end1 is copied:, select CopyRelationship.
What happens if I set the argument to True?
When the Arg8 argument is set to True, and a document with a numeric or character sequence revision scheme type is revised (for example, RevNum or Seq (Char)), the software checks for the existence of a minor sequence to
increment, and if it does not exist, the document is automatically created with the next major revision in the sequence.
Can the system automatically inherit existing document relationships when creating a new document revision?
Yes, by following the steps given below:
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, select Workflow Step Definitions.
2. Search for Revise Document Versions, and select Actions 
 > Update.
3. In the Revise Document Versions step definition dialog, select the Workflow Step Type tab, and update the Process class name by appending the name with one or more unique ids of the relationship definitions of the document
where each value should be semicolon-separated.
Example:
ClaimAndReviseRelatedDocuments,,SDAChangeRequestDocVersions,True,,,,,False,FDWDocFDWTag;FDWDocRevAsset
Where:
the unique ids FDWDocFDWTag and FDWDocRevAsset tells the software to copy and assign the existing document to asset and document to tag relationships to the document revision.
 Ensure that the End1 parameter in the FDWDocFDWTag and FDWDocRevAsset relationship definitions is correctly configured to replicate and assign the existing relationships (document to tag and document to asset) to the
new document revision. To update, do the following:
1. Search and select the corresponding relationship definition.
2. Select Actions 
 > Update.
3. In the End1 details section, for Copy behavior when end1 is copied:, select CopyRelationship.
The following examples show each type of the ClaimAndReviseRelatedDocuments process step:
<SPFStepDef>
    <IObject UID="STP_SOPSClaimToProject" Name="Claim to project" Description="Claim change request to project" ContainerID="SOPS.ProjComsSecurity" />
    <ISPFAdminItem />
    <ISPFStepDef />
</SPFStepDef>
<Rel>
    <IObject UID="STP_SOPSClaimToProject.WSC_SOPSClaimToProject" ContainerID="SOPS.ProjComsSecurity" />
    <IRel UID1="STP_SOPSClaimToProject" DefUID="SPFStepDefStepClass" UID2="WSC_SOPSClaimToProject" />
</Rel>
<SPFStepDefClass>
    <IObject UID="WSC_SOPSClaimToProject" Name="Claim to project" Description="Claim change request to project" ContainerID="SOPS.ProjComsSecurity" />
    <ISPFAdminItem />
    <ISPFStepDefClass SPFProcessClassName="ClaimAndReviseRelatedDocuments,SDAChangeRequestProject,SDAChangeRequestDocVersions,True,,,,,False" />
</SPFStepDefClass>
<Rel>
    <IObject UID="WSC_SOPSClaimToProject.WST_P" ContainerID="SOPS.ProjComsSecurity" />
    <IRel UID1="WSC_SOPSClaimToProject" DefUID="SPFStepClassStepType" UID2="WST_P" />
</Rel>
<SPFStepDefType>
    <IObject UID="WST_P" Name="P" Description="Process" ContainerID="Core.Workflow_Admin" />
    <ISPFStepDefType />
    <ISPFAdminItem />
</SPFStepDefType>
Create a consolidated PDF rendition object
What is a consolidated PDF rendition?
A consolidated PDF rendition includes PDFs of all the documents and files attached to a workflow. This improves the readability of the complete workflow for a recipient.
Consolidated PDF rendition
The maintenance planning workflow automatically creates a consolidated PDF rendition object in the document review process for all the files attached to each document of the workflow. The consolidated PDF is generated after the
document has been consolidated and contractually approved, but before it is sent to the recipient.
Process step class
This process step class can be used on a maintenance planning workflow object to generate the consolidated PDF rendition. The arguments define what the workflow does when a workflow document object is generated as a consolidated
PDF rendition.
Process class name: SDACreateDocConsolidatedPDFRendition
Syntax: SDACreateDocConsolidatedPDFRendition,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3,Arg4,Arg5,Arg6,Arg7,Arg8,Arg9,Arg10,Arg11,Arg12.
Example:
SDACreateDocConsolidatedPDFRendition,SDAMaintenancePlanningDocAllFilesGraph,FT_pdf, &Obj->SPFFileComposition_12.Name&,True,True,True,CurrentDate,True,,False,,-SDAWorkPackageMarkupFiles
Where:
Arguments
Definitions
Arg1
The graph definition from workflow items to all files.
Arg2
UID of the target file type (Optional).
Arg3
Dynamic expression that is used by the system to evaluate each file to add the bookmark (Optional).
For example, the expression &Obj->SPFFileComposition_12.Name& tells the system to add document name
related to the file as the bookmark.
Arg4
Do not include markups (Optional).
Arg5
Do not include references (Optional).
Arg6
Include labels (Optional).
Arg7
Label name (Optional).
Adds the label text to the pdf rendition. For example, to include current date in the pdf rendition, this argument can
be defined as CurrentDate.
Arg8
Include watermark (Optional).
Arg9
Watermark name (Optional).
Arg10
Relate generated PDFs with individual files (Optional).
Arg11
Document Revision Criteria (Optional).
If you don’t select anything, all documents linked to the workflow item will be considered.
If set to CURRENT, only documents in the Current state will be considered.
If you choose WORKING_IF_NO_CURRENT, the system first checks for a Current revision. If one exists, it will
be used. If not, the system checks the document’s status and uses the Working revision if available.
Arg12
The relationship definition from the source object to the markups (Optional).
For example, from the work package to the required markups.
The following examples show each type of the SDACreateConsolidatedDocPDFRendition process step:
Step definition class
<SPFStepDefClass>
<IObject UID="WSC_SDAGenerateMergePDFRendition" Name="SDAGenerateMergePDFRendition" Description="Generate merge PDF rendition to the files attached" ContainerID="SDA.ProjComsWPWF" />
<ISPFAdminItem />
<ISPFStepDefClass SPFProcessClassName="SDACreateDocConsolidatedPDFRendition,SDAMaintenancePlanningDocAllFilesGraph,FT_pdf,&amp;Obj-
&gt;SPFFileComposition_12.Name&amp;,True,True,True,CurrentDate,True,,False,,-SDAWorkPackageMarkupFiles" />
</SPFStepDefClass>
Relationship between step definition class and type
<Rel>
<IObject UID="WSC_SDAGenerateMergePDFRendition.WST_P" ContainerID="SDA.ProjComsWPWF" />
<IRel UID1="WSC_SDAGenerateMergePDFRendition" DefUID="SPFStepClassStepType" UID2="WST_P" />
</Rel>
Step definition type
<SPFStepDefType>
<IObject UID="WST_P" Name="P" Description="Process" ContainerID="Core.Workflow_Admin" />
<ISPFStepDefType />
<ISPFAdminItem />
</SPFStepDefType>
Relationship between step definition and class
<Rel>
<IObject UID="STP_SDAGenerateMergePDFRendition.WSC_SDAGenerateMergePDFRendition" ContainerID="SDA.ProjComsWPWF" />
<IRel UID1="STP_SDAGenerateMergePDFRendition" DefUID="SPFStepDefStepClass" UID2="WSC_SDAGenerateMergePDFRendition" />
</Rel>
Step definition
<SPFStepDef>
<IObject UID="STP_SDAGenerateMergePDFRendition" Name="SDAGenerateMergePDFRendition" Description="Generate Merge PDF rendition to the files attached" ContainerID="SDA.ProjComsWPWF" />
<ISPFAdminItem />
<ISPFStepDef />
</SPFStepDef>
Create document PDF renditions
The software automatically creates a PDF rendition object in the document review process after the document has been consolidated and contractually approved, but before the document is sent to the recipient contractor or supplier.
Process step class
This process step class can be used on any workflow object and must be configured as the last process step before an interactive step. The arguments define what the software does when a workflow document object is generated as a
PDF rendition for a technical query, an action, a document, or any responses.
Process class name: SDACreateDocPDFRenditions
Syntax: SDACreateDocPDFRenditions, Arg1, Arg2, Arg3, Arg4, Arg5, Arg6, Arg7, Arg8, Arg9, Arg10, Arg11, Arg12, Arg13, Arg14, Arg15
Example:
SDACreateDocPDFRenditions,SDAObservationDocVersions,SCLBResponseMarkupFiles,SCLBResponseDocRenditions,DRC_Response,
True,,,,EDG_SCLBProjComsIssuedResponses,CS_SCLBActionCS,,Obj,Obj,True,SDAWatermarksForWF
Where:
Arguments
Definitions
Arg1
The relationship definition from the source object to document version (for example, from the Technical
Query/Action/Document revision to document version).
Arg2
The relationship definition from the source object to the markups (for example, from the technical query to the
required markups).
Arg3
The relationship definition from the source object to the rendition object (for example, from the technical query to the
rendition).
Arg4
Rendition category.
Arg5
Include action pin details.
Arg6
Not used.
Arg7
Not used.
Arg8
Timeout in milliseconds. (Optional)
Arg9
Edge definition or relationship definition to the starting objects. The starting objects for the rendition are the technical
query responses and the documents with markup responses. If argument 11 or 12 is specified, argument 9 becomes
the starting object for the markups. (Optional)
Arg10
UID of the column set that will be used for the changemarks summary page appended to the end of the PDF
rendition. The default value is CS_SPFObject.
Arg11
Relationship definition or edge definition to navigate from the source object to the documents. (Optional)
Examples:
Obj would navigate to the workflow item.
Obj->(relationship definition or edge definition) would expand the workflow item to the relationship
definition or the edge definition.
Arg12
Relationship definition or edge definition to navigate from the source object to the starting object for the rendition.
(Optional)
Examples:
Obj would navigate to the workflow item.
Obj->(relationship definition or edge definition) would expand the workflow item to the relationship
definition or edge definition.
Arg13
Relationship definition or edge definition to navigate from the source object to the responses, and from the
responses to the document version/rendition. (Optional)
Examples:
Obj would navigate to the workflow item.
Obj->(relationship definition or edge definition) would expand the workflow item to the relationship
definition or edge definition.
Arg14
A boolean value to control the creation of a PDF which is on filetype to filetype relation. If set to True, this skips the
condition evaluation to force the PDF generation. By default, it is set to False, which means it honors the condition to
evaluate and generate the PDF. (Optional)
Arg15
Apply specific watermarks when creating a workflow step rendition in the Web Client. You can apply multiple
watermarks while using | operator. For example, SimpleWaterMark|WaterMarkRevision|... and so on. For more
information, see Configure watermarks. (Optional).
 The parameters set in the “Use” form section for watermarks do not impact this scenario. Watermarks
associated with workflow steps will consistently be applied regardless of the settings.
Examples for each process step
The following examples show each type of SDACreateDocPDFRenditions process step:

<SPFStepDefClass>
<IObject UID="WSC_SCLBGenerateDocPDFRenditionsForTQ" Name="SCLBGenerateDocPDFRenditionsForTQ" Description="Generate document PDF renditions for technical query" />
<ISPFStepDefClass SPFProcessClassName="SDACreateDocPDFRenditions,SCLBTQReferenceDocVersions,
           
           SCLBCommunicationItemsMarkupFiles,SCLBTQReferenceDocRenditions,DRC_TQ,True,,,,,
           
           CS_SCLBActionCS,,,," />
<ISPFAdminItem />
</SPFStepDefClass>

           SCLBCommunicationItemsMarkupFiles,SCLBActionReferenceDocRenditions,DRC_Action,True,,,,,
           
           CS_SCLBActionCS,,,," />
<ISPFAdminItem />
</SPFStepDefClass>

           SCLBDocVersionsMarkupFiles,SDADocumentRenditions,DRC_Consolidated Document,True,,,,,

renditions" />

renditions" />

renditions" />

<ISPFStepDefClass SPFProcessClassName="SDACreateDocPDFRenditions,SCLBTQPrimaryDocVersion,SCLBCommunicationItemsMarkupFiles,
SCLBTQPrimaryDocRendition,DRC_TQ,True,,,,,CS_SCLBActionCS,,,,,sampleWaterMark" />
