</SPFStepDefClass>
Create document review objects
This process step class creates document review objects and sends a notification to a user's inbox or a step to the user's To Do List to review a document revision.
When a document goes through this step, the software evaluates all review plan items related to that document. The review plan items must already be generated by another process. For each review plan item matching the issue purpose
of the current document, a SCLBDocReview object is generated. The user is assigned to the review plan item using the SDAObservationOriginatingUser relationship definition.
In addition, a single consolidator review is created for the recipient of the issue step in the workflow. The consolidator review step has the SCLBProjComsIsConsolidatedReview property set to true.
Each review generated is created with a calculated due date. The due date is based on the contract's SCLBProjComsConsolidatorReviewResponseDays or SCLBProjComsReviewerReviewResponseDays property, depending on whether
it is a consolidator review or regular review.
The default status of the review is DRAFT.
Process Class Name: SCLBCreateDocReviews
Syntax: SCLBCreateDocReviews,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3,Arg4
Example: SCLBCreateDocReviews,SCLBProjComsConsolidateDocReviewResponses,SCLBProjComsPerformReview,Document receipt notification,False
Where:
Arg1: Step definition to identify the consolidator in the current workflow.
Arg2: Step definition to identify the reviewer in the current workflow.
Arg3: Step definition to identify the user who has a document receipt notification step in the current workflow. (Optional)
Arg4: A boolean value to control the creation of a review object for a user who has the document receipt notification step. If set to True, a review object is created for that user. By default, it is set to False, which means a review object is not
created for that user. Argument 4 is honored only if argument 3 is set. (Optional)
Create document review objects based on appended recipients
This process step class gets the recipient from the Perform QA Review step and generates a SCLBDocReview object by relating the document review object of the user for the latest issue purpose of the document using the
SDAObservationOrginatingUser, SDAObservationOriginatingOrg, SCLBWFStepRecipientDocReviewObject, and SCLBDocReviewIssuePurposes relationship definitions. If the document review object already exists for the user for that
issue purpose of the document, the system updates the SCLBDocReview status to Draft, terminates the SCLBWFStepRecipientDocReviewObject relationship, and creates a relationship between the document review object and the new
recipient object.
Process Class Name: SCLBAddReviewObjectForAppendedReviewer
Delete latest revision(s)
This process class name deletes, or terminates, all document revisions and related items created as part of an internal change action that has been rejected. If the document is reserved, only attached files are deleted.
Process class name: RemoveRevs
Syntax: RemoveRevs,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3,Arg4,Arg5
Example:
RemoveRevs,SCLBActionPrimaryDocVersion,SOPSActionInitialDocVersions,,,False
Where:
Arg1: Defines the relationship definition used to navigate from the starting object, such as an Action, to the primary document version.
Arg2: Defines the relationship definition used to navigate from the starting object, such as an Action, to the initial document version.
Arg3: Defines the relationship definition used to navigate from the starting object, such as an Action, to the revisable grouping object.
Arg4: Option to define relationship definition to identify and remove any related renditions.
Arg5: Option to delete or to terminate document revisions and their related items. The default option is False, which deletes the objects.
Delete relationships between markups and a change request
This process step deletes the relationship between markups and the change request, based on the Arg1 value, when the change request is either completed or rejected .
Process class name: SDAClearTemporaryChangeRequestAsync
Syntax: SDAClearTemporaryChangeRequestAsync,Arg1
Where:
Arg1: Is a list of the relationship definitions separated by ; that specify the relations between the change request and the markup files.
Examples:
SDAClearTemporaryChangeRequestAsync,SDAChangeRequestMarkupFiles
SDAClearTemporaryChangeRequestAsync,SDAMarkupActionMarkupLayer;SDAChangeRequestMarkupFiles
Where:
SDAChangeRequestMarkupFiles: Is the relation between the temporary change request and the markup files.
SDAMarkupActionMarkupLayer: Is the relation between the normal change request and the markup files associated with the action pin.
Delete workflow markups
This process step can be used on a workflow object to delete the markup files.
Process class name: SDAAutoDeleteMarkups
Syntax: SDAAutoDeleteMarkups,Arg1
Example:
SDAAutoDeleteMarkups,SDAWorkPackageMarkupFiles
Where:
Arg1 defines the relationship definition used to navigate to the markup files.
The following examples show each type of the SDAAutoDeleteMarkups process step:
Step definition class
<SPFStepDefClass>
<IObject UID="WSC_SDAAutoDeleteMarkups" Name="SDAAutoDeleteMarkups" Description="Deletes markup(s) after the successful generation of Merge PDF generation" />
<ISPFAdminItem />
<ISPFStepDefClass SPFProcessClassName="SDAAutoDeleteMarkups,SDAWorkPackageMarkupFiles" />
</SPFStepDefClass>
Step definition
<SPFStepDef>
<IObject UID="STP_SDAAutoDeleteMarkups" Name="SDAAutoDeleteMarkups" Description="Deletes markup(s) after the successful generation of Merge PDF generation" />
<ISPFAdminItem />
<ISPFStepDef />
</SPFStepDef>
Expand a definition and instantiate the relationship
This process step is used to expand relationship, edge, and graph definitions from the workflow object and instantiate the relationships.
Process class name: SDAExpandAndInstantiateRel
Syntax: SDAExpandAndInstantiateRel,Arg1
Example: SDAExpandAndInstantiateRel,EDG_ChangeActionPrimaryWorkAreaOrg:SDAChangeActionPrimaryOrg;EDG_ChangeActionSubscribeWorkAreaOrg:SDAChangeActionSubscribeOrg
Where:
Arg1: Is a list of definitions and relationships separated by colons. The items in the list are separated by semi-colons.
Export and compress the files related to the source object
This exports the files associated with the documents to a specified directory path with optional PDF generation and compression into a Zip file, creates a design file based on the Zip file, and relates the design file with the source object.
Process Class Name: ExportAndCompressAsyncStep
Syntax: ExportAndCompressAsyncStep,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3,Arg4
Example: ExportAndCompressAsyncStep,MTH_ExportTransmittalStructure,SPFCFromInternalUser:SCLBProjComsFromCCUser,OBJ->SPFCFromInternalOrg_12.SDAEmailAddress,SCLBShareFileEmail
Where:
Arg1: Method to indicate whether to compress the files and relate them to the source object. This method can also be associated to transmittals to be available on the shortcut menu of a transmittal to export the object interactively.
Arg2: Defines the relationship for the list of users, separated by a colon, from the source object that will be used to find the users to send an email notification.
Arg3: Path definition from the source object to the optional email address that will be used to send an email notification, when no users are found with the given relationship definitions.
Arg4: Email definition to identify the information only reviewer in the current workflow.
Generate document reviews for the latest issue purposes
This step generates the reviews for the latest issue purposes on the document for the given step recipients on the review, consolidation, and information only steps.
Process Class Name: SCLBGenerateReviews
Syntax: SCLBGenerateReviews,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3,Arg4,
Example: SCLBGenerateReviews,SCLBProjComsConsolidateDocReviewResponses,SCLBProjComsPerformReview,,False
Where:
Arg1: Step definition to identify the consolidator in the current workflow.
Arg2: Step definition to identify the reviewer in the current workflow.
Arg3: Step definition to identify the information only reviewer in the current workflow. (Optional)
Arg4: A boolean value to create the review object for information only reviewers. If set to True, a review object is created for that user. By default, it is set to False, which means a review object is not created for that user.
Identify the reviewers in the document review process
This step identifies the reviewers, consolidators, and information only reviewers in the QA review workflow.
Process Class Name: SCLBIdentifyReviewers
Syntax: SCLBIdentifyReviewers,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3
Example: SCLBIdentifyReviewers,SCLBProjComsConsolidateDocReviewResponses,SCLBProjComsPerformReview
Where:
Arg1: Step definition to identify the consolidator in the current workflow.
Arg2: Step definition to identify the reviewer in the current workflow.
Arg3: Step definition to identify the information only reviewer in the current workflow. (Optional)
Merge a workflow item
This process step merges the object related to the workflow step from a lower configuration to a higher configuration.
Process class name: SDAMergeWorkflowItem
Syntax: SDAMergeWorkflowItem,Arg1
Example: SDAMergeWorkflowItem,True
Where:
Arg1: Is a Boolean value to determine whether or not the workflow item needs to be merged from a lower configuration to a higher configuration.
Merge to as-built
This process step takes mergeable items related to an Action, such as documents, from across multiple configurations and merges them into a single configuration. If any items cannot be merged, the step is rejected and a message lists
the items that cannot be merged.
Process class name: MergeAttachedItems
Syntax: MergeAttachedItems,Arg1
Examples:
MergeAttachedItems,+SCLBActionPrimaryDocVersion,SDAProjectComsReferenceData_12,-SPFFileComposition,EDG_SPFViewableFiles,
Where:
Arg1: Defines the relationship definitions, or edge definitions, used to navigate from the starting object, such as an Action, to any object that can be merged, including documents and files. For relationships, the direction to navigate needs
to be included with the UID in the format of + or _12 for end 1 to end 2 navigation, and – or _21 for end 2 to end 1 navigation.
Propagate milestone property
This process step is used to set the progress milestone property for the given issue purposes from the document revision property or from a given value.
Process class name: PropagateMilestoneProperty
Syntax: PropagateMilestoneProperty, Arg1, Arg2, Arg3, Arg4
Where: The PropagateMilestoneProperty process step class supports multiple arguments. Each of the arguments are described below:
Arg1: Defines the relationship definition between document revision and issue purpose, used to query the progress deliverable milestones.
Arg2: Specifies the document revision property or the source property on the document revision.
Arg3: Specifies the target property on the milestone.
Arg4: This is an optional argument. The system sets the given argument value on the milestone property. If the argument value is blank, the system clears the milestone property.
Propagate reason for rejection
This process class name is used to automatically populate the reason for rejection comment required when a step in a collaboration workflow is rejected.
The process class name should be used just before ValidateProperty for Reason for the Rejection process step. For more information, see Validate comments when a step is rejected.
By using the PropagateProperty,Args process class name, the software populates the comments from the source, and then uses the ValidateProperty process step class to validate the comments.
Process class name: PropagateProperty,Args
Syntax: PropagateProperty,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3
Examples:
PropagateProperty,OBJ.Description,PREVIOUSINTERACTIVE.SPFStepComments
PropagateProperty,OBJ.SCLBProjComsResponseRejectReason,PREVIOUSINTERACTIVE.SPFStepComments
PropagateProperty,OBJ.SDAProjComsRejectReason,PREVIOUSINTERACTIVECLAIMED.SPFStepComments
PropagateProperty,OBJ.SDAProjComsDueDate,NOW + Obj->SDAProjectComsContract_12.SCLBIncomingActionDefaultResponseDays(d),TRUE
Where:
Arg1: OBJ is a prefix that means current object. SCLBProjComsResponseRejectReason is the property definition that populates the comments.
Arg2: SPFStepComments is the step where comments are provided from the source.
Using the keyword PREVIOUSINTERACTIVE as an argument reverts to the previous step in the workflow where user interaction is required for signing off the step.
Using the keyword PREVIOUSINTERACTIVECLAIMED as an argument reverts to the previous claimed step in the workflow where user interaction is required for signing off the claimed step.
Arg3: A Boolean value that defines if nonworking days need to be considered for date property calculations, such as due date. If set to True, non-working days are not considered for calculations. If set to False, non-working days are
considered for calculations. The default value is False. (Optional)
Propagate roles or users to a workflow process step
This process step is used to automatically assign users or roles to a workflow step and automatically route items to them. This allows items to be assigned as needed.
 This process step must be configured before the work step, and it should not be used as a PostProcessStepClass if the step has multiple success steps.
Process class name: PropagateRecipients
Syntax: PropagateRecipients,Arg1,Arg2,...,ArgX
Where: The PropagateRecipients process step class supports multiple arguments with the following conditions:
If an argument relationship definition expansion returns any values, the process step will assign recipients and will not process any additional arguments.
If the relationship definition expansion on all arguments does not return any value, the default recipient for the step is assigned.
Each argument is a combination of RelDefUID with direction (a single relationship definition, multiple relationship definitions, edge definition, or property definition), reason for receipt, recipient format, and workflow information steps
delimited by ~. This set of parameters can be repeated with different values by separating each set with a comma. Each of the four parameters are described below:
Parameter1: Defines the relationship, edge, or property definition that will be used to find the users or roles to assign to the step.
Parameter2: Defines the reason for receipt.
Parameter3: Defines the format in which the recipient receives files associated with the workflow.
Parameter4: Displays workflow information steps to the user if set to True. If the argument is set to False, the workflow information steps are not displayed.
For more information and examples, see Configure dynamic assignment of recipients.
Relate the latest issue purposes to the document
The SCLBRelateLatestIssuePurposes is a process class specific to the QA Review Workflows. In this step, all previous issue purpose relationships to the document revision are terminated first. You can filter out the latest issue purposes
for which the review is pending and then relate the review to the required issue purposes.
 The QA review workflow is initiated for a single document.
Process Class Name: SCLBRelateLatestIssuePurposes
Revise a SDx2 Projects workflow object
The software automatically revises any collaboration workflow object that has been rejected, such as a technical query, action, or document review.
When a collaboration workflow object is revised, the object's class definition realizes the object’s interface definition. For example, an object using the SCLBAction class definition realizes the ISDARevisableObject.
When the object is revised, the major and minor revision scheme is incremented based on arguments defined in the SCLBReviseObject process step class. The SDARevObjSuperseded property is set to True on the superseded revision. A
search for all the objects shows the objects that are only in the latest revision (not superseded).
 If you want to see a previous version, select the project communication object, and click Actions > All revisions.
Process step class
The process step class can be used on any collaboration workflow object. The arguments define what the software does when a workflow object is revised.
Process class name: SCLBReviseObject
Syntax: SCLBReviseObject,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3,Arg4,Arg5,Arg6,Arg7,Arg8,Arg9,Arg10
Example: SCLBReviseObject,SDAProjectComsContract:SDAFromExternalUser,SCLBProjComsDaysToRespond, ,FALSE,RS_Rev01A,FALSE, ,TRUE,FALSE,TRUE
Where:
Arg1: The relationship definitions to instantiate to the new revision. These are separated by a colon.
Arg2: The properties to clear. These are separated by a colon.
Arg3: The properties to terminate. These are separated by a colon.
Arg4: The Boolean value for the major and minor revision. If set to True, the next major revision is honored. By default, it is set to False, which means the next minor revision is honored.
Arg5: The revision scheme.
Arg6: A Boolean value for attaching the revision to the new workflow. If set to True, the revision is attached to a new workflow. By default, it is set to False, which means the revision is not attached to a new workflow and the revision
continues in the original workflow.
Arg7: Leave blank unless changing to a new workflow. If changing the workflow, provide the name of the new workflow.
Arg8: Boolean for copying the attached files to the revised object.
 While revising an object through the workflow that uses the SCLBReviseObject process class, all the properties of the revised object are copied to the new instance, except the following properties:
Initiated date
Initiated by
Due Date
Reject Date
Reject Reason
Rejected By
Arg9: A Boolean value for copying the latest version of the attached documents when the object is revised through the workflow. By default, it is set to False. (Optional)
Arg10: A Boolean value for copying the markups on the attached files to the revised object. By default, it is set to True.
Process class name: SCLBReviseObjectWithForm
Syntax: SCLBReviseObjectWithForm,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3,Arg4,Arg5,Arg6
Example: SCLBReviseObjectWithForm,FALSE,RS_Rev01A,FALSE, ,TRUE,TRUE
Where:
Arg1: The Boolean value for the major and minor revision. If set to True, the next major revision is honored. By default, it is set to False, which means the next minor revision is honored.
Arg2: The revision scheme.
Arg3: A Boolean value for attaching the revision to the new workflow. If set to True, the revision is attached to a new workflow. By default, it is set to False, which means the revision is not attached to a new workflow and the revision
continues in the original workflow.
Arg4: Leave blank unless changing to a new workflow. If changing to a new workflow, provide the name of the workflow.
Arg5: Boolean for copying the attached files to the revised object.
When revising an object through the workflow using the SCLBReviseObjectWithForm process class, all the properties and the relationships set with the copy12 property of the revised object are copied to the new instance.
You can explicitly specify the properties as the display items that are required to be cleared or set to default for the new object in the SCLBTQReviseSection New form section. The form purpose is then set as Revise.
The properties that need to be cleared on the new object are the display items with the default revise values set to null.
You can explicitly specify the relationships that do not have the copy12 property set as the display items in the SCLBTQReviseSection New form section. The form purpose is then set as Revise so that these relationships are copied
to the new object.
Arg6: A Boolean value for copying the markups on the attached files to the revised object. By default, it is set to True.
Revise document attached to an action
This process class automatically revises documents attached to an action at a plant or project level and creates a new relationship between the action and the revised document.
Documents in a working state will not be revised.
Documents that cannot be revised are rejected and the user receives a message.
Can the system automatically inherit existing document relationships when creating a new document revision?
Yes, by following the steps given below:
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, select Workflow Step Definitions.
2. Search for SOPSRevDocuments, and select Actions 
 > Update.
3. In the Update Workflow Step Definition dialog, select the Workflow Step Type tab, and update the Process class name by appending the name with one or more unique ids of the relationship definitions of the document where
each value should be semicolon-separated.
Example:
ReviseRelatedObjects,SCLBActionPrimaryDocVersion,SOPSActionInitialDocVersions,False,FDWDocRevAsset;FDWDocRevTag
Where:
the unique ids FDWDocRevTag and FDWDocRevAsset tell the software to copy and assign the existing relationships (document to asset and document to tag) to the new document revision.
 Ensure that the End1 parameter in the FDWDocRevTag and FDWDocRevAsset relationship definitions is correctly configured to replicate and assign the existing relationships (document to tag and document to asset) to the
new document revision. To update, do the following:
1. Search and select the corresponding relationship definition.
2. Select Actions 
 > Update.
3. In the End1 details section, for Copy behavior when end1 is copied:, select CopyRelationship.
Process class name: ReviseRelatedObjects
Syntax: ReviseRelatedObjects,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3,Arg4
Examples:
ReviseRelatedObjects,SCLBActionPrimaryDocVersion,SOPSActionInitialDocVersions,False,FDWDocRevAsset;FDWDocRevTag
Where:
Arg1: Defines the relationship definition that is created on the revise step between the starting object, such as an Action, and the primary document. If the document is reserved, it will not be revised.
Arg2: Defines the relationship definition between the starting object, such as an Action, to the initial document version that will be revised or the reserved document.
Arg3: Determines if the minor revision scheme, if supported, should be incremented when a document is revised. If set to True, the minor revision is incremented. If set to False, the major revision scheme is incremented. The default is
False.
Arg4: Copies and assigns the existing document relationships to the new document revision. (Optional)
Revise document attached to the change request
This process class automatically revises the documents attached to the change request at a plant level on approving the change request evaluation, and thereby creates a new relationship between the change request and the revised
document.
Can the system automatically inherit existing document relationships when creating a new document revision?
Yes, by following the steps given below:
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, select Workflow Step Definitions.
2. Search for Revise Document Versions, and select Actions 
 > Update.
3. In the Revise Document Versions step definition dialog, select the Workflow Step Type tab, and update the Process class name by appending the name with one or more unique ids of the relationship definitions of the document
where each value should be semicolon-separated.
Example:
ClaimAndReviseRelatedDocuments,,SDAChangeRequestDocVersions,True,,,,,False,FDWDocFDWTag;FDWDocRevAsset
Where:
the unique ids FDWDocFDWTag and FDWDocRevAsset tells the software to copy and assign the existing document to asset and document to tag relationships to the document revision.
 Ensure that the End1 parameter in the FDWDocFDWTag and FDWDocRevAsset relationship definitions is correctly configured to replicate and assign the existing relationships (document to tag and document to asset) to the
new document revision. To update, do the following:
1. Search and select the corresponding relationship definition.
2. Select Actions 
 > Update.
3. In the End1 details section, for Copy behavior when end1 is copied:, select CopyRelationship.
Process class name: Revise Document Versions
Example:
ClaimAndReviseRelatedDocuments,,SDAChangeRequestDocVersions,True,,,,,False
The following examples show each type of the Revise Document Versions process step:
<SPFStepDef>
<IObject UID="STP_SOPSReviseDocuments" Name="Revise Document Versions" Description="Revise Document Versions" />
<ISPFStepDef />
<ISPFAdminItem />
</SPFStepDef>
<Rel>
<IObject UID="STP_SOPSReviseDocuments.SPFStepDefStepClass.WSC_SOPSReviseDocuments" />
<IRel UID1="STP_SOPSReviseDocuments" UID2="WSC_SOPSReviseDocuments" DefUID="SPFStepDefStepClass" />
</Rel>
<SPFStepDefClass>
<IObject UID="WSC_SOPSReviseDocuments" Name="Revise Document Versions" Description="Revise Document Versions" />
<ISPFStepDefClass SPFProgressWithoutAcknowledgement="False" SPFPreventRejectInd="False" SPFPasswordReqdForSignoff="False" SPFProcessClassName="ClaimAndReviseRelatedDocuments,,SDAChangeRequestDocVersions,True,,,,,False" />
<ISPFAdminItem />
</SPFStepDefClass>
<Rel>
<IObject UID="WSC_SOPSReviseDocuments.SPFStepClassStepType.WST_P" />
<IRel UID1="WSC_SOPSReviseDocuments" UID2="WST_P" DefUID="SPFStepClassStepType" />
</Rel>
<SPFStepDefType>
<IObject UID="WST_P" Name="P" Description="Process" />
<ISPFStepDefType />
<ISPFAdminItem />
</SPFStepDefType>
Running and attaching an OpenXML Word report
This following process step is used to run an OpenXML Word report. For details on configuring the report, see Generate OpenXML Word report for an object.
Name:
Process class name: RunAndAttachOpenXMLWordReport, Arg1, Arg2, Arg3, Arg4
Where:
Arg1: Name of the template.
Arg2: Truncate time.
Arg3: Report category for classified objects.
Arg4: File class definition. If argument 4 is specified, the software creates a report file object of that class definition and attaches it to the object for which the OpenXML Word report is generated.
Example used in transmittals: RunAndAttachOpenXMLWordReport, Internal Transmittal Word OpenXml Template, False, ,SPFDesignFile
Running and attaching an OpenXML Word report asynchronously
This following process step is used to run an OpenXML Word report asynchronously. For details on configuring the report, see Generate OpenXML Word report for an object.
Name:
Process class name: RunAndAttachOpenXMLWordReportAsync, Arg1, Arg2, Arg3, Arg4
Where:
Arg1: Name of the template.
Arg2: Truncate time.
Arg3: Report category for classified objects.
Arg4: File class definition. If argument 4 is specified, the software creates a report file object of that class definition asynchronously and attaches it to the object for which the OpenXML Word report is generated.
Example used in transmittals: RunAndAttachOpenXMLWordReportAsync, Internal Transmittal Word OpenXml Template, False, ,SPFDesignFile
Schedule a task to start a review temporary change workflow
This process step is used as part of the temporary change request workflow. It schedules a task to start a review temporary change workflow for temporary changes that are expiring soon.
Process class name: SDACreateChangeRequestSchedulerTask
Syntax: SDACreateChangeRequestSchedulerTask,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3
Example:
SDACreateChangeRequestSchedulerTask,OBJ.SDAChngReqExpiryDate,3,WD_HEX_SOPSTempChngReqReviewWorkflow
Where:
Arg1: Specifies the expression to return the expiration date.
Arg2: Defines the number of days before the expiration date that the change request should be attached to the workflow.
Arg3: Defines the workflow to attach to the change request.
Set the target date on the review step
This process class is to set the target date on the newly added review step in the QA review workflow.
Process class name: SCLBSetTargetDateForNewStep
Syntax: SCLBSetTargetDateForNewStep,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3
Example:
SCLBSetTargetDateForNewStep,SCLBProjComsConsolidateDocReviewResponses,OBJ->FDWDocRevisionContract_12.ConsolidationResponseDays,OBJ-> FDWDocRevisionContract_12.ReviewResponseDays
Where:
Arg1: Step definition, of the consolidation step, to calculate the target date for the new review step.
Arg2: Navigation path to get the consolidation response days.
Arg3: Navigation path to get the review response days.
Sign off latest revision
This process class name is used to sign off the latest document revisions in the current configuration that are related to an internal change action. During the sign off of a document revision that is related to a change action, the process
step checks to see if the document version related to the action is the latest. If it is not, the step changes the relationship to the latest document version.
Process class name: SignOffAttachedDocs
Syntax: SignOffAttachedDocs,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3
Examples:
SignOffAttachedDocs,SCLBActionPrimaryDocVersion
SignOffAttachedDocs,SCLBSubmittalDocumentVersions_12,FALSE,EDG_AllReleasedRevisions
Where:
Arg1: Defines the relationship definition, or edge definition, used to navigate from the object attached to the workflow, such as an action or technical query, to its attached document versions.
Arg2: A Boolean value when set to True, relates the latest document version to the starting object, such as an Action. When set to False, the system does not relate the latest document version to the starting object. If the argument is
blank, the value is set to True. (Optional)
Arg3: Defines the relationship definition, or edge definition, used to navigate to all older document revisions, and sets the revision state to superseded. (Optional)
Sign off latest version of the document attached to the change request
This process class name is used to sign off the latest document revisions in the plant configuration that are related to the change request after reviewing and approving the change. During the sign off of a document revision that is related
to a change request, the process step checks to see if the document version related to the action is the latest.
Process class name: SignOff Change Request
Example:
SignOffAttachedDocs, SDAChangeRequestDocVersions, FALSE
The following examples show each type of the SignOff Change Request process step:
<SPFStepDef>
<IObject UID="STP_SOPSSignOffChangeRequest" Name="SignOff Change request" Description="Sign Off Change request" />
<ISPFStepDef />
<ISPFAdminItem />
</SPFStepDef>
<Rel>
<IObject UID="STP_SOPSSignOffChangeRequest.SPFStepDefStepClass.WSC_SOPSSignOffChangeRequest" />
<IRel UID1="STP_SOPSSignOffChangeRequest" UID2="WSC_SOPSSignOffChangeRequest" DefUID="SPFStepDefStepClass" />
</Rel>
<SPFStepDefClass>
<IObject UID="WSC_SOPSSignOffChangeRequest" Name="SignOff Change request" Description="Sign Off Change request" />
<ISPFStepDefClass SPFProgressWithoutAcknowledgement="False" SPFPreventRejectInd="False" SPFPasswordReqdForSignoff="False" SPFProcessClassName="SignOffAttachedDocs,+SDAChangeRequestDocVersions,FALSE" />
<ISPFAdminItem />
</SPFStepDefClass>
<Rel>
<IObject UID="WSC_SOPSSignOffChangeRequest.SPFStepClassStepType.WST_P" />
<IRel UID1="WSC_SOPSSignOffChangeRequest" UID2="WST_P" DefUID="SPFStepClassStepType" />
</Rel>
<SPFStepDefType>
<IObject UID="WST_P" Name="P" Description="Process" />
<ISPFStepDefType />
<ISPFAdminItem />
</SPFStepDefType>
Skip document reviews
When a document goes through this step, the software determines if the given issue purposes of the document have document review objects. For those issue purposes that do not have a document review object, a document review
object is generated, and the status of that object is set to ReturnedWithoutReview.
Process class name: SCLBSkipDocReview
Terminate a relationship between the objects
Process class name: TerminateRelationship
Syntax: TerminateRelationship,Arg1,Arg2
Examples:
TerminateRelationship,SPFNotificationEmaildef
TerminateRelationship,SPFNotificationEmaildef_12,SPFReldef_12
Where:
Arg1: Specifies the UID of the relationship to be terminated.
Arg2: Relationship definition or edge definition to navigate from the source object to the target object. (Optional)
If the relationship definition expansion is not specified, the object is terminated both ways.
Validate a common relationship between a workflow object and its child objects
SDAValidateWFItemAndChildrenShareAnyRels process class is created to validate whether the workflow object and its child object share a common relationship.
Syntax: SDAValidateWFItemAndChildrenShareAnyRels,Arg1,Arg2,Arg3,Arg4
Arguments: Arg1, Arg2, Arg3, Arg4
Where:
Arg1: Relationship definition or edge definition to navigate from workflow object to child objects.
Arg2: Required relationship from each child object to compare with workflow object relationships.
Arg3: Required relationship from workflow object used for comparison.
Arg4: Resource number of the message.
Example of a Technical Query workflow: SDAValidateWFItemAndChildrenShareAnyRels,Obj->SCLBTQReferenceDocVersions_12->EDG_VersionMaster_12,Child->SDADocOrganizations_12, Obj-
>SDAProjectCommunicationToOrganization_12,2823.
 If the user configures a resource number, then the system checks the resource files for the corresponding configured message to be displayed.
Validate a condition against the workflow object
This process step class validates a condition against the workflow object. If the condition fails, the workflow goes back to the previous step and displays the error message indicated.
Process class name: ValidateByCondition
Syntax: ValidateByCondition,Arg1,Arg2
Example: ValidateByCondition,SCLBIsConsolidatorSet,3120
Where:
Arg1: Is the condition name.
Arg2: Is the resource name (or number) of the error message.
Validate comments when a step is rejected
This process step is used to automatically verify that comments were provided when a workflow step is rejected. Comments are required if the step is rejected. This process step is used with the PropagateProperty process step in the
collaboration workflows and can only be used on a reject step.
Process class name: ValidateProperty, Args
Syntax: ValidateProperty, Arg1, Arg2, Arg3, Arg4
Example: ValidateProperty,ISDAManagedLifecycleDetails,SDAProjComsRejectReason,Rejected ( ),False
Where:
Arg1: Defines the interface that exposes the property. In the example above, ISDAManagedLifecycleDetails is the interface.
Arg2: Defines the property definition name where the comments are stored. In the example above, SDAProjComsRejectReason is the property definition name.
Arg3: The Rejected ( ) string is required for workflow steps that can be rejected. It is used to pass and store the comment on the property definition. The argument displays as Rejected (comment) on the property definition defined in Arg 2.
If a comment is not provided, a message appears requiring the comment to be provided before proceeding. The display of the message depends on Arg4.
Arg4: This string must be set to False for a message to appear. The False string validates the Rejected ( ) string in Arg3 and results in a message to the user when a comment is not provided. If set to True, no error message is given, and
the reject step will proceed.
Validate if a document with a given issue purpose has been reviewed
When a document goes through this step, the software evaluates if the revision with a given issue purpose has been reviewed. If the revision is:
Not reviewed - the accept path is taken.
Reviewed - the reject path is taken. The administrator needs to configure the reject path. If the reject path is not configured, the accept path is taken.
Process class name: SCLBIsDocAlreadyReviewed
Validate if a document with a given issue purpose has any reviewers
When a document goes through this step, the software determines if the revision with a given issue purpose has any reviewers.
Process class name: SCLBHasAnyReviewerSignedUp
Syntax: SCLBHasAnyReviewerSignedUp,Arg1
Arguments: Arg1
Where:
Arg1: Review plan item allowed statuses.
Example: SCLBHasAnyReviewerSignedUp,e1SCLBReviewPlanItemStatusACCEPTED
 You can also specify multiple statuses by separating them with a semicolon.
Validate the user email address
This process step is used to validate a user's email address by navigating to it using Arg1 from the workflow item object.
Process class name: SDAValidateUserEmailAddress
Syntax: SDAValidateUserEmailAddress,Arg1
Example: SDAValidateUserEmailAddress,SDADocSigners_12
Where:
Arg1: Is the relationship to navigate from the user to the object that needs to be validated, with direction.
 You can also specify multiple relationship definitions by separating them with a colon (:).
Merge workflow process steps
This process step type merges a set of workflow process steps into one process step as you create a workflow process step definition. This enhances the create workflow functionality as you can merge multiple step classes in a defined
order of execution.
As you create the workflow definition, the SPFMergeProcessStepClasses relationship definition is configured between the workflow step definition and the process step classes so that the individual process steps are executed in the order
they are related.
Process class name: MergeProcessSteps
Syntax: MergeProcessSteps
Example: MergeProcessSteps
 For more information on how to use this workflow process step type, see Create a merge workflow process step type.
Configuring a computer to support file mode share
In rare cases, you may need to share data not to HxGN SDx2, but to an XML file. This functionality can be useful when debugging.
This functionality is not configured to work out-of-the-box, but you can activate it, if you need it, using the following steps on every computer that will be sharing in file mode.
1. Create a local directory to store the CommonSchema.xml file.
2. Copy the CommonSchema.xml file from the HxGN SDx2 server to the new directory on the local machine.
3. Update the following environment variable on the local machine: SPCLCommmonSchemaFilePathFileMode. Provide the full path of the local copy of the CommonSchema.xml file, for example: D:\SIODirectory\CommonSchema.xml.
 If you want to enable file mode share for the current user only, update the environment variable in the User variables list. To allow any user on this computer to share in file mode, add the environment variable to the System
variables list.
 No mechanism exists to compare this local copy of the CommonSchema.xml file to the copy on the server. If changes are made to the copy on the server, you will need to update this local copy manually.
Getting a copy of the latest CommonSchema.xml file
1. Log on as a system administrator.
2. Click Design Tool Integration > Common Schema Document.
3. In the results, click on the Actions menu for the schema file.
4. Choose Save Target As and download the schema file to your local computer.
Request a tenant reset
If you need to completely reset the data storage for your tenant, you can request a tenant reset.
 A tenant reset is a drastic action. We recommend pursuing alternative solutions prior to requesting a tenant reset. This process can reset your tenant to the original, delivered status:
No tools are registered.
No classifications, disciplines, classification mappings, issue purposes, revision schemes, or plant breakdown structures are loaded.
No users are configured.
No extensions to the Common Schema remain.
No objects, tags, assets, or models remain.
 Only select individuals from your company can request a tenant reset.
To request a tenant reset:
1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> and create an Estate Request case. The following information is required:
The complete tenant URL.
The date and time frame when the reset should take place.
 Your tenant is inaccessible during the tenant reset, and depending on the amount of data that you must reload, you must plan for the time it takes you to restore your tenant settings.
Changes to the tenant including:
Resetting the database or restoring from a backup.
Resetting the Binary Large Object (BLOB) storage.
Retaining or removing all tenant users.
2. If you have integrated design tools, turn off tool sharing services, such as the Share Service for the tool, the Intergraph Schematic Scheduler, or SmartPlant.NET.
3. Notify your users of the HxGN SDx2 planned unavailability.
4. Wait for notification from Hexagon that we have completed the tenant reset for you.
5. Recreate your digital twin by following the procedures at Create your digital twin.
 If you have integrated design tools, you must re-register those tools during the process of recreating your digital twin.
Platform recommendations
HxGN SDx2 can be used on desktop, laptop, and portable devices that meet the following recommendations.
Hardware recommendations
Desktop and laptop computers (64-bit) must meet the following minimum requirements:
The graphics card has a minimum of 128MB RAM or more.
Graphic cards that do not meet these requirements are still supported, but software performance may be degraded.
Gaming and workstation graphics cards with a large amount of memory give the best performance. Integrated graphics cards may struggle with heavier workloads.
GVC does not support software acceleration, no GPU, or headless operation.
The default GPU maximum value used by the GVC is 1 GB.
The device uses the latest available drivers and has been updated within the last six months.
Mobile devices should meet the following minimum requirements:
Memory
3GB RAM
CPU
2-core processor
Screen size    
8.3 inch or greater
 Larger screen sizes make for a better user experience, so use the largest size available to you.
For mobile devices, it is important to understand that the combination of device manufacturer, operating system, web browser, and other configuration options can have a significant impact on user experience.
 For desktop computers, laptop computers, and tablets, the hardware and software must support WebGL to view files. To test your system, point a browser on that system to https://get.webgl.org/.
Desktop and laptop supported software configurations
Operating systems:
Windows 11 Professional or Enterprise edition (64-bit), version 21H2 or later
Windows 10 Professional or Enterprise edition (64-bit), version 21H1 or later
Web browsers (the two most recently released major versions):
Google Chrome
Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based)
Additional software:
Excel for Microsoft 365 or Microsoft Excel 2021 is required to view files that are exported from HxGN SDx2 to Excel format.
The latest version of SmartPlant.NET Client is required for ALI Design Tool integration. It must be installed on the Design Tool client along with Microsoft Edge WebView2.
Mobile device supported software configurations
Microsoft operating system
Version
Windows 11 Professional edition (64-bit), version 21H2 or later
Windows 10 Professional edition (64-bit), version 21H1 or later
Browser    
Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based) (the two most recently released major versions)
Android operating system
Version
Android 13 or later
Browser    
Google Chrome (the two most recently released major versions)
iOS operating system
Version
iOS 17 or later
Browser    
Safari 17 or later
 You interact differently with the user interface on Apple iPads or Android tablets as there is some variation in functionality depending on the device being used. When using Android tablets, we recommended you use Portrait mode or
use a Floating Keyboard in Landscape mode to give you the maximum screen real estate when using a tablet.
Understanding sessions and licensing
When you end a HxGN SDx2 session by logging off or by leaving the session inactive for a defined period of time, the license for that session is released back to the system.
However, closing a browser or browser tab that is holding a HxGN SDx2 session does not result in an immediate implicit log off due to the limitation of browser technology. In this case, the session becomes inactive and returning the
license is handled as follows.
Session inactivity and license expiration
The HxGN SDx2 tenant has a number of property settings that a tenant administrator can use to control the length of time that a session can remain inactive before a license is released.
Session inactivity timeout
The SPF Web Client inactivity timeout (minutes) property in the HxGN SDx2 tenant sets a value that controls the length of time a session can remain inactive. When the timeout occurs, the user is logged off, and the license for that
session is released. The default inactivity timeout value is 10 minutes (600 seconds).
For example, with the inactivity timeout value set to 10, the timeout occurs after 10 minutes (600 seconds) of inactivity. The tenant resets the timeout countdown every time there is further activity within the browser. So, if the session is
inactive for five minutes (300 seconds), any activity in the browser resets the activity timeout countdown for another 10 minute period.
 The inactivity timeout countdown is suspended during a file upload, file download, or when using View and Markup
Access Token lifetime expiration
The Access Token Lifetime (in seconds) property in the tenant sets a lifetime limit on each licensing token, which has the effect of setting a maximum time limit for each session. The default Access Token Lifetime property value is set
to one hour (3600 seconds), so the token expires after one hour (3600 seconds).
For example, when the maximum time limit for the session of one hour (3600 seconds) is reached, and the user is no longer using the HxGN SDx2, the session is ended and the license released.
 Both the inactivity timeout and the access token lifetime properties are managed by your tenant administration team.
Multiple sessions license consumption
 HxGN SDx2 supports Google Chrome™ and Microsoft Edge® browsers for use with multiple sessions.
A license is consumed when you first log onto a HxGN SDx2 tenant using a browser. A new license is consumed every time you log on to a different tenant. Logging out of the HxGN SDx2 releases a license.
Additional licenses are not consumed when:
You select New Window in the Search option to perform your search in a new browser window.
You open a new browser window from the Actions menu.
You paste a HxGN SDx2 URL into a new tab using the same browser – Edge to Edge or Chrome to Chrome.
You paste a HxGN SDx2 URL into a different supported web browser. For example, you copy the URL from an Chrome™ browser and paste it into a Microsoft Edge® browser, or vice versa.
You use Incognito mode in Chrome™ or InPrivate mode in Microsoft Edge® and paste a HxGN SDx2 URL into a new browser window. This automatically continues the private browsing session.
 If you use Mozilla® FireFox® and experience an increase in license consumption, we recommend that you use a supported browser.
