Glossary
access token
A system object that contains client and user information used by APIs to authorize data access. An access token can be validated by a resource. Clients request access tokens and forward them to an API.
actions
Common operations performed on one or more selected objects. Actions are specific to an object type and include commands such as create, update, view, delete, and manage. In HxGN SDx2, actions are grouped by type and displayed
in the Actions menu. In Data Take-On , actions are executed during the import, validation, and load processes, depending on how column headers are mapped, whether to objects, properties, or relationships.
active scope
A configuration in which you may view, create, modify, and delete information in the software.
alias tag
A tag that is named differently, but represents the master tag in the system.
API
The Application Programming Interface.
API Services
A configurable interface that provides access to data within a site through an Application Programming Interface (API), which uses RESTful APIs provided in a standardized format using the Open Data Protocol (OData).
application integration
The process of connecting software systems to exchange data using application-specific protocols or APIs, ensuring accurate data delivery and validation. Unlike basic data integration, it supports frequent, bi-directional communication and
is ideal for high-value, real-time interactions between applications.
as-built
The set of data that describes the existing conditions of a plant or site; the completed and approved state of a project.
asset
A physical object which is the physical part located in the facility, with its own serial number.
attribute
An object characteristic or property.
auto-generate
An option to automatically generate validation rule definitions, load mappings, and rules, based on actions. The validation rules are used to validate imported and loaded data and the load mappings ensure that the column headers match
the objects and properties found in the HxGN SDx2 database.
brownfield
An existing project or area that has constraints imposed due to prior work and contains existing data.
business administrator
A user that is responsible for the project infrastructure and configuration, as well as managing contracts, disciplines, users, the WBS, and the PBS.
business objects
Complex objects representing more than one class definition.
business process integration
The alignment of various business processes across systems to ensure smooth workflow and data sharing. It integrates processes like project management, procurement, and scheduling.
cache
Memory that stores recently accessed data so that subsequent requests to access the same data can be processed quickly.
calculate
The second step of the Integration Engine that performs necessary calculations, such as naming rule evaluations on the data objects gathered during the cumulate step, preparing them for correlation and further processing, and so on.
cardinality
A setting on a relationship definition that specifies how many instances of a relationship are valid for the objects at the end of the relationship. For example, a tag cannot exist without a primary classification relationship and also cannot
have more than one primary classification related to it.
check in
The process of moving a file from a user location to a storage location and recording that location in the database.
client API
A .dll (dynamic link library) that performs a particular function in the software. Client APIs are processed by a specific section of code in a component.
column headers
They are used in the software as the basis for mapping new data in columns to match an existing structured database.
common assignee
A user that has a common role across a single project or multiple projects and can be assigned workflow tasks for projects based on that common role.
Common Schema
A standardized data model used in HxGN SDx2 to define and manage all objects, properties, and relationships such as tags, documents, and data shared from design tools for seamless integration.
compare
The final step of the Integration Engine that identifies differences between incoming correlated objects and those in the consolidated design component, triggering updates, additions, deletions, or no changes, and generating exception
reports to guide seamless data integration.
composite applet
A small software component that combines multiple functionalities into a single interface, such as a tool that aggregates project data, resource tracking, and schedule updates.
condition
An object that can restrict access to a method, workflow, or relationship based on specified criteria.
configuration
A sub-set of a database system that acts as a suppository for managing configuration items as well as relationships to the assets for that specific part or plant. A database system can be made up of multiple configurations.
configuration tree
A representation in a tree list, which may include plant, areas, units, and projects, that indicates the structure in which the data is stored.
consolidate
The step where objects with correlation values are matched to existing objects and merged based on type hierarchy and precedence rules, ensuring data is updated or copied appropriately within the consolidated design component.
contract
A group of documents that are collected and issued for bid, construction, review, and so on.
contractor
A company or industry that undertakes a contract to supply a product, materials, or a service for a specific project, such as the construction of a plant.
correlate
A step in the Integration Engine that assigns a unique correlation value to each object based on predefined rules, enabling the system to identify and match equivalent objects across different integrated design tools, even when described
differently.
correlation ID
A unique ID generated by the correlate service by applying correlation rules to the objects. This is used to create and update consolidated objects in the consolidate design component.
correlation rules
A logical definition that determines how to generate correlation values for objects, using if-else conditions to check applicability and then applying an ordered set of rules to uniquely identify the same object across different integrated
applications.
create scope
A configuration for data creation, modification, and termination in the software.
CSV
A comma-separated value (CSV) file is a file format used to store tabular data as a spreadsheet or datasheet, which can be imported or exported from a program.
cumulate
The initial step of the Integration Engine that gathers all relevant objects, relationships, and metadata required for processing, ensuring that subsequent steps, such as calculate, correlate, consolidate, and compare, have the necessary
context and data.
current document
A document that has been signed off. Current documents can be revised in the software, but not checked out or in.
Data Capture
A feature in HxGN SDx2 that processes technical documents by extracting and mapping data based on predefined tag and document classifications.
data cooperation
The seamless sharing, synchronization, and use of data between different systems or applications, enabling consistent and coordinated operations across teams or tools.
data files
A job can process multiple input files and therefore use multiple input mappings. Import mappings are defined on the job definition.
data integration
The process of combining data from different sources into a unified and accessible format, enabling seamless sharing, analysis, and decision-making across systems or plants.
data silos
A system where information is stored separately and is inaccessible or poorly integrated with other systems, leading to inefficiencies and communication barriers.
Data Take-On
A platform that validates, transforms, and migrates data, ensuring information quality while reducing time and costs involved in the data integration process.
Data Take-On job
A defined object that carries information for the progress of data through a selected workflow, such as when data is staged, validated, and loaded into the HxGN SDx2 database.
database
Collection of files of comprehensive information that have predefined structure and organization; a specific program can communicate, interpret, or process these files.
delimiter
A character or a sequence of characters used to separate individual elements in a string, file, or data stream.
deliverable milestone
Defines a targeted result and tracks the progress for the deliverable.
deputy
A user that is selected as a substitute for an out-of-office or disabled user.
design component
A distinct part of the design process, such as 3D modeling, P&ID, instrumentation, or electrical systems that contributes to the planning, functionality, and integration of a project’s overall design.
design file
A file generated by a design tool, such as Smart P&ID or Aspen Basic Engineering.
display item
Object used to present data or relationships on a form in the software.
distribution matrix
A list of people who will receive a transmittal created in the software and an indication of what is expected from each recipient. Workflows can also be configured to use a distribution matrix for sending To Do List or e-mail notifications to
workflow step recipients.
document
An object used to track revisions to a design file in the software.
document attribute
A constant which allows additional information to be added to the data created in the software.
document master
An object used to group all the revisions of a document in the software.
document renditions
Copies of a document generated in formats different from the originals, typically created during the document review, technical query, or actions workflow process. A PDF rendition is a type of alternate rendition, which is a viewable version
the original file, often used for consistent display and review purposes.
document review
A document review allows your organization's technical experts, engineers, and stakeholders to conduct quality assurance reviews of documents delivered by your suppliers and contractors.
document revision
An officially recognized change to a document.
domain
A set of data that is segregated in a domain and managed independently in the software. Data segregation improves performance and maintains data integrity.
domain tag
A representation of the tag, specifically from a discipline within a domain.
edge definition
Single or multiple relationship definitions with direction. In the schema, an edge definition is used to traverse from a starting object to related objects.
enterprise
Refers to an organization engaged in the planning, execution, or support of projects and related activities within the domain.
entry point
Provides access to the underlying database data to the Web Client. It can be configured to provide full access control for each exposed object class definitions or interface definitions. The system automatically configures any valid
relationship or edge definitions between the exposed types.
enumerated entry
A member of an enumerated list that defines one possible value for a property in the schema. Enumerated values are sometimes called enumerated entries or abbreviated to enums.
exposes
The relationship between interface definitions and property definitions in the schema. Interface definitions expose the property definitions for class definitions.
extensible markup language (XML)
A data format commonly used for exporting and importing data between systems. XML files must follow the structure defined by the application schema. In integrated application environments, all documents shared use this standardized
format.
feature sets
A configured set of object functions that can be used to quickly access data or run reports and commands.
file properties or attributes
Values that are stored on the file object. The data is transferred to the document if the file is selected for data extraction.
file type
A setting in the software that specifies the format of attached files based on file extension. This setting determines how files are viewed, edited, and printed in the software.
folder
A grouping object that can contain a number of items. A folder can contain other folders, to build up a folder hierarchy. A folder can have an owner, which makes it visible only for that user, and it can have an owning group, which allows
access for members of the group. Some uses of a folder include grouping objects, putting a folder's contents through a workflow, and generating a data list from a folder's contents.
form
A part of the software user interface that allows users to specify values for class definition properties.
function
A graphical tab that runs a predefined process, task, or administrative action, such as a search. Related functions can be grouped into sets relevant to a selected feature set.
A computed functional code run at import, where the output value of a function depends only on the arguments that are input to the function.
function code
A computed code function run at import, where the output value of a function depends only on the arguments that are input to the function.
GET
A request-response method between a client and server. A GET requests data from a specified resource.
granular objects
The data elements or entities that are broken down into smaller, more detailed components, allowing for more precise management, analysis, and customization in systems or processes.
Graphic Data Service (GDS)
Provides graphics and associated information services to the Graphic View Control (GVC) when viewing Hexagon 3D models or 2D drawings streamed from the Visualization Data Service (VDS) to a HxGN SDx2 for Project As-Built
configuration or when viewing Hexagon 2D drawings in HxGN SDx2.
graphic report
A filtered view of a drawing or model, displayed graphically in the software.
Graphic View Control (GVC)
The viewer that provides viewing capabilities of Hexagon 3D models and 2D drawings.
greenfield
A project or area that is completely new and does not have any constraints imposed by prior work or existing data.
hierarchy
A classified structure with superiors, or roots, and subordinates, or dependents, used for grouping data.
host
A computer that stores files.
hotspotting
The process of adding hyperlinks in a document on the area where the tag exists in the file.
implies
The relationship between two interface definitions in the schema. If an interface definition implies another interface definition, then any class definition that realizes the first interface definition can also realize the implied interface definition.
import definition
A defined mapping of imported file objects, properties, and relationships from existing column headers to objects, properties, and relationships found in the staging area database during the import process.
import mappings
The definitions that specify how data from an external source should be matched or transferred to the appropriate fields or structures in the HxGN SDx2 database during the import process.
import process
The process that manages the import of data in the data file or files to the staging area using a defined mapping.
Info Map
A display system that uses a systematic approach to analyze, organize, and present clear and user-focused information, so that you can quickly visualize the relationship details of an individual object.
integration
Technology that standardizes and improves communication among the various authoring tools used in design, construction, and operation of a plant. Integration manages data exchange among these authoring tools, which enables sharing
and re-use of plant information throughout the plant lifecycle.
integration environment
A testing environment that manages data exchange between various design tools and plant operations, enabling seamless communication, information sharing, and reuse across the entire plant lifecycle.
interface definition
A named collection of property definitions that represents a role for a class definition in the schema.
issue purpose
A designation given to a deliverable item to describe why the item has been issued, such as for review or construction.
IWA
Integrated Windows Authentication (IWA) refers to automatically authenticated connections between Microsoft Internet Information Services and other active directory aware applications.
job definition
A combined set of components configured for the import, validation, and loading of data into the HxGN SDx2 database.
JSON
JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) is an open standard format that uses human-readable text to transmit data objects, consisting of attribute-value pairs, between a server and web application. JSON is an alternative to XML.
load mapping
The process that maps the imported objects, classes, and properties to the existing structure of the objects, classes, and properties found in the HxGN SDx2 database.
load process
The load process uses a defined mapping, based on the structure of the HxGN SDx2 database to manage the loading of the data into the database.
mapping
The process of defining logical relationships between corresponding objects, data fields, or attributes across different tools, models, or systems, (such as Smart 3D or Smart P&ID) to ensure consistent, accurate, and integrated data flow
throughout the design, construction, and lifecycle of a project. For more information, see import definition and export mapping.
markup layer
A layer that contains saved actions and comments raised by a document reviewer on a document in the collaborative document review process.
master document register (MDR)
A master document register (MDR) is a submittal type initiated by the contractor to send documentation data to the owner company.
master file
The file that is used for data and content extraction.
master tag register (MTR)
A master tag register (MTR) is a submittal type initiated by the contractor to send master tag data to the owner company.
master tags
Extracted tags that follow a set of standard naming conventions in a project or a plant that the engineers should follow for defining tag names.
merge
To promote applicable project data to the as-built plant.
metadata
Data that provides information about other data, describing its attributes, context, structure, or characteristics to help identify, manage, and understand the underlying data.
method
A call to an API that allows users to perform actions on objects or interfaces in the software.
model
A physical object which is the requisitioned item and includes a model number.
notifications
Alerts to specific events in the software that appear by registering your interest in objects or object classes, such as the latest revision to a document.
OAuth
An open standard for authorization. OAuth provides client applications with a secure delegated access to server resources on behalf of a resource owner.
object weighting
A process that emphasizes the contribution of an object in a set of data to a final effect or result, thereby deciding its weight in the analysis. This affects the order in which the data is loaded, helping to achieve the desired result.
OData
Open Data Protocol (OData) is an OASIS standard that defines the best practices for building and consuming RESTful APIs.
orphan tags
Tags that are not related to any document or domain tag.
owning group
The user group to which an object is assigned in the software.
picklist
A list of applicable values for a given property in the software.
pinboard
An area in the software where you can add or pin objects so you can quickly recall and manage them in the system. Objects added to the pinboard are available to you between log in sessions.
pins
Bookmarked objects that provide quick access to new items or objects added to the pinboard so you can quickly recall and manage them in the system. Pins added to the pinboard are available to you between log in sessions.
plant
An object that can represent the top level in the delivered data hierarchy. If you use a custom hierarchy, the object at the top level of the hierarchy may have a different name.
port
A database object that represents a connection interface or entry point, designed for linking systems, components, or data flows.
POST
A request-response method between clients and servers. A POST submits data to a specified resource for processing.
precedence rules
Defines the order in which tasks or activities must be executed to ensure efficient project management and successful completion.
progress
Functionality that allows users to track the development of deliverables, such as documents or items, against a project plan, indicating any deviations from the original plan.
project
A logical unit of data that is a subset of the items that make up a plant. A project is used for making controlled, incremental changes to the data in a plant. There can be multiple projects for a plant at any given time.
property
An object characteristic or attribute.
property definition
A basic attribute shared by all members of an object class. Property definitions are grouped using interface definitions in the schema.
property group
A group of properties.
property list
Allows you to create property group names (interface definition names) and properties (property definitions), which can be related to a business object (class definitions).
QA review
A process where the user can verify the documents issued by a contractor.
query
A search based on object properties.
query definition
A comma separated list of properties that can be entered in the Load Object Query Definition box, which identifies the object in the HxGN SDx2 database along with the class definition. These properties can be retrieved by navigating
the relationships.
query scope
A configuration for data queries and relationship expansions in the software.
raw attribute format
Where the data in a CSV file is organized in a vertical format and each row contains properties and relationships for the same object. Also referred to as an inverted CSV file. The data in a standard CSV file is typically organized
horizontally.
realizes
The relationship between class definitions and interface definitions in the schema. Class definitions realize interface definitions. The interface definitions that are realized by a class definition expose the properties for that class definition.
reason for receipt (RFR)
The reason why a specific recipient was included on a transmittal or workflow step.
register
The process of linking a design tool to an HxGN SDx2 project by associating it with a designated work area and work package, enabling data integration and synchronization based on organizational type.
regular expression
A pattern that is used for tag matching and defining selected file parts.
Relationship Builder
A tool to quickly and easily select objects and relate them to each other using selected relationship definitions.
relationship definition
Associations between interface definitions in the schema. Relationship definitions identify two specific objects that fulfill the roles on each end of the relationship.
relationship property
A property on the link interface which is part of the relationship definition. The link interface allows properties to be created on the relationship itself.
rendition
An alternate representation of the original document version that is created during the document review consolidation process. A PDF rendition is a type of alternate rendition, which is a viewable file of a different file type.
representational state transfer (REST)
A set of architectural principles used to design Web services that focus on systems resources, including how resource states are addressed and transferred over HTTP by a wide range of clients written in different languages. Web services
employing a REST architecture read a designated Web page containing an XML file that describes and includes the desired content.
responder
A user sending a response.
response
A reply from an external organization directed to an internal organization.
RESTful APIs
When Web services use REST architecture, they are called RESTful, or REST APIs.
review assignment
Review assignment rules define which documents should be assigned to a review plan and to whom, either a discipline or an individual.
revision
An officially recognized change to a document. Each revision of a document may have multiple versions.
revision scheme
A numbering convention for document revisions.
role
Determines a user's level of access to data and functionality in a specific plant/project configuration. Users can belong to more than one role per configuration. Roles are defined by system administrators and are based on related access
groups, design components, and owning groups.
rules
A logical formula used to evaluate and verify whether the data in an imported record meets the requirements and data standards specified for the HxGN SDx2 database.
rulesets
A combined set of rules that can be run as a set during job processing.
SaaS
Software as a Service (SaaS) is a cloud-based software delivery model where applications are hosted and managed by a third-party provider and made available to users over the internet.
schema
A model used to describe and validate the structure of XML files.
scope
A selected configuration in which users can view, create, modify, and terminate data.
scoped by
The relationship between property definitions and property types in the schema. The scoped by relationship specifies the property type that defines acceptable values, or scopes, a particular property definition. Every property definition in
the schema is scoped by one and only one property type. All properties of that property definition must be of that property type.
section
A collection of display items used on forms in the software.
security codes
Configuration-independent items that are used to set different security levels, which can be applied to objects in a configuration. They can be used to control the confidentiality or restrict the availability of the objects.
security rules
Security measures that limit the types of data that users have access to in the system. A user’s access group is used to determine which security rules are relevant.
separator
Separates file parts and tag parts. Common separators include a dash (-), slash (/), tilde (~), and so forth. For example, the instrument tag LCV-157 uses the dash (-) separator to separate the tag name (LCV) from the drawing name (157).
server
A computer that stores or processes files.
share
The process of transferring design data and graphics from a registered tool to active projects or plants within a defined design component using integration rules.
sign off
To approve a particular revision of a document in the software. Signing off a document sets the document to be the current released revision, makes it official, and supersedes any previous released revisions. Document revisions that have
been signed off are frozen and cannot be checked out.
SSL
Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) provides a secure, encrypted connection between a web server and a browser application, such as a website. This allows you to transmit encrypted private data online.
staging
The staging or staging area or staging database used is the first part of a combined set of processes, where imported data is held and validated before being exported to a site.
status
The state of a change object at the completion of each step in a workflow.
step
A process that must be performed in order to complete a workflow.
submittal
A control file used to send documents and files to the owning company's system. The submittal contains all the details of the submitted objects, including tags and any document file attachments.
sub-review
A technical query to an internal resource to provide more information to complete a document review, referred to as Internal Technical Query in the user interface.
subscribe
To register interest in an object so that you receive a notification when the object is modified. You can manually subscribe to change notifications in the software.
superseded
Indicates that a newer, working version of the selected document exists.
tag
A database object that is the physical part as designed.
tag document register (TDR)
A tag document register (TDR) is a submittal type initiated by the contractor to send tag and document relationship data to the owner company.
tag naming system
Defines the parts of a tag name and relates them to existing objects in the database.
technical query
A technical query is a question raised on a document or object that is progressed through a workflow.
tenant
An instance of the software operating within a shared digital platform or system, allocated to and configured for a specific customer or organization.
terminate
The action of changing an objects status to terminate makes the object and its history inaccessible except for documents. For documents, terminating a document version or revision hides it from general searches unless you select Show
Removed Items icon.
text reader
Captures simple text files and processes the data directly without needing an application to extract the data.
thumbnail
A small size image representation of a larger file intended to make it easier and faster to manage it.
title block
The portion of a drawing that contains information about the drawing, such as who created the drawing, when it was created, who approved it, and so on. The type of information included in the title block varies by drawing type, industry,
and organization.
To Do List
Displays all workflow objects assigned to you or your role that require action or completion. It serves as the central interface for interacting with workflows, allowing you to view task details, attachments, workflows, and to action or complete
tasks as needed.
transmittal
A transmittal is used to distribute a controlled set of documents in the system to a specific set of recipients. The transmittal records and tracks the distribution of the documents, the recipients, and the reason for their issue.
transpose
Returns a vertical range of cells as a horizontal range, or vice versa.
UID
Unique Identifier is used to uniquely identify an object within a system. UID definitions must be set on all objects that are loaded from the staging area if the unique key or query definition is left blank. The staging area UID definitions must
match the UID definitions in the database.
unique key
A set of values guaranteed to be unique for each object in a relation, and can be used to identify objects in the target system.
unit
Group of parts of the schematic and individual worlds of a plant that together perform a given process function. The identifying number of the unit is unique within the project and within the plant. Most companies, but not all, use the
concept of unit.
unstructured data
The data that doesn't have a predefined format or structure, such as text or images, making it harder to analyze and process using traditional data models.
UoM
A unit of measurement.
URL
A uniform resource locator is a web address that contains a specific character string that references a resource.
user
An object that specifies data about a person who is authorized to use the software.
user profile
Information about configurations, preferences, and interface settings saved by the software when you close the application and used to restore your environment when it is reopened.
validation process
A process that evaluates the imported data against a defined set of rules to ensure the validity of the data.
validation rule
A logical formula used to evaluate the imported data in one or more fields to determine whether it matches the existing criteria and hierarchy found in the target system database. There are two types of validation rules:
1. Rules that determine if the data is valid for the target system schema (these rules can be autogenerated.
2. Rules that determine if the data meets specific business criteria, such as naming format.
vendor
A vendor is a company or manufacturer that is supplying a product or service for sale.
version
An intermediate update to an existing document that is tracked by the software.
View and Markup
The functionality that provides precision view and markup capabilities for documents and is used in the document review process.
visualization data service (VDS)
Provides graphics and associated information services to the Graphic View Control (GVC) when viewing Hexagon 3D models or 2D drawings in a HxGN SDx2 client application.
wildcard
A character that helps you narrow your search for objects in the database. You can use wildcards in any text box in the Find and Query dialog boxes.
Text wildcards include the following:
? - Finds any single character
* - Finds any string of characters
% - Performs the same function as *
work area
A classified object used to define and filter the scope of data synchronized between HxGN SDx2 and other Hexagon products (such as Smart 3D, Smart Electrical, and Smart P&ID), with access and usage controlled based on the
associated organization.
work breakdown structure
A structure for grouping documents, tags, and other information for handing over deliverables to the client.
work package
A detailed scope of work to be executed and completed in a scheduled amount of time.
work process diagram
A key part of a work process diagram is the use of color-coded swimlanes. A swimlane helps illustrate a process that spans across different applications and is represented as a row containing activities specific to the application or role
involved.
workflow
A series of steps defining actions to be taken on an object in the software.
working document
A document that has not been signed off in the software.
Copyright
Copyright© Hexagon AB and/or its subsidiaries and affiliates. All rights reserved.

# SOURCE: vendor_hexagon\ReferenceData.pdf
---

Define Reference Data - Level 2 Diagram

Intergraph Proprietary Information

Enterprise Work Process for Materials Management

1

Intergraph Proprietary Information

Define Reference Data - Level 2

1.  As a reference data user, I need to define the commodity rules so that I can build intelligent commodity codes in the group-part hierarchy.
2.  As a reference data user, I need to define the commodity properties and values so that I can build commodity codes.
3.  As a reference data user, I need to define the commodity codes so they can be used for specifications.
4.  As a reference data user, I need to define the dimensional data so that the proper combination of commodity code and dimensions can be established.
5.  As a reference data user, I need to map properties and dimensions for consumers so that the data can be used in the tools.
6.  As a reference data user, I need to define the unique component codes (Ident codes) so that I can use them in the SmartPlant Enterprise, including SmartPlant Materials, and deliver them to consumer systems.
7.  As a reference data user, I need to define the CMS (component management system) classification hierarchy to define the structure so that I can organize the components.
8.  As a reference data user, I need to define the CMS class properties to define how to describe the components.
9.  As a reference data user, I need to define the CMS components so that I can use them in the SmartPlant Enterprise, including SmartPlant Materials, and deliver them to consumer systems.

  See the following Integration Capability Statement for more information:

  Defining Parts and Specifications for 3D Design

10.  As a reference data user, I need to define what specifications rules are required to satisfy the company and/or project standards. These specification rules may include branch tables, wall thickness tables, and so forth.
11.  As a reference data user, I need to define and maintain the specifications using the commodities (defined in steps 6 and 9) and the rules (defined in step 10) to meet all engineering and design conditions in my company's projects. This includes    bold

selection tables, gasket selection tables, and so forth.

  See the following Integration Capability Statement for more information:

  Defining Parts and Specifications for 3D Design

12.  As a reference data user, I need to define and maintain the catalogs, as required by the design tools. These catalogs contain components and/or specifications to be used by those tools in their project designs.
13.  As a SmartPlant Enterprise tool user, I need to receive the catalog data from SmartPlant Reference Data so that I can perform design activities for the required disciplines.
14.  As a design tool user, I need to receive the catalog data from SmartPlant Reference Data so that I can perform design activities for the required disciplines.
15.  As an ERP tool user, I need to receive the catalog data from SmartPlant Reference Data so that I can integrate the Ident codes with the ERP system and workflows.
16.  As a SmartPlant Materials user, I need to use the components and/or specifications from SmartPlant Reference Data in the BOM and all other downstream modules.
17.  As an SmartPlant Reference Data user, I need to obtain and maintain prices for the components so that the budget for bulk material can be established.

Enterprise Work Process for Materials Management

2




# SOURCE: vendor_hexagon\Single Line Diagrams.pdf
---

Develop Overall Single-Line Diagrams (Rev P0 or 1) - Level 2 Diagram

Develop Overall Single-Line Diagrams (Rev P0 or 1) - Level 2

1.  As a plant layout specialist, I need to develop the plant layout so that the electrical engineer can use it for designing the conceptual electrical network.
2.  As a plant layout specialist, I need to generate general arrangement drawings.
3.  As an electrical engineer, I need to develop overall single-line diagrams (Rev P0 or 1) using the information from the general arrangement drawings and the electrical load list.

  When creating the overall single-line diagrams, we are assuming that the equipment is connected with the appropriate cables.

4.  As an electrical engineer using ETAP, I need to perform electrical analysis studies using the overall single-line diagrams so that I can ensure a stable, reliable electrical network.
5.  As a member of the electrical department, I need define the locations of the major electrical equipment and the electrical rooms so that the plant layout can be updated. I also need to allocate space for raceway.
6.  As a plant layout specialist, I need to update the plant layout so that the electrical engineer can use it for designing the conceptual electrical network.
7.  As a member of the electrical department, I need to publish the overall single-line diagrams (Rev P0 or 1) to SmartPlant Foundation for downstream users.
8.

If the document has publishable data, the document and the data are loaded into SmartPlant Foundation and are subsequently available for use by other applications. If the document does not have publishable data, it is available for viewing in
SmartPlant Foundation.

Intergraph Proprietary Information

Enterprise Work Process for Electrical Design

2




# SOURCE: vendor_hexagon\SMARTPLANT+ENTERPRISE+FOR+OWNER.pdf
---

SMARTPLANT® ENTERPRISE
FOR OWNER OPERATORS

SMARTPLANT® ENTERPRISE FOR
OWNER OPERATORS

Owner operators face many demands to minimize CAPEX and OPEX expenditures; deliver projects as quickly
as possible; ensure safe, sustainable production; demonstrate compliance to meet regulatory authority
requirements; and many more. To help meet these demands, Intergraph® offers SmartPlant® Enterprise for Owner
Operators (SPO).

FROM CONSTRUCTION TO OPERATIONS

SPO Systems Completion takes a project from construction

closeout to operations. Systems Completion is arguably the most

critical and complex phase of the facility life cycle. Success dur-

ing this phase is dependent on access to complete, correct, and

consistent data. SPO Systems Completion helps maintain the

integrity of the virtual plant asset throughout the project to sup-

port mechanical completions, commissioning, preservation, and

pre-startup safety checks. Allocation of check sheets is automat-

ed based on user-defined rules, saving months of manual labor.

Check sheets can be completed offline in the field and uploaded

into SPO. Graphical 4D reporting on 3D plant models and intel-

ligent P&IDs provides timely and effective oversight of the status

of completion processes.
MANAGING OPERATIONS CHANGE &
INSPECTIONS

SPO Operating Plant supports Management of Change in

the operating facility. This solution manages proposed changes

through the initiation, review, approval, and implementation cycle.

Change impact analysis is supported by tight integration with the

SPO leverages the engineering design basis to provide an exten-

sive portfolio of integrated, pre-configured solutions addressing key

owner operator work processes across the plant life cycle. These

processes leverage the underlying virtual plant asset in Intergraph’s

industry-leading SmartPlant Foundation repository and are sup-

ported by integrated SmartPlant engineering design tools. SPO

offers a comprehensive platform to support critical initiatives such

as process safety management and asset integrity.

Date-stamping of all information changes and electronic workflows

underlying engineering design basis to identify potential conflicts

provide auditable traceability to demonstrate compliance for regula-

and synergies between changes. SPO offers interoperability

tory authorities.

PROJECT DELIVERY

SPO Project Execution manages key processes for successful

project delivery. These include management of: interfaces be-

tween project stakeholders, project change, non-conformities, and

technical queries (also known as Requests for Information). Tight

integration between these processes and the underlying engineer-

ing design basis enables effective impact analysis within and across

these processes. SPO Project Execution is suitable for use with

both CAPEX projects and turnaround projects.

The processes offer complete auditable traceability and manage-

ment overview of these critical processes during project delivery to

prevent CAPEX cost overruns and delays in project schedules.

with other enterprise operations systems – such as enterprise

asset management (EAM), reliability and asset integrity, process

safety, and digital control systems – ensuring that information in

those systems is maintained to accurately reflect changes in the

dynamic engineering design basis.

SPO Operating Plant also supports equipment inspections.

Inspection scheduling, execution, and punch item follow-up

are supported. This provides complete records of inspections

performed, results, and identification of overdue and upcoming

inspections. Inspections are supported by offline check sheet

completion on Microsoft® Windows® tablet and laptop PCs.

REQUIREMENTS TRACEABILTY

SPO Requirements Management and Traceability is designed

to support owners and contractors in highly regulated industries

that need to maintain and demonstrate compliance to changing

stakeholder requirements through the plant design, configura-

tion, and operational phases. It provides a dynamic line-of-sight

between each requirement and its associated fulfillment in the plant

design or documentation.

A document decomposer tool offers a fast and easy way to identify

discrete requirements in documents and design specifications.

A configurable workflow gives flexibility to determine which users

are authorized to identify, approve, link, and aggregate require-

ments. Approved requirements can then be associated to objects

in the plant design basis or documents. A management of change

and impact analysis process ensures that requirements and their

associated fulfillment items are re-evaluated when there are future

changes to either the requirement or the design basis.
SUPPORTING MOBILE WORKERS

Owner operators undertake many activities on the plant floor, at re-

mote sites, vendor premises, etc. where online access to informa-

tion is not available. SPO Mobile Applications takes information

and documentation wherever it is needed. With SPO Mobile Ap-

plications, information and documentation are immediately available

on tablet PCs or laptops from the plant floor or any remote location.

A subset of data and documentation is taken offline into a briefcase

where it can be easily accessed through a highly intuitive interface.

Photo-realistic laser scans, 3D models, and intelligent schematics

can help the user navigate quickly and easily to find the information

needed. This eliminates the need to take cumbersome hardcopy

documents into the field. Information such as photographs, notes,

and inspection check sheets can be captured and uploaded back

to SPO with a single click once network connectivity is available,

avoiding the burdensome task of manually transcribing hardcopy

markups when back in the office.
MANAGING THE VIRTUAL PLANT ASSET

SPO Core provides the platform and common processes for

managing data and documentation throughout the plant life cycle.

These include management of the plant breakdown structure;

work breakdown structure; central tag allocation and registers;

documents and transmittals; tight integration with Microsoft Office;

electronic dossiers such as system operating manuals and vendor

data books; and risk-reducing measures. Engineering business

rules ensure accurate and unique allocation of tag and document

numbers for projects and modifications.

key benefits

•	Rapid, low-risk implementation
of preconfigured solutions sup-
ports key O/O work processes.

•	Integrity of engineering informa-
tion is maintained throughout
the life cycle.

•	Auditable traceability meets

regulatory authority demands for
demonstrable compliance.

•	Data handover and validation

from projects are managed in a
secure and controlled manner.

•	Collaboration is supported both
within the O/O and with contrac-
tors and suppliers.

•	Work processes are enforced.

•	Data can be accessed quickly

and simply through filtered que-
ries, 2D/3D graphical user inter-
face and photo-realistic TruView
laser scans.

•	Universal Web portal is role-

based and user-friendly.

•	Solution integrates with existing
third-party and legacy systems.

•	Management visibility reports

are provided.

•	Flexible and configurable solu-
tion rapidly incorporates cus-
tomer-specific requirements.

•	Mobile offline access and infor-
mation capture support plant
floor and remote workers.

PROJECT DATA HANDOVER & BROWNFIELD DATA TAKE-ON

The handover of data from CAPEX projects and plant turnarounds is a formidable and

costly undertaking. It can involve the transfer of thousands of documents and millions

of individual data items. SPO provides three complementary solutions that address

different aspects of the handover and take-on of data and documentation. One or

more of these solutions can be included in a comprehensive information management

strategy to reduce the cost and effort of data take-on.

SmartPlant Fusion enables the automated processing of unstructured documen-

tation and extraction of available intelligence. This uses techniques such as optical

character recognition and processing of 2D CAD files to extract lists of tag and docu-

ment references to create references to a plant structure and to generate hotspots

for navigation. Once documentation is processed by SmartPlant Fusion, it can be ac-

cessed and browsed in SmartPlant Fusion or made available in SPO alongside other

information in SPO Core.

SPO Validation, Transformation, and Loading (VTL) manages verification and

import of structured data (e.g., Microsoft Excel®, CSV) submitted from contractor or

from legacy systems.

Data is imported into a staging area where it is held in quarantine prior to validation.

Validation is performed against user-definable business rules. VTL enables improved

management of the take-on of data from projects and brownfield facilities, reduces

the time and effort needed for verification, and ensures a higher level of data quality.

Data can be selectively exported and loaded from the staging area into target sys-

tems, which can be Intergraph or third-party systems.

SPO Handover enables the handover of information between two or more Smart-

Plant Foundation-based systems. Packages of information and documentation can

be handed over on a periodic, incremental basis between project stakeholders – for

example, from an EPC contractor using SmartPlant Enterprise to an owner operator

using SPO. The transfer of information preserves the inherent intelligence from the

source system.

Learn more at www.intergraph.com/go/spo.

aBOUT INTERGRaPH

Intergraph is the leading global provider of engineering and geospatial software
that enables customers to visualize complex data. Businesses and governments in
more than 60 countries rely on Intergraph’s industry-specific software to orga-
nize vast amounts of data to make processes and infrastructure better, safer and
smarter. The company’s software and services empower customers to build and
operate more efficient plants and ships, create intelligent maps, and protect critical
infrastructure and millions of people around the world.

Intergraph operates through two divisions: Process, Power & Marine (PP&M) and
Security, Government & Infrastructure (SG&I). Intergraph PP&M provides enterprise
engineering software for the design, construction, operation and data management
of plants, ships and offshore facilities. Intergraph SG&I provides geospatially pow-

ered solutions, including ERDAS technologies, to the public safety and security,
defense and intelligence, government, transportation, photogrammetry, and utilities
and communications industries. Intergraph Government Solutions (IGS) is a wholly

Schema Concepts

The data model is the structure that defines what information you can store in your database and what relationships objects can have with each other. For
example, the data model defines what attributes can define a tag. It also describes the relationships a document can have with other objects, such as plants or

projects, contracts, or organizations.

A schema comprises a number of components that work together to define your data, including:

Class definition - A named description of a set of objects that play similar roles and share some of the same properties and relationships.

For example, an object that is an instance of an organization may play one or more of the following roles: internal organization, external organization,

supplier, manufacturer, or contractor.

Interface definition - A collection of properties available to an object that, as a set, represent the role an object plays. Interface definitions also represent
the ends of relationship definitions.

Each role is represented by a different interface definition that exposes properties and supports relationships specific to that role.

Relationship definition - A definition of how objects can be related to each other.

For example, a relationship definition describes the relationship that can exist between an organization and a contract. The relationship definition can

include information such as how many organizations each contract can be related to and what happens to an organization when a contract is deleted.

Property definition - Attributes that describe objects. Properties are related to a class definition through an interface definition.

Copyright 2018-2024, Hexagon AB <https://hexagon.com/company/divisions/asset-lifecycle-intelligence> and/or its subsidiaries and affiliates
