Data Model Overview

Browse to Smart Community <https://hexagonppm.com/support/ppm-smart-community> to download diagrams that provide a detailed overview of the SDx
data model. After logging into the Smart Community portal, navigate to Downloads > SDx Operations and SDx Projects 2019 > Freeware Tools and
Utilities > Samples to access the diagrams.

 You must have a Smart Community account to download the diagrams.

Identify customized schema objects

You can easily identify any customized Common Schema objects using properties in your system that have been updated or modified as part of a project from
the delivered out-of-the-box (OOTB) as-built configuration. You can do the following things:

Identify all out-of-the-box (OOTB) objects.

Identify if a customer has modified an OOTB object.

Know if a customer has added a new object.

Know the source of the an OOTB object.

You can also identify in which configuration and version the objects were modified and from what product the modification came, such as HxGN SDx.

The types of modifications that are supported include:

Modifications made by a specific product, such as HxGN SDx.

Modifications made by the customer, such as to objects, properties, or relationships that are added, modified, or terminated.

Configurations modified by the customer and subsequently updated by a product update.

Modifications made over a timescale, so you can query for objects between different dates and then compare the results.

Identification properties and their use

The following properties can be used to identify any modifications when you create or modify any objects and relationships in the system.

Property

Database Build

Update Release

Description

ContainerID

Not used by product

Not used by product

SDxExternalVersion

Not instantiated

Not instantiated

SDxByCustomer

Not instantiated

Not instantiated

SDxInternalVersion

Set on all configurations

Set to first delivered version only

Set on out-of-the-box delivered new, updated,

terminated objects and relationships

applicable to OOTB Schema and

SDxSource

Set to the same layers as used in the xmlsrc
to manage upgrades

manage upgrades

Displays the source from which the
object originated. Set when the
database is built, or upgrade is
delivered.

Where a customer makes a modification to an out-of-the-box object and subsequently a product release updates the same object, the SDxByCustomer
property is set to Modified.

When a relationship is changed, it changes the structure of the object, so the parent object is also marked as changed. For example, when adding or
changing the display of a column item, the column set is considered to be the parent and also marked as changed.

Customer modifications and relationships

As a customer makes changes to an object in a project, the changes can be identified by the SDxByCustomer property being set to Modified on the object
and its relationships. You can search for and filter on the object column set to find any modified properties using the SDxByCustomer property.

However, in some cases, the change can also affect the parent object and be identified the same way. For example, if a new column item is added to an
existing column set, the column set is also considered to have been changed, so both the column item and the column set will have the SDxByCustomer
property set to Modified.

Relationships are identified by the SDxInternalVersion property displaying the product configuration version applicable when the SDxByCustomer was set to
Modified. The target object on the owning end of the relationship will also have the SDxByCustomer property set to Modified.

Upgrades

The SDXInternalVersion and SDxSource properties are set on the Schema and Admin objects when they are delivered by the upgrade process. For

example, if a new method is delivered in Update 19 then the SDXInternalVersion is set to the version 10.00.19.xxxx. If the same method is then delivered in
Update 20, then the method version is still set to first version 10.00.19.xxxx that was delivered.

 The SDXInternalVersion and SDxSource properties are set on the Schema and Admin objects irrespective of if they are new or modified.

Glossary

3D reader

Validates tags, creates and cross-references the tags to the documents, and extracts visual file and tag data from the 3D models.

access token

A system object that contains client and user information used by APIs to authorize data access. An access token can be validated by a resource. Clients

request access tokens and forward them to an API.

action pins

Markup elements you can add to a markup layer to create a graphical locator that is linked to a newly created action.

actions

A common operation on one or more selected objects. Actions are specific to an object type and contain commands, such as create, update, view, and
manage objects.

In the Web Client, they are grouped for display in the actions menu and grouped by type.

In Data Validator, the validation and export process can run update and delete actions on the object data. These operations can vary depending on what
the column header is mapped to, such as an object, a property, or a relationship.

active scope

A configuration in which you may view, create, modify, and delete information in the software.

alias tag

A tag that is named differently, but represents the master tag in the system.

API

The Application Programming Interface.

API Services

A configurable interface that provides access to data within a site through an Application Programming Interface (API), which uses RESTful APIs provided in a
standardized format using the Open Data Protocol (OData).

as-built

The set of data that describes the existing conditions of a plant or site; the completed and approved state of a project.

asset

A physical object which is the physical part located in the facility, with its own serial number.

attribute

An object characteristic.

authorization token

A system object that contains enough information for the process to achieve sufficient confidence in the binding to request that the user can be authenticated
by the Authorization Server.

auto-generate

An option to automatically generate validation rule definitions, export mappings, and rules, based on actions. The validation rules are used to validate imported

and exported data and the export mappings ensure that the column headers match the objects and properties found in the target system.

brownfield

An existing project or area that has constraints imposed due to prior work and contains existing data.

business administrator

A user that is responsible for the project infrastructure and configuration, as well as managing contracts, disciplines, users, the WBS, and the PBS.

business objects

Complex objects representing more than one class definition.

cache

Memory that stores recently-accessed data so that subsequent requests to access the same data can be processed quickly.

cardinality

A setting on a relationship definition that specifies how many instances of a relationship are valid for the objects at the end of the relationship. For example, a
tag cannot exist without a primary classification relationship, and also cannot have more than one primary classification.

check in

The process of moving a file from a user location to a storage location and recording that location in the database.

client API

A .dll (dynamic link library) that performs a particular function in the software. Client APIs are processed by a specific section of code in a component.

column headers

They are used in the software as the basis for mapping new data in columns to match an existing structured database.

common assignee

A user that has a common role across a single project or multiple projects and can be assigned workflow tasks for projects based on that common role.

condition

An object that can restrict access to a method, workflow, or relationship based on specified criteria.

configuration

A sub-set of a database system that acts as a suppository for managing configuration items as well as relationships to the assets for that specific part or plant.

A system can be made up of multiple configurations.

configuration tree

A representation in a tree list, which may include plant, areas, units, and projects, that indicates the structure in which the data is stored.

content discovery task

Extracts content from the master file, relates master and alias tags to the documents, and relates documents to the organizational items.

contract

A group of documents that are collected and issued for bid, construction, review, and so on.

contractor

A company or industry that undertakes a contract to supply a product, materials, or a service for a specific project, such as the construction of a plant.

CORS

Cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) is a mechanism that allows restricted resources found in a web site, such as Fonts, JavaScript and so on, to be
requested from a domain outside of the originating domain.

create scope

A configuration for data creation, modification, and termination in the software.

CSV

A comma-separated value (CSV) file is a file format used to store tabular data as a spreadsheet or datasheet, which can be imported or exported from a
program.

current document

A document that has been signed off. Current documents can be revised in the software, but not checked out or in.

data files

A job can process multiple input files and therefore use multiple input mappings. Import mappings are defined on the job definition.

Data Validator

Data Validator, the software used for importing data into the staging area, validating the data, and/or exporting data to a target system.

database

Collection of files of comprehensive information that have predefined structure and organization; a specific program can communicate, interpret, or process

these files.

database domain discovery task

A process that uses a defined database reader pattern to connect to a database and extract documents and tags along with their properties as defined within

the pattern.

database reader

Reads data directly from a database. Administrators can define specific tables and fields to be transferred so that they can be used for data comparisons.

database reader pattern

Defines the database tables and properties that need to be extracted and mapped to classes and properties within the software database.

delimiter

Identifies the end of a tag.

deliverable milestone

Defines a targeted result and tracks the progress for the deliverable.

deputy

A user that is selected as a substitute for an out-of-office or disabled user.

design file

A file generated by a design tool, such as Smart P&ID or Aspen Basic Engineering.

display item

Object used to present data or relationships on a form in the software.

distribution matrix

A list of people who will receive a transmittal created in the software and an indication of what is expected from each recipient. Workflows can also be
configured to use a distribution matrix for sending To Do List or e-mail notifications to workflow step recipients.

document

An object used to track revisions to a design file in the software.

document attribute

A constant which allows additional information to be added to the data created in the software.

document discovery

Reads documents (typically office generated files) and using patterns loads them into the software.

document discovery pattern

Defines the base directory that needs to be crawled, the file name pattern to extract and the document name pattern that needs to be created. Additionally,
document attributes can also be defined.

document discovery task

A process that uses a document discovery pattern to crawl a directory system and load documents and files that match the specified file pattern. After which
thumbnails are created and related to the document.

document index

A defined excel list or a defined data object within the software that defines metadata about a file. This is referenced when files are loaded into the system and

if a match is found the file will be loaded with the properties defined from the document index.

document master

An object used to group all the revisions of a document in the software.

document name pattern

Defines a document name for files with a specific file name pattern comprised of the parts, constants, and documents attributes.

document naming system

Defines the parts of a file name. Additional information can be defined to define constants and relate them to existing objects in the database when data
extraction occurs.

document renditions

A copy of a document in a format other than the original that is created during the document review, technical query, or actions workflow process. A PDF

rendition is a type of alternate rendition, which is a viewable file of a different file type.

document review

A document review allows your organization's technical experts, engineers, and stakeholders to conduct quality assurance reviews of documents delivered by

your suppliers and contractors.

document revision

An officially recognized change to a document.

domain

A set of data that is segregated in a domain and managed independently in the software. Data segregation improves performance and maintains data integrity.
For example, data about workflows is stored in a separate domain from data imported through SmartPlant Fusion or Data Validator.

domain tag

A representation of the tag, specifically from a discipline within a domain.

drawing reader

Extracts cross-referenced and linked information contained in a drawing file.

duplicate document

A document with multiple files attached but different file types or multiple versions of the same document attached to it.

edge definition

Single or multiple relationship definitions with direction. In the schema, an edge definition is used to traverse from a starting object to related objects.

entry point

Provides access to the underlying database data to the Web Client. They can be configured to provide full access control for each of the exposed object class

definitions or interface definitions. The system automatically configures any relationship definitions or edge definitions that are valid between the exposed
types.

enumerated entry

A member of an enumerated list that defines one possible value for a property in the schema. Enumerated values are sometimes called enumerated entries or

abbreviated to enums.

export mapping

The process that maps the imported objects, classes, and properties to the existing structure of the objects, classes, and properties found in a target system.

export process

The Export process uses a defined mapping, based on the structure of the target system, to manage the loading of the data into the final destination system.

exposes

The relationship between interface definitions and property definitions in the schema. Interface definitions expose the property definitions for class definitions.

feature sets

A configured set of object functions that can be used to quickly access data or run reports and commands.

file index

Specifies the file name, associated name, and attributes for a document before it is processed by the document discovery task.

file name parts

Sections that represent data in a file name.

file name pattern

Consists of one or more file parts with each file part being defined as a document naming system item.

file properties or attributes

Values that are stored on the file object. The data is transferred to the document if the file is selected for data extraction.

file server

A service that handles direct file transfer between vaults and the software.

file type

A setting in the software that specifies the format of attached files based on file extension. This setting determines how files are viewed, edited, and printed in
the software.

FIPS

Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) are issued to establish requirements for various purposes, such as ensuring computer security and
interoperability, and are intended for cases where suitable industry standards do not already exist.

folder

A grouping object that can contain a number of items. A folder can contain other folders, to build up a folder hierarchy. A folder can have an owner, which
makes it visible only for that user, and it can have an owning group, which allows access for members of the group. Some uses of a folder include grouping
objects, putting a folder's contents through a workflow, and generating a data list from a folder's contents.

form

A part of the software user interface that allows users to specify values for class definition properties.

function

A graphical tab that runs a predefined process, task, or administrative action, such as a search. Related functions can be grouped into sets relevant to a
selected feature set.

A computed functional code run at import, where the output value of a function depends only on the arguments that are input to the function.

function code

A computed code function run at import, where the output value of a function depends only on the arguments that are input to the function.

GET

A request-response method between a client and server. A GET requests data from a specified resource.

Graphic Data Service (GDS)

Provides graphics and associated information services to the Graphic View Control (GVC) when viewing Hexagon 3D models or 2D drawings streamed from

the Visualization Data Service (VDS) to a HxGN SDx for Project As-Built configuration or when viewing Hexagon 2D drawings in HxGN SDx.

graphic report

A filtered view of a drawing or model, displayed graphically in the software.

Graphic View Control (GVC)

The viewer that provides viewing capabilities of Hexagon 3D models and 2D drawings.

greenfield

A project or area that is completely new and does not have any constraints imposed by prior work or existing data.

hierarchy

A classified structure with superiors, or roots, and subordinates, or dependents, used for grouping data.

host

A computer that stores files.

hotspotting

The process of adding hyperlinks in a document on the area where the tag exists in the file.

identity token

A system object that contains information about the user and details on how that user was authenticated and validated by the software.

image reader

Extracts tag and other related information contained within any image using an Optical Recognition (OCR) engine.

implicit delete

A component process used by the software, where a user can decide to implicitly delete or terminate a group of objects from the target system, because they
are no longer in the supplied input data submission.

implies

The relationship between two interface definitions in the schema. If an interface definition implies another interface definition, then any class definition that

realizes the first interface definition can also realize the implied interface definition.

import definition

A defined mapping of imported file objects, properties, and relationships from existing column headers to objects, properties, and relationships found in the
staging area database during the import process.

import process

The process that manages the import of data in the data file or files to the staging area database using a defined mapping.

Info Map

A display system that uses a systematic approach to analyze, organize, and present clear and user-focused information, so that you can quickly visualize the
relationship details of an individual object.

integration

Technology that standardizes and improves communication among the various authoring tools used in design, construction, and operation of a plant.
Integration manages data exchange among these authoring tools, which enables sharing and re-use of plant information throughout the plant lifecycle.

interface definition

A named collection of property definitions that represents a role for a class definition in the schema.

inverted CSV file

See raw attribute format.

issue purpose

A designation given to a deliverable item to describe why the item has been issued, such as for review or construction.

IWA

Integrated Windows Authentication (IWA) refers to automatically authenticated connections between Microsoft Internet Information Services and other active
directory aware applications.

job

A defined object that carries information for the progress of data through a selected workflow, such as when data is imported, validated, and exported to a

target system.

job definition

A combined set of components configured for the import, validation, and export of data to a specific target system or multiple target systems.

JSON

JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) is an open standard format that uses human-readable text to transmit data objects, consisting of attribute-value pairs,
between a server and web application. JSON is an alternative to XML.

laser scan reader

Captures Leica TruView HDS information created from plant scans.

mapping

A defined process where data is correlated from existing column headers for the objects, properties, and relationships in the imported data to the correct

column headers for the objects, properties, and relationships found in another database, such as the staging database or target system. For more information,
see import definition and export mapping.

markup layer

A layer that contains saved actions and comments raised by a document reviewer on a document in the collaboration document review process.

master document register (MDR)

A master document register (MDR) is a submittal type initiated by the contractor to send documentation data to the owner company.

master file

The file that is used for data and content extraction.

master tag register (MTR)

A master tag register (MTR) is a submittal type initiated by the contractor to send master tag data to the owner company.

master tags

Extracted tags that follow a set of standard naming conventions in a project or a plant that the engineers should follow for defining tag names.

merge

To promote applicable project data to the as-built plant.

method

A call to an API that allows users to perform actions on objects or interfaces in the software.

model

A physical object which is the requisitioned item and includes a model number.

notifications

Alerts to specific events in the software that appear by registering your interest in objects or object classes, such as the latest revision to a document.

OAuth

An open standard for authorization. OAuth provides client applications a secure delegated access to server resources on behalf of a resource owner.

object weighting

A process that emphasizes the contribution of an object in a set of data to a final effect or result, thereby deciding its weight in the analysis. This affects the
order in which the data is exported, helping to achieve the desired result.

OData

Open Data Protocol (OData) is an OASIS standard that defines the best practices for building and consuming RESTful APIs.

orphan tags

Tags that are not related to any document or domain tag.

owning group

The user group to which an object is assigned in the software.

picklist

A list of applicable values for a given property in the software.

pinboard

An area in the software where you can add or pin objects so you can quickly recall and manage them in the system. Objects added to the pinboard are
available to you between log in sessions.

pins

Bookmarked objects that provide quick access to new items or objects added to the pinboard so you can quickly recall and manage them in the system. Pins
added to the pinboard are available to you between log in sessions.

plant

An object that can represent the top level in the delivered data hierarchy. If you use a custom hierarchy, the object at the top level of the hierarchy may have a

different name.

POST

A request-response method between clients and servers. A POST submits data to a specified resource for processing.

progress

Functionality that allows users to track the development of deliverables, such as documents or items, against a project plan, indicating any deviations from the
original plan.

project

A logical unit of data that is a subset of the items that make up a plant. A project is used for making controlled, incremental changes to the data in a plant.
There can be multiple projects for a plant at any given time.

property

An object characteristic.

property definition

A basic attribute shared by all members of an object class. Property definitions are grouped using interface definitions in the schema.

property group

A group of properties.

property list

Allows you to create property group names (interface definition names) and properties (property definitions), which can be related to a business object (class
definitions).

publish

To share a document and its data in an integrated environment with other authoring tools by exporting an XML file containing the document data and
relationships. When a document is published, the software places the XML file in the appropriate vault and loads the data from the XML file into the database.
After the document is published, users can retrieve the data from the XML file located in the vault into other authoring tools.

In non-integrated environments, like SDx, documents and data cannot be published or retrieved by authoring tools. However, data that was previously

published in an integrated environment can be loaded into the database, using Data Validator.

QA review

A process where the user can verify the documents issued by a contractor.

query

A search based on object properties.

query definition

A comma separated list of properties that can be entered in the Target System Query Definition field, which identifies the object in the target system along
with the class definition. These properties can be retrieved by navigating the relationships.

query scope

A configuration for data queries and relationship expansions in the software.

raw attribute format

Where the data in a CSV file is organized in a vertical format and each row contains properties and relationships for the same object. Also referred to as an
inverted CSV file. The data in a standard CSV file is typically organized horizontally.

realizes

The relationship between class definitions and interface definitions in the schema. Class definitions realize interface definitions. The interface definitions that
are realized by a class definition expose the properties for that class definition.

Reason For Issue (RFI)

The reason a document is released as part of a transmittal.

Reason For Receipt (RFR)

The reason why a specific recipient was included on a transmittal or workflow step.

register interest

To subscribe or indicate a stake an object. When a new one becomes available or is updated, a notification appears in your notification list.

regular expression

A pattern that is used for tag matching and defining selected file parts.

Relationship Builder

A tool to quickly and easily select objects and relate them to each other using selected relationship definitions.

relationship definition

Associations between interface definitions in the schema. Relationship definitions identify two specific objects that fulfill the roles on each end of the
relationship.

relationship property

A property on the link interface which is part of the relationship definition. The link interface allows properties to be created on the relationship itself.

rendition

An alternate representation of the original document version that is created during the document review consolidation process. A PDF rendition is a type of
alternate rendition, which is a viewable file of a different file type.

responder

A user sending a response.

response

Can be a reply from an external organization directed to an internal organization.

REST

A set of architectural principles used to design Web services that focus on systems resources, including how resource states are addressed and transferred
over HTTP by a wide range of clients written in different languages. Web services employing a REST architecture read a designated Web page containing an
XML file that describes and includes the desired content.

RESTful APIs

When Web services use REST architecture, they are called RESTful, or REST APIs.

retrieve

A process that imports document data from an .XML file submitted to maintain data consistency across the system. When you retrieve a document, the

retrieved data is analyzed in the database, which can place workflow tasks on a user's To Do List. The tasks on the To Do List allow you to create, delete, or
modify items at the appropriate time in the design or review process.

review assignment

Review assignment rules define which documents should be assigned to a review plan and to whom, either a discipline or an individual.

review plan

A review plan allows you to control the documents you want to review when new documents are added to the system or when existing documents have been
revised or updated in the system.

review plan item

An object that is created based on the review plan and used to identify the documents for review.

revision

An officially recognized change to a document. Each revision of a document may have multiple versions.

revision scheme

A numbering convention for document revisions.

role

Determines a user's level of access to data and functionality in a specific plant/project configuration. Users can belong to more than one role per configuration.
Roles are defined by system administrators and are based on related access groups, domains, and owning groups.

rules

A logical formula used to evaluate and verify whether the data in an imported record meets the requirements and data standards specified for the target
database system.

rulesets

A combined set of rules that can be run as a set during job processing.

schema

A model used to describe and validate the structure of XML files.

scope

A selected configuration in which users can view, create, modify, and terminate data.

scoped by

The relationship between property definitions and property types in the schema. The scoped by relationship specifies the property type that defines acceptable
values, or scopes, a particular property definition. Every property definition in the schema is scoped by one and only one property type. All properties of that
property definition must be of that property type.

section

A collection of display items used on forms in the software.

security codes

Configuration-independent items that are used to set different security levels, which can be applied to objects in a configuration. They can be used to control
the confidentiality or restrict the availability of the objects.

security rules

Security measures that limit the types of data that users have access to in the system. A user’s access group is used to determine which security rules are

relevant.

separator

Separates file parts and tag parts. Common separators include a dash (-), slash (/), tilde (~), and so forth. For example, the instrument tag LCV-157 uses the
dash (-) separator to separate the tag name (LCV) from the drawing name (157).

server

A computer that stores or processes files.

sign off

To approve a particular revision of a document in the software. Signing off a document sets the document to be the current released revision, makes it official,
and supersedes any previous released revisions. Document revisions that have been signed off are frozen and cannot be checked out.

site

The server settings that point to administration and data databases. Each site allows you to run the software operations on data associated with plants and
projects configured in the database.

SSL

Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) provides a secure, encrypted connection between a web server and a browser application, such as a website. This allows you to
transmit encrypted private data online.

staging

The staging or staging area or staging database used is the first part of a combined set of processes, where imported data is held and validated before being
exported to a site.

status

The state of a change object at the completion of each step in a workflow.

step

A process that must be performed in order to complete a workflow.

submittal

A control file used to send documents and files to the owning company's system. The submittal contains all the details of the submitted objects, including tags
and any document file attachments.

sub-review

A technical query to an internal resource to provide more information to complete a document review, referred to as Internal Technical Query in the user
interface.

subscribe

To register interest in an object so that you receive a notification when the object is modified. You can manually subscribe to change notifications in the

software.

superseded

Indicates that a newer, working version of the selected document exists.

tag

A database object that is the physical part as designed.

tag discovery pattern

Used to extract the master and alias tags from the content of a master file.

tag document register (TDR)

A tag document register (TDR) is a submittal type initiated by the contractor to send tag and document relationship data to the owner company.

tag naming system

Defines the parts of a tag name and relates them to existing objects in the database.

target system

The system or database used as the destination for exported data that has been validated before export. It is also used as the basis for creating import
mappings, implicit delete, and validation rules.

technical query

A technical query is a question raised on a document or object that is progressed through a workflow.

terminate

The action of changing an objects status to terminate makes the object and its history inaccessible except for documents. For documents, terminating a
document version or revision hides it from general searches unless you select Show Removed Items icon.

text reader

Captures simple text files and processes the data directly without needing an application to extract the data.

thumbnail

A small size image representation of a larger file intended to make it easier and faster to manage it.

title block

The portion of a drawing that contains information about the drawing, such as who created the drawing, when it was created, who approved it, and so on. The

type of information included in the title block varies by drawing type, industry, and organization.

To Do List

A graphical list of tasks that require attention from the user.

In the software, the To Do List contains workflow steps assigned to the user.

In the authoring tools, the To Do List contains create, delete, and update tasks generated when a user retrieves a document, such as a P&ID.

transmittal

A transmittal is used to distribute a controlled set of documents in the system to a specific set of recipients. The transmittal records and tracks the distribution
of the documents, the recipients, and the reason for their issue.

transpose

Returns a vertical range of cells as a horizontal range, or vice versa.

UID

Unique Identifier is used to uniquely identify an object within a system. UID definitions must be set on all objects that are exported from the staging area if the
unique key or query definition is left blank. The staging area UID definitions must match the UID definitions in the target system.

unique key

A set of values guaranteed to be unique for each object in a relation, and can be used to identify objects in the target system.

unit

Group of parts of the schematic and individual worlds of a plant that together perform a given process function. The identifying number of the unit is unique

within the project and within the plant. Most companies, but not all, use the concept of unit.

UoM

A unit of measurement.

URL

A uniform resource locator is a web address that contains a specific character string that references a resource.

user

An object that specifies data about a person who is authorized to use the software.

user profile

Information about windows, configurations, preferences, and interface settings, saved by the software when you close the application and used to configure

the application when you reopen it.

validation process

A process that evaluates the imported data against a defined set of rules to ensure the validity of the data.

validation rule

A logical formula used to evaluate the imported data in one or more fields to determine whether it matches the existing criteria and hierarchy found in the target

system database. There are two types of validation rules:

1. Rules that determine if the data is valid for the target system schema (these rules can be autogenerated.

2. Rules that determine if the data meets specific business criteria, such as naming format.

vault

A folder where files are stored on a host computer.

vendor

A vendor is a company or manufacturer that is supplying a product or service for sale.

version

An intermediate update to an existing document that is tracked by the software.

View and Markup

The functionality that provides precision view and markup capabilities for documents and is used in the document review process.

virtual directory

A Web folder created in IIS that points to a physical folder on the Web server. Virtual directories are used by the software to run applications and services from

the application server and to transfer files between file servers and clients.

Visualization Data Service (VDS)

Provides graphics and associated information services to the Graphic View Control (GVC) when viewing Hexagon 3D models or 2D drawings in a HxGN SDx
client application.

wildcard

A character that helps you narrow your search for objects in the database. You can use wildcards in any text box in the Find and Query dialog boxes.

Text wildcards include the following:

? - Finds any single character

* - Finds any string of characters

% - Performs the same function as *

work breakdown structure

A structure for grouping documents, tags, and other information for handing over deliverables to the client.

work package

A detailed scope of work to be executed and completed in a scheduled amount of time.

work process diagram

A key part of a work process diagram is the use of color-coded swimlanes. A swimlane helps illustrate a process that spans across different applications and is

represented as a row containing activities specific to the application or role involved.

workflow

A series of steps defining actions to be taken on an object in the software.

working document

A document that has not been signed off in the software.

XML

Extensible Markup Language; a format frequently used for exporting data from the database or importing data into the system. XML files must conform to the
structure defined by the application schema. In an integrated SmartPlant Foundation environment, all documents published or retrieved in an integrated

environment use this format.

Mandate Purpose

 At logical level, with examples of possible implementation

architectures

parties

OpenO&M and other standards are being used and
interlinked

 Ensure industry relevance

more detailed work is done

ARCHITECTURE

Architecture: The purpose and motivation

 Enterprise systems have to meet requirements of many different types

It is difficult to keep everything in focus,
and too many system architectures are
only about technology

How can architecture help create
systems that better meet their
requirements throughout the life-cycle?

 The Business Context

Dimension

Dimension

Architecture: The Business Context Dimension

 “Why is the system there in the first place”

 A lifecycle view of system operation

 PISTEP lifecycle model

 Engineering and Process value chain

 Control and Procurement supply chain

 Scoping of information lifecycle management

Integrated Process Plant Engineering systems

Integrated
Plant
Maintenance
systems

Architecture: The Technology Configuration Dimension

 “How is the system layered and populated”

 A topological view of system structure

 Purdue Enterprise Reference Architecture

 Physical environment and production system

 Intelligent devices, manufacturing control

 Enterprise applications

Architecture: The Knowledge Content Dimension

 “What does the system know”

 A topical view of domain knowledge

 Application domain specific terms

 ISO 15926 Data Model

Reference Data for
Oil & Gas applications

Production
Control  Ontology
(relationships between
domain specific and

MIMOSA Common Conceptual Object Model (CCOM)
