#### General

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Required Information** | **Description** | **Status** | **Format[[21]](#footnote-21)** |
| Drafting Standards | Standards to be adopted for all drawings, including symbol library | **S** | **N** |
| Supplier Data Dossiers | Vendor information for all purchased equipment, including specifications, diagrams, drawings, maintenance procedures, etc. | **E** | **N / S** |
| Engineering Reports & Calculation Packages | Independent consultant reports and calculations for critical plant and equipment | **E** | **N** |
| Contractor's Intention of Major Change | Notification that the contractor intends a major design change | **S** | **N** |
| Design Change Control Procedures |  | **S** | **N** |
| Technical Query (TQ) Form |  | **S** | **S** |
| Advance Revision Notice (ARN) |  | **S** | **N** |
| Interface Records | Technical Interface Records | **S** | **S** |
| Maintenance and Preservation Procedures | During project phase to include programme | **S** | **S** |
| Change Control Records | Audit trail summarising status and outstanding actions associated with all change to originally agreed design / statement of requirements. Will be referenced for completion of outstanding works post handover | **S** | **S** |
| Design Deviation Reports | Audit report on variances from design found at the as-built / post-construction stage of the project along with their justification and reasons for acceptance. Document to be referenced when modifications carried out | **S** | **N** |
| Maintenance Information | Comprises equipment manufacturers' information necessary to specify and support maintenance (planned and breakdown) and inspection activities. To include manufacturers' recommendations for maintenance and inspection, fault-finding guidance, drawings, procedures, parts lists, lubrication details, adjustments, settings and tolerances. Certain of this information may already be specified separately | **E** | **N** |
| Operating Information | Information in the form of instructions, procedures, drawings, tables, etc. for the operation stop, start, and the emergency shutdown of the equipment, including details of operational limits, function testing, possible interruptions, corrective actions and potential hazards and corrective measures to be taken | **E** | **N** |
| Spares Listings | Recommended holding of operating and insurance spares. The operating spares recommendation is a listing of spare parts required to support the day-to-day operation and maintenance of the equipment for a period of 'N' years. Insurance spares are parts / complete units not normally required on a day-to-day basis, but needing to be held as an insurance against breakdown / outage of critical utility and process plant. To be provided with relevant supporting documentation (equipment details, drawings, parts list, etc.) to permit full evaluation of the recommendation.  Commissioning Spares to be separately listed | **E** | **S** |

#### Multiple Format Exceptions

Documents that require or are normally provided with seals of approval shall be provided in 3 formats: original hardcopy (H), image (I), and where possible native (N) format.

Similarly, signed documents in which the form cannot be mandated shall be provided in 3 formats: original hardcopy (H), image (I), and where possible native (N) format.

#### Equipment Data Requirements (Attributes)

The information presented in this Section constitutes XX’s minimum handover requirements for data attributes.

##### Common Data

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Required Attribute** | **Description** |
| Tag Number | Location and functional identifier of item currently associated with equipment |
| Service | Duty of item currently associated with equipment |
| Description | General description of equipment |
| Parent Tag | Associated main or major item currently associated with equipment |
| Location | Area location |
| Fire Area |  |
| Systems | Identifier of the process utility or other system of which the equipment forms part. Note, there may be multiple classifications (e.g. a given heat exchanger can be part of a process facility and a utility facility) also required to associate facilities and materials with commissioning systems |
| Criticality Rating | Equipment criticality rating derived from the formal criticality assessment carried out by the project in conjunction with the operator |
| Drawing and Document References | References to all documents listed as main requirements that are applicable for a given item. This will enable navigation from items in the database thatr are associated with drawings and documents |
| Serial Number | Manufacturer's unique identifier |
| Vendor | Person or source upon whom order is placed |
| Order Number | Number of PO placed on vendor |
| Type / Model | Manufacturer's generic identifier for an item currently associated with equipment |
| WIN Number | For plant identification currently associated with equipment (sub class of materials) |
| Manufacturer | Source or origin of the item currently associated with equipment |

#### Lists and Schedules

|  |
| --- |
| **Required Attribute** |
| Cable Number |
| Number of Cores |
| Cross Sectional Area |
| Estimated Length |
| Location |
| From Tag |
| From Location |
| From Gland |
| From Termination Drawing |
| To Tag |
| To Location |
| To Gland |
| To Termination Drawing |
| Intrinsically Safe (Y/N) |
| Type of signal carried |
| System that cable is part of |

|  |
| --- |
| **Required Attribute** |
| Line Number |
| Size |
| Service |
| Spec |
| From |
| To |
| Max Design Pressure |
| Design Pressure (normal) |
| Max Design Temperature |
| Min Design Temperature |
| Max Operating Pressure |
| Min Operating Pressure |
| Max Operating Temperature |
| Min Operating Temperature |
| Normal Operating Pressure |
| Insulation Thickness |
| Insulation Specification |
| Trace Heating (Min Temp) |
| P&ID Number |
| Isometric Drawing Number |

##### Control System I/O List

A list of inputs and outputs for each control system node is required.

##### Drawing and Document List Attributes

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Required Attribute** | **Description** |
| Area | Module or area code associated with the document |
| Discipline | Discipline identifier as per the numbering system |
| Document number | Document number as per the numbering standards |
| Document type | Document type |
| Filename | Document electronic filename |
| Other document number | Supplier or contractor document number |
| Other revision | Supplier or contractor revision number |
| PO number | Purchase order number of most recent issue |
| Document Reference To | Refers to Documents |
| Contractor / Originator | Originator Code |
| Remarks | General remarks for the document |
| Revision date | Date of revision issue |
| Revision number | Revision number |
| Sequence number | Sequence number portion of the document number |
| Subsequence number | Sheet number or subsequence number |
| System | Process system number |
| Title | Document title |

#### Instrument Data

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Required Attribute** | **Description** |
| Equipment / Line Number | The tag number of the equipment or pipe line number that the instrument tag is associated with |
| Mechanical Connections | The type of connection associated with the instrument |
| Gasket Type | The type of gasket required for sealing the mechanical connections of the instrument |
| Calibrated Span | The calibrated operating range (including minimum and maximum settings) for the instrument |
| Output Range | The output range for the instrument |
| Trip Category | The trip category as defined in appropriate standards |
| Set Point High | The trip or alarm high setting |
| Set Point Low | The trip or alarm low setting |
| Proportional Band / Gain | Details of controller setting |
| Integral Action / Reset | Details of controller setting |
| Derivative Action | Details of controller setting |
| ANSI Leak Class | The leak class for control valves for sealing / shut-off requirements |
| Maximum Leakage Rate (Valves) | The maximum valve leakage to determine sealing / shut-off requirements |
| Maximum Test Pressure (Valves) | The maximum test pressure to determine sealing / shut-off requirements |
| Bench Setting | The valve stroke and related information for setting up a control valve |
| Operating Time to Open | The time a control or ESD valve takes to open |
| Operating Time to Close | The time a control or ESD valve takes to close |
| Valve Travel | The total length of the valve stem travel. Valve action on failure |
| Valve Action on Failure | The final position of the control or ESD valve on loss of input signal |
| Hazardous Area Classification | The certification, gas grouping and temperature rating for hazardous area equipment |
| Certifying Authority | The Certifying Authority for hazardous area equipment |
| Ex Code |  |
| Gas Group |  |
| ATEX Code |  |
| Temperature Class |  |
| IP Code |  |
| Certifying Number | The number of certificate as issued by certifying authority |
| Reference Document Number | The document number for reference information |

#### Fire and Gas Data

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Required Attribute** | **Description** |
| Set Point High | The trip or alarm high setting |
| Set Point Low | The trip or alarm low setting |
| Detector Range | The designated coverage envelope for a detector |
| Detector Sensitivity | The sensitivity of a detector loop |
| Hazardous Area Classification | The certification, gas grouping and temperature rating for hazardous area equipment |
| Certifying Authority | The Certifying Authority for hazardous area equipment |
| Certificate Number | The number of certificate as issued by Certifying Authority |
| Ex Code |  |
| Gas Group |  |
| Temp Class |  |
| Reference Document Number | The document number for reference information |

#### Telecommunications Data

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Required Attribute** | **Description** |
| Loop Type | The power supply source from yellow, red or post-red distribution boards |
| Loud Speaker Trip Settings | The loud speaker trip setting |
| Power Rating | The operating power of the unit |
| Voltage | The operating voltage of the unit |
| Licensed RF | The licensed operating frequency range of the unit |
| Licence Expiry Date | The licence expiry date for radio equipment |
| Cable Type | The cable details (impedance, tolerance, etc) for specialised installations e.g. antenna and LAN |
| Power Supply Type | The type of power supply - UPS, 240V, 110V etc |
| Hazardous Area Classification | The certification, gas grouping and temperature rating for hazardous area equipment |
| Certifying Authority | The Certifying Authority for hazardous area equipment |
| Certificate Number | The number of certificate as issued by Certifying Authority |
| Ex Code |  |
| Gas Group |  |
| Temp Class |  |
| Reference Document Number | The document number for reference information |

#### Electrical Data

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Required Attribute** | **Description** |
| Overload Rating and Type | The type and setting of overload device for motors |
| Contactor Rating and Type | The type and setting for contactor |
| Lubrication Details | The lubrication requirement for rotating equipment and actuators |
| Bearing Details (Drive and Non-drive End) | The bearing type, size details for rotating equipment |
| Fuse Type / Rating | The fuse type and current rating for equipment |
| Actuator Detail | The type and size of the valve actuator |
| Torque Setting | The torque setting for actuators |
| Voltage Rating | The maximum and minimum voltage rating for equipment |
| kW Rating | The kW power rating for the equipment |
| kVA Rating | The voltage ampere rating for the equipment |
| Phase | The voltage / current relationship for the equipment |
| Frequency | The operating frequency for the equipment |
| Speed | The normal operating rotational speed of the equipment |
| AH Rating | Capacity rating of a battery |
| Voltage / Cell anfd Total | The voltage per cell and total voltage for a battery |
| Full Load Current | The full load current for the equipment |
| Anti-Condensation Heater Detail | The type and make of heater |
| Weight Dry / Empty | The weight of an item when dry and empty |
| Transformer Ratios | The input/output ratio for a transformer |
| Frame Sizes | The class and frame size for motors |
| Switchgear Protection Rating | The load protection level on the system equipment |
| Ingress Protection Rating | Rating for protection against ingress of foreign bodies for safety and hazardous equipment |
| Hazardous Area Classification | The certification, gas grouping and temperature rating for hazardous area equipment |
| Certifying Authority | The Certifying Authority for hazardous area equipment |
| Certificate Number | The number of certificate as issued by Certifying Authority |
| Ex Class |  |
| Gas Group |  |
| Temp Class |  |
| Reference Document Number | The document number for reference information |

#### Mechanical Data

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Required Attribute** | **Description** |
| Design Code | The industry standard to which the equipment complies |
| Design Pressure | The pressure to which the equipment has been designed |
| Design Temperature | The temperature to which the equipment has been designed |
| Hydrostatic Test Pressure | The pressure to which the equipment is to be hydraulically tested |
| Shell Test Pressure | The pressure to which a heat exchanger shall be tested |
| Tube Test Pressure | The pressure to which a heat exchanger tube bundle shall be tested |
| SWL | The safe working load |
| Test Load | The load to be used in proof load testing an item |
| Test Requirements | Defines the description of test parameters for an item e.g. SWL x 1.5 |
| Certification Requirements | The test or certifying authority for an item |
| Weight Dry / Empty | The weight of an item when dry and empty |
| Tube Bundle Weight | Th weight of a heat exchanger tube bundle |
| Material | The physical composition of material for an item |
| Operating Pressure | The normal operating pressure for the equipment |
| Operating Temperature | The normal operating temperature for the equipment |
| Connection Size and Rating | The dimensions, type and pressure rating of mechanical connections |
| Gasket Type | The gasket details for mechanical connections |
| Bursting Pressure | The design pressure at which a bursting disc will rupture |
| Cold Set Pressure | The pressure to which a pressure relief valve is set up on a test bed |
| Design Setting | Design set point for pressure relief devices |
| Back Pressure | The normal process pressure condition into which a relief valve vents |
| Speed | The normal operational rotational speed for equipment |
| Rotation | The direction of rotation as viewed from the non-drive or drive end (specify which) |
| Peak Load | The maximum working load / output power of the equipment |
| Capacity | The volume of fluid / medium held or capable of being moved in a given time |
| Number of Stages | The number of pressure stages in the equipment |
| Seal Details | The manufacturer, type and size of seal |
| Driver Details | The prime mover description |
| Coupling Detail | The manufacturer, type and size of the coupling |
| Certification Date | The certification test date (load test, pressure test, etc) for lifting equipment, vessels and PSVs |
| Element Detail | The manufacturer, type and size for filter elements |
| Well Control Equipment Certificate of Conformity Number | The certificate number for an item |
| Well Control Equipment Certificate of Conformity Date | The issue and expiry dates for a certificate of conformity |
| Reference Document Numbers | The document numbers for reference information |

#### Valve Data

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Required Attribute** | **Description** |
| Type / Specification Number | The valve type /specification number as detailed in valve specification |
| Line Number | The identifier of line in which a valve is located |
| Size | Valve connection and bore size |

### Native Format Requirements For Intelligent Applications

#### SmartPlant Foundation

##### Scope

The contractor is required to use the 2009 R3 Version of SmartPlant Foundation as the delivery mechanism for all project data and document deliverables.

The contractor is required to use the 10.2.0.5 of the Oracle database.

SmartPlant Foundation will be provided pre-configured to the contractor based on XX data handover requirements.

It is required that the contractor fully understand that SmartPlant Foundation is the master of record for not only the tags and non-tags (buildings, process systems, vendor packages, etc.), but also for documents.

It is required that the contractor use reports/documents originating from SmartPlant Foundation as construction documents. SmartPlant Foundation is required to be the keeper of the “record of copy” for all documents. It is required that all disciplines have “view only” access to the data and documents to improve communication. Traditional hardcopy transmissions of documents will not be made. Contractors to download the documentation they require from SPF.

It is required that the contractor use SmartPlant Foundation as the record keeper for redlines and mark-ups of all data and documents. This is by the use of the completions and the change package functionality within SmartPlant Foundation. XX acceptance of “as-built” status will be through these mechanisms. The contractor is required to staff sufficient personnel to this activity so that the final handover of as-built data and documents is kept to {state the minimum acceptable time here}.

It is required that the contractor provide tabulated totals of the number of outstanding as-built documents to XX every {state acceptable timing or schedule here}.

It is required that the OO and EPC agree the project plant breakdown structure. This is required to be identical within all the SmartPlant Engineering tools (i.e. “Plant – Area – Unit”) such that data is deposited in the correct functional locations.

The contractor is required to comply with XX Tag and document naming convention and standards as detailed in {name and number of the Tag numbering specification or detail here}. NOTE: Ensure you list all Tag-numbering specifications, including the DCS/PLC “soft Tag” numbering to ensure that “real-time” Tag numbers are consistent and generated from SmartPlant Instrumentation as the basis.

SPF will be used as the global tag master for the allocation of tag numbers, especially important for brownfield developments.

Access will be made available to SmartPlant Foundation either as a system hosted by XX or hosted by the contractor. Final decision on this will be at FEED contract award.

##### Deliverables

XX will decide what types of documents and data will be delivered progressively by the contractor. The contractor is required to transfer data and documents to XX when requested, and should allow for incremental data and document transfers per key engineering activity.

Key milestones for data delivery required by the contractor are:

* Issued for review (IFR)
* Issued for approval (IFA)
* Issued for construction (IFC)
* As-built (Current)

{XX will need to identify the type of information to be delivered at each stage or delivery milestone.}

Based on the SmartPlant Foundation implementation strategy, {XX states how the data and documents are transferred}

Tag/document cross-references shall be transferred. Reference to an overall package tag or to all tags in a package is not acceptable.

Vendor documents to be cross-referenced to a standard contents list of chapters to facilitate the electronic compilation of User manuals.

Contractor to deliver all documentation required to satisfy regulatory agencies and for example comply with CE marking, ATEX directives.

It is required that the contractor must remove all temporary drawings, test drawings, test symbols, temporary filters, reports etc. from the SmartPlant Foundation data before delivery.

The contractor is required to ensure that the delivered SmartPlant Foundation data is free of any database exceptions and orphan entries.

The contractor is required to as-build the SmartPlant Foundation data before its final delivery based on XX as-built requirement.

The contractor is required to provide an Oracle dump file of the data and administrative databases of SmartPlant Foundation.

The contractor is required to provide copies of all vaults and their contents on DVD with an index of the contents at vault level only. It is required that the vault name have to only match the XX SmartPlant Foundation vault name.

The contractor is to provide a copy of the reference data, active service pages, XSL style sheets, any custom dll, and any source code used to create the dll files to ensure that SmartPlant Foundation will work in an identical way within the XX environment.

#### SmartPlant P&ID

##### Scope

The contractor is required to use the 2009 SP3 version of SmartPlant P&ID to create all P&ID drawings unless otherwise specified by *XX*.

The contractor is required to use the 10.2.0.5 version of Oracle database.

The contractor is required to use Intergraph’s SmartPlant P&ID software for the purpose of creating intelligent P&ID drawings.

The resulting P&IDs are required to be published by the contractor to SmartPlant Foundation according to a delivery schedule agreed upon between *XX* and the contractor as a minimum. The contractor is free to publish P&IDs at any time.

The contractor is required to redraw any vendor package P&IDs using SmartPlant P&ID to the same drafting standards as all other SmartPlant P&IDs.

Border templates and title blocks for the P&IDs should be customized to match *XX* border standards as detailed in *{name and description of the document or include details here}.* The contractor is required to check with *XX* for the availability of these borders so as to avoid having to reproduce them. NOTE: It is suggested that these be issued to the contractor as part of the FEED package.

It is required that the contractor ensure that the project plant structure comply with the Hierarchy 7 that is available within the SmartPlant Engineering Manager (i.e. “Plant – Area – Unit”). All drawings are required to be created at the unit level only. It is not allowed to have dummy units.

The contractor is required to provide a legend sheet of all symbols used with a description of the use of that symbol. In addition, it is required that the contractor describe all equipment, instrument, loop, and line number “Tag Numbering” conventions. It is required that *XX* officially signed off the P&ID legend sheet before any work begins on P&IDs. The contractor is required to check with *XX* for the availability of the legend sheet so as to avoid having to reproduce them. NOTE: It is suggested that these be issued to the contractor as part of the FEED package.

It is required that *XX* review all CAD drafting standards before the start of any P&ID work.

The contractor is required to utilize the ANSI-based P&ID reference data (i.e. symbols, templates, assemblies, report templates, rules, insulation spec, etc.), as delivered with the SmartPlant P&ID software for the creation of all P&IDs. NOTE: Clients might want to provide their own reference library at pre-FEED.

In the event that an appropriate ANSI-based P&ID symbol is not available within the software-delivered reference data, the contractor is required to create such symbol based on *XX* symbols and legends as defined in *{name and description of symbol and legend specification or include it in the document here}*. It is required that *XX* review and approve all contractor-created symbol data before that symbol is used. *XX* full intention is to use delivered ANSI-standard-based P&ID symbol reference data to the maximum extent possible.

The contractor is required to use the SmartPlant P&ID default font (Arial) for items, notes, labels, general notes, etc.

The contractor is required to create new user-defined attributes based on the project requirement. It is required that the same attributes be added to the data dictionary under relevant categories. It is required that the contractor provide a summary of added attributes as requested by *XX* or at the “Issued for Construction” stage. It is required that it should be submitted as a separate document describing such attributes. This document is required to be stored in SmartPlant Foundation.

The contractor is required to resolve or approve all design inconsistencies in the P&IDs as part of their normal work process. The contractor is required to follow the best engineering design practices for setting up SmartPlant P&ID consistency rules.

The contractor is required to fill in all applicable attributes to the fullest extent for all P&ID components, such as pipe runs, equipment, inline instruments, etc. This is to ensure that *XX* receives the complete and meaningful P&ID data in SmartPlant P&ID format.

The contractor is required to ensure that P&ID instrument tags have association with the appropriate instrument loops. It must be stated that this is a firm requirement of the *XX* and will be strictly enforced.

The contractor is required to follow *XX* tag naming conventions for P&ID instrument tags, loop tags, equipment tags, and line tags. Tag numbers must be entered identically to within tag indexes and 3D model to ensure traceability across applications.

The contractor is required to use the capability to create line, instrument, and equipment lists from within SmartPlant P&ID and not create these documents outside SmartPlant P&ID. However, the master list for these items is considered to be SmartPlant Foundation. Only lists from SmartPlant Foundation are accepted as record of copy.

The contractor is required to follow their best design practices for putting P&ID face value information, such as labels, annotations, descriptions, notes, sizes, etc., as detailed in the approved design standards.

##### Deliverables

The contractor is required to as-build the P&ID data before its final delivery based on *XX* as-built requirements. The P&IDs are considered “as-built” when all redlines and markups are updated from the field at start-up. SmartPlant Foundation will be used to track such redlines and markups.

The contractor is required to remove all temporary drawings, test drawings, test symbols and assemblies, temporary filters, reports, etc. from the SmartPlant P&ID data before delivery.

The contractor is required to use the SmartPlant P&ID “Re-Create P&ID from Database” functionality before final Q&C and delivery to *XX*.

The contractor is required to ensure that the delivered SmartPlant P&ID data is free of any database exceptions and orphan entries. The contractor is required to run all appropriate utilities as delivered with the software for identifying and resolving database issues.

The contractor is required to provide a back up of SmartPlant P&ID site and plant with work share and project disabled, including the P&ID Reference Data (symbols, templates, assemblies, report templates, rules, insulation spec, modified dlls, etc.) used with the P&ID database on completion of the as-built activity *{or state timing or schedule}.*

It is required that the contractor provide a document specifying customizations carried out in reference data, data dictionary, formats, rules, and reports and store the same in SmartPlant Foundation.

The contractor is required to provide any modified source code for all dlls that were created for the SmartPlant P&ID project and must highlight the modifications vis-à-vis delivered code. This code is to be sufficiently documented such that *XX* can understand what the function is required to do. It should be understood from the start of the project that *XX* owns all code created for the project. It is a strict requirement that all code be issued to *XX* to enable the recompilation of the binary dll at a future date when the SmartPlant P&ID software is upgraded.

It is required that the contractor sufficiently document approved deviations from standard specifications. This document is required to be stored in SmartPlant Foundation.

#### SmartPlant Instrumentation (INtools®)

##### Scope

The contractor is required to use the 2009 SP3 version of Intergraph’s SmartPlant Instrumentation software for the creation and management of all instrumentation deliverables, including field instrumentation, fire & gas and telecommunications.

The Plant/Area/Unit definition contained within SmartPlant Instrumentation is to match that of SmartPlant Engineering Manager. No additional definitions can be made that are not identical to those defined in the SmartPlant Engineering Manager. An Instrument Tags unit number should also match exactly with the SmartPlant P&ID and SmartPlant Electrical.

It is required that the contractor use the tag numbering scheme as defined in the project tag numbering specification *{name and description of the document or describe here}.* NOTE: Ensure that all instrumentation components (cables, control system tags, cabinets, panels, etc) are defined in this document.

It is required for the contractor to use the same service description on the instrument datasheets as for the control system point description. Standard abbreviations apply as per *XX* standards.

It is required that the contractor customize all border file templates, title blocks for the various instrumentation documents, and reports to meet *XX* border standard as detailed in *{state number and description of the document or define here}.* It is required that the contractor check with *XX* for the availability of these borders so as to avoid having to reproduce them.

It is required that the contractors customize all the Instrument Specification sheets to meet *XX* instrument datasheet standard. It is required that the contractor check with *XX* for the availability of these sheets so as to avoid having to reproduce them.

It is required that the contractor will populate the SmartPlant Instrumentation project data completely, and include all the various available modules such as Index, Process Data, Calculation, Instrument Specifications, Wiring, Loop drawing, Hook-up, etc. All spare capacity such as free terminals and wires to be registered, not just those used by the development.

It is required that the contractor generate all required instrument deliverables from within SmartPlant Instrumentation. Any deviation from this request is required in writing and must be approved by *XX* before any work starts.

It is required that the contractor generate all loop drawings using Enhanced Smart Loop functionality available within SmartPlant Instrumentation. Where required for readability, the reports generated by Enhanced Smart Loop shall be manually adjusted and locations saved.

It is required that the instrumentation portion of vendor package data, including local control systems, be re-keyed into SmartPlant Instrumentation and the resulting deliverables be issued from SmartPlant Instrumentation for construction purposes.

It is required that the contractor provide official data sheets for control valve and flow elements from SmartPlant Instrumentation.

It is required that the contractor use SmartPlant Instrumentation for storing the data for the alarm and controller set-point summary. It is required that this data be passed to the main control system as the start point for configuration. A work process is required to be in place by the contractor to ensure that the alarm and set point summary is kept in synchronization with the DCS/PLC systems. It should be noted by the contractor that it is required that SmartPlant Foundation be the “record of copy” for this data as this will have management of change wrapped around it once in use by operations and maintenance.

It is required that the contractor use the SmartPlant Electrical interface to pass I/O point data to the SmartPlant Electrical application. It is required that the contractor have a work process in place that keeps these tags synchronized.

It is required that the contractor shall customize all required CAD symbols in SmartSketch format. These symbol files are to be stored in one location as it is required that the contractor hand over these files at “Issued for Construction” time.

In the case where the selected DCS vendor is Honeywell, Delta V, or Yokagowa, it is required that the contractor check for the latest vendor definitions for I/O cards with the vendor before any work starts.

It is required that if the contractor uses “user-defined fields” a summary of usage should be submitted to *XX*. This is required to be a separate document describing its use to ensure that the methodology is continued in operations and maintenance. This document is to be stored in SmartPlant Foundation and be easily available to all SmartPlant Instrumentation users.

It is a strict requirement that the contractor as-build the SmartPlant Instrumentation data before its final delivery based on *XX* as-built requirement document.

##### Deliverables

The contractor is required to as-build the SmartPlant Instrumentation data and documents before its final delivery based on *XX* as-built requirement. The SmartPlant Instrumentation data and documents are considered “as-built” when all redlines and markups are completed from the field at start-up. SmartPlant Foundation will be used to track such redlines and markups.

The contractor is required to ensure that the delivered SmartPlant Instrumentation data is free of any database exceptions and orphan entries.

It is required that the contractor remove all test data, test CAD blocks, temporary browser views, temporary reports, etc. from the SmartPlant Instrumentation database and system directories before delivery.

The contractor is required to deliver the SmartPlant Instrumentation database in a Watcom backup format. The contractor is required to run the appropriate utilities as delivered with SmartPlant Instrumentation for identifying and resolving database issues such as “CheckDB.” The contractor is to provide the log file from this utility showing that there are no errors. It is the contractor’s responsibility to fix such errors.

The contractor is required to provide ALL of the following:

* CAD Blocks, symbols, border templates, title block PSRs
* Customized PSR files for reports and datasheet templates
* Document specifying approved deviations from standard specification

The above electronic deliverables will be made available on the DVD media and includes an index to all that is contained on the DVD.

#### SmartPlant Electrical

##### Scope

The contractor is required to use the 2009 SP3 version of Intergraph’s SmartPlant Electrical software for the creation and management of the Electrical database and deliverables.

The contractor is also required to use the 10.2.0.5 version of Oracle database.

Border templates and title blocks for the schematics and SLDs are required to be customized by the contractor to match *XX* border standards. The contractor is required to check with *XX* for the availability of these borders so as to avoid having to reproduce them before any work starts.

It is required that the project plant structure must comply with the Hierarchy 7 available with SmartPlant Engineering Manager (i.e. “Plant – Area – Unit.”)

The contractor is required to create an electrical reference database and the reference data (symbols, schematic blocks, report templates, etc.) for use with SmartPlant Electrical drawings that will comply with *XX* standards.

The contractor is required to use SmartPlant Electrical default font (Arial) for deliverables.

The contractor is required to use SmartPlant Electrical to create all possible electrical deliverables. Any exceptions are required in writing and will need approval by *XX* before starting work. This includes one-line diagrams, motor schematics, and motor and load lists. However, SmartPlant Foundation is required to be the “record of copy,” and therefore, only documents held within SmartPlant Foundation are to be used for construction.

The contractor is required to create new user-defined attributes based on the project requirements. Any new attributes are required to be added to the data dictionary under relevant categories. A summary of added attributes should be submitted as a separate document describing such attributes. This document is required to be stored in SmartPlant Foundation and be easily available to all SmartPlant Electrical users.

The contractor is required to follow *XX* tag naming conventions for various electrical items, such as cable, motor, transformer, etc. as detailed in *{name and description of the document or describe here}.*

The contractor is required to follow the best engineering design practices for setting up SmartPlant Electrical consistency rules.

The contractor is required to fill in all applicable attributes fully for all indexed tags. This is to ensure that *XX* receives the complete and meaningful SmartPlant Electrical project database.

It is required that the contractor use the SmartPlant Instrumentation interface to receive I/O point data into the SmartPlant Electrical application. It is required that the contractor have a work process in place that keeps these tags synchronized.

The contractor is required to store all electrical datasheets and non-SmartPlant Electrical deliverables as documents within SmartPlant Foundation. It is also required for the contractor to relate the electrical tag number to the new electrical document within SmartPlant Foundation.

It is required that the contractor must remove all temporary drawings, test drawings, test symbols, temporary filters, reports, etc. from the SmartPlant Electrical data before delivery.

The contractor is required to ensure that the delivered SmartPlant Electrical data is free of any database exceptions and orphan entries.

The contractor is required to as-build the Electrical data before its final delivery based on *XX* as-built requirements.

##### Deliverables

It is required that the contractor provide a SmartPlant Electrical site and plant backup, including the reference data (reference schema, templates, symbols, schematic blocks, formats, rules, etc.) used with SmartPlant Electrical project data.

The contractor is required to document specifying customization carried out in reference data, data dictionary, formats, rules, and reports.

The contractor is required to document specifying approved deviations from standard specification.

The above electronic deliverables must be made available on DVD media with an index of contents.

#### SmartPlant 3D

##### Scope

The contractor is required to use the 2009.1 versionof Intergraph’s SmartPlant 3D software for the creation and management of the 3D model deliverables.

The contractor is required to use the 2005 versionof MSSQL database.

*XX* will inform the contractor the coordinates away from “0,0,0” to start work.

It is required that the SmartPlant 3D project (Plant) database name shall be the project name.

It is required that the SmartPlant 3D Catalog (Reference Data) name shall be the project name with the appendix “\_Catalog.”

It is required that the SmartPlant 3D Catalog shall be created using *XX* SmartPlant 3D catalog template. Dimensional tolerances must be specified, for example Main structure +/- 10 mm, piping +/- 50 mm, field equipment +/- 250 mm.

It is required that the Plant Breakdown Structure (System Hierarchy) for all disciplines shall be created as per the contractor’s best design practices but must comply with the project overall plant/area/unit structure.

It is required that the contractor shall use their best design practices to determine additional attribute requirements for equipment, pipe runs, etc.

It is required that the SmartPlant 3D reference data be customized for *XX* piping material classes, piping commodity data, instrument data, specialty data, tap data, piping assemblies, etc.

It is required that the pipe supports be modeled physically.

The contractor is required to use *XX* naming convention for equipment, nozzles, line number, line ID, columns, beams, etc. This naming convention should be using the full tag number and be identical to that in SmartPlant Foundation.

The modeling extent will per *XX* requirement as given below and per contractor’s best practices.

The contractor is required to as built the SmartPlant 3D data before its final delivery based on *XX* as-built requirements.

It is a requirement that the SmartPlant 3D model be populated sufficiently to produce required documents, such as isometrics, plan and elevation drawings, etc., as well as piping material takeoffs.

The modeling extent includes but is not limited to the information provided below. Contractor to model vendor packages to the same level of detail as the rest of the plant.

##### Piping (Using SmartPlant 3D)

It is required that the following piping and piping components shall be modeled:

* All process and utility piping including all piping components, all welds to be included. Shop and field welds to be distinguished by separate symbols. Nozzles to be shown with Nozzle nr as on P&ID.
* Valves including hand wheels, actuators, etc.
* Piping accessories, such as strainers and steam traps
* Underground piping
* Pipe supports
* Jacketed pipes
* Insulation outline
* All major process attributes and identification attributes will be captured

##### Instrumentation (Using SmartPlant 3D)

It is required that the following instrumentation components shall be modeled:

* All in-line field instrument, control valves, and on-off valves incl telecom field equipment
* All process connections for instrument on piping and equipment
* Offline field instrument as point locations, directional field equipment to show orientation
* Junction boxes, analyzer houses, and local control panels
* All major process attributes and identification attributes will be captured

##### Equipment (Using SmartPlant 3D)

Equipment models should be kept as simple as possible, while still showing a reasonable representation of the equipment for presentation and interference detection. Equipment parametric shall be used whenever possible.

* Equipment outline shapes and nozzles
* Ducts, platforms, support columns, and stacks
* Packaged equipment; outline of all tagged equipment and valves
* Major proprietary items and ancillary equipment
* Equipment envelops

The minimum attribute requirements for equipment are equipment number, insulation thickness, and a description. The minimum required nozzle attributes are tag number, size, pressure rating, end prep, and pipe material class. The equipment Tag numbers are to be the same as used by SmartPlant Foundation.

##### Civil/Concrete/Structure (Using SmartPlant 3D)

* Site: terrain
* Drainage and sewerage: routing outline and level, drips, manhole, catch basin, and pits
* Foundation: outline, top, and bottom level
* Concrete: outline, top, and bottom level
* Steel: beams, columns, bracings, fireproofing, all floors with top and bottom level, stairs, platforms, ladders, hoisting beams, and handrails. Major sections will have to be labeled. Welds and non-welded connections.
* Stanchion: outline
* Underground cable duct: routing, outline, and top and bottom level trenches
* Underground conduits: outline
* Buildings: outline incl doors, floors, ceilings, walls

##### Electrical (Using SmartPlant 3D)

* Outline for Electrical motors, lighting fixtures, junction boxes, cable routes (such as trays, trenches, and conduits), local control station, local panel, lighting panel, cable routing nodes and socket outlets, CCTV
* Transformers, sub-station: outline

##### Fire Protection/Fire-fighting Systems (Using SmartPlant 3D)

* Spray systems
* Fire ring main routing, hydrants, block valves, fixed water monitors, and hose reels
* Fire alarm push buttons
* Fire detectors
* Deluge valve manifolds

##### Miscellaneous (Using SmartPlant 3D)

* Safety showers and wash fountains
* Anodes, HVAC ducting and welds , Escape routes, handling and access routes

##### Deliverables (Using SmartPlant 3D)

It is required that the final project deliverables should include:

* Complete SmartPlant 3D Project Backup performed from the SmartPlant® 3D project management environment.
* Complete backup of the project “Symbols” share, including complete backup of the directory structures for drawings and reports created from SmartPlant 3D. This includes but is not limited to:
  + Customized/added symbol definitions
  + Symbol executables (.dll files)
  + Symbol source codes
  + SolidEdge files
  + Symbol 2D files
  + Drawing Border Files
  + Drawing View Style definitions
  + Drawing Label definitions
  + Final extracted Isometric Drawings
  + Ortho Drawings
  + Reports

Secondary Archive:

* ZIP file MSSQL server database backup files for Site, Site Schema, Plant Model, Catalog, and Catalog Schema
* All MS Excel Catalog Bulk load files for all disciplines. It is mandatory that these files reflect 100 percent of the catalog database (e.g. such that the entire catalog contents can be recreated by bulk loading these files).
* The following reports are required to be submitted:
  + Final IFC (Interference Check Report) Report
  + Final Database Consistency Check Report
  + Complete Catalog Verification Report
  + Final Project To-Do-List Report
  + Line List Report

##### Data Checks and Cleanup (Using SmartPlant 3D)

It is required that the contractor ensures that the delivered data are not corrupted and/or contain inconsistencies before delivery. As a minimum, the following checks and cleanup shall be performed:

General:

* Temporary files and directories shall be removed prior to delivery.
* SmartPlant 3D Database Consistency Check report should be run without reporting inconsistencies.
* The SmartPlant 3D To-Do-List shall be empty.

Piping:

* Piping isometrics must be extracted successfully for all pipelines. Lines that fail to extract correctly need to be modified prior to delivery.

#### Plant Design System (PDS 3D)

The following specification for 3D model delivery is written based on Intergraph’s PDS 3D software. Alternatively, *XX* will accept the use of Intergraph’s SmartPlant 3D software as the 3D modeling system.

##### Scope

The contractor is required to use {*state version*} of Intergraph’s PDS 3D software for the creation and management of the 3D model deliverables.

The contractor is required to use {*state version*} of RIS.

*XX* will inform the contractor the coordinates away from “0,0,0” to begin work.

*XX* will provide 3D model scope, deliverable specifications based on SmartPlant 3D prior to the beginning of the 3D modeling activities. However, should the contractor use PDS 3D, the following software is required:

* PDS Isogen for Isometric Drawings extraction
* SmartPlant Review for Design Review
* Orthoview/Orthogen for Ortho drawing generation using PDS data set

The contractor shall use ‘Mixed Units’ for PDS 3D project:

* Linear dimension = mm
* Nominal piping diameter = in
* Temperature = °F
* Pressure = psig

The contractor shall use following attributes to devise PDS 3D model names and descriptions:

* Discipline code
* PDS 3D module code
* PDS 3D Area Name
* Sequence Number

Reference library names shall be devised based on following attributes:

* Project name
* Default reference library file names
* PDS 3D project schema names will be as per
* Schema Type, such as pd, dd, ra, ru, re, ee, etc.
* Project name
* Design Area and Model breakdown for all disciplines as per the contractor’s best design practices. However, it should result in improved access and navigation throughout the PDS 3D models and database.

PDS 3D Graphic symbology for piping, equipment, structural, and electrical raceway models shall be reviewed and approved by *XX*.

The contractor is required to use his or her best design practices to determine additional attribute requirements for equipment, pipe segment, etc.

PDS 3D reference data shall be customized for *XX* piping material classes, piping commodity data, instrument data, specialty data, tap data, piping assemblies, etc.

Relevant reference data libraries are required to be modified to accommodate the above customization.

It is required that pipe supports be modeled physically.

The contractor is required to use *XX* naming convention for equipment, nozzles, line number, line ID, columns, beams, etc. This naming convention should be using the full tag number and be identical to that in SmartPlant Foundation.

The modeling extent will be per *XX* requirement as given below and per contractor’s best practices.

The contractor is required to as build the PDS 3D data before its final delivery based on *XX* as-built requirements.

**Modeling Requirements (Using PDS 3D)**

##### General (Using PDS 3D)

The 3D modeling system must be able to produce required documents, such as isometrics, plan, and elevation drawings, etc., as well as piping material, take-offs, etc. The main objective of the 3D modeling system must be the interference checking and data integrity to reduce the rework during construction to an absolute minimum. The modeling extent is as provided below (but is not limited to the below list).

##### Piping (Using PDS 3D)

The following piping and piping components shall be modeled:

* All process and utility piping including all piping components
* Valves including hand wheels, actuators, etc.
* Piping accessories, such as strainers and steam traps
* Underground piping
* Pipe supports
* Jacketed pipes
* Insulation outline
* All major process attributes and identification attributes will be captured

##### Instrumentation (Using PDS 3D)

* All in-line field instrument, control valves, and on-off valves
* All process connections for instrument on piping and equipment
* Offline field instrument
* Junction boxes, analyzer houses, and local control panels
* All major process attributes and identification attributes will be captured

##### Equipment (Using PDS 3D)

Equipment models should be kept as simple as possible, while still showing a reasonable representation of the equipment for presentation and interference detection. Equipment parametric shall be used whenever possible.

* Equipment outline shapes and nozzles
* Ducts, platforms, support columns, and stacks
* Packaged equipment; outline of all tagged equipment and valves
* Major proprietary items and ancillary equipment
* Equipment envelopes

The minimum attribute requirements for equipment are equipment number, insulation thickness, and description. The minimum required nozzle attributes are tag number, size, pressure rating, end prep, and pipe material class.

##### Civil/Concrete/Structure (Using PDS 3D)

* Site: terrain
* Drainage and sewerage: routing outline and level, drips, manhole, catch basin, pits
* Foundation: outline, top, and bottom level
* Concrete: outline, top, and bottom level
* Steel: beams columns, bracings, fireproofing, all floors with top and bottom level, stairs, platforms, ladders, hoisting beams, and handrails; major sections must be labeled
* Stanchion: outline
* Underground cable duct: routing, outline, and top and bottom level trenches
* Underground conduits: outline
* Buildings: outline

##### Electrical (Using PDS 3D)

* Outline for Electrical motors, lighting fixtures, junction boxes, cable routes (such as trays, trenches, and conduits), local control station, local panel, lighting panel, and socket outlets, CCTV
* Transformers sub-station: outline

##### Fixed Fire Protection/Fire-fighting Systems (Using PDS 3D)

* Spray systems
* Fire ring main routing, hydrants, block valves, fixed water monitors, and hose reels
* Fire alarm push buttons
* Fire detectors
* Deluge valve manifolds

##### Miscellaneous (Using PDS 3D)

* Safety showers and wash fountains

##### Deliverables (Using PDS 3D)

The final project deliverables should include:

* Complete PDS 3D Project Archive
* RIS Dump of all schemas used in the project

Secondary Archive:

* ZIP file of complete project directory structure, including reference data
* Oracle export file of PDS 3D project table-spaces used for the project
* Seed Files:
  + Piping Libraries
  + Label Description Library
  + Standard Note Library
  + Graphic Commodity (EDEN) Library
  + Physical Data Library (Dimensional Data)
  + Material Description Libraries
  + Short Description Library
  + Long Description Library
  + Specialty Description Library
  + Piping Assembly Library
  + Pipe Support Reference Libraries
  + Neutral and Object Data Files
  + Piping Materials Class (PMC) Data
  + Piping Commodity Data (PCD)
  + Piping Specialty Data
  + Instrument Data
  + Tap Properties (Tap Tables)
  + Size Dependent Material Data
  + Implied Material Data
  + Component Insulation Exclusion Data
  + Flange Insulation Exclusion Data
  + Construction Tolerance Exclusion Data
  + Isogons Options and Settings Files
  + Equipment Libraries
  + Graphic Library (EDEN Definitions)
  + Tutorial Definition Library (Forms Definitions)
  + Cell Libraries (If used)
  + Electrical Libraries
  + Structural Steel Libraries
  + HVAC Libraries
  + Drawing Border Files
* Plot Plan of PDS 3D Project Layout
* Final extracted Isometric Drawings
* Ortho Drawings generated from within PDS 3D; Contractor will provide OrthoView/Orthogen data sets and generated ortho drawings should they use Orthogen for generating ortho drawings.
* The following Reports are required to be submitted:
  + Final IFC (Interference Check Report) Report
  + Final Database Verification Report for all the models
  + RDB Conflict Report for all the models
  + Equipment Parametric Report.
  + Model List Report
  + Line List Report

##### Data Checks & Cleanup (Using PDS 3D)

The EPC contractor shall ensure that the delivered data is not corrupted and/or contains inconsistencies before delivery. As a minimum, the following checks and cleanup shall be performed:

General:

* All design files shall be compressed (MicroStation Compress Command).
* Temporary files and directories shall be removed prior to delivery.
* Complete Material Take Off (MTO) should be extracted for each model file prior to delivery, and the reports should be available in the appropriate report directories.

Piping:

* Piping isometrics must be extracted successfully for all pipelines. Lines that fail to extract correctly need to be modified prior to delivery
* PDS 3D Piping Design Database Verification should be run without errors for all piping models.

Structural:

* The Frameworks Verify Model command shall be run without errors on all models.
* Temporary FWP views shall be removed.
* Propagated model files shall be updated and compressed.

#### SmartPlant Review (SPR)

The contractor is required to use the 2010 version of Intergraph’s SmartPlant Review software for interactively reviewing and analyzing large, complex 3D models of process and power plants. The software shall provide all the visualization tools needed to review designs during engineering, construction, or maintenance.

The acceptable 3D model deliverables are Intergraph’s PDS 3D models or Intergraph’s SmartPlant 3D models provided in Intergraph’s SmartPlant Review software VUE format to ensure integrity and protection of the 3D project data and to help secure information that may be proprietary.

The software shall be capability of reviewing models within the engineering office environment or at a construction site. Display the entire plant model and associated data at any remote location, totally independent of the office network. The software will support a modular approach to additional functionality.

##### Scope

The SmartPlant Review visualization environment is for interactively reviewing designs and identifying problems graphically. Real-time, high-resolution dynamic navigation enables a variety of 3D motions, including:

* Walk-throughs
* Fly-throughs
* Fly-arounds
* Other path or dynamics-based motions via mouse or joystick
* Encircle an object
* Set far and near clipping planes

Access associated project data by moving the mouse over objects in the view. Include the following abilities:

* Associate tags or markers with objects, and save the view for later recall
* Display object attributes
* Associate new text and data with an object
* Query objects-based attribute data
* Measure and display the minimum distances between any two objects
* Save and recall attribute queries
* Allow attribute queries to control view display

Attach external data file to any object. External data files include but are not limited to:

* Notepad – Data annotation
* Microsoft Word – Datasheets
* Animations – Maintenance instructions
* Microsoft Excel – Material lists

The software will also support a basic viewer and a modular approach to adding functionality. Add-on modules will include:

* Construction module for dynamic collision detection, creating image files, and overall project management. Also include integration of the project schedule by linking to Primavera or Microsoft Project planning tools.
* API module with programming libraries to customize SmartPlant Review for your enterprise
* Collaboration module that allows multiple users to view and interrogate the model simultaneously
* On-site Drawing Generation modules for intelligent hidden-line display
* Photo Realism module takes still images to a new level of quality
* Point Cloud Integrator module for the display of existing condition data captured from laser scanners
* Simulation and Visual Effects for creating animations and motion studies

##### Deliverables

Deliverables will include:

* Snapshots of the main view at any size
* High-quality still images
* Camera motion animations
* Constructability plans
* Crane and heavy equipment and rigging studies
* At defined milestones full model view files

#### SmartSketch

##### Scope

The contractor is required to use the 2009.1 version of Intergraph’s SmartSketch software for the creation and management of any 2D CAD drawing types that are not covered by the other SmartPlant suite of tools. Such drawings may include but are not limited to plot plans, site layouts, equipment details, mounting details, cable tray reports, etc.

The contractor is required to follow the best engineering design practices for setting up the drawing environment, including directory structures, file naming conventions, and the location of symbol libraries and other reference data.

Border templates and title blocks for the 2D CAD drawings are required to be customized by the contractor to match *XX* border standards. The contractor is required to check with *XX* for the availability of these borders to avoid having to reproduce them before any work begins.

It is required that the contractor review and follow all *XX* CAD drafting standards, including standard fonts, line styles, hatching styles, etc., when creating 2D CAD drawings with SmartSketch.

The contractor is required to provide a legend sheet of all symbols used with a description of the use of that symbol.

##### Deliverables

The contractor is required to provide a back up of all SmartSketch drawing files, including any associated reference data (symbols, drawing templates, report templates, etc.) and any linked files (office documents, images, etc.)

The above electronic deliverables must be made available on DVD or CD media with an index of contents.

#### SmartPlant Materials

##### Scope

The contractor is required to use 2008.10 version of (to be used with the 10.2.0.5 version of ORACLE RDBMS) as the delivery mechanism for the following information:

* Detailed specifications, coding, and numbering of purchased components across all disciplines
* Piping classes and details with sufficiently granular data to feed either PDS 3D or SmartPlant 3D
* Purchase order details, including detailed supplier information and vendor data/drawing requirements
* Spare parts information (detailed specifications and coding, purchase order/supplier details, and on-hand inventory details)

SmartPlant Materials will be provided pre-configured to the contractor based on *XX* data handover requirements.

It is required that the contractor uses SmartPlant Materials as the master library for all standard components and piping classes. Furthermore, it is required that the contractor maintains the standard (piping, electrical steel structure..) component and class data in a level of detail that permits to entirely populate the PDS 3D and SmartPlant 3D reference databases.

It is recommended that the contractor use SmartPlant Materials as the master library for all other disciplines as well as piping.

It is required that the contractor uses SmartPlant Materials as the record keeper for all suppliers, purchase orders, contracts, and associated vendor data requirements (VDR).

Furthermore, it is required that the contractor uses SmartPlant Materials as the keeper of handover-relevant inventory information, such as spare parts inventories.

##### Deliverables

*XX* will decide what types of business objects and/or documents will be delivered progressively by the contractor. The contractor is required to transfer business objects and documents to *XX* when requested.

An opportunity (in terms of time and cost savings) is that no data or documents are actually delivered if *XX* hosts the SmartPlant Materials® implementation.

*XX* to identify the type of information to be delivered at each stage or delivery milestone.

# SOURCE: vendor_hexagon\Wiring.pdf

Develop Detailed Wiring - Level 2 Diagram

Intergraph Proprietary Information

Develop Detailed Wiring - Level 2

1.  As a member of the instrumentation department, I need to design instrumentation that require electrical supply.
2.  As a member of the instrumentation department, I need to publish the electrical power element browser so that the power requirements of the instrumentation and cabinets can be included in the electrical wiring design.
3.

If the document has publishable data, the document and the data are loaded into SmartPlant Foundation and are subsequently available for use by other applications. If the document does not have publishable data, it is available for viewing in
SmartPlant Foundation.

4.  As a member of the electrical department, I need to retrieve the electrical power element browser as published by SmartPlant Instrumentation so that I can use this information when developing the cable block diagrams and wiring diagrams.

  See the following Integration Capability Statement for more information:

  Sharing Electrical Power Requirements between Instrumentation and Electrical

5.  As an electrical designer, I may develop the cable block diagrams so that I can show or connect cables between electrical equipment.
6.  As a member of the instrumentation department, I need to design the electrical I/O assignment and wiring.
7.  As a member of the instrumentation department, I need to publish the electrical I/O so that it can be included in the electrical wiring design. The published document will include the process interlocks and process I/Os.
8.

If the document has publishable data, the document and the data are loaded into SmartPlant Foundation and are subsequently available for use by other applications. If the document does not have publishable data, it is available for viewing in
SmartPlant Foundation.

9.  As a member of the electrical department, I need to retrieve the electrical I/O signal data that was published by SmartPlant Instrumentation so that I can use it to develop the wiring diagrams.

  See the following Integration Capability Statement for more information:
  Sharing I/O Signal Data between Electrical and Instrumentation

10.  As an electrical designer, I need to develop the wiring diagrams so that this information can be incorporated in the electrical schematics which are used to connect equipment.
11.  As an electrical designer, I need to further develop the electrical schematics (Rev IFC) by incorporating the wiring information.
12.  As a member of the electrical department, I need to publish the electrical schematics (Rev IFC) to SmartPlant Foundation for use by downstream users.
13.  If the document has publishable data, the document and the data are loaded into SmartPlant Foundation and are subsequently available for use by other applications. If the document does not have publishable data, it is available for viewing in

SmartPlant Foundation.

Enterprise Work Process for Electrical Design

Digitizing Projects with SmartPlant Enterprise
Fluor is Shaping Change with a corporate wide initiative named “Unison” to digitize all aspects
of the Engineering, Procurement, Fabrication and Construction activities on projects.

• What is Fluor’s Unison?
• What is Hexagon’s Digital Transformation?
• Hexagon’s Digital Transformation meets Fluor’s Unison
• Fluor’s SmartPlant Enterprise Integrated Environment
• Fluor’s Data Centric Engineering Framework
• Mine SmartPlant Enterprise Data for Knowledge

• Use a Collective Database as the Standard
• Use IBM Watson and Hexagon PPM as advisors
• Use Predictive Analytics to evaluate data
• Project Execution with Business Intelligence
• Cloud Based Software and Data Distribution

3

What is Fluor’s Unison?
• Unison is not a tool or software, it is a new way of working to enhance digital intelligence.

• Unison is an integral part of Fluor’s drive towards data-

centric execution. It is data standardization, consistent
work processes and the capture and application of
historical data to achieve accurate forecasting for more
predictable project outcomes.

• Unison provides the following benefits:

• Standardized Data
• Comprehensive Estimating
• Informed Planning
• Predictive Analysis
• Automated Reporting

• Unison complies with Hexagon’s Digital Transformation
rules as a guide to create Project Wide Data Centricity

Standardized Data Collection and Governance
• These Standard Data Types were identified as needed to facilitate Comprehensive Estimating,

Informed Planning, Predictive Analysis and Automated Reporting

Standard Data Types in the Historical Collective Database
• Work Breakdown Structures

• Estimating Templates

• Reference Plant Types

• Engineering Effort Hours

• Cost Account Categories

• Subcontract Unit Rates

• Project Scheduling Data Points

• Construction Unit Effort Hours

• Direct and Indirect Field Cost

• A strong data governance program to oversee the overall management of the availability,

usability, integrity and security of the data types employed in a project

9

Unison Data Governance Program
• A sound data governance program includes a governing body or council, a defined set of

procedures, and a plan to execute those procedures.

• Quality – The right data, context, accuracy, format,

place and purpose

• Standards – Identifies reference and master data
attributes with one set of definitions and usage

• Data Stewardship – Manages data assets and

enforces policies  across projects and applications

• Practices & Work Instructions – Govern Data
modeling, naming, retention, quality and security
